text:
In our time, when the service of mankind has appeared scuba diving and other modern equipment, allowing to descend to the bottom of any reservoirs, the finds of the pyramids under water no longer surprise anyone.
It turns out that the famous pyramids of Ancient Egypt are not as lonely as it seemed 150 years ago.
There are dozens of them on our planet and not only on the surface, but also on the bottom of various reservoirs.
In 1936, Dr. F. Morgan noticed pyramids at the bottom of Lake Rock, located near Meryland, United States.
A group of scuba divers thoroughly examined the site and officially confirmed that these are real pyramids.
Some of them have a square base, others have a rectangular base.
The vertices of all are “cut off ”.
In 1968, American pilot R. Brash, flying over the Bahamas of Bimini and Andros, accidentally noticed under water sufficiently long stone structures.
After this sensational communication, an archaeological expedition was sent to these islands, equipped with the most modern underwater equipment.
And as it turns out, it's not in vain.
On the ocean floor, numerous ruins of large buildings were discovered; interesting pyramids with a base of 54 by 42 m; well-distinguishable streets; strong walls surrounding the city on all sides; a port with giant breakwaters; mysterious circles of large boulders, etc.
In 1973, from the ship "Academician Petrovsky" took some excellent pictures, which clearly view the block structures at the bottom, erected by representatives of some unknown civilization 360 kilometers from the coast of Portugal.
In 1985, near the coast of Jonaguni, K. Aratake, a diving instructor, suddenly saw an unusual object.
It was a huge building that went far away.
The plates were wide and covered with ornaments of various geometric shapes.
From the top there was a clear descent, the stairs went deep.
Since the object was near the shore, which was well known to Aratake, he knew that in this place the depth was about 27 m. Near the wall there was a fairly deep trench, it stretched along the entire object.
All parts of this object together were surprisingly similar to the famous design of the stepped pyramids of Ancient Shumer.
On these ruins in the scientific world there are still no violent disputes, international debates and conferences.
They are attended by world-class science luminaires.
However, scientists do not recognize the artificial origin of the object, calling it "a game of nature".
Although the structure has polished planes, smooth arcs and straight ribs.
In addition, the complex was destroyed in the rock.
Well-known specialist in the geology of the seas and oceans, prof.
M. Kimura believed in the artificial origin of the object.
And it turned out to be right.
The Scuba Expedition confirmed his hypothesis.
Subsequently, a group of archaeologists, commissioned by Discovery Channel, descended to the bottom, found a piece of sculpture, a human head decorated with feathers.
It was like statues of indigenous people in Central America.
Numerous analyses were carried out using the most modern technologies and it was found that the mysterious object off the shores of Jonaguni Island and the sanctuary of the Machu Picchu Inca have the same structure.
The builders used L-shaped blocks in the construction of the corners.
This technology of ancient masters allowed them to create beautiful buildings and structures that survived numerous earthquakes and centuries of the strongest hurricanes and storms.
Scientists are still solving the mystery they were given by the discovery off the coast of Jonaguni.
How could a culture spread at the foot of the Andes leave its mark on another continent?
In 1991, the oceanographer W. Meyer, speaking at a conference in Freeport, mentioned in his report that when surveying the bottom of the so called.
The "Bermuda Triangle", right in the center at a depth of 610 m, found two huge pyramids, above the Heops pyramid.
And the most incredible thing he claims was that they were built in the middle of the twentieth century.
The technology of construction works on Earth is unknown, the building material is similar to heat-resistant or armored glass.
Meyer presented a detailed study report.
In conclusion, he said that this year he was organizing a new expedition to the pyramids.
More than a quarter of a century has passed since then, but no reports of the expedition to the "insidious triangle" have been received.
Early research results are classified.

Text: Xu Xiuhao We all began to learn to write essays from a young age, but only when individuals entered the field of practice did we really know what the essays should be.
Recently, the topic of “If I Have a New Fridge ” has attracted considerable debate. I share my thoughts as a Chinese and English teacher, who is the favorite in Chinese and English, with 20 years of experience in English writing (lawyers are writing Chinese and English documents every day), and parents of students in bilingual schools (we can observe how foreign English teachers teach their essays).
Honest advice: Chinese literature and education, should refer more to the basic requirements of the English world for the writing of articles.
It sounds strange to refer to the requirements of English writing to practice Chinese writing, but it is not at all.
Because writing is only a matter of thought, the emphasis is on how the author presents and conveys ideas, the requirements of English writing in this respect are far more mature than that of Chinese writing.
Chinese writing training is a study of literature, does not require structure, English writing is the opposite of your previous school experience in university.
The structure of the article is "undefined law", and students know the basic concept of "starting and ending", but do not strictly require the structure of the article.
In short, Chinese writing is a study of literature, does not require a structure, and does not attach much importance to "communication" and "readers."
On the contrary, the structure of writing is the first lesson of writing from a small age, because the most basic purpose of writing is to communicate effectively with the "reader" and convey your thoughts (this can refer to countless English writing guides).
I remember more than 20 years ago, when I first entered the school, I asked a senior, she was studying LL.M. (Master of Laws) in a famous American school, and I was able to read nearly 100 pages of English literature every day. Do something else?
She shrugs her shoulders and says that she often doesn't finish reading at all, but English writing has its rules. If there is no time, each article should generally look at the first paragraph and the last paragraph carefully, and each paragraph only looks at the first and last lines.
Of course, the ability to read this way is not as good as that, but it is often enough to do so.
It sounds like a fluke approach, but knowing more about English writing makes sense.
That is, because English writing emphasizes "architecture", emphasizes "communication" with "readers", and conveys your ideas to the reader, the overall structure of each article serves this purpose.
The first sentence of each paragraph is a topic sentence. The last sentence is a small conclusion, which echoes the main point of the paragraph, and each paragraph is focused on only one key point.
Every sentence in the full text must be made sure that the reader follows and understands it.
So if an English article is titled "Pyramid - The Miracle of Human Civilization", the first paragraph will emphasize the special nature of the pyramid, and why it is a miracle of human civilization (conclusion and emphasis).
In the next few paragraphs, from different points of view, support for the pyramids is a miracle of human civilization (support argument). For example, the first paragraph says that it is "huge in structure, not seen in the past", the second paragraph says "engineering difficulties, how to apply it to date", and the third paragraph says "Mummies with mystery, curses with strange culture," and finally concludes that the pyramid is a miracle of human civilization.
After each sentence, each sentence must support or call the subject (a more detailed supporting argument), for example, the first paragraph says "massive", and every sentence in the envelope must give the supporting details of how the pyramid is "massive" from the long, wide and high levels.
The above requirements for writing in English seem to be for training graduate students. In fact, on the contrary, English writers have been learning this stuff almost from an early age.
I look at my daughter ’ s English work in bilingual schools from the beginning of childhood, which is usually a short text, and then ask the student to critically read, and then ask: What is the thrust of this article? What is the topic sentence?
What are the arguments that support this claim? What are the main points the author wants to convey? What is the purpose of the author, is it to convince, explain, sigh, or otherwise?
The answer is usually not in the context of a mysterious word, but in the context of an article.
When the reader is familiar with this writing, he or she will have to use the same standard for later writing, so that the article can be clearly understood.
The ultimate value of the article is based on the obvious benefits of effectively communicating with the reader in this way — forcing the author to think, what is said: you want to say clearly at the beginning, don ’ t shy away from the ambiguity of the conclusion; your claim must be supported by arguments, don ’ t confuse the reader with beautiful and unclear words, and you must think before writing.
In addition to the sophistication of the text, this kind of writing requires more emphasis on the training of thinking.
You don't have the ability to think. You just put the words together and don't even call it writing.
Such an English writing requirement, after an individual starts working as a lawyer, is applied to any writing in both Chinese and English, including written pleadings and legal opinions in both Chinese and English.
So any manuscript takes ten seconds to see the conclusion, return the manuscript; there are empty claims without supporting the argument, return the manuscript; the text is beautiful but can not understand what you want to say, return the manuscript...
Documents produced without such strict requirements are worthless, because customers do not understand, do not pay, judges do not understand, the documents have no role in persuading or supporting our position.
The ultimate value of the article should be based on whether it can effectively communicate with the reader.
Looking back at the subjects of Chinese essays such as the past academic tests / HKCEE, we can see that their problems are there.
Many of these topics have no direction, stress on life or art, the author's imagination or monologue, but do not know what to expect students to write, or what to think about.
The article entitled "If I Have a New Fridge" is attached with two reference articles.
By writing the author's point of view in a well-versed Chinese language, I do not see what the two words are intended to express, nor do I draw the main points.
"Our feelings are the refrigerator... cold, but very fresh," Yun Yun, what does that mean?
"A big new refrigerator is just a refrigerator, it is a kind of imagination, a kind of imagination, a kind of possibility."
In an earlier review of Wu Meng-tou's phrase, "Where are you feeling sad?" and the overall style of his work, Wu Meng-tou said, "The words of Wu Meng-tou, like the Seven Treasures Tower, dazzle your eyes, break them down, and break them down." (Beautiful appearance, but slightly analyzed, no specific content).
The same comment applies to many subjects and templates written in different countries – vague, contemptuous, and unrealistic.
If students are familiar with and proud of this writing style, they may not even be able to write the instructions for the use of electrical products in the future.
Examinations guide teaching and teaching guide students' thinking ability.
My favorite Chinese ancient texts teach me culture and thinking, but with the practical and clear language of writing, I truly feel that the common requirements of writing in English will benefit students more than a day.
The stone of his mountain can be conquered, but the kingdom is chosen by the people.

MOSCOW, 27 Apr — RIA Novosti, Tatiana Pichugina.
The search for alien living matter in meteorites is not very popular in the scientific community.
It is believed that in millions of years space will destroy any organic matter.
However, not everyone agrees with this view.
Correspondent RIA Novosti visited the sector of astrobiology in JINR (Dubna), where they are looking for evidence of extraterrestrial origin of life.
Astrobiology at the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research began last year.
I am met by the head of the sector, academician Alexey Rozanov, and in the past, Director of the Paleontological Institute of the Russian Academy of Sciences.
First of all, it shows an illustrated atlas "Fossil bacteria" with many photographs of modern and ancient microorganisms, traces of which are found in rocks.
And explains that 20 years ago, he became the ancestor of a new direction in science — bacterial paleontology.
This was prompted by the work of NASA's American researcher David McKay, published in 1996.
McKay suggested that the stone meteorite ALH84001 contains structures very similar to microorganisms.
The meteorite ALH84001 was found in the ice of Antarctica in 1984, where it fell about 13,000 years ago.
Isotope composition indicates its Martian origin.
Researching the sample using a scanning electron microscope, scientists discovered an elongated body resembling a bacterium.
To count it as fossilium, that is, a fossil trace of a living being, did not allow the size of a few dozen nanometers, which is even smaller than the nucleus of any living cell.
Although the scientific community was extremely skeptical of the NASA message, they had followers on both sides of the ocean.
Proud cosmic mystery Young employees of the laboratory Anton Ryumin and Mikhail Kapralov work behind the scanning electron microscope.
They are studying samples of the Orgeus meteorite transferred to Dubna by the Marshall Space Center and the Foundation for Planetary Research (USA).
The meteorite Orgeus fell in southern France on 14 May 1864 with numerous witnesses.
Its composition was immediately analyzed and noted with surprise the high carbon content.
The press leaked information about organized beings allegedly found in the debris.
According to contemporaries, due to public resonance, the stones were examined by the famous biologist Louis Pasteur, but did not find signs of living.
A century later, in 1961, American scientists Niji and Henessy again turned to Orgeus samples and identified there hydrocarbon compounds that could be relevant to the life of microorganisms.
They later reported organized elements in Orgea and the Ivuna meteorite found in Tanzania in 1938.
Like the French meteorite, Ivuna contains a lot of carbon in the form of rounded structures — chondron.
Microscopic organized elements in the meteorite substance were very similar to dinoflagellates — the simplest single-cell organisms.
But in a detailed study, they turned out to be a modern organic matter that was randomly included in the samples.
This discussion about fossils in meteorites has been quiet for a while.
In the late 1970s, Orgeus again drew attention.
It found nitrogenous bases - uracil, adenine, guanine, which are part of RNA and DNA.
But by that time, scientists had already known that the primers of life were easily synthesized in a test tube, and they were probably formed in the process of wandering a meteorite in space.
Academician Rozanov also decided to explore Orgy using the latest electronic microscope.
He saw formations there, in form reminiscent of cyanobacteria and other microfossils about 20 micrometers in size.
According to the scientist, the scientific community is not ready to accept these results due to the inertia of thinking.
"There is a lot of liquid water in the pores of Orgeus - a key factor for microorganisms," explained the co-author of academician Richard Hoover, visiting professor of the center of astrobiology of the University of Buckingham (UK), in the past - an employee of the NASA astrobiological laboratory.
According to Hoover, microorganisms could have been preserved in Orgea because it was part of a comet kernel protected by a solid outer crust.
Wandering millions or even billions of years in space, the comet approached the Sun, warmed up and exploded.
One of its debris fell into the field of attraction of the Earth and eventually collided with the surface.
If there was something alive in it, it died many million years before the fall, absorbed by minerals.
The scientific community is very skeptical about the discussion around the traces of microorganisms in meteorites, which unfolded in the 1960s, is associated with the imperfection of analytical technology of those years, believes Dmitry Badiukov, head of the laboratory of meteoritics of GEOCHI RAS.
In subsequent years, scientists found out that spheroides — as they call rounded structures similar to microorganisms — are naturally formed in the substance of some meteorites.
"All structures that resemble organelles may be of inorganic origin.
They have nothing to do with life, "the scientist comments RIA Novosti.
According to him, the meteorite is bombed by harsh galactic radiation.
Any organic matter under its influence over time is completely destroyed.
"The plastic put into orbit turns into a trumpet in a few years.
And the average life of the meteorite in space is ten to fifteen million years and more, "explains Badiukov.
It is possible to determine traces of fossil microorganisms in meteorites only by very accurate analytical methods: isotope, chromatography combined with mass spectrometry.
"We're in the lab studying mineralogy, meteorite petrography.
We decipher the history of their formation.
We have a lot of experience, and we wouldn't have looked at something unusual.
I am 99.9% sure that there are no trace of microorganisms in meteorites, "the scientist said.

Image copyright Getty Images Recently, I went to Japan to witness a ceremony.
The ceremony took place 1300 years ago in Japan and is now the 66th.
During the festival, the prince's concubine led a group of priests of the Taoism, moving objects from the old shrine to the newly built shrine.
Since the 7th century AD, the Ise Grand Shrine has been rebuilt on a prototype basis every 20 years.
The temple is one of the many rituals of the Church of God.
The construction of the shrine is easy to damage and the reconstruction of the shrine will not only preserve the integrity of its distinctive buildings, but also facilitate the next generation of craftsmen to learn the construction techniques.
Japan has many of the oldest companies in the world.
The country has a unique love for the restoration, and thus has been able to maintain the shrine and ritual for thousands of years.
In these countries, many artifacts and buildings created by people can be preserved for a long time.
Every piece of work brings us some inspiration.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The Shrine of Ise, Japan, is rebuilt every 20 years (Credit: Alamy) In the past 20 years, I have worked with the Long Now Foundation to build a “Halloween Clock ” with computer scientist Danny Hills and a team of engineers to warn people of the scale of the monument. Focus on long-term considerations.
Our idea is to create a clock with a large scale and a long span of time. When faced with it, it will provoke people to think about the long-term future.
We can imagine that it would be very difficult to solve the problem of climate change within five years, because the time span is too small.
But if the time span is adjusted to 500 years, then even the most unlikely thing is starting to become easy to handle.
To build a machine with a lifespan of 10,000 years, make reference to past and present experiences to fully understand how relics are preserved. Although we can slow down the clock, we can control the length of human life by ticking the pointer within 10,000 years.
But what kind of materials do you need to build? For the past 20 years, I have been studying the structures of other surviving buildings and exploring the secrets of their durability.
And do what I can to see a part of it in person.
Some sites have been preserved for reasons such as being forgotten or buried; others have been preserved in the eyes of people because of their sheer quality; others have applied many mechanical strategies.
Few man-made objects and tissues can survive for centuries, let alone thousands of years.
Only stories, myths, religions, and a small number of organizations can be preserved for such a long time.
The preservation of buildings and cultural relics is not intentional, but rather an accident of history.
Nuclear waste sites, genetic genealogy and seed storage banks have been established, with the intention of setting the time span for millennia.
We have learned a lot from history and current experience.
Such as material science, engineering, and even ideological consciousness.
I would like to mention a few of these points when it comes to the inspiration for us to build the Millennium Bell.
The loss and resurrection of some of the most unique and meaningful cultural relics in history is not a deliberate effort to preserve them.
On the contrary, people have lost it and found it again by chance.
For example: Dead Sea Scrolls, Rosetta Stone, Antikythera Device, etc., could not have been preserved without the initial loss.
Antiquicilla machinery has a special meaning for me, because it is also a planetary clock, and it is very ahead of time, several centuries ahead of that era.
A 2000 year old shipwreck was discovered near Antiquicera, Greece, with a large number of oxidized transmission devices, later Antiquicera devices.
No similar device was found at that time.
The technique of the Anticyzilla device, the movement of the gears and the understanding of the motion of the object are remarkable.
Because of many views and mechanical principles, it was not until 1300 that Europe began to emerge from the Middle Ages that the day could be revisited.
In addition, the construction of such facilities requires many iterations.
As a result, people have not found other similar equipment, which is a deeper puzzle.
We can only explain this: we can see the device now because it was lost at the time.
When X-rays were used to study it, it was revealed that the true identity, an effective astronomical model, was dimmed by Antiquizilla devices in storage for decades before they were discovered to be complex and useful.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The Antiquizilla device is an ancient mechanical device built in the 2nd century BC (Credit: Getty Images). In addition, we have an inspiration that mechanical devices are more suitable for future archaeologists' self-recording than electronic devices.
It is not hard to imagine that, after 2000, it would be difficult to speculate on the use of our current electronic equipment in the Mediterranean Sea.
To a large extent, this is why we have to build mechanical clocks.
Assuming that only a few accessories can remain for 10,000 years, the use of the device can be predicted by its mechanical structure, and it can be repaired if necessary.
The dominant cities in remote areas are mobile.
A city can prosper for more than a thousand years.
However, every 10 years, the meaning of the city changes.
Every piece of cultural relics that was cast in the city a thousand years ago, and every piece that survived, has countless unsurvived, destroyed by war, change, and accident.
In contrast, the preservation of these products is more advantageous in remote areas.
Spectacular sites such as Petra and Machu Picchu can be preserved because they are far from modern cities.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The Global Seed Caption is located on a mountain in the Svalbard Islands.
A few years ago, I went to the global seed bank in the Svalbard Islands, located at 78 degrees north latitude, the northernmost point of human settlement.
This is where the seeds of the world's crops are stored, and at the time of the design, it was hoped that the seed bank would be used for a thousand years.
During the construction process, the designers did not realize that the seed bank would arouse the curiosity of the world.
The global seed bank was not designed to receive visits, and dignitaries flew from all over the world, but could only visit its exterior.
And I set the time for the seeds to be stored twice a year, and then the door of the seed bank will open.
On the record of the visitors, I see the names of previous delegations, including former US President Jimmy Carter, former UN Secretary General Ban Ki-moon, and others.
Geographical location makes it a more curious presence.
Remote areas are not self-driving attractions.
It takes a lot of time to get to these places, and it takes a lot of time to get to these places.
It takes a certain amount of time to travel to these places, both round and round.
We set the site for the CenturyLink in West Texas, a few hours' drive from the airport, and a day's walk.
The purpose of the Millennium Plan is to change the way people think about time.
A remote location gives people a certain amount of time to think about, as well as a certain amount of expectation.
The distance of the isolation allows the perpetual clock to be well preserved and to be quite mysterious.
Many well-preserved cultural relics have spent most of their time underground.
The underground environment protects cultural relics from sunlight and usually has a very stable temperature. High and low temperatures accelerate the process of oxidation and aging of cultural relics.
In fact, craftsmen perform rapid aging tests mainly by repeatedly lifting and lowering temperatures (which also involve chemical reactions, as discussed below).
The magnificent tombs in Luxor, Egypt, the cavernous mural of Lascaux in the valley of Lessege in the south-western French province of Dordoña, and exquisite art works such as the Dead Sea Scrolls have been preserved underground for thousands of years.
It is no wonder that today's global seed banks, nuclear waste disposal depots, and various archives are all built below the surface.
Image copyright The Long Now Foundation Image caption The Long Now Foundation is installing part of the Credit: The Long Now Foundation.
I have visited nuclear waste depots in the United States and Europe, and I have visited seed depots in the world, the Mormon archives, and so on.
Water is unstoppable, as it has always been for centuries.
How to successfully reconcile the conflict with water?
The answer is: Stops are less rusty.
For thousands of years, the ancient Asian rice farmers have carefully drawn water, and the rice fields have proved their success in water management.
It is not only necessary to keep the clocks in place, but also requires precision in timing.
Metals will shrink due to temperature changes, so we need a clever design to keep the length of clocks and other accessories consistent, so as to ensure the precision of the clocks when they go.
The smaller the temperature changes around the clock, the more accurate the timing will be.
However, there is a waterproof problem in almost every underground storage site I have visited.
Therefore, consideration must be given to how this problem can be solved.
Our underground base is built on the top of the mountain to avoid underground drainage areas as far as possible. Even so, we think water will seep in.
To avoid this possibility, we will use clocks as a center to allow the surface to tilt around, and water can flow through the bottom of the foundation without blocking it anywhere.
We can't stop the flow, but we can choose where to lead it.
Selection of materials I have consulted many materials scientists on what materials can create objects that have been maintained for thousands of years.
One of them made a very convincing point of view, saying, "Everything is burning at different speeds." The aging in our eyes is actually the oxidation that the scientific community says.
It's like being rusty.
Most people think of precious metals such as stones and gold, which are not easy to oxidize.
However, if the chemical environment at the time of storage is appropriate, even the corpse can remain intact for thousands of years.
The mummies of Egypt are an example.
Recently, Armenia discovered a pair of well-preserved leather shoes, more than 5,500 years ago.
As a result, the life span of a material is often not entirely in itself, and the environment in which it is located is even more important.
In the case of leather shoes, it was found buried in a cave, covered with sheep manure, which provided a perfect oxygen-free environment and guaranteed temperature stability.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption This pair of Mokacin soft shoes has a history of 5,500 years and is said to be the oldest leather shoe in the world (Credit: Getty Images) and we are going to build the centuries-old clock to interact with people for thousands of years.
In this way, the environment is not very well controlled.
The oxygen needed for human respiration degrades the material, and the dust must be carried on people ’ s clothing, covering the skin with grease.
Therefore, the construction of this century-old bell, which is accessible to all, must be long enough in itself.
The bearing of watches and clocks may be the best example.
All rotating parts of the clock require a certain bearing surface for smooth rolling with minimal friction.
However, there are several problems with traditional bearings.
Conventional bearings are typically made up of a row of hard steel balls and two tracks (rollers) in a special shape.
For a long time, steel and even stainless steel bearings are simply bonded together.
If the electric potential of the two bearings is different, then there will also be metal electric couple corrosion.
If you have seen how coins are corroded on the metal surface, this is how the electric dolls are corroded.
In addition to these problems, common bearings require lubrication, which means regular maintenance and allows bearings to be contaminated with dust and grit.
More than 20 years ago, the project was established, and I started looking for suitable bearings.
We then found the perfect solution: all-ceramic bearings for satellites and spacecraft.
This type of bearing is made of industrial ceramics, with a hardness close to that of a diamond, and can achieve permanent, non-lubrication work in a vacuum environment in space.
At the time, there was only one problem with this kind of bearing: when it was known that there was such a thing, they were priced up to tens of thousands of dollars and used only for aerospace.
However, with the advancement of the centuries-old project, all-ceramic bearings are becoming more and more common.
It is now also used in roller skates and fingertips, at a cost of just $10 each.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The walls of the Taj Mahal have many treasures that can be easily removed, which helps to protect the building itself from being damaged by the predators (Credit: Getty Images) to extend its life span, as well as a strategy of sacrificing certain properties of the object itself.
We have seen this in nature as well: the attacked lizard breaks its tail and escapes.
Some sites have similar conditions.
For example, in the Egyptian Valley of Kings, the most well-preserved engraving and color painting was completed yesterday. Cemeteries often spend time on carrying gold objects out of their graves and feel they have scoured up the property and dug out the valuable ones.
However, the wall art in the tomb remains intact.
This is rare compared to other sites.
The walls of the Taj Mahal contain many precious stones, which protect the building itself. When the robbers remove the precious stones, they will think that they have stolen their wealth.
This brings an interesting question to the selection of the site.
Should we also create a "value layer" that is relatively easy to take away, and if it is stolen, it can also protect the clock from destruction for thousands of years?
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The transformation of Afghan ideology led to the tragedy of the destruction of the Bamiyan Buddha (Credit: Getty Images).
In recent years, the world's oldest remains have been destroyed simply by conflicts of values or ideologies.
The most heartbreaking among them is the Taliban's destruction of the Buddha of Bamiyan in Afghanistan.
There is no more harmless religious symbol than the Buddha, but it has become a threat to the Taliban, allowing them to spend weeks destroying stunning cultural relics on the edge of the cliff.
How, then, can we protect these values from being stolen or destroyed by people, which are linked to the city and which are quite culturally meaningful?
In fact, this is the most important problem in the construction of such things.
Engineering problems such as materials and operations are not difficult to solve, and human civilization is the real problem.
The civilization we hope for is a civilization that looks at the present and cares about the future.
We hope that the construction of the millennium will break the technical barriers and enhance people's moral outlook.
May it enable us to challenge ourselves and become the better ancestors of our children and grandchildren.
Ross is the executive director of the Everpresent Foundation in San Francisco, a project he and his colleagues are working on.
Please visit BBC Future to read the original English language.

American senators will carefully study the biography of the candidate for the post of CIA Director Gina Haspel, as stated by the Republican John McCain.
In particular, legislators intend to find out how Haspel treats torture during interrogation of suspects.
She is known to have run the CIA's "Cat Eye" torture prison.
Earlier, European human rights organizations even called on Berlin to arrest Hespel if it appeared in Europe.
Why Donald Trump did not stop on a more neutral candidate for the position of head of the special service — in RT material.
The U.S. Senate will carefully review the curriculum vitae of the candidate for the position of Director of the Central Intelligence Agency, Gina Haspel, before approving her appointment.
This was stated by Senator John McCain, Chairman of the Upper House Committee of the Congress for the Armed Forces.
The senator noted that the use of torture by intelligence agencies after the events of September 11 "wasted the precious moral authority" of the government.
McCain is certain that this was an erroneous decision, the consequences of which are still known.
At the same time, the senator added, according to the current legislation of the United States, torture is prohibited, and any candidate for the post of director of the CIA must unconditionally defend these norms.
“The torture of prisoners in US prisons over the past decade has been one of the darkest pages in American history, ” McCain said.
So Gina Haspel should explain to the senate how she participated in the CIA interrogation program.
The candidate for the post of head of department will also tell the senators about his views on torture.
Recall that on March 13, the head of the White House Donald Trump made personnel changes: Mike Pompeo, who held the post of director of the CIA for a year, was appointed to the position of Secretary of State, Trump proposed Deputy Pompeo - Gina Haspel for a vacant position at the head of the intelligence agency.
The director of the CIA for the first time in history will be a woman.
Gina Haspel was born on October 1, 1956.
According to official data, in 1985 she joined the CIA, where she was engaged in operational activities abroad, and also headed residences in a number of States.
For obvious reasons, most of the information related to Haspel's activities is classified.
American butchers However, according to the press, Haspel is involved in the operation of infamous CIA secret prisons abroad.
This became known after the publication of the report of the Special Intelligence Committee of the United States Senate in 2014 on the investigation of cases of torture by intelligence officers.
In 2002, Hespel was allegedly the head of a CIA torture prison in Thailand called the Cat's Eye, where, among others, suspected of having links to Al-Qaida were held.
See also: The new head of the CIA supervised torture in a secret prison in the United States Recall that after the attacks of September 11, 2001, the American special services threw all their strength to fight against one of the largest ultra-radical international terrorist organizations.
In order to formally circumvent national legislation prohibiting torture, the CIA has established a network of “secret prisons ” abroad, where torture — the so-called“ advanced interrogation technique ” — has been used against detainees.
The torture involved beatings, sleep deprivation for several days, music stunning-up, drowning imitation, freezing, drilling teeth and so on.
However, many of the people subjected to torture were not involved in terrorist activities.
The most famous CIA overseas prison is located at the US naval base in Guantánamo Bay.
Although in 2009 Barack Obama issued a decree to close all secret prisons, Guantánamo still operates — Donald Trump in January 2018 repealed his predecessor's decree to close it.
One of the first prisoners to be tortured after September 11 was Abu Zubeid, allegedly a senior al-Qaeda leader, near Osama bin Laden.
During interrogations, including those conducted at the Cat's Eye Prison, he was unable to confirm to the CIA that Al-Qaida had weapons of mass destruction.
He lost one eye as a result of prolonged torture.
The involvement of Gina Haspel in the organization of torture interrogations may not be limited to the management of one of the prisons.
On March 13, 2018, NSA ex-employee Edward Snowden announced on his Twitter that Haspel had played a "key role" in the torture system.
“The new director of the CIA played a key role in torture of suspects and in their illegal concealment.
Her name is indicated in a top-secret document, according to which all records (torture. — RT) must be destroyed in order not to get into Congress.
"Unbelievable," Snowden wrote.
In addition, last year, the German office of the European Centre for Constitutional Rights and Human Rights (ECCHR) requested the German authorities to issue an arrest warrant for Haspel in the case of torture.
According to Wolfgang Kalek, head of the human rights group, a senior CIA official should be arrested if she goes to Germany or Europe.
However, no response has yet been received from the German Public Prosecutor's Office to the request of human rights defenders.
"Exemplary Officer" In 2003, Hespel headed the office of José Rodríguez, Director of the CIA Counter - Terrorism Centre.
She continued to work with Rodriguez after he was promoted to the head of the National Secret Service.
In 2007, a scandal erupted around Rodriguez and his subordinate: according to the media, it was Hespel who led the destruction of evidence proving the use of torture by the Department of Torture in 2005, which involved hours of video recordings of interrogations.
Although the government investigation against CIA officials lasted about three years, no one was ultimately charged.
The authorities explained this by the expiration of the statute of limitations for the institution of criminal proceedings.
Condoleezza Rice © Nick Agro In torturing the detainees, CIA officers acted on orders from the George Bush administration.
According to the report of the Senate Intelligence Committee published in 2009, Condoleezza Rice, who was then presidential adviser on national security, gave the first permission to torture.
George W. Bush later admitted himself that he had allowed the intelligence services to resort to torture suspects.
After nearly a decade of democratic power in the United States, the new administration is ready to return to the old methods.
Under Barack Obama Haspel, although she did not go to trial, she faced a problem with promotion — in 2013, the Senate refused to approve her candidacy for the position of Director of the National Secret Service.
Haspel was in fact the head of the unit, but had to be satisfied with the post of Deputy Director of Service.
According to the experts, this was not due to the commitment of the Barack Obama administration to the principles of legality.
The differences between Democrats and Republicans are not strategic, but only tactical — the representatives of the Democratic Party try to act more veiled than the Republicans.
With the victory in the presidential election of the Republican career, Haspel again sharply went uphill - in early 2017, she became Deputy Director of the CIA, responsible for the collection and analysis of intelligence, counter-intelligence information, secret operations and relations with foreign intelligence services.
It should be noted that a number of high-ranking representatives of the United States Democratic Party opposed this appointment, remembering Hespel's alleged involvement in the organization of torture.
However, Haspel is formally an exemplary American citizen and has a number of state awards.
Her predecessor, Mike Pompeo, said that Haspel was "an exemplary intelligence officer and a loyal patriot".
In the opinion of the International Institute for Humanitarian and Political Studies expert Vladimir Bruter, Haspel will be appointed head of the CIA, despite the apparent concerns of the Democrats.
Today, people with very radical views, quietly occupy senior positions in the United States, the expert explained.
“On the part of the human rights community in the United States, the reaction is also very selective — only when it comes to violating the rights of those who are loved in America, ” added Brooter.“ In other cases, American human rights activists are silent or say that this is necessary for national security.
They know well when to open the mouth, and when to keep it closed. ”
CIA headquarters Experts differ in their views on the reasons why Trump was forced to appoint as CIA director a controversial figure like Gina Haspel.
According to Professor Dmitry Evstafiev, Hespel was actually the curator of the torture program and led secret prisons not only in Asia, but also in Europe.
In general, "her activities" pull "to the Hague Tribunal", says the expert.
“And the fact that she is, in fact, on the threshold of a prison where she can be sent at any time makes her very dependent on political leadership.
She will execute absolutely any order, and that's why she was appointed Director of the CIA.
In fact, it is about placing the CIA under the personal control of Trump and his environment, "said Dmitry Evstafiev in an interview with RT.
Vladimir Bruter believes that Trump did not stop on a more neutral candidate, primarily because he himself is an opponent of moderation in politics.
"Tillerson's resignation as Secretary of State is also linked to this trend.
Trump came to power with a team of people personifying a balanced approach and a national pragmatic course.
But he no longer needs them, Trump has taken a course on national hegemony, the focus will be on far-right Republicans, not on good professionals capable of dialogue.
The main feature of the new Secretary of State is that he is unable to negotiate at all, he has never done so.
But today ’ s focus is only on stiffness, turning into cruelty, ” concluded Vladimir Bruter.

Photo: pixabay.com, - said Jessica Barnes, a planetologist from the University of Arizona (USA) and one of the authors of the research. Over the past ten years, planetologists have discovered many evidence that on the surface of Mars once there were rivers, lakes and entire oceans of fresh water, which was theoretically suitable for the birth of life of the terrestrial type. According to scientists, there were about the same amount of water, as well as in the terrestrial Arctic Ocean.
Moreover, the total amount of water on the young Mars would be enough to cover its surface with a layer of moisture of 140 meters thick.
Where this water disappeared and when it appeared on the surface of the Red Planet, scientists can not yet accurately tell. These problems, as Barnes notes, were partly due to the fact that most of the water that was on Mars in the past, almost completely flew into space.
This drastically changed the isotope composition of water vapor in the Martian atmosphere, as a result of which scientists can not recognize its source and understand when water appeared on the surface of Mars. This could be learned from the meteorites that came to Earth from Mars.
However, these attempts also failed, as the ratio of hydrogen isotopes was very different in different celestial bodies.
From the standpoint of generally accepted theory, which describes their formation, this is impossible., Barnes explains. Studying the mineral and isotope composition of several Martian meteorites that formed in different epochs of existence of the Red planet — about 3.9 and 1.5 billion years ago, the authors of the article found that this is not really the case. Measurements showed that in both meteorites there were identical shares of hydrogen isotopes.
These data coincided with measurements for the modern surface of the planet, which were made by the Mars Rover Curiosity.
This was a surprise for geologists who expected the isotope composition of water in the near-surface rocks of Mars to change over the last 4 billion years. In an attempt to find an explanation for this coincidence, scientists analyzed the minerals they studied meteorites, NWA7034 and ALH84001, as well as other known fragments of the Mars cortex.
It turned out that all Martian meteorites consist of two types of volcanic rocks - "enriched" and "depleted" shergotites.
The ratio of hydrogen isotopes in both types was very different. The first type of rocks, as scientists explain, was more similar to the celestial bodies from the far edges of the solar system, in the subsoil of which there are large stocks of deuterium, a heavy hydrogen isotope.
The second type of minerals was closer to the earth rocks in relation to the proportions of hydrogen isotopes in the water stored in them. Such differences, as the calculations of planetologists showed, can be explained only if the mantle and bark of Mars were formed not from one source of rocks, but two different protoplanetary bodies that collided with each other or with the ancestor of the Red Planet in the first moments of life of the Solar System.
This fundamentally changes the picture of the evolution of Mars and other "neighbors" of the Earth, scientists conclude.

The idea that the chemical composition of the celestial bodies of the Solar System was unchanged turned out to be a myth.
This was discovered by researchers from the University of Lund (Sweden), who examined 43 space objects that fell to Earth 470 million years ago and were found in Russia.
Their article was published in Nature magazine.
In 2016, geologist Birger Schmitz found a meteorite in the Swedish career, named Österplana 065.
The scientist called the discovered celestial body "extinct" because of its unique chemical composition, which had not previously met in any other meteorite.
In addition, the object fell to Earth during the Ordovian period (485-443 million years ago).
This led the geologist to believe that the ancient meteorites were completely different.
Researchers working in the laboratory of astrogeobiology in Lund analyzed seabed deposits near the north-eastern shores of Russia.
With the help of acids, scientists dissolved several tons of sedimentary rocks to find chromium oxide grains that came to Earth as part of meteorites.
Geologists conducted chemical and isotope analysis to find out what type of space bodies related to grains were.
It turned out that the ancient meteorites were really very different from the modern ones.
According to scientists, this indicates that about 500 million years ago an event occurred in the Solar System, which affected the chemical composition of space objects.
However, that was exactly what it was, so far remains unknown.

Former U.S. Secretary of State Condoleeezza Rice recently published her memoirs under the pathos title "No Above Honour.
Memories of my years in Washington. "
The book aroused great interest among experts, who immediately began to analyze its content.
It turned out that Ms. Rice had a very strange and outrageous idea of the Russian people.
In her book, she makes a thick hint that Russians hate Georgians because of darker skin.
This is how Ms. Rice recalls one of her visits to the USSR, which she made in her youth.
"An ordinary man on the street without any hints will tell you that the troubled inhabitants of the Caucasus are thieves and bandits.
One time I stayed in a very lost Moscow hotel "Ukraine".
When I was coming back from dinner, the excited floor duty officer (in the Soviet Union, these elderly civilians were on duty all night, keeping an eye on the guests coming and leaving) told me that I had been asked by some Georgians.
She said her unpainted anti-Georgian tyrada without even noticing that I too am black. "
It is clear that Ms. Rice was unlikely to interrogate in a mass manner ordinary people on Moscow streets about their attitude towards Georgians.
She just transferred to most Russians the words of a hotel attendant, who, by the way, only tried to warn a young black girl about suspicious guests.
In order to finally dispel all doubts about his views, Rice told American journalists: “I think the Russians needed confrontation with Georgia.
I also explain in my book that many Russians had almost pathological hatred of Georgians... "
According to psychologists, Rice has a clear "transfer" of his problems to our country.
Little Condoleeezza's personality was formed against the background of racial segregation in the United States.
For example, her parents were forced to send her to a local school for black children.
The simple question is whether it is possible to imagine the existence of such institutions in the USSR or modern Russia?
When Condoleezza was eight years old, her high school friend died when a racist bomb exploded at an African-American Sunday school.
But that's not all.
Rice recalls many cases of harassment related to the color of her skin - in the store when buying things, she was forced to use a storage room, not a fitting room, she was not allowed to enter the circus in the recreation park, she was denied admission to hotels and even, according to her, served unedible food in restaurants.
I would like to remind the Secretary of State that all this happened to black people in the United States, not Georgians in the USSR and Russia.
The era of segregation in the United States deeply crashed Rice into memory and hardly added love to white people.
She probably still sees a hidden threat in them.
Everything that happened to her does not allow the former Secretary of State to understand that the history of other countries was different.
And when in the United States hung signs "only for white", the USSR was headed by Georgians.
So today's political disagreements between Russia and Georgia are not about racism.
Therefore, to stand on the side of the official Tbilisi because of the color of the skin is simply ridiculous and inappropriate.
In addition, Georgians are not black at all, but quite white people according to any racial standards.
The “AN ” remains only to sympathize with our diplomats who deal with such dull Western politicians.
Unfortunately, they sometimes reach the very top of power – representing Rice M. Gorbachev, President Bush Sr. said: “With all my knowledge of the Soviet Union, I owe it. ”

In the UK, they found a meteorite that fell on February 28.
It belongs to the rarest charcoal chlorides that contain "bricks of life", reports the Natural Nature Museum of London.
Read us on Telegram: verified facts, only important As noted, this is the first meteorite found in the UK since 1991.
Its remains, weighing about 300 grams, were found on the driveway to the house in Golstershire, England.
Residents of Vinchkomba heard the noise on the night of February 28, looked out the window, but saw nothing, write in the museum.
In the morning, they found black traces and the rest of the meteorite on the track in front of the house.
They've reached out to the meteor communities that have transferred the meteorite to the museum.
It turned out that the meteorite belongs to extremely rare charcoal chondrite (carbonaceous chondrite), which consists of a mixture of minerals and organic compounds, including building blocks of life itself - amino acids, written in the museum.
The found piece also probably contains soft clay minerals, and this suggests that it was once filled with frozen water ice.
"It's really exciting.
Around 65,000 meteorites are known all over the world, and only 51 of them are charcoal chondrites, ” says Sarah Russell, a researcher of this type of meteorite.
See also: Auction in New York.
Rare meteorites, one age of 7.5 billion years, will bid: Russell notes that the find is especially important against the background of space missions to collect samples of Hayabusa2 asteroids (in December 2020, 4.5 g of stone was delivered to Earth) and OSIRIS-RE (should return more than 60 g in 2023).
"Such meteorites are the relics of the early Solar System, which means they can tell us what the planets are made of.
We also think that such meteorites may have brought water to Earth by providing the planet with oceans...
I was a graduate student when the last meteorite in Britain fell.
I've always dreamed of carbon hondrite, but I didn't expect it at all.
It's a dream come true, "added Sarah Russell.
On February 28, a meteorite fell in the UK.
He formed a bright fireball when it fell.

Millions of years ago in the era of the lower Paleolithic in the territory of modern Russia lived our ancestors - ancient people.
How do we learn about the peculiarities of their everyday life and culture?
What do we know about the climate they lived in?
In Olga Orlova ’ s “Hamburg Account ” on the Public Television of Russia, Khizri Amirkhanov explained how modern archaeologists use a geological approach, and said that the Caucasus looked very different than now when people of the species Homo erectus settled there.
Traditionally, we publish a transcript of this conversation. — Khizri Amirkhanovich, please tell us where and when traces of the oldest people were found in the territory of modern Russia.
Khizri Amirkhanovich Amirkhanov is the head of the department of archeology of the stone age of the Institute of Archeology of the Russian Academy of Sciences.
He was born in 1949 in the village of Andi in Dagestan.
In 1972 he graduated from Dagestan State University.
In 1977 he defended his thesis on the upper Paleolithic of the North Caucasus.
In 1989, he received his doctoral thesis on the theme "Palaeolithic of the South of Arabia".
Since 1977 he has been working at the Institute of Archaeology of the Russian Academy of Sciences.
In 2003, he was elected Corresponding Member of the Russian Academy of Sciences.
Participant and head of expeditions in the North Caucasus, Siberia, Kazakhstan, Moscow region and Yemen.
Member of editorial boards of leading archeological journals.
The author of more than 280 scientific works, including 9 monographs. — Since 2005, specialists of the Moscow Institute of Archaeology of the Russian Academy of Sciences, the Novosibirsk Institute of Archaeology and Ethnography of the Siberian Branch of the Russian Academy of Sciences, the Makhachkala Institute of History, Archaeology and Ethnography of the Dagestan Scientific Center of the Russian Academy of Sciences have been working in certain areas of Kabardino-Balkaria, Kalmykia, Stavropol Territory and Dagestan.
Before us, 40 years ago and earlier, archaeologists had already worked here, but then they could not imagine that there could be monuments of such antiquity in these latitudes.
There was no such question at all.
It took at least half a century for monuments of 1.5 million or more years ago to be discovered outside the birthplace of mankind — Africa.
And, of course, not only in the territory of Russia. — What is it related to?
Has new equipment appeared? — The humanitarian science of archaeology is much more able to obtain new information if it acts in alliance with natural sciences, for example, geology, paleontology and other areas.
This also affects the method of search.
Previously, scientists looked at the surrounding landscape from the point of view of its adaptability to life in our time: could people live there according to the ideas of modern man?
It depended on where to start the excavation.
Our approach is completely different, not humanitarian, but geological.
The key question is whether there are geological deposits in the area that can, by age and nature, contain the remnants of human activity?
The possibility or impossibility of detecting monuments of the required time in these geological conditions is checked.
This method is differentiated for coastal areas.
For example, here we proceed from the identification of the ancient sea terraces and the deposits of these terraces, known to date — known when they formed.
There's a very different approach in the mountains.
There is another form of relief, river terraces, and a different geological approach associated with the continental nature of the formation of layers. — How do you explain that ancient people settled in the mountain areas of the Caucasus?
In the regions of Dagestan, North Ossetia, Kabardino-Balkaria very harsh places.
How to grow food crops there? - So I say that we need to abandon our ideas, how difficult or easy it was for ancient people to live there.
There are obvious facts: the fact is that the landscapes themselves and the animal and plant world were completely different in the distant times in question: from 1 million to 2 million years ago.
We don't know everything about what they looked like, and we're going to install it on the monuments we found.
The Caucasus is known to be young mountains.
Geologists have the expression: "The Caucasus grew before the eyes of primitive man."
It is known that in 1 million years, the Caucasian mountains, according to approximate estimates of geologists, should have risen by 1 km.
In the two million years we've been able to reach, it's about two kilometers away.
Therefore, if we talk about the mountains of the present Dagestan and other regions of the Caucasus, we should bear in mind that 1.5 - 2 million years ago they were not so high.
Now the height level on which the monuments we are digging are located is 1600 − 1700 m. But at the time when people lived there, their height was not more than 300 − 500 m. This is very low.
Sometimes, during the transgressions of the Caspian Sea (exits beyond ordinary borders), it splashed right at these monuments, which is significantly higher than the modern coastline of the sea. — The oldest parking places are Mukhkai I, Mukhkai II and Ainikab, right? — Yes.
There are now identified and to varying degrees investigated eight monuments. — Eight different parking lots? — What is a parking lot?
A place where one group of ancient people lived for a certain time.
Well, the parking lots in these eight monuments are a hundred.
The sections are more than 70 m in capacity. The cultural layers on only one monument have been identified 34.
This means at least 34 parking lots in one line.
The number of cultural layers on the monuments we explore is different, but the geological age, the total date, content, composition of materials and the nature of culture are the same. — You can describe the world through the eyes of an ancient man on Mukhkai I — what does he see around?
The landscape, the animals, the plants? - Ten years ago, we couldn't say anything about it, and now we have a specific material.
It has been established which species of animals lived nearby, which grew woody, herbaceous and other plants.
We see from the sum of all the data that the climate was different at that time.
The current climate is sharply continental, four seasons.
There were no four seasons.
There were two distinct seasons, moist and drier, hot.
Reminds of the landscapes of modern savannah, Africa.
Of course, there are African analogies, especially in fauna.
The so-called southern elephant, the ancient giraffe, the zebroid horse, the antelope, is a savannah fauna and at that time it was represented in the Caucasus.
They are defined according to the bones found by us on specific archaeological sites. — Did elephants live in the Caucasus? — Indeed, at an altitude of 1,700 m, elephants would hardly walk.
But there were no such high mountains there at the time.
Yes, elephants.
Yes, giraffes.
Yes, antelopes of different types: vintorogie and goralo-like antelopes, an ancient species of deer.
Predators have been identified - the same antiquity.
Paleontologists confirm the ancient age of the entire fauna complex in question.
So not only in terms of climate, but also in terms of animal composition and some species of vegetation, the Caucasus, including the North, was similar to Africa.
But he wasn't identical to her.
The Eurasian elements are still present here.
The Caucasus was at the junction, having on the one hand features of the south, and on the other hand - Eurasian features of the north. - What opinion do paleoanthropologists hold on the fact that the biological species of ancient people lived in the Caucasus? - This is indicated by materials from monuments near Dmanisi in Eastern Georgia, the age of finds on Mukhkai I and Mukhkali-Ari II. They have racing date: 1.8 million years.
These ancient people are defined as a species of Homo erectus.
As is known, the erection was preceded by Homo habilis.
Representatives of this species lived in Africa.
And perhaps even later than the Caucasian erection.
It would seem that the time in the Caucasus could also be Homo habilis, the creator of the Oldovan culture.
But no, there's an erection.
It is believed that there were two varieties of erection.
In Africa, too, there was an erection, of course, but it had some differences from the Eurasian erection. — Please tell us more about the geological methods used in the excavation of paleolithic monuments. — The beginning of the excavation should always be preceded by an understanding of stratigraphy — both geological and archaeological.
To do this, a trench is laid on the entire thickness of geological deposits, thanks to which the entire sequence of surfacing is exposed.
This is where all the research begins.
Then archaeologists, together with geologists and specialists of several other natural science disciplines, select many hundreds of samples for a variety of laboratory studies, which are already done at Moscow and St. Petersburg academic institutions, as well as at the Moscow State University.
An example of our use of the geological approach in the search for monuments is implemented, for example, as follows.
Suppose you approach the geological exposure of a certain capacity.
If you see such a section in the valley of the river, and if it is known that this river was formed not earlier, but not much later than 800 thousand years ago, then it is clear that all these deposits, cut by the river, can not be younger than the time of formation of the river itself.
The result of further searches will depend on several other factors, not excluding luck.
Explaining the geological factors of the genesis of the studied sediments helps to understand the essence of the cultural layer itself.
For example, at Mukhkai II, archaeologists do not just dig up a layer with a disorderly weighing of the tools and bones of animals, but investigate exactly the level of habitat, the surface on which certain specific acts of activity of particular people took place.
These are places where people lived 1.95 million years ago, and no later.
There are also found bones of elephants, giraffes, horses, antelope and deer, that is, a complex of fauna characteristic of that time.
The functions of the stone tools that we find in these parking lots are limited to the economic practices that these people had.
What is this practice?
Food extraction, processing and consumption.
This requires knives, heavy scraping and cutting tools, for example, to break skulls, eat the brains of large animals.
For ancient people, it was a very nutritious and useful food.
And there are tools 3 − 4 cm long, which can perform fine labor operations, delicate actions... - That is, to delicate actions, these people were also able? - Yes, their brush was adapted to delicate production operations.
And since the brush was so fit for such a job, accordingly, it reflected on other bones and muscles that lead to the brush, in general, on the entire skeleton.
Erectus were people in the full sense of the word. — What do you not understand about ancient people at the moment?
What is the biggest mystery for you? — The most intriguing and important problem (certainly, not only archaeological, but complex: anthropological, philosophical) is the line between man and the kingdom of animals.
Theoretically, it's easy to describe.
But how can we find out in a instrumental way? — Do you want to understand how to determine the proportion of Homo in ancient people? — I would like to see the material remains that we study and treat as a person, and we have no doubt that we are right about this, still find a defining human property, when it can be mistakenly distinguished from the activities of higher monkeys, such as chimpanzees.
In recent years, a lot of work has been done in the direction of search, which is called "chimpanzee culture".
After all, the pack also uses tools, say, stones to break nuts.
The big stone with the little one is combined, and quite reasonable.
And if you don't know that this group of objects is left to chimpanzees, how to distinguish?
It would seem that these are traces of human activity... - Hizri Amirkhanovich, thank you very much for the conversation!
We wish you new discoveries!

Scientists, studying the inclusion in meteorites, found particles of star dust more than 5 billion years old, when the solar system did not exist, RIA Novosti reports.
It is noted that this is the oldest solid material ever found on Earth.
Some meteorites, in addition to protoplanetary dust, included tiny solid particles of star dust that were contained in interstellar gas even before the Sun was formed.
This substance was the only material evidence of the period prior to the Solar System.
Scientists have isolated it from fragments of the Murchison meteorite that fell in Australia in 1969.

Researchers from the University of Rochester (USA) first applied a mechanism for studying the magnetic properties of meteorites and managed to determine that water-rich and amino acid asteroids arrived in the Solar System in the first five million years of its existence.
The study was published in the journal Nature Communications Earth and Environment, and a summary of the work is posted on the website of the University of Rochester.
New data will help to better understand the evolution of the solar system and the origin of the Earth as a livable planet.
Some of the meteorites that have fallen to Earth are known to be debris from larger space objects, including asteroids.
After separation from their "parent bodies", they "lived" for a period of time until they entered the atmosphere of our planet and hit its surface.
This is the kind of debris that scientists have decided to explore.
The study was based on an analysis of the level of "magnetization" of meteorites.
A special model has been developed for this purpose.
It helped researchers to find out exactly when the studied objects were formed and where they were at the beginning of the history of our solar system.
"A few years ago, we realized that we could use the magnetism of meteorites ripped off from asteroids to determine how far these meteorites were from the Sun when their magnetic minerals were formed," says John Tarduno, lead author of the paper.
In particular, researchers studied the magnetic properties of the meteorite Allende, which fell in Mexico in 1969.
It is the largest carbon chondritis meteorite discovered on Earth.
It contains minerals, inclusions of calcium and aluminum, which are considered the first solid substances formed in the Solar System.
For decades, Allende was considered a classic model of a meteorite pulled out of the asteroid's "parent body".
Despite being one of the most studied meteorites, he was able to give researchers new data and new clues about the evolution of the Solar System.
First of all, it was necessary to solve a long scientific paradox: how exactly did the meteorites get their magnetic properties?
Previously, it was believed that they had received it from a kind of core - were magnetized by the inner "dynamo machine" of the planet, such as the Earth, for example.
However, new experiments have shown that magnetism is a common feature of mineral-rich meteorites such as Allende.
The simulation showed that the solar winds tightly enveloped the early bodies of the Solar System.
It was the solar wind that magnetized them.
Solving this paradox, scientists began to analyze the magnetic properties of other meteorites containing minerals.
With the help of computer modeling, researchers determined that parent asteroids, from which the carbon chondritis meteorites they studied, came to the Asteroid Belt in the outer part of the Solar System about 4,562 billion years ago.
That is, it happened during the first five million years of the solar system itself.
"This early arrival of carbon chondrite asteroids laid the foundation for further dispersion of water-rich bodies, potentially on Earth," says Tarduno. "This later played a role in the development of the Solar System.
It is possible that this is a pattern common to exoplanet systems. "

Geologists revealed the secret of Machu Picchu ’ s location The ancient sanctuary of the Incas Machu Picchu is considered one of the greatest architectural achievements of mankind.
It was built on the top of the mountain range at an altitude of 2,450 meters above sea level.
In 2007 Machu Picchu was awarded the title of the New Wonder of the World.
The location of the sanctuary has long been puzzled by scientists who did not understand why the ancient Incas built it in such an inaccessible place.
The new study suggests that the cause may be related to geological fractures that are located under the sanctuary.
Brazilian geologist Rualdo Menegat presented the results of a detailed geoarchaeological analysis, which suggests that the Incas deliberately built Machu Picchu in a place where tectonic faults occur.
Using a combination of satellite imagery and field measurements, Menegat mapped a dense network of intersecting faults identified under the UNESCO World Heritage Site.
The analysis showed that tectonic features differ in scale: from tiny cracks to large lineaments with a length of 175 kilometers.
Menegat found that some fractures were oriented to the north-east-south-west, others to the north-west-south-east, and together they create the letter "X", crossing exactly under Machu Picchu.
Mapping suggests that other sanctuaries, as well as individual buildings and stairs, are oriented according to the direction of these major fractures.
This gave the Incas a number of advantages.
The main among them was a ready-made source of water, as tectonic fractures were directed by melt and rain water, which flowed directly to the site of the sanctuary.
The height of the location also helped to isolate the site from avalanches and landslides too common in this environment.

Science News: Scientists from the University of Germany are investigating the details of the meteorite fall on the ground.
Scientists from the Wilhelm II University of Westphalia in Münster said that the meteorite that fell in September 2019 in Germany is a planetesimals, that is, the "fetus" of the planet.
It is reported to the research of the university.
Thus, planetologists published a report on the study of a stone weighing only 24.5 grams, which a local resident found in his yard after the meteorite exploded in the sky.
Scientists were assisted by colleagues from a dozen and a half scientific institutions.
"Paleontologists have found that the meteorite contains minerals formed in the presence of water on small planetesimals, formed in the earliest periods of the solar system," the results of the study reported.
It is also reported that the meteorite belongs to a very rare species of carbon chondrites.
At the moment, it is the only stone of this type that fell from the sky, found in Germany.
A scanning electron microscope has been found to contain minerals such as carbonates and sheet silicates that are formed in the presence of water.
The age of the meteorite is estimated at 4.56 billion years, that is, it is older than the Earth.

Scientists found in the Sahara a fragment of the protoplanet 10.03.2021, 16: 34 2,400 Meteorite broke away from the protoplanet and turned out to be older than the Earth.
French and Japanese scientists have studied a meteorite that was discovered in Sahara in 2020.
In their opinion, it broke away from the protoplanet, and its age exceeds the age of the Earth, reports Naked science.
It is noted that the remains of protoplanets are rarely studied due to lack of samples.
Now the scientists have such a chance.
Chips with a total weight of 32 kilograms were found in Erg-Shesh in south-western Algeria.
They immediately attracted the attention of scientists, because they were not among the most common type of meteorites - chondrites.
The researchers examined the samples and found traces of pyroxene in them.
In this regard, scientists attributed the sample to achondrites, which consist of magmatic rocks, or breccia, resulting from shock processes.
It is assumed that such meteorites have formed in large bodies, which within themselves have divided into a core and a bark.
The Achondrites are not found often, 95% of them are attributed to the debris of the Moon or Mars.
Basalt is usually found in such bodies, but it is not found in the fragments found.
But scientists found the volcanic rock Andesite.
After studying the radioactive isotopes of aluminum and magnesium, scientists were able to determine the age of the meteorite.
It turned out that these elements crystallized 4.565 billion years ago as part of the parent body (it itself formed 4.566 billion years ago).
That is, the meteorite has appeared before our planet, which is known to be 4.54 billion years old.
Scientists believe that they have discovered the oldest known specimens of magmatic rock.
And this, they say, sheds light on what the bark of the first protoplanets of the Solar System looked like.

The meteorite from the Sahara turned out to be a fragment of the crust of the protoplanet Planetologists found that the EC 002 meteorite found last year in the Sahara is the oldest of the known magmatic rocks in the Solar System — its age is 4,565 billion years.
It appears to be a fragment of the crust of a destroyed protoplanet, according to an article published in Proceedings of the National Academy of Science.
Despite the fact that today scientists have about three thousand samples of meteorites, the source of which is bark or robe of rocky bodies, they represent a limited sample of differentiated objects formed in the early Solar System.
It is believed that 75 per cent of such meteorites are fragments of the bark of an asteroid (believed to be Vesta), and another 20 per cent originated in a large ureilite parent body robe.
The study of such meteorites allows you to understand the processes that took place in large bodies at the dawn of the Solar System, and the diversity of the composition of their robes and bark.
A group of planetologists, led by Jean-Alix Barrat of the University of Western Brittany, reported on the results of a study of the fragment of the Andesite Achondrite Erg Czech 002 (or EC 002) found in May last year in Sahara.
Scientists analyzed the age, structure, phase and mineralogical compositions of the meteorite using optical, scanning electron and autoelectron microscopes, as well as energy dispersion X-ray spectrometry, optical emission spectrometry with inductively coupled plasma and ion microprobe.
Top: image in reflected electrons of polished spline EC 002 (pyroxens are shown in colors from gray to light gray, and feldspar dark gray).
Bottom: X-ray map of the spline, colors are responsible for different elements.
Jean-Alix Barrat et al. / PNAS, 2021 EC 002 is a greenish, low-porous (8 percent of the total volume) rock, the size of the grains constituting the meteorite is from 1 to 1.5 millimeters.
It consists of 45 per cent of plate-like albite feldspar (plagioclase and possibly anortoclase) containing potassium feldspar inclusions, 38 per cent of anhydride pyroxene and 5 per cent of silica minerals (cristobalite and tridimite).
The meteorite analysis showed that we have the oldest magmatic rock of 4,565 billion years of age.
It is assumed that the parent body of the meteorite was formed by accretion 1.5 million years after the formation of inclusions rich in calcium and aluminum (CAI), which are considered one of the first objects formed in the Solar System.
The meteorite itself formed on the surface or close to the surface of the maternal body, thus it is a fragment of the magmatic crust of the ancient protoplanet, subsequently destroyed.
Further study of EC 002 and similar meteorites can provide valuable information on the processes of formation of rocky planets, such as Earth.
