The Peruvian authorities have discovered the famous ruins of the Inca highland town of Machu Picchu for the only Japanese tourist who has waited for this chance for more than seven months.
CNN writes about it.
Jessie Katayama, a 26-year-old boxing instructor from Osaka, arrived in Peru in March 2020.
He had already bought an entrance ticket and permission to enter Machu Picchu on March 16, but it was at this time that the authorities of the country decided to close the attraction to visit due to the coronavirus pandemic.
But Jesse didn't get confused and decided to wait for his chance.
He rented a room in the town of Aguas Caglientes nearby and used his free time to visit attractions in the vicinity – the Callientes Waterfall and the Putucusi Mountains.
He also taught boxing to local children and made friends here.
Takayama said he was going to open his own boxing hall after returning to Osaka, so he used the time spent in Peru for training.
"I ran every morning and saw Machu Picchu from afar.
I thought that I would never get there, but I was satisfied with everything, because I had a great time here "- but the leadership of the city of Aguas Calentes, impressed by the persistence of the Japanese, appealed to the Peruvian authorities to let a tourist to Machu Picchu.
They met and Jesse Katayama became the only tourist on the territory of the ancient Inca city.
Photo: Machu Picchu Travel Tours He was accompanied by only two photographers and the head of the site dedicated to Machu Picchu.
On October 16, happy Jesse will return to Japan.
And the ancient city is going to be reopened for visitors, but in a limited mode.
Machu Picchu is a city of Inca civilization located high in the Andes on the territory of Peru over the valley of the Urubamba River.
This town was founded in the 15th century and then abandoned.
A characteristic feature of this historic monument is that all stone walls there are built without the use of any reinforcing materials.
Also on the territory of Machu Picchu there are unusual astronomical structures.

Scientists have found that the meteorite found in northern Africa is much older than the Earth.
Its age is about 4.6 billion years, while our planet is about 4.54 ± 0.05 billion years.
Researchers believe that it was formed when the Solar System was formed.
It is believed that this process began with a gravitational collapse of a small part of a giant interstellar gas-dust cloud that could have been caused by an explosion of a massive star or a supernova.
The meteorite is the size of an orange and has green inclusions.
Its current owner purchased the stone in 2016 in an African market and sent it for research.
Scientists have been skeptical about the discovery for a long time.
At some point, the meteorite hit a student at the University of New Mexico, Purne Srinivasan.
She conducted a study under the guidance of Professor Carl Aigee.
The object was named Northwest Africa (NWA) 11119.
The analysis showed that the stone is of extraterrestrial volcanic origin.
NWA 11119 by 30% consists of tridimite, a rare mineral belonging to the silica class.
As Livescience.com writes, this ratio has never been found in meteorites before.
Scientists have seen such a composition only in some volcanic rocks of the Earth.
Further chemical analysis showed that NWA 11119 had much in common with previously found meteorites NWA 7235 and Almahata Sitta.
According to experts, they may have been parts of one parent body in the early solar system.
Moscow, Ivan Gridin Moscow.

Scientists of the Southwest Research Institute (SwRI) studied the composition of a small meteorite and found that it is part of a previously unknown to science parent object of the time of the formation of the solar system, possibly a huge asteroid or dwarf planet.
The study is published in the journal Nature Astronomy, and a summary of it is presented on the website of the Southwest Research Institute.
This is a small meteorite that was found in the Sudan in 2008.
The study specifies that it is part of a nine-ton meteoroid with a diameter of about four metres, which entered the Earth's atmosphere 12 years ago and exploded over the Sudan by about 600 meteorites.
By the way, this was the first time that scientists managed to accurately predict the collision before the "alien" entered our atmosphere.
The meteoroid was named Almahata Sitta (AhS) from the Arabic word "Sixth Station".
This was the name of the railway station next to the drop point.
In a new study, a 50-milligram sample split off from AhS was studied.
According to Vicky Hamilton, the lead author of the study, an infrared microscope was used to analyze its composition.
Spectral analysis revealed the presence of a number of hydrated minerals, in particular amphibol.
This indicated that water had long been present at the parent site, of which the meteorite was a part.
The presence of hydrated minerals is not typical of carbon chondritic meteorites, which make up the majority of the cosmic bodies found on Earth.
But it is the minerals that "register" geological activity at the earliest stages of development of the Solar system and give an idea of the history of parent bodies.
In this case, according to scientists, the parent body was a very large and previously unknown to science asteroid the size of the planet.
It probably entered the Solar System at an early stage of its existence.
According to scientists, it could be the size of Ceres, the dwarf planet is the largest object in the main asteroid belt.
It is believed to have been formed in the presence of water and at variable temperatures and pressures.
"Some meteorites are dominated by minerals that indicate the effects of water at low temperatures and pressures," says Vicky Hamilton. "The composition of other meteorites indicates heating in the absence of water.
But so far there has been little evidence of metamorphism in the presence of water under intermediate conditions. "
Amphibols are rarely found in carbon chondritis meteorites.
Previously, their traces were found, for example, in the Allende meteorite.
Therefore, AhS proved to be an invaluable, albeit somewhat random, source of information for scientists about the materials of the early Solar System, which have not yet been presented in astronomical collections.
Asteroids, as well as meteoroids and meteorites that come from them, are the remnants of material from the time of the formation of our solar system, that is, their age reaches 4.6 billion years ago.
Most of them are in the main asteroid belt between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter.
But some of these objects were thrown into the interior of the solar system as a result of collisions and other events.

Scientists from the United States and Switzerland said that they managed to find an amazing finding - the oldest material on Earth, which is about 7.5 billion years old.
This is reported by the Air Force.
According to researchers, this material came to Earth from space along with the meteorite Merchison, which fell in Australia in 1969.
Scientists took meteorite particles and turned them into powder.
The paste was dissolved in acid to leave only stardust.
To determine the age of dust, scientists have calculated how many times the meteorite has been affected by cosmic rays.
It turned out that it is mostly particles, which are from 4.6 to 4.9 years old, but there is also dust, which is about 7.5 billion years old.

Scientists came across traces of extraterrestrial organic matter in South Africa Scientists claim to have discovered traces of extraterrestrial organic matter buried in volcanic sediments over 3.3 billion years ago.
The discovery in South Africa can serve as a confirmation of the theory that, along with meteorites, individual forms of life could be brought to Earth, writes Science Alert, reports Novoe Vremya.
“This is the first time we have discovered actual evidence of the presence of extraterrestrial carbon in terrestrial rocks, ” explained astrobiologist Francis Westall of the CNRS Center for Molecular Biophysics in France.
In the Josefsdal Chert volcanic field, which is part of the Mahonjwa region (also known as the Barberton Green Belt), Westall and her team discovered a layer of 2 mm thick rock that is characterized by two "abnormal" signals.
Using electron paramagnetic resonance spectroscopy, researchers found that the 3.3bn age rock contains two types of insoluble organic substances, both of which assume extraterrestrial origin.
One of the signals is reminiscent of what scientists saw earlier in carbon chondrites: ancient samples of meteorites containing organic compounds.
Another anomalous signal is the particles of nickel, chromium and iron, which are not usually found in terrestrial rocks.
This is another argument for finding an extraterrestrial substance.
“Nickel-rich chrome spinels, also called“ space spinels ”, are formed when extraterrestrial objects enter the Earth ’ s atmosphere, ” explained the author of the study, chemical engineer Didier Gurier of the PSL Research University.
“It is difficult to imagine a blow that would preserve the organic matter and spinels in a thin sedimentary layer.
On the one hand, the organic matter can survive only if the temperature at a fall does not exceed several hundred degrees.
On the other hand, spinels are formed only at a high melting point after space material enters the atmosphere of the Earth, ” the authors of the work write.
In their hypothesis, researchers suggest that the hail of micrometeorites could be mixed in the atmosphere with the clouds of volcanic ash, and when the substance slowly drifted to the surface of the Earth, traces of extraterrestrial carbon were stored together with newly formed space spinels, and preserved together for billions of years. * If you notice an error in the text of the news, highlight it and press Ctrl + Enter ♪ Oh,

The surface of Ceres is carbon rich According to a new study published in Nature Astronomy, the surface of the dwarf planet Ceres may contain a carbon concentration several times higher than the concentration in the richest primitive meteorites found on Earth.
It is believed that Ceres originated about 4.6 billion years ago, at the dawn of the solar system.
NASA Dawn data previously revealed the presence of water and other volatile substances such as ammonium derived from ammonia.
And now they are joined by a high concentration of carbon.
This set suggests that Ceres was formed in a cold environment, possibly outside Jupiter's orbit.
“Thanks to these results, Ceres has gained a key role in assessing the origin, evolution and distribution of organic species in the Solar System, ” said the leading researcher, Dr. Simona Marchi of the Southwest Research Institute.
Geophysical, composite and column models based on Dawn data have shown that the partially differentiated interior of Ceres has been modified by liquid processes.
Dawn data also indicate the presence of an additional dimming agent, probably amorphous carbon rich in carbon organic material.
According to a new study, 50-60% of the upper crust of Ceres may have a composition similar to primitive carbon chondrite meteorites.
“Our results suggest that Ceres either somehow fused with ultra-carbon materials, or that carbon was concentrated in its crust, ” said Dr. Marchi.
“Both potential scenarios are important because the mineralogical composition of Ceres points to some global event with a change in aquatic rock that can provide conditions favorable to organic chemistry. ”

TASS, August 24.
Democratic Party presidential candidate Hillary Clinton named her rival and current head of the Republican's White House, Donald Trump, "a jerk who likes to intimidate women."
An excerpt from the memoirs of the former Secretary of State was released on Wednesday by ABC.
He had at his disposal a fragment of Clinton's memoirs on the second round of television debates of presidential candidates.
The discussion took place on October 9, 2016 in St. Louis, Missouri.
The majority of the media then awarded the victory in a verbal duel of the former First Lady, but Trump ’ s behavior turned out to make her feel “incredibly uncomfortable ”: the Republican approached the rival too close and literally hung over her.
"What would you do?
Would you remain calm and keep smiling, as if he was constantly not invading your personal space? "Clinton asks rhetorical questions." Or would you turn around, look him in the eyes and say loudly and clearly: "Step back, you moron, get away from me.
I know you like to intimidate women, but you don't intimidate me, so step back. "
The former Secretary of State himself responds that she preferred to focus on the content of the debate, which she "learned from the life experience of having a hard time communicating with men who are trying to throw you out."
Clinton's Memoirs Clinton's Book of Memoirs entitled "What Happened?" will be released on September 12 this year by Simon and Schuster and will be her third such work.
In the first book Living History (2003), Clinton describes the years she spent in the White House as First Lady.
The second book "Difficult Choice" (2014) tells about the events of 2007-2013: rivalry with Barack Obama for the presidency and service in his administration as head of the foreign policy department.
"I do not have all the answers, and this book will not be an exhaustive report on the 2016 race.
This is my story, and I just want to raise the veil of mystery over the events of dizzying, fun, instructive, furious and just confusing, - Clinton concludes in a fragment published by ABC. - It was not easy to write, because all the time that I was a presidential candidate, I knew: millions of people are counting on me, and I couldn't imagine I'd let them down.
I couldn't handle my job, and now I have to live with it for the rest of my life. "
Clinton headed the State Department from 2009 to 2013.
On 12 April 2015, she announced her candidacy for the Democratic Party presidential election.
On July 26, 2016, Clinton was approved at a party convention and became the first female presidential candidate in the United States.
According to the voting results of the electoral college, she lost to Trump (304 votes against 227), but by the number of votes ahead of the Republican: 48.07% against 45.99%.

INTERFAX - Former United States post representative Nikki Haley will write a book about his experience in this post, Associated Press reports on Wednesday.
According to him, Hayley signed a contract with St. Martin's Press.
The book is expected to be available this fall.
N.Hayley will tell readers not only about her work as a United States post prep to the United Nations in 2017-2018, but also about how she was previously the governor of South Carolina.
According to the publishing house, the story will focus on "important actions, decisions, disagreements and backstage negotiations that have an impact on national and world events".
In St.Martin's Press, it was stated that N.Haley would share his "very personal" opinion on the events she had witnessed.
At the end of last year, Hayley left the post of Permanent Representative of the United States to the United Nations.
She has held this position since January 2017.
Prior to that, she was governor of South Carolina from 2011 to 2017.
The US administration has not yet determined who will now represent the US at meetings at the United Nations.
Hayley is considered to be the rising star of the Republican Party and a loyal supporter of US President Donald Trump.
In this regard, observers are interested in what will be its next steps in the American political world.

According to Mehr News Agency, Arizona State University researchers who published their research results in the journal "Description of the American Academy of Sciences Debates" (PNAS) in an article entitled "Molecular Unsymmetry in Extraterrestrial Chemistry: Prospects Toward a Primary Meteor" showed that the organic matter in the very old meteorite had unsymmetrical molecules.
These scientists discovered that there is no molecular symmetry in some of the amino acids that are similar to Earth's biomolecules.
In fact, when these molecules are combined in the lab, half of them move right and half of them move left and form a symmetric structure, and instead, natural molecules of DNA and RNA are right rounded.
Meanwhile, in the amino acids present in the meteorite, all the molecules were turned to the left.
These scientists believe that in their research, the meteorite "Marchison", which collapsed in Australia in 1969, left hemispheric acid molecules can be identified as a "cosmic signature" for all amino acids.

Machu Picchu resumed the reception of tourists after almost eight months of break Photo: EPA / Vostock-photo Moscow.
2 November.
INTERFAX.RU - Peru authorities allowed to resume tourist trips to the ancient city of the Inca Machu Picchu after a break that lasted almost eight months, the Air Force News reports.
On November 1, a solemn ceremony was held there, the participants of which thanked the gods in accordance with the rites of the tribe.
Machu Picchu in Peru has reopened after being closed for nearly 8 months due to the Covid-19 pandemic.
Only 675 tourists will be allowed a day for safety reasons, which is about 30% of previous capacity. pic.twitter.com / IfEenBSHVP – BFM News (@ NewsBFM) November 2, 2020 Now Machu Picchu will take 675 tourists per day, this is about 30% of the usual flow ♪ Oh,
Work with the teams will be carried out in compliance with all the sanitary and epidemiological requirements imposed due to the coronavirus.
The closure of the complex, which provides employment to tens of thousands of residents of the Cusco mountain area, was a severe blow to the tourism industry in Peru.
All the time the city was closed, it was visited by only one person - a tourist from Japan Jesse Katayama.
He arrived in Peru in March to inspect Machu Picchu, and the complex was shut down due to the coronavirus.
Katayama was stuck in Peru because of the closed borders.
In the autumn, he sent a special application to the Government requesting permission to inspect the archaeological site and an exception was made.
In mid-October, Katayama was brought to Machu Picchu, and he bypassed the city in a mask and in complete solitude.
Archaeologists believe that Machu Picchu ("Old Mountain", translated from Quechua) was built in the XV century on the top of the mountain range at an altitude of 2,400 meters above sea level by Emperor Pachakutek as his own sanctuary residence.
The town was there from the 1430s to the 1530s, then it was abandoned and forgotten.
Machu Picchu was rediscovered by American explorer Hiram Bingham in 1911 after the pilots told about the ruins.
The city is now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
The complex remains ruins of about 200 stone structures - temples, residences and economic buildings.

2 June 2020 2020-06-02T04: 35: 17 + 03: 00 Where and why in Mexico pyramids, explained scientists Photo: Alexander MESHKOV Change the size of the text: A A Scientists of the National Institute of Anthropology and History of Mexico (INAH) conducted research work and found out the reason for the construction of pyramids by the ancient inhabitants of Mesoamerica, which was located on the territory of modern Central America.
A summary report on the presentation of the study is published on the website of the organization.
Having carefully studied all the known pre-Spanish objects found in Mexico, experts suggested that the pyramids were sacred copies of the mountains.
Thus, ancient inhabitants tried to imitate the nature in which they met pyramidal forms - for example, silhouettes of volcanoes.
In the study, it is specified that the pre-Spanish societies of Mesoamerica, when creating such objects, also tried to create some sacred meaning in them.
Archaeologists suggest that the pyramidal form was used as often as possible, as it facilitated the task of erecting a high monumental object.
Recall that one of the most famous monumental structures in this region considers the pyramid of El Castillo (Kukulkan), located in the ancient city of Maya Chichen-Itza (Mexico).
It is recognized as one of the seven new wonders of the world.

The U.S. Department of State agreed to provide Associated Press with all the working schedules of Democratic Party presidential candidate Hillary Clinton during her tenure as U.S. Secretary of State from 2009 to 2013.
According to AP, the schedules of the former chief of American diplomacy will be provided by mid-October, that is, before the presidential elections, which will be held on November 8.
The Agency asserts that these documents will allow "detailed familiarization with Clinton's daily routine" for all four years of her work as Secretary of State.
At the moment, the State Department has published only half of the work schedules of the former head of the department.
It was originally planned that the remainder would be submitted by December 30, but AP objected to it.
Previously, the agency analyzed 154 Clinton meetings with individuals who represented or spoke to private entities on their own behalf.
It turned out that 85 of them had transferred money to her family charitable foundation, the Clinton Foundation, or otherwise supported its international charitable programs.
AP claims that the total amount of donations from these people was $156 million, with at least 40 people transferring more than 100 thousand each, another 20 giving the fund more than one million.

2018 2018-01-15T16: 39: 08 + 03: 00 Resize text: A A South African scientists from the University of Johannesburg (UJ), applying the latest research methods, carefully studied the meteorite found in 1996 in the Sahara desert.
And we came to the conclusion that he's not from the solar system.
The meteorite includes such mineral compounds and trace elements that are not found in "our district".
Moreover, they are in some unimaginable proportions.
In previously known meteorites and comets, nothing like this has ever been found.
And then we found even the inclusions of aluminum in absolutely pure form and a lot of microscopic diamonds.
According to Professor Jan Kramers, the head of the group of researchers, one of two is possible: either the meteorite was formed from the same interstellar dust, from which the bodies of our solar system "stuck" later, or it originated much earlier somewhere far away.
If the first option is correct, scientists will have to correct existing ideas about the laws of formation of planets.
And to explain how substances can be grouped in such a bizarre but natural way - as it happened in the meteorite studied.
The fragments of "Hypatia", which were analyzed by South African scientists.
So far, scientists do not doubt only one thing: the meteorite was formed at a temperature of minus 196 degrees Celsius.
The meteorite, which now so greatly surprised the researchers, was found by the Egyptian geologist Ali Barakat.
Without understanding, colleagues of geologists then suggested that it did not fall from the sky at all, but represented a glass that was formed already on Earth several tens of millions of years ago as a result of a meteorite 's impact on the sand surface.
The true — extraterrestrial — nature of the object in 2013 was revealed by the same Jan Kramers together with our compatriot Gergy Belyanin.
Scientists continued their research and came to the current, even deeper, conclusions - about the supernatural composition of the object.
The meteorite was named "Hypatia" in honor of an incredibly educated female mathematician who lived and taught in Alexandria in the 4th century AD.
A place where a supernatural meteorite was found.
Now it is believed that the diameter of "Hypatia" was initially about 10 meters.
The meteorite has split into many fragments.
The composition of several of them was analyzed by the Kramers team.
The results were reported in the journal Geochimica et Cosmochimica Acta.

Machu Picchu.
The lost city of the Inca Machu Picchu is actually the place for which we jumped from the cozy sofa and went to the edge of the world - to Peru. - But if in the case of other Peruvian sights, everything can be done on the spot impromptu, then the visit to Machu Picchu is a little more difficult.
Only 2,000 people can enter the lost city of the Incas a day.
The thought that we would fly across the Atlantic Ocean, get to the Sacred Valley of the Incas and... not see Machu Picchu was just unbearable.
Therefore, a month before the trip, we began "hacker attacks" on the official website of the Department of Culture of Peru - www.machupicchu.gob.pe (only there are tickets you can buy without overpayment and intermediaries).
In March 2017, the ticket to Machu Picchu cost 152 salt, and with a visit to the neighboring mountain of Waina Picchu - 200 salt.
We made two attempts to buy a ticket.
The first time the money was written off from the card, the addressee was not reached and returned in a couple of days.
The second time was even cooler - a successful transaction, confirmation of purchase, but no tickets,.. the site took and hung up!
The bank said it would take a month to find out.
There was no time, we flew to Peru without any guarantees.
The only thing I did was write a small and tearful essay on the topic "How we were robbed by the Department of Culture of Peru" and translated it into Spanish by an almighty Google translator for the purpose of disassembling on the spot:)) Our way to the dream looked like this: the plane "Moscow - Paris - Lima" (18 hours), the plane "Lima - Cusco" (1 hour), the minibus "Ollantaytambo (1.5 hours), Ollantaytambo - Aguas Calentes train (1.5 hours), Aguas Calentes - Machu Picchu bus (15 minutes).
At first glance, it didn't look humane.
But on the forums, people painted routes even more monstrous, including a long journey on the rails...
I didn't want to go on the rails at all.
Therefore, we bought train tickets in advance - on the official website of Peruvian Railways www.perurail.com (54 salts round-trip).
And the real tickets had to be picked up, coming to Peru, in one of their offices, the addresses were attached.
Print from the site was not considered tickets.
We picked up in Lima - in the shopping center "Larkomar" (2nd floor, not difficult to find).
This was our first success on the way to Machu Picchu:) Getting to Cusco, we immediately moved to the office of the Department of Culture to somehow get their legal tickets.
Luckily, my Spanish essay was a success, an Indian woman counted the infias from our passports, and we were given tickets.
The real ones!
It was happiness.
By the way, tickets to Machu Picchu are offered in each gate, but the address of the official ticket office: Calle Garcilaso, Cusco, works from 7.00 to 20.00 every day except Sunday.
We had to find a minibus to Ollantaitambo:) At 6: 00 the next day we went to the "nest of minibuses".
I printed out a beautiful map for his search.
We had to get to Ollantaitambo half an hour before the departure of our train, that is at 7.55:) Drive to Ollantaitambo - 10 salt per person.
Let's sit down.
The marshrutka is almost full.
By the logic of things, we're about to go.
The only thing that worries is not a single European and almost everyone sleeps.
And then it starts: as soon as a new passenger arrives, the driver pushes one of the sleeping people and the one comes out! How?!
They're all fake!
:))) In Peru, there is also such a profession - a non-passenger passenger.
In general, the minibus was taken for another half an hour, not less.
But we still got on our train.
The train was happy.
The ticket price included a light breakfast (tea and coffee, gingerbread and popcorn).
On the ceiling of the car there were viewing windows - we drove among the mountains, which "to the sky!".
And outside the window ran the talking river Urubamba - from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calentes, all the way, without turning around.
The journey was very pleasant.
Aguas Calentes is a tiny town at the foot of Machu Picchu.
The population is only 1,600 people.
Completely pedestrian.
You can only get there by rail.
There's no other message.
There's no car in town.
All goods are delivered by iron and transported by special slaves by workers.
So in Aguas Caglientes, it's quite possible to find a bed stealing through the streets...
... or a guy with a cart running at a speed of 10 to 20 km / h.
It'll move and it won't blink!
In Aguas Caglientes only tourist buses are allowed, which carry previously tired on Machu Picchu.
You can walk!
You can:) But we were not ready for such heroism.
Especially since the welcome rain began, and the road became disgusting and slippery.
A round-trip bus ticket cost $24.
The toad, of course, strangled.
But I wanted to be on Machu Picchu as soon as possible!:)) And here on such a serpentine, in the rain, periodically hanging over the abyss to skip the oncoming bus, we climbed higher and higher - where the Incas built their mysterious and inaccessible "city in the heavens" or "city among the clouds", as it is called.
By the way, the height of Machu Picchu is only 2,450 meters above sea level, so after Cusco with its 3500, we felt almost like birds - there was absolutely no miner.
Just a feeling of wings behind your back!
:) When we finally got up, the city really looked lost...
Streets and buildings seem to have drowned in acid clouds.
And only occasionally the wind pulled this cloud blanket, and whispered to us: “Here is your Machu Picchu.
Look out for it. "
12,000 kilometers, the other side of the world, eight hours of time difference, so much effort and see only the edges of the mountain in the fog?
It's a shame.
And that's where the miracles started.
The sun, hanging in the endless misty sea, like a big fish, suddenly began to swallow clouds - one after another:) In an hour Machu Picchu was seen as on the palm of his hand.
The city was built by the Incas in the XV century, it stretches for 8 kilometers and precisely on giant steps descends on the slope of the mountain.
Machu Picchu is the name of the mountain, translated from Quechua - "old mountain".
As the city itself was called, no one knows.
But now they call it by the name of the mountain.
According to legend, Machu Picchu was abandoned by the Incas a hundred years after the construction, the conquistadors were just beginning their large-scale conquests in Latin America.
The Incas were strong in prophecies, so as soon as they felt the "smell of death", they grabbed the golden box and disappeared overnight.
The city has been in total oblivion for 400 years.
The conquistadors never found their way there.
Reconstruction.
Source photo terra-z.com.
Machu Picchu has for many years turned into legends and ambitions of archaeologists.
He wanted to find everything!
The most fortunate American professor, Hiram Bingham, and his team.
In July 1911, during an expedition to study the Andes, they stayed overnight in one of the Indian huts.
The little son of the owners was shocked - he saw such tall and white people for the first time!
The boy did not step back from Bingham, and he gave him a shiny coin in one salt.
On the joy of the boy passed the whole city:))) The whole world recognized the road to Machu Picchu, and Bingham bravely removed 5,000 artifacts to the United States.
Well, at least the stones are still in place.
From prying eyes, the city was hidden by a tropical forest.
And the very first picture was that.
This photo was taken in 1913.
Source: www.nationalgeographic.com.
"Sacred Rock" repeats the contour of the mountains, which behind it (clouds, alas, did not let us see it) and... slightly resembles a sleeping kui (guinea pig).
Scientists say that this rock is something of a kind of road pillar, denoting the northern outskirts of the city and the way to Wina Picchu (a new mountain), but the mystics claim that behind the stone is a portal with the strongest energy, which once was stepped by all the residents of the city...
:))) We, just in case, decided not to go for the stone.
You can climb Wina Picchu, too.
However, the limit is 400 people a day and tickets must be bought in advance.
Wina Picchu is the highest peak in the city.
The neighborhood near it was considered to be elite.
There was the Palace of the Supreme Inca of Pachakutek, all the main temples, the observatory, the central square, cascade fountains and nobility houses.
According to the images found, Pachakutek was blissful and harmless:) Although history assigns him a large-scale seizure of land, and a cold-blooded execution of his brother.
Even the name Pachakutek translates from Quechua as "changing the world".
And the names of the Inca were not spotted for nothing!
But the fact that he with such a face could go to the portal behind the Sacred Rock at the first danger - I believe.
The Incas were brilliant masons.
They built a city without the use of mortar, with the stones perfectly aligned to each other (the so-called polygonal masonry).
In addition, scientists found that some buildings have a unique design: in an earthquake, their stones move in different directions, and then return to the place, saving buildings from destruction.
It's kind of fantastic!
And there are walls, between the stones and the coin will not slip:) Judging by archaeological findings, Machu Picchu was a rich city.
Its inhabitants cultivated high-mountain garden gardens, made wine, weaving, hunted, healed, studied the starry sky, predicted the weather.
Well, of course, they sacrificed to the ancient gods.
The temple of three windows looking east (this is what it is called here).
Why three?
Perhaps in the name of the three most venerated Inca gods - the Sun, the Moon and the Wind.
In front of the temple stands the Inca stepped cross - chakana.
It is buried in the ground by exactly half and is oriented so that at the sunrise on the day of the summer solstice the shadow lies exactly in the foot, forming with the upper above-ground part a full figure of chakanas (in the photo is almost perfect).
The chakana has so many meanings that I do not take into all this to deepen:)) In its simple forms - all life values, all totem animals and all the energy worlds of the Inca civilization.
She also keeps the answer to the question, "What is the meaning of life and in general?"
And for the Incas, this answer is obvious: in love, knowledge, and work.
Source photo with shadow wikimedia.org.
The image of chakana is present in Peru everywhere - in architecture, patterns of carpets, decorations, dishes.
Sometimes even the windows of houses in the form of chakana:) She is like a reminder: “Live as your ancestors taught and bequeathed. ”
In the same part of Machu Picchu the temple of the Sun, more like the tower of the princess.
And next to him cascade fountains:) In the rainy season, perhaps there is a real water extravaganza, but we have enough to appreciate the very idea.
Condor Temple.
Stone wings fly, before the entrance a large stone resembling the head of a bird, in which the drain for blood.
Predatory temple:) The house in which the "Brides of the Sun" lived - selected virgins, on Quechua - Acli.
These are the most beautiful girls of the empire - concubines of the Supreme Inca, his sons and welmoges (on the residual principle).
On the floor of the house unusual circles with a recess.
These are water mirrors.
They say to watch the lights.
The sacred polygonal stone of Intihuatan, which is translated from Quechua, means "the place where the sun is tied".
The medieval Inca Observatory, the sundial and the place of special power.
It used to be possible to touch it with your hands to recharge the energy of the Sun, but after filming the advertising of the Cusquena beer in 2000, when a 450-kilogram lifting crane fell on the stone, a piece of it was broken off, Intihuatana was disconnected from tourists.
Next to the stone is a strict guard who pretends to sleep, keeps a poisoned dart ready.
The Incas used every meter of the mountain, adjusting to the existing relief.
And even though a large number of tourists walk around Machu Picchu (2000 people a day - it 's the same number!), the city is arranged so that you do not feel any crowd.
Well, if only at the very beginning, where everyone catches that very postcard look in the lens.
But as soon as people enter the main city gate, they easily get lost in labyrinths among the high walls, find secluded corners and stay with the city of Inca almost tete-a-tete:) Why almost?
Because the Lamas are walking freely in the streets of Machu Picchu!
You can stand in some cozy Inca courtyard, and suddenly, from the corner, the lama will come out unceremoniously, poke his face in the tablet, say a couple of words in the Old Lama and start licking the wall:))) It 's very funny.
It's kind of a "buzz," but it's a Peruvian subvert.
"There's no supper."
And there are whiskies on the mountain:) In secret, I, of course, dreamed of seeing at least one, even from afar...
And oh, miracle!
Here it is:) It looks like a rabbit, a squirrel, and even a little dwarf kangaroo at the same time.
It jumps, by the way, too.
Mountain whiskies (whiskies) - relatives of the chinchillas.
They are found in Peru, Bolivia, Chile and Argentina.
They live at an altitude of 2,400 to 5,000 meters.
They feed on mosses, lichens and various herbs.
They live in families in the cracks between the rocks, they love to settle near the water.
Therefore, on Machu Picchu whiskies party near the cascade fountains:)) And they are like this!
Well, in general, I don't have words about how mimimic they are.
And there are a lot of them!
♥ In terms of botany, the lost city of the Incas also found something to surprise:) Callas, orchids, strelections and many other wild flowers growing so easily - on the paths and among the stones.
Machu Picchu was included in the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage List in 1983, and was named the New Wonder of the World in 2007.
By the way, it was UNESCO who opposed the construction of a cable car in these places, and recently proposed to reduce the number of guests of Machu Picchu to 800 people per day and increase the prices of tickets.
They're crazy!
We spent the whole day on Machu Picchu and went down to Aguas Caglientes on the last bus - at 17.00.
And there - in the city under the clouds, among the stone walls and velvet peaks, painted by the setting sun, remained a part of our thoughts and feelings.
The magic of the place was even stronger than I expected ♥ Back in Russia, we decided that we would not rush to the last train, but spend the night here at the foot of the great mountain - in Aguas Calentes, and booked a room in the guest house.
I think even by Peruvian standards, the guest house was "zero stars."
The window went out into the elevator shaft, the room smelled of chlorine, the declared Wi-Fi was not, the light bulb reminded me of a dying firefly, and the hostess Pores-Tores-Marina for everything was just spreading her hands.
The only plus is the price (1.5 thousand for a double room).
But we really didn't have a choice, with this influx of tourists.
On the other hand, all these household inconveniences have more than compensated for the day spent in the most magical place on Earth!
♥ And Aguas Calentes himself was the sweetest town.
The central square with the statue of Pachakuteka is the most hanging place in the city.
It's the only street named after the same Pachakutek.
It's got a lot of little restaurants on it.
We managed to check only one - El Manu Restaurante Pizzeria (Av.
Pachacutec 709).
Ten points on the system!
Well, it is not for nothing that he has chakana over the entrance:)) The menu is full of traditional dishes, the prices are inadequate (but this is Peru! and we are already used to it).
Specified in salts (multiply by 18 to estimate how much it is in rubles).
I ordered Rocoto Relleno (I ate it twice in Peru).
From the sight of the usual red bell pepper...
But don't believe it!
In practice, it's hot rocoto peppers.
It is once in 10 sharper jalapeno in raw form, and in boiled unexpectedly loses its "thermonuclear" properties:))) Pepper stuffed with roasted alpaca meat and omelette, and on top put on a small "hat" of white melted cheese, then baked.
It's very tasty!
My reverances to the chef:) Aguas Calentes is not so much fun.
It is clear that in the house of Porez Torez Marina every night movies, wine and dominoes, but wanted more.
Thermal springs (and they are there! enough to reach the end of the street) we refused to visit.
The entrance costs 20 salt, and for the locals about three, so they use them as public baths.
Photo source: gardkarlsen.com.
We found another fun for ourselves.
It turned out that in 2015 in Aguas Callentes began a large-scale improvement, within which the river Urubamba appeared embankment, and in the city itself about two dozen sculptures telling stories from the life of the Incas and singing gods.
That's what we decided to look for in the light of the lanterns.
The Urubamba River, by the way, was considered a continuation of the Sky River.
In their legends at night, the Sun continued its way under Urubamba and gained strength, drinking its water.
In winter, when there was little water, the sun cooled down:) Sculptures are "scattered" throughout the city.
And everything is done according to the laws of the ancient Incas - organic and in the already existing landscape.
For example, there was a huge wild stone, a master came... and the stone immediately acquired the features of Pachakutek:)) with bushes on the top.
Well, that's cool, isn't it?
One of the myths of the Incas tells about how the traveler god Kuniraya Virakocha, the patron of the moon and pregnant women, treated the virgin goddess Kaviliaka with the fruit of lucuma...
According to Kaviliak, everything was like a fog.
Well, then a baby was born.
The angry Viracocha (the god of all and all) turned the fallen goddess into a stone with the child near the blue sea:) And this sculpture is called "Kiss of the Gods".
In the frame - the god of the Sun (Inti) and the goddess of the Moon (Mama Kil).
When the day comes into contact with the night, painting the sky in a million shades, we say, "Sunset!", and the Incas: "Kiss the gods..."
Romantics.
Pachamama is a goddess of fertility and a patron of the harvest.
She's also responsible for earthquakes.
It was considered by the Incas to be one of the wisest and most revered goddesses.
The Incas were denouncing vilification.
According to one of the legends, they once killed a village slut, and her body was cut in half (so that others would be disrespectful!).
Suddenly, a bush that brings happiness and gives effect to viagra grew out of the body.
Inca named the plant "Mama Coca" - in honor of the goddess of happiness and health.
Initially, coca was allowed to chew and rub into a member only to men of the highest class.
By the way, Mum Coca still controls from heaven all the bed scenes;) And in general, half of the sculptures are devoted to the theme of love and family values.
This one is called the Family.
There is also a chapel, where you can contact Pachamama, Pachapapa and anyone who can affect the life and well-being of Aguas Caglientes:) its residents and guests.
In the morning, Porez-Torez-Marina made us some wonderful drink using quail eggs.
Must be your signature pisco sour "zero stars".
And we, having had a cheerful breakfast, got on a train and went to Ollantaytambo - to meet new adventures;) Continuation follows.

Write in the name of Tatiana Afanasieva and long live our common and endless "Gulbarium"!

According to a report by the Taiwan Freedom Times, the Legislative Yuan National Party (KMT) held a venue to block the voting process for the nomination of the procuratorate, the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) legislator started to clear the ground around 3: 40 p.m., and the Blue Green burst into the first violent physical clashes.
After about an hour of tug-of-war, the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) successfully snatched back the podium, and Legislative Yuan President Yu Xikun entered the Chamber to announce the opening of the House of Representatives.
The National Party legislators shouted at the scene that "voting is invalid" and that "the Democratic Progressive Party is cheating".
According to the report, the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) cleared YU Sik-kun into the Legislative Yuan and confirmed that he was exercising the authority of personnel consent.
At about 4: 30 p.m., Yu Shyi-kun, president of the Legislative Yuan, entered the Chamber of Deputies of the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) under the "protection", announcing the outcome of the meeting.
According to the report, the Legislative Yuan was scheduled to review the authority of personnel consent this week, but because the Kuomintang dominated the podium to boycott the proceedings, the review would not be able to proceed.
At about 4: 30 p.m., despite Chen Yu-chen, the chairman of the National People's Party (DPP), the DPP legislator, YU Hsi-kun, the chairman of the Legislative Yuan, under the protection of the DPP legislator Kuo Kuo-wen, Ke Chien-ming, Luo Chi-cheng, and Lin Jun-hsien, entered the rostrum and began to read the conclusions of the meeting. The committee then voted on the contents of the DPP proposal, and several women legislators, including Wang Meihui, pushed Chen Yu-chen out.
As the Kuomintang delegation disagreed with the outcome of the meeting announced by Premier Yu Shyi-kun, the Chamber of Deputies began to vote on the proposal of the Kuomintang group and confirmed the outcome of the meeting.
The report said that the Kuomintang legislators shouted at the scene that "the vote was invalid" and "the Democratic Progressive Party cheated", and General Secretary Lin Wei-zhou questioned that the legislators should vote in person, but the vote did not count whether the legislators were present.

Chen Ju-kuo made a speech on his Facebook page, expressing his views on the nomination of Chen Ju as the director of the Senate. It is an honour for me to do my part for Taiwan. ” 、 “I will use my first heart to protect Taiwan and protect human rights for the rest of my life. ”
According to Chen Ju, first of all, she has received many blessings from everyone over the past few days to cheer her up and cheer her up.
He added, "Your support and trust, give me the courage to persevere, so that I can continue on this long and difficult road to democracy for 50 years."
According to Chen Ju, unfortunately, when she arrived at the Legislative Yuan that day, she was unable to enter the Chamber, explaining to everyone how she led the reform of the Chamber of Supervisors, set up a national human rights institution, and responded directly to many of the opposition's questions about her.
Therefore, she also made a video of the full contents of the written report on her Facebook page today, taking into account the efforts she had made in the past on human rights, and expecting that in the future, she would have to show fairness and justice.

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated that the trip was Malley.
Lois visited Taiwan for the first time since he took office in March this year.
Marley Lois, who has taught at the university, is the executive director of private enterprises and has a long experience in education, culture and women 's entrepreneurship. Lin Tai-hua believes that if a senior executive of the Executive Yuan really orders the collection of information on her public information service, it will become a serious constitutional problem.
(Photo by Lin Hong-tsung) In response to media reports, senior executives of the Executive Yuan suspected to have ordered the collection of information on the Green Committee's advisory services, Lin Dai-hua expressed surprise at the official announcement this evening. If the information is true, she thinks it will be a serious constitutional problem.
In addition, in view of the fact that the survey was conducted by the outside world, which involved a vicious primary election campaign within the Kaohsiung Mayor's Green Party, Lin Tieh-hwa believes that it is inevitable that the people of the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) will be able to provide relief and competition, but if it turns into a factional struggle, it will not only damage the party's image and development, nor will it be acceptable to the public opinion.
However, she did not want to stir up unnecessary spellings before the full picture of the incident was determined, because "before undertaking, first learn to bear", she would continue to visit the grassroots to gain public recognition.
Lin Dai Hua issued a statement earlier, saying that she was surprised to hear that senior officials of the Executive Yuan had ordered the collection of information on her inquiry, because she had never heard of it.
She stressed that she was amazed and in a calm mood. She had been in politics for more than 10 years, and she was confident in her government, service, and all her efforts.
Mr Lam stressed that before there was any clear evidence, he was reluctant to make the news a malicious political act.
"But if the news is true, I think it will be a serious constitutional problem," said Ms Lam, adding that she was even more worried about whether this act would undermine the checks and balances between the executive and legislative powers in the constitution.
She said that not only was Lin Day-hua, but the news also posed a hidden concern for the other 112 legislators.
She respects the Executive Yuan's response to this news today, but I am afraid that this is not enough. I hope Executive Yuan President Lin Chuan will conduct a complete investigation into this, and make the findings clear to the Legislative Yuan, so that the legislature can check and balance the supervision of the executive branch and return to the constitutional order.
(Mid Time)

Science News: About 10,000 years ago, the vast majority of mammals disappeared from the face of the Earth.
However, then not only mammoth died out, but also many other representatives of a large fauna.
In both Americas, for example, almost all animals that weighed more than a ton and lived here until the quaternary period (started 2.5888 million years ago and continues to this day) died out.
Anthropogenic factor The assumption that people are responsible for the extinction of large animals of the ice age, including mammals, was voiced in the nineteenth century by the British naturalist, biologist and anthropologist Alfred Wallace (previously, however, he was more inclined to the version, which was the cause of tremendous physical changes that accompanied the ice age).
The theory soon gained great popularity.
In particular, it is assumed that the person was actively hunting mammoth and other major prehistoric fauna, which led to a rapid decline in their population.
Of course, the influence of various climatic changes on the reduction of the mammoth range, supporters of anthropogenic theory do not deny, but they hold the view that it was active hunting that played a key role in the disappearance of these mammals.
In support of this view, researchers at the University of California, Berkeley, and the University of Pennsylvania, in 2009, reported a major study: scientists found out that shortly after people settled in North America, mammalian species diversity fell by at least 15 to 42 per cent over the millions of years of species diversity.
The mammoth as a valuable source of food, as well as materials for the creation of clothing, tools and weapons, people were hunted not only in North America.
For example, according to a study conducted by scientists of the Polish Institute of Systematics and Evolution of Animals and published in 2015, the role of these mammals was very important in the farm of Cromanians - their bones were used to create a variety of objects, and also served as fuel for fires.
In the same year, the paleontologist Daniel Fisher and his colleagues from the University of Michigan were able to provide further evidence in favour of the anthropogenic version, conducting a study of the chemical composition of 15 tusks belonging to the Siberian mammals between the ages of three and 12.
In particular, they have found that in the last 30 millennia of the species, the age of young children at the time when mothers stopped feeding them with milk has, on average, decreased by three years.
It is known that usually when there is a shortage of feed due to climate change, this age tends to increase on the contrary.
In this regard, scientists came to the conclusion that it was the load on the population caused by the hunters that led to acceleration and early transition to independent food search.
Another unexpected discovery that may indicate the influence of humanity on the extinction of mammoth, very recently managed to make a team of foreign scientists in Siberia - about this expedition tells a new project Discovery Channel "Siberia: DNA of the ice age".
In Yakutia, researchers discovered a large number of animal remains inhabiting these lands at the time, including mammoth, woolly rhinoceros and a cave lion.
One of the important findings was the mammoth scalp.
"This is a really unusual fragment.
The interesting thing is, he doesn't look like he's been ripped off.
In my opinion, rather, the shape of its edges suggests that it was cut off, "says one of the expedition participants Daniel Fisher." I have never seen anything like this in my entire life. "
The scientist suggests that this is the work of a man who lived in the glacier period: removing a scalp from a mammoth, he could get access to the brain of an animal that contained about five kilograms of protein - at the time of this amount was enough to live two weeks.
Daniel Fisher believes that this finding is one of the evidence of people's influence on the fate of the mammoth.
Transcend into the era of these prehistoric animals, learn more about the flora and fauna of that time, as well as get acquainted with the views of leading scientists, including the world famous genetics and molecular engineer George Church and an employee of the London Museum of Natural History, paleontologist Tori Herridge, about the extinction and prospects of mammoth revival can be in the program "Siberia: DNA of the glacial period", which will be released on May 22 at 22: 00 on the Discovery Channel.
Climate factor Despite the popularity of the theory that the responsibility for the extinction of mammoth lies primarily with humanity, there are many who believe that the role of the anthropogenic factor in this issue is overvalued.
In favour of this view, it is argued that many other species have disappeared together with the mammals, which were of no interest to people either as a prey or as a threat.
The direct evidence that our ancestors actively hunted mammoth, for all the time research has been found quite few.
Skeletons of the megafauna of North America, who lived in the current state of Illinois and died in the late Pleistocene (0.126-0.0117 million years ago).
Showcase at the Illinois State Museum.
From left to right: a giant ground sloth, a mastodont, a giant beaver The main cause of the disappearance of these animals is often called the climate factor - this is the second of the two main versions explaining the extinction of mammoth.
This assumption does indeed seem very convincing, given that during the existence of these animals there were a variety of climatic shocks, including not one climate warming, and this could not but have had a significant impact on their range, fodder base and other living conditions.
An analysis of all published radiocarbon dates of fossil residues of six mammals living in Alaska and Yukon between 18,000 and 9,000 years ago led Dale Guthrie of the Institute of Arctic Biology to conclude that changes in the diet associated with changes in vegetation caused by climate shifts played a major role in the extinction of mammoth.
In addition, in general, mammoth disappears gradually (unlike, for example, from their contemporaries - woolly rhinoceros, which died out in a relatively short time, despite the large population), and people, if they played a role in this process, rather secondary - a group of scientists from 43 scientific institutions in the world came to this conclusion, which analyzed the mitochondrial DNA of some representatives of the megafauna of Eurasia. and North America.
However, although anthropogenic and climatic theories often contradict each other, many scientists believe that both factors could play a comparatively significant role: for example, Professor of the Department of General Ecology of the Faculty of Biology of Moscow State University Leonard Polishchuk notes that, although sometimes these versions are perceived as alternative, they should most likely be considered complementary.
By the way, the ecologist in his time put forward a hypothesis that the larger the species, the greater the tendency to extinction it has.
Indeed, the impressive size dictates a lower population density.
In the case of mammals whose range has been reduced many times, this factor could indeed have played a significant role in accelerating their disappearance.
Space theory Despite the fact that most of the scientific community is inclined to one of the above two theories, there are other interesting assumptions about the causes of mammoth extinction.
The most famous of them is the fall of a large meteorite.
Thus, in 2007, American scientists managed to find eight tusks and bones of these animals with holes from the particles of the cosmic body (most of them were found in Alaska, and one skull was found in Yakutia).
The researchers suggested that these traces could be evidence of a collision between the Earth and the meteorite.
This is supported by the fact that on all the samples found the holes were only on one side - probably facing the epicenter of the explosion.
At one time, this gave rise to the theory that it was the meteorite that served as a catalyst for the extinction of mammoth.
However, the majority of scientists questioned it, considering that the alleged collision, as the analysis showed, occurred about 30-34,000 years ago, and the mammoth died much later.
However, in 2012, a group of other scientists investigated a 10-centimeter layer of sediments in Lake Quizeo, Mexico, which contained high concentrations of percussion microparticles, and a layer of charcoal particles formed in forest fires.
Scientists have suggested that this layer of sediment is evidence of a collision with a comet or asteroid.
And chronologically, it coincides with Heinrich ’ s latest event (cyclic changes in the composition of bottom deposits, which are often associated with rapid climate change): it just falls on the late drias, which causes the extinction of mammoth.
However, although this study has added the weight of meteorite theory, many still consider it rather marginal.

Former First Lady Michelle Obama's new book, Becoming a Memoir of Michelle Obama, appeared in 30 languages around the world on November 13.
The Obamas sign a book contract with the publisher Penguin Random House in 2017.
But the Financial Times quoted people familiar with the matter as saying that the contract was worth 600 million US dollars, more than twice as much as when two other former presidents, Clinton and Bush, wrote their memoirs.
The book finally came out in the public eye.
"I've never been a political junkie," the former first lady said in the book.
This has not changed since the last decade.
Mitchell was a family worker who grew up in a black neighborhood on the south side of Chicago and then became a lawyer.
Although she had made it clear that she did not like politics, during her tenure as First Lady, she exercised her influence and faced many of the concerns of the American people: poverty, loss of family, malnutrition, citizenship and gender equality.
She also took advantage of her vision to promote designers such as Prabal Gurung of Nepalese origin and Wu Jigang of Asian origin.
As soon as the new book went on sale, the book combines Obama's two terms and 2,923 working days in the White House.
It is divided into three parts: To be me, to be us, to be more.
In this way, it looks at the past, the present and the future.
She describes how she grew up in a worker's family and experienced bullying during her childhood, and what impact it had on her personality.
When her husband chose to run for the presidency, how did the couple spend their time campaigning and governing?
She reveals her fears about how political enemies affect her children and their privacy.
"I understand that they will attack whatever Barak has done, whether or not that is helping the country or not," she said.
"They just want to see Barack get crushed," she said.
She reveals herself to the deepest part of her heart.
She even revealed that she and her husband had been involved in marriage counseling and eventually gave birth to two daughters through a probationary procedure.
American novelist Keith Laymon commented in December 2018 in the American literary and art magazine The Floating World: "We see Michelle Obama free, very much looking forward to her going high - and then a spin kick."
"When they go down, we go up high," said Barack Obama in a speech for Hillary Clinton.
In the book, Michelle Obama accuses Trump of being "rude and insinuating" about her husband's birthplace, threatening her family: "I will never forgive him for that."
Trump responded last week by saying that Michelle Obama "took a lot of money" to write the book.
The current president also "will never forgive" his predecessor for making America "very unsafe."

According to Mehr News Agency, according to Standard Evening, scientists believe that a star dust formed about 5 to 7 billion years ago and fell on Earth with a meteorite half a century ago is the oldest solid discovered on Earth.
The stars are created when the dust and the floating gas collide in space and create a lot of heat.
Stars are flaming for millions of years and eventually dying and throwing dignity particles into the surrounding space.
These particles or the dust of stars gradually form new stars, planets, moons, and meteorites.
Philip Heck, the senior author of this research and assistant professor at the University of Chicago, says: It was one of the most interesting research I have ever done.
These particles are the oldest solids discovered to date, and they teach us about how stars form in the Milky Way.
The materials that have been investigated in this study are the presolar grains-minerals that were created before the sun was born.
Hack says these particles are solid samples of real star dust.
However, particles before the Sun are very small and rare, and only 5% of meteorites have been observed falling on Earth.
The Field Museum, however, has the largest piece of the Morchison meteorite, which landed in Australia in 1969.
The pre-solar particles studied in this study were isolated and isolated about 30 years before the Morchison meteorite.
The process involves crushing and powdering parts of the meteorite.
When all the particles were separated, there was a paste that smelled bad and similar to the smell of rotten peanut butter.
This meteor paste was dissolved in acid, and eventually only the particles remained before the sun.
In the next phase, researchers found out what type of stars these particles belonged to and how old they were.
Scientists also found that some of the pre-sun particles in this meteorite sample were the oldest particles discovered on Earth.
Scientists found that most of the life expectancy of particles was between 4.6 and 4.
It's nine billion years old, and some particles are over 5.5 billion years old.

After the Beidaihe Conference, the General Writer of the Communist Party of China, Xi Jinping, appeared today (19) in Gansu Province to inspect the Mogao Grottoes in Dunhuang.
It has been learned that this is the first public appearance of Xi Jinping by a senior Chinese Communist Party official since the beginning of August.
Gansu Provincial Committee Secretary Lin Duo (front row, right) accompanied Xi Jinping on the inspection.
(Xinhua News Agency) According to Xinhua News Agency, on the afternoon of August 19, Xi Jinping went to Dunhuang Mogao Cave, China 's key cultural relics protection unit, to inspect the situation of cultural relics protection and research, and promote outstanding historical and cultural achievements.
He stressed that cultural relics, which are bound to the traditional Chinese culture, should be well protected and managed, and that research and utilization should be strengthened to allow history to speak and cultural relics to speak.
As early as February 2013, Xi had visited the village of Wengudui, Weiyuan County, Dingxi City, Gansu Province, at an altitude of more than 2400 meters, and the village of Bringgou, an autonomous county of the Dongxiang Autonomous Prefecture of the Linxia Hui Autonomous Prefecture, at an altitude of more than 1900 meters.
The province of Gansu is the main battleground for the fight against poverty in the country. The number of poor people in the province has decreased from 2.56 million at the end of 2016 to 1.11 million at the end of 2018, and the incidence of poverty has decreased from 12.97% to 5.6%.
According to the world's cultural heritage, Mogao Cave is located in the eastern foothills of Mingsha Mountain, 25 km southeast of Dunhuang City, and was built in the pre-Qin period of the 16th country.
From the 4th to the 14th century, the cave was built in a series of stone caves with a length of more than 1,000 meters.
Many of the grotto buildings in Mogao Cave are outstanding works of ancient architecture, which have great research value.
The grottoes are rich and colorful, absorbing the ancient Chinese and Western art, reflecting more than 1,000 years of Buddhist culture, folklore and historical changes. It is of great value to the study of the history of China and Central Asia.
Since the 1950s, China has invested a lot of manpower and financial resources in the protection, maintenance and research of Mogao Cave cultural relics. In 1961, Mogao Cave was listed as one of the first major cultural relics conservation units in China. In 1987, it became a World Heritage Site.

February 6th, 2020 In Peru, a huge metropolis of Inca ancestors was found using laser scanning In Peru, a huge metropolis of Inca civilization with an area of about 32 kilometers was found using laser scanning, and they were able to find completely ancient destroyed structures using the most modern technologies.
The new city of the Incas will open the veil of mystery, from where the civilization of the Incas appeared.
Archaeologists explored an ancient settlement on the top of a mountain in the Peruvian Andes, which preceded the famous Inca town of Machu Picchu.
The settlement is located in an archaeological area known as Wat'a, which is translated from the local indigenous language as "island", at an altitude of about 13,000 feet (4 km).
It's about 5,000 feet higher than Machu Picchu, the crown of Inca civilization.
“There are almost no trees and vegetation, there are majestic mountains around, and there is a beautiful view of the surrounding valleys, ” scientists said.
National Geographic researcher Albert Lin, along with archaeologists Adan Chocke Arce and Thomas Hardy, used a revolutionary technology known as LiDAR to uncover the entire length of this city, which was populated by the Incas and tribes, often referred to as pre-Incas.
This technology uses devices installed on the plane that emit laser pulses at a frequency of hundreds of thousands of times per second, which allows you to create detailed 3D maps that reveal the terrain and any ancient artificial objects that are usually not visible from above.
The city was explored earlier, using traditional archaeological methods.
Then the scientists found traces of graves, ceremonial squares, residential areas and a large surrounding wall.
However, with the help of LiDAR, archaeologists have been able to identify many more unparalleled features.
Among these features, the team was able to identify the characteristic Inca terrace, as well as the circular structures associated with pre-Inca.
Researchers say that this place was, in a sense, a template for the town of Machu Picchu, which was built in the mid-15th century.

Scientists successfully discovered some very ancient particles from a meteorite that fell to Earth in the 1960s – older than the Solar System – from 7.5 billion years ago.
This is the oldest material ever discovered on Earth, older than anything.
The Mercheson meteorite is listed at the National Museum of the United States.
Scientists have successfully discovered very ancient particles from a meteorite that originated 7.5 billion years ago.
(Wikipedia) meteorite particles observed under electron microscope. (JANAÍNA N.
ÁVILA is an American and Swiss meteorite named Murchison, which fell to Australia in 1969.
It was discovered by scientists a long time ago that the meteorite had a variety of amino acids that were of great interest.
The latest discovery is that the meteorite has particles older than the Solar System on its surface.
Our planet was born about 4.54 billion years ago, 20 million years after the formation of the Solar System.
This means that meteorites have traveled 3 billion years in the vast universe before arriving in the Solar System to visit Earth.
Scientists collected samples from the surface of the meteorite, measured the concentration of neon 21 per sample, calculated how much cosmic rays the meteorite received in the universe, and then estimated the age of the particles.
As a result, scientists found that 10% of the particles were 5.5 billion years old and 60% were between 4.6 and 4.9 billion years old.
This means that meteorites experienced an area of "a lot of cosmic dust" between 4.6 and 4.9 billion years ago, possibly a large amount of cosmic dust from the formation of the Solar System, attached to the Mercheson meteorite that appeared almost simultaneously near the Solar System.
The age of particles found in meteorites was 5.5 billion years ago.
All records were broken 7.5 billion years ago.
The cosmic environment is much quieter than the Earth, which has been experiencing constant crust movement, so meteorites are like time capsules.
The study of the composition of meteorites provides a glimpse of the universe from outer space, before the formation of the Earth.

Kamala Harris reveals what is happening in American politics. Senator Kamala Harris ’ s historic presence as the first vice-president in the campaign has undoubtedly turned the issue of gender into an unwavering issue during this election period.
But is the rules really different for a woman to enter a campaign?
This article, with the help of analysis by Dubai Walsh, director of the American Women ’ s Center and policy at Rutgers University, explains how Kamala Harris ’ s engagement represents the problems women active in politics have been involved for decades, as well as how the situation is changing.
The art of not being angry with the source of the image, Getty Images Women in politics must move on a narrow border between being strong and confident on the one hand and being strong and imposing on the other.
Research shows that institutionalized assumptions about women make them look aggressive while the same traits may be interpreted as confidence in a man.
In the case of women from racial minorities, in addition to gender assumptions, racial assumptions are also added to issues.
In the United States, the "angry black woman" stereotype is a common and ironic stereotype that emerged from the nineteenth century, in which the black woman is depicted as a non-feminine, irrational, and vulgar being.
Debbie Walsh explains: "Women are often accused of not being strong enough for the president or vice president.
On the other hand, you don't know how to show strength without being angry or angry. "
Walsh thinks that in the debate of the first deputies, Mrs. Harris was well able to move on this sensitive boundary and meet expectations.
He adds that Harris would sit back a little bit at some point, though his polite answers were really great when the other side interrupted him, and it was quite understandable for many women who had the experience of running through their words.
Is he good for leadership?
Some women ’ s analysts believe that they should be able to prove their capabilities during the campaign while the sentence does not apply to men, even when the gender of the candidate is not a difficult issue for voters.
People may be able to speculate on a man's capabilities, but a woman must be able to prove and implement them.
The results of the Economist joint survey and the Yoo-Gaw Institute in August showed that the current vice president is much more popular at a very short distance than Kamala Harris.
A quarter of American participants did not know how they felt about Mrs. Harris, and 14 percent of them did.
After the debate, most voters felt that Harris had won, but when they were asked which of them seemed to them more appropriate to become president, the results of some polls were very different.
The Yoo-Goose Institute poll showed that in most Americans, 56% of participants, Mr. Pence would be a better option if Mr. Trump could not continue to serve.
Fifty percent of the participants considered Ms. Harris appropriate.
Among the independent voting population 53% trusted Mr. Pence's ability, and 44% trusted Mrs. Harris.
Although some participants may have felt that a vice president in office would be more efficient than a newcomer senator, from the perspective of activists seeking to realize a more bold presence of women in politics, women should always work harder to prove their adequacy than male competitors.
In his speech, when Kamala Harris accepted Biden's proposal for the Democratic Party during the election period, he said that the title of the First Vice President was undoubtedly extraordinary, but the title most valuable to him was the mother of the family (or so-called Mamuela herself).
In introducing herself to the American people as the candidate for the first vice president, Ms. Harris has a strong emphasis on her life and family values.
She is the mother of two children.
His mother raised him alone, and his stepfather's family later became an important part of his life.
Image source, Getty Images Image description, Sarah Palin was criticised by the media and some Democrats for her parenting style. Mrs. Welsh explains that for the men who are active in the political scene, always talking about family life, such as credit added, but women are the political scene only in recent years that use family life to gain credit.
In her opinion, the situation of women is complicated because if a politician woman does not have children or talks about them, it becomes questionable to others why she does not speak about them, and if a politician woman talks about children, and if the children are young, it becomes a question of who then takes care of their children.
On the contrary, when a man defines his family life, a commendable picture of a family man is made of him, but there is no question about family priorities and who cares for children.
All we need to do is look back a little bit at the time when Sarah Pilin was the candidate for the first vice president in the 2008 election and remember what was the result of talking about family affairs.
Pilin, who was governor of Alaska at the time, was criticised by the media and some Democrats for his parenting style.
At the time, Mrs. Pilin had five children, one of whom was very young and suffering from Down's disease.
Critics said he had failed to join the campaign in his family affairs.
The stereotype of motherhood is undoubtedly changing, but active women in politics can not yet be completely freed from the expectations of motherhood.
Recently, when Judge Amy Kenny Barrett mentioned being the mother of seven children in his engagement speech to attend the Supreme Court, the Republican Party members promoted him to be his strong point, which could ensure that he would be a better judge.
Image source, Getty Images Image description, according to some women 's analysts, should be able to prove their capabilities during the campaign, while in the case of men it is not so, Mrs. Walsh points out that Kamala Harris is in a certain position because she has two older adopted children.
