The history of the Albaza island is becoming more and more evident through the centuries due to the fact that the Albazins made "unsubscriptions" about the state of affairs, and the Manchu intelligence did not sleep.
As a result, after the translation of the Manchu ancient documents, Russian and Chinese maps coincided.
This fact was discovered by the Amur historian Vladimir Trukhin.
He published his observation in the second issue of the regional almanac "Dialogue" of the Amur Regional Scientific Library named after N. N. Muravyov-Amursky.
Vladimir Trukhin took up this research in 2014, when in the image "Chinese figure depicting the defense of Albazin" found in the network an atlas in good quality, consisting of four maps, as reported in his blog on the website "Amur.info".
Interestingly, the document is stored in the library of the United States Congress.
It's available online.
"... [They] inspected the city; a fence was built of wood; a width of more than fifteen" to "(tuo); a length of more than twenty" to "(tuo); a number of fighters were built around; in the south of the city, an empty place measuring more than one" to "(tuo) was left at the gate; in one" to "(tuo) the shelter was made of broken stakes; in the other" to "(o) The wooden fence was in two rows; [there] there are three hundred men..."
This description of the island, translated by Vladimir Trukhin into Russian, dates back to 1681.
Details can be found in the Lifanyuan Report to the State Chancellery on Russian people illegally crossing the border, living in Yaksa and other places.
Yaksa is the name of the Albaza island in Manchu.
There was another attack - three years later, which gave a description of the changes: from the superstructure of the towers, to the construction of new buildings.
The first description corresponds to the island built by Nikifor Chernihiv, the second - 1684.
Both sources indicate that there are about 300 people in the island.
The towers were completed, as evidenced by the drawings made by an unknown Manchu observer.
He climbed up to a snot in the distance, drew and recalculated.
Vladimir Trukhin found out that there was an observer at the island more than once: the towers in the description divided into new and old.
It was confirmed that the second floor on the traffic tower did not appear immediately, the Manchu scout, however, simplified the drawing.
Interestingly, during the reconstruction of the Albaza island in the Moscow Museum-Reserve "Kolomenskoye", the travel tower received forms similar to the Manchu drawing - low and without superstructure.
And the following description can "pull" to the opening.
The indication of a wall with alternation in one and two rows of logs may suggest that the wall of the island could have a "cockroach" structure with a step of about two meters.
This fundamentally changes the idea of the walls of the Albaza island, since in most of the reconstructions they were presented in the form of a rear.
That is, the island was surrounded not by a palisade of centuries-old pines, but by a structure of two rows of hedges, between which earth or crushed stone is poured.
These and other secrets of the Albaza island and not only revealed the Amur local history almanac "Dialogue of Time".
The second issue was published recently in the Amur Regional Scientific Library named after N. N. Muravyov-Amursky.
Working in close cooperation with scientists and teachers of educational institutions, amateur local lore and local lore associations allows the library to publish it.
Meanwhile, Vladimir Trukhin actively continues to explore the history of the Albanian island, in 2018 he prepared an extensive report with drawings and historical figures, which was announced at a symposium in Korea.

The electronic page of Almanakh is posted on the website of the SBUK "AONB named after N.N. Muravyev-Amursky" at: www.libamur.ru The first issue of almanac was held in 2018 in the year of the 160th anniversary of the formation of the Amur region.

Mr. Farrokhnejad was already...

Isna / Markazi Shahab Naragh on August 18, 1353, after 13 million years of space rotation, inclined towards the Earth and finally landed in Narraq, a city in the city of Delijan, the functions of the Markazi Province.
The three kilos and two hundred grams of meteorite hit the roof of Masoumi Narraq High School on the landing.
A high school, of course, was being built at the time, and at that hour, fortunately, no one was inside the building.
Part of this school has now become the Meteor Museum and various meteorites have been exhibited.
Although this museum does not have a good location and facilities, it can have something to say in terms of the scientific and diversity of the meteorite, especially since it is the first meteorite museum in the world, which was built at the landing site of the meteorite, and it was opened a while ago with the presence of Monsan, Minister of Cultural Heritage.
The issue that attracts attention in this museum is the absence of a sparse meteor!
In fact, the location of a meteorite, which is an excuse for making a museum in this museum, is said to have a piece of it exhibited in this museum and what was actually the basis for the report, was what former mayor of Narraq said at the opening ceremony about this meteorite.
Moradi said: “Meteor Narraq was once lost and after they informed him of his discovery, they did not give us himself or any sign of it! ”
As mentioned on the City of Narraq website, this meteorite and pieces were first taken home by the workers working at the school with the idea that diamonds or white gold were found, but were eventually discovered by the City Council, Gendarmerie and the Mayor, and kept briefly in Gendarmerie Narraq.
The publication of the news of the fall of the meteorite sends reporters and scientists from scientific centers around the country and eventually the stone is transferred to the Geophysical Institute of the University of Tehran.
From Narraq to Tehran, traveling with the meteorite Professor Iraj Eshghi is now a professor at the University of Aachen, Germany.
Years ago, he worked at the Iranian Geophysical Organization and is the first expert to have seen this meteorite.
At the opening ceremony of the Shahab Sang-e Narraq Museum, he said: I had gone to Zanjan on behalf of the Iranian Geophysical Organization for a mission that the organization informed me that a meteorite hit the ground in Iraq and you should check on it.
The gendarmerie had picked up the meteor, 'added a love.
During the correspondence with Tehran, the country ’ s general gendarmerie ordered Gen. Narraq to deliver the meteorite to the representative of the geophysical organization, the servant.
I took the asteroid and took it to Tehran.
According to ISNA, the city website of Narraq wrote: "The first experiments were performed on this celestial rock, Dr. Eshghi and his colleagues at the Institute, during preliminary studies, confirmed that this stone is a celestial meteorite of a silicate rock type (cordierite).
The preliminary report of this research, called Report No. 64, was published in January 1974, and according to this report, this stone is a metallurgical and construction type and its constituents are chondrite.
The microscopic view of this elliptical celestial rock, some parts of which have been sharper and edged by fracture in space or collision with the earth.
The main color of the stone is dark gray and a bit green, in some parts of which metal minerals are seen in golden or light gray.
In the external part of the stone, two kinds of black shell can be seen, one due to the impact of the asphalt on the roof of the innocent high school, which is still slightly bitumen and other ground objects attached to it and black, the other is a thin shell with a width of about 1 mm and caused by burns due to the impact of the atmosphere.
Microscopic tests of this meteorite show that it is a crystalline and uniform rock in which there are metallic minerals of nickel and iron compounds in the form of black spots and without geometric shapes.
In the following, because of lack of laboratory facilities, a few grams of this stone was transferred to Germany by Dr. Eshghi in 1983 and the rest of which is 2.900 grams is delivered to the Geophysical Department of the Geophysical Institute of Tehran University.
Dr. Eshghi conducts numerous research on stone at the University of Aachen, Germany, and provides the results to researchers around the world.
Research has led to the production of several scientific articles in reputable journals including NASA and the news of meteorites called Narraq 's celestial meteorite from researchers in Belgium, Germany, Portugal, America and...
And the name of the city is located next to the asteroid. "
According to ISNA, the mayor of Narraq decided in 2012 that in cooperation with Professor Eshghi who was a resident of Germany at the time and a professor at the University of Aachen, he would establish a meteorite museum in Masoumi Narraq High School, the meeting place.
Meteor, where are you?
Mahmood Moradi, Mayor of Narraq, told ISNA about this meteorite: Since seven years ago, we have tried to track the status of Narraq meteorite and, if possible, transfer it to the meteorite museum.
But the geophysical organization initially denied the existence of this meteorite and said that such a meteorite has never been delivered to the geophysical organization!
Moradi continued: Therefore, after a long time of searching, we connected with Professor Akashi, the founder of the Iranian Geophysical Organization and its chairman at the time of the encounter of the meteorite.
He gave us a bill that proved that the geophysical organization had delivered this meteorite.
When we showed these masks to the geophysical organization, they could no longer deny the issue of having a meteorite.
He added: It is true that the minutes caused the other geophysical organization to fail to deny the possession of the asteroid Narraq, but in principle, there was no change, because the geophysical organization now said that the asteroid Narraq is missing!
Meteor Narrak, hidden sight!
Of course, the meteorite was found after years!
Moradi says: The geophysical organization of 2015 wrote a letter saying that the meteor Narraq has been found but is not willing to deliver it to us.
Interestingly, this organization wouldn't even give us a picture of this meteor.
The photo of the meteorite was not even given to Professor Eshghi who was once a member of the organization and is now an international figure!
Some argue that meteorites have a lot of material value, that given that the missing meteorite was once spoken of, there is even a possibility of being sold, especially knowing that meteorite parts have a lot of material value.
It's an undeniable fact that these sky stones have high material value.
With a simple search on the Internet, you can see jewellery that a piece of meteorite has been used in making them, and that has greatly increased their price.
A simple 14-carat gold ring, which only costs $70, is sold for $1,400 after being decorated with a meteorite.
Accordingly, I tried to get at least a new photo of Shahab Sangi to be published in ISNA, but the contact with the Geophysical Organization was limited to the office of the head of the organization and he finally said: Our answer to you is the same answer we gave to the municipality of Narraq.
I stressed that the interest of missing and even the sale of the meteorite is important, and if they give us a new photo of the meteorite, all the doubts will be resolved, the work that came to this point, the head of the office of my number, and he said he would discuss my talk with the head.
A few days passed and no news or photographs came.
I called again, and the same thing happened between me and the head of the office, and we didn't get any photos of Narraq's meteor.
According to ISNA, meteorites are meteorites as long as they're in space, and they have their own light, but after they come to Earth, they become meteorites and we see their light only until they hit.
From now on, it is our duty to shine light on them to find out where they are and what they are, and of course, as a scientific and even material capital that can attract tourists and scientists to their downward regions, where they have come from and what they have come to.
In particular, Narraq, in addition to being the landing site of this valuable meteorite, has a valuable historical texture and therefore has a valuable place in the eyes of tourists, and any new element that can add to the special position of Narraq as a tourist city should be taken seriously, let alone that this element is a party of space.
The end of the message

What secrets will Latvia discover in the "archives of the KGB" of Latvia decided to publish the so-called archives of the KGB - and this immediately caused a lot of excitement in the country.
The public longs for spectacles and revelations of the "bloody Soviet regime".
What will they actually find in the published documents, and why would a Christmas fireworks box be opened instead of Pandora's box?
The part of the archives of the KGB of the Lithuanian USSR, which is in the possession of Latvia, must be published by law until December 31 (the term of office of the structures occupied by them ends).
Then they decided to celebrate the Catholic and Protestant Christmas in this way, but in the end it was decided not to delay and publish the first set of data quickly.
There is no practical point in this manipulation of dates, it is no more than an attempt in this way still to remove the hype.
This is about the next one.
Despite the fact that these materials can not be used in everyday life and do not have any political consequences, people crave spectacles.
The interest of society is especially concentrated on public figures, especially artists and scientists, which generates petty scandals of a yellow character.
However, all those waiting for scandals will have to be disappointed.
Latvia followed the road that Lithuania had profiled, and there were no names in the published information (except for the events of the 1940s and 1950s).
An agency file and part of the so-called Delta file will be published, which mainly lacks real names, and only undercover nicknames are present.
In some cases, people can be identified on indirect grounds, but in most cases this is a pointless set of data 30 – 50 years ago.
The library of the KGB agency in alphabetical order — 10,612 cards.
It includes the names of both the agents active at the time and the excluded.
Also, the First Department of the Republican KGB (external intelligence) entered into this archive general data on persons who planned to leave the USSR abroad, and the names of potential candidates for recruitment in the event of war or an unforeseen situation.
Another KGB agency statistical register consists of 4141 cards.
It still has the same names as the alphabetical one, just grouped by belonging to the structural units of the State Security Committee.
This archive was used in Soviet times for reporting and operational value was not presented.
The account card contains the name, patronymic, date and place of birth, address, place of work, position of the recruited person.
The name and title of the recruiter, the number of the KGB department, the name of the head of the department who approved the recruitment, the date of recruitment and the marks on the exclusion of a person from the agency are also indicated.
A file of “KGB freelance operational workers ” will also be published — persons who, on a voluntary basis, provided“ assistance in counter-intelligence activities at transport, industrial facilities, as well as individual institutions of the Ministry of Defense ”.
There are 75 cards in this archive.
Freelance operators were not given call signs, but they created an abbreviation from the first letters of the name, surname and patronymic.
And it should be remembered that this data is only the republican KGB, not the "KGB in general", and with the central accounting system in Moscow they have no connection.
Here, the beautiful sign of the publication of the "archives of the KGB" also fails.
There has never been access to central accounting systems in Moscow in Riga.
At the same time, the real names and other initial data on the persons who were in operational development (in the KGB of the USSR there was no fashionable foreign concept of "dossier", all this was called DOR - "case of operational development"), will be subdued.
The fact is that the anti-Soviet laws of Latvia do not comply with the EU principles on the protection of personal data.
And at first, only this pointless set of data will be publicly available, and with difficult navigation: for example, it will be impossible to find any data by simply entering a query in the search bar.
This will require either a few hours to read almost the entire data array, or an accurate knowledge of the archive code of the required document.
And to a simple user, he is initially unknown.
In addition, much of the information from public access is simply withheld for reasons of national security.
This is particularly true in cases of corruption, smuggling and currency fraud, which were also in the process of being drafted by the Republican KGB.
There is also a suspicion that the file itself was subordinated back in the 1990s according to frankly corrupt schemes: there are people who could afford to simply buy out the data on themselves, and in the vast majority these are the respected pillars of Latvian society.
All this archive publishing system is copied from the Lithuanian one already operating in the same mode.
As in Lithuania, it contains many open data that are classified as secret.
For example, biographies of members of the Central Committee of local Communist parties, employees of the Governments of the LitSSR and the LatSSR, as well as background data on senior officers of the republican KGB.
All this can be found with some desire in ten minutes, without resorting to any tricks or the use of something "declassified".
All these are public people with public biographies, who lived a whole life under Soviet rule in the Baltic States.
It should also be remembered that not only the "acting" agency was entered into the file, but also potential individuals for recruitment.
A person may not even know that he was the subject of the DOR, and the appearance of his name in the “KGB archives ” may give rise to a charge of defamation.
Thus, the primary publication of the “KGB archives ” is a meaningless event that has nothing to do with modern life and is similar to ritual grave digging (only without tangible consequences).
Another thing is that the subsequent publication of an array of agent reports can give a huge material to historians as an authentic material of the era.
In this regard, the experience of Lithuania is characterized, where the "exposing" publications of an array of agency reports have turned into a fascinating reading, and none of the agents have suffered.
Reading the already published Lithuanian analogue of the "KGB archives" can deliver cannibal and aesthetic pleasure.
Among the reports of the agency are exceptional masterpieces that characterize both the era and the people who are involved in counter-intelligence activities.
Here's, for example, the report of Agent Daiva.
Judging by the context, this is a young girl working as a guide-interpreter in the Vilnius branch of Intourist.
Lieutenant Colonel Posemauskas AV received an undercover report on 17 April 1985, but all the literature is clearly Daiva's.
The agent was assigned a task to work with a group of Lithuanians from immigrant families in the United States and Canada, who in the 1980s actively came to the LitSSR to pull up the forgotten mother tongue and generally join the Motherland.
Among them were many young people who studied exchange at Vilnius University and in Moscow.
Agent Diva herself, judging by the context, recently studied in the United States also on exchange.
But in addition to the murderous characteristics of the American Lithuanians, she describes the morals of the Intourist.
To begin with, the characteristics of a Lithuanian girl who took courses in a foreign language for work in Intourist.
"(...) (here and then the name of the object of operational development is faded, and so will be in any published archival material - LOOK) during her studies at the university communicated mainly with Kairikshten Migley, all other acquaintances ceased quite quickly, especially with the guys who first bother her, and then also quite quickly refuse to communicate.

But basically the array of information consists of operational summaries of regional offices, which can be interesting only for very narrow historical studies, and then as an auxiliary material.

The publication of a part of the captured archives of the republican KGB in the 1990s attracted increased attention as a source of local sensations (although it was intended as an exclusively ideological event).

The ancient Inca sanctuary Machu Picchu, one of the greatest architectural achievements of mankind, was deliberately built in a remote place at the top of a narrow ridge to protect it from avalanches, landslides and floods.
The report on the new study was presented at a meeting of the Geological Society of America, and briefly tells Science Daily about it.
Machu Picchu is the most famous city of the Incas, which is believed to have served as a huge sanctuary.
It was built in a hard-to-reach place, which has long been puzzling scientists.
Why did the Incas choose a site that is not easy to get to?
A new study shows that the answer to this question may be related to the geological fractures that lie under the sanctuary.
Thus, Rualdo Menegat, a geologist at the Brazilian Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul, suggests that the Incas deliberately built not only Machu Picchu, but also some other cities over tectonic faults.
The analysis carried out by the researchers showed that the site near Machu Picchu is literally saturated with cracks in rocks.
And their sizes vary markedly - from tiny cracks on individual stones to large 175-kilometer faults in river valleys of this region.
Scientists have found that a whole set of fractures, the so-called "fracture zones", have been formed here for the last eight million years, thus forming the mountains of Central Andes.
Some are oriented from the north-east to the south-west, while others are oriented from the north-west to the south-east.
Together form the shape of the letter "X", and Machu Picchu stands just at their intersection.
The study confirmed that areas of the city and surrounding agricultural fields, as well as individual buildings and stairs, are oriented along the main fault lines.
A similar "model" was found in other Inca cities - Ollantaitambo, Pisak and Cusco.
Each of them is located precisely at the intersection of geological faults.
According to scientists, this gave the Incas some advantages.
For example, melt and rain water in the cracks drained into the city, so there was always a filled source.
In addition, the city was protected from avalanches and landslides.
Fractures and cracks at Machu Picchu also helped to drain this area during intense showers.
"About two thirds of the efforts to build the sanctuary were related to the laying of underground drainage," Menegat says. "The existing fractures have contributed to this process, and now help us to explain their remarkable safety."

A group of scientists from the United States, Switzerland and Australia managed to determine the age of star dust particles extracted from the Merchison meteorite that fell in the Australian area in 1969.
It turned out that they were formed long before the emergence of the Solar System, during the period of increased star formation.
Their age is more than five billion years, the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences reports.
Recall, the age of the Sun is about 4.6 billion years, the Earth is 4.5 billion.
Interstellar dust is an important component of our galaxy.
It affects star formation, as well as the thermal and chemical evolution of the galaxy.
While dust is less than one per cent of the mass of the interstellar environment, it is a key component of stars and habitable planetary systems, which can help to learn more about their composition and life cycle.
The meteorites available to humans also consist of this dust.
In some of them (only five percent of the total number of meteorites dropped on Earth) microscopic solid particles of interstellar matter contained in interstellar gas can be detected even before the Sun is formed.
They're called pre-solar grains.
Scientists believe that these are the only material evidence available today to man of the period preceding the Solar System.
It was such grains, in the amount of 40 pieces, managed to allocate scientists from the Merchison meteorite.
Information on the composition, structure and size of interstellar dust can be obtained from astronomical spectroscopic observations, and with the help of more sophisticated models to estimate the lifetime of dust.
Most of these models now predict the average life time of interstellar grains of about 100 million years.
However, there is also a longer lifespan of up to billions of years.
Interstellar dust dating was not possible directly by astronomical methods.
With the decomposition of long-lived radioactive nuclides, too, due to an unknown initial isotope composition.
Therefore, scientists used data on the time of action on the grains of cosmic rays - flows of high-energy particles flying through our galaxy and penetrating into the solid.
When interacting with these beams, new elements are formed, and the longer the grains are exposed, the more these elements are formed in them.
As a result, the researchers came to the conclusion that they received the oldest solid materials ever found.
Most of the pre-solar grains were between 4.6 and 4.9 billion years old, but some reached 5.5 billion years.
This suggests that the period of active star formation occurred in our galaxy five to seven billion years ago.

The Shanghai Planetarium (Shanghai Science and Technology Museum Branch), the largest astronomical museum in the world under construction, held a press conference yesterday afternoon to announce China's first complete recovery of the crater, the first time it has obtained a full record of meteorite sightings in Xishuangbanna.
The Shanghai Museum of Science and Technology has become the first museum in China to fully recycle craters.
The meeting showcased the largest main crater in the Xishuangbanna meteorite rain recovered recently by staff of the Shanghai Science and Technology Museum and the "meteorite hunter" in Yunnan, as well as the crater No. 1 and No. 2.
At the same time, the Shanghai Science and Technology Museum has released a collection of 10 kilograms of iron meteorites, which will be named "Wei Li".
It is reported that Xishuangbanna meteorite body and crater will meet with the public at the opening of the Shanghai Planetarium.
At about 2145 hours on June 1 this year, a fire meteor suddenly lit up over Jinghong City, Xishuangbanna Dai Autonomous Prefecture, Yunnan Province, and quickly disappeared a few seconds later.
Xishuangbanna meteorite rain has discovered more than 500 meteorites with a total weight of not more than 50 kg, classified as ordinary chondrite L6, and is applying to the International Institute of Meteorites for the name "Mangui meteorite". The angle is about 70 degrees.
Xu Weibiao, a researcher at the Zijinshan Observatory of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, announced the scientific research results for the first time at a news conference.
Most of the individual meteorites that landed were covered with a black shell of about 0.5 mm thick, with a clear boundary between the crust and the internal matrix of the meteorite.
Most of the meteor matrix recrystallization is severe, the outline of the chondroids is ambiguous, the diameter is generally about 0.5 mm. The meteor section is rich in black melt veins, the width is about 0.3 mm, and some melt veins pass through the entire meteor section.
These fusion veins retain the information of major impact events occurring in space by meteorite parent asteroids, and are excellent samples for studying the history of impact collisions between planets in the solar system.

Since the birth of mankind, as a society, the mysteries of the night sky have occupied the thoughts of many people.
Every time we look at the stars, one thought comes to us – whether we are alone in the universe.
At present, the human race is only taking the first steps in the exploration of outer space around our planet.
With the fact that we are not using stone age technology.
It would be very presumptuous to think that modern man (and man in general) is the crown of intelligent thought, the only one in the vast universe.
On the other hand, at the present time, the latest archaeological research, information about which is increasingly publicized in public, leads humanity to the idea that the generally accepted historical picture that was taught to us at school and university, at least, is incomplete.
Moreover, some of its provisions require a fundamental review.
This is particularly evident when archaeological finds demonstrate an unprecedented level of civilization.
The world around us is full of mysteries and mysteries.
Despite the seeming exploration of our planet.
There's still a lot of facts on it that can't get an explanation from scientists.
Undoubtedly, the most interesting mystery that awakens the minds of many generations are the pyramids in Egypt.
Scientists and current historians present the fact that the pyramids are a structure built no more than four thousand years ago.
However, even among official scientists, there is an opinion that the age of pyramids is much more formal.
The most piquant fact related to modern science and pyramids is that the former still has no explanation of appointment last.
The complex, consisting of seven pyramids of Egypt, located on the banks of the Nile, is significantly different from others.
And this dissimilarity suggests that their construction was carried out by some ancient civilization, much earlier than the appearance of the first Egyptian dynasties.
Among the supporters of alternative history there is an opinion that the pyramids are the result of the work of aliens and are specially left on the ground, as a reminder to humanity.
Perfectly designed blocks, which consist of pyramids puzzled by modern builders and architects, who, having the most modern tools, can not boast of such accuracy in processing.
And in general, the view of the pyramid from a bird's eye view leads us to the idea that it is not just a building, it is part of some cyclopical unit that uses unknown types of energy.
It is possible that the pyramids were used as a base or launch pad for alien spaceships.
Relatively recently (in 1947) a strange stone structure was found in Egypt, built about seven thousand years ago.
Preliminary studies have already shown that this structure is an ancient astronomical observatory - stone structures are located in it in a circle.
A special role in this observatory is played by specially established sightings aimed at the constellation Orion and the Great Dog, in which the star Sirius is located.
What caused the interest of ancient scientists in Orion and Sirius?
For what purpose have they been singled out out of all stars?
About half a century after the discovery of this observatory, it became even more famous and not because of itself.
The fact is that in 1994 archaeologists announced that the special mines located inside the Cheops pyramid are also strictly oriented towards the stars in the constellation Orion and the star Sirius.
In addition, mines coming from the pyramids to the north point to the Polar Star.
That is, the whole giant complex was conceived and built with the orientation, according to modern scientists, ventilation mines, on the main stars of importance for the Egyptians.
The Egyptians were convinced that their deities had come down from heaven.
They attached great importance to these legends – Egypt was a deeply religious state and the power of the Pharaohs was based on the powerful support of the then clergy in the form of priests.
Perhaps the direction of the pyramid mines and the observatory on these stars directly pointed to the place where the aliens came from.
Traces of alien visits to our planet can be found not only on the banks of the Nile in Africa.
For example, in the south of Great Britain there is an amazing ancient structure of Stonehenge.
It is believed that this giant ancient observatory was built about ten thousand years ago.
Amazing accuracy in the location and orientation of the stones.
In general, the riddles of Stonehenge include not only the method of its construction or use, but also the fact of its appearance.
Nowhere in the immediate areas was anything like this.
In addition, it is difficult to imagine where the stones from which Stonehenge were made came from.
Perhaps this building is also part of the alien infrastructure?
Also mysterious are the gigantic structures in Central America, which modern science refers to the fruits of the culture of Maya and Aztec Indians.
But in them riddles and questions are no less than in the Egyptian pyramids.
In addition, the mystery of the disappearance of the majority of the inhabitants of highly developed civilizations in those places, long before the emergence of Europeans on the American continent, remains unclear.
Could the primitive tribes create such structures?
Many, even official scientists, doubt that.
Humanity, in conducting research in space, must understand that sooner or later it will face representatives of other civilizations.
And perhaps one day, a man will discover life outside his planet, and then people themselves will become aliens, aliens for someone.
No one knows what trace this will leave in the life of the found creatures.
And how then will the influence of humanity on their culture, science and worldview be reflected.
Source: tainy.net

Scientists: Pyramids are connected to the mountains 14.06.2020, 10: 00, Different Specialists of the National Institute of Anthropology and History of Mexico (INAH) published a study that explains why ancient Central American residents erected pyramids.
The leading authors of the scientific work, which appeared on the INAH website, were archaeologist Coleta Almanza and Dr. Pedro Francisco.
Scientists, led by Almanza and Francisco, analyzed the whole complex of grandiose objects of the pre-Spanish period existing in Mexico.
These are, in particular, the pyramids built by the inhabitants of Mesoamerica.
Why pyramids?
What was the purpose of pyramidal objects that are found throughout Central (and not only) America?
Trying to answer these and similar questions, scientists eventually presented a fairly simple explanation. – Pyramids in Mexico are not the same – everyone has unique features.
They have only a pyramidal shape.
With this in mind, we have suggested that the pyramids are a kind of sacred copy of the mountains.
The inhabitants of Mesoamerica imitated nature, in which pyramidal structures often occur, so they built pyramids.
We believe that the greatest impression on people was made by volcanoes, which were considered places where life came from.
Accordingly, copies of volcanoes were of enormous importance, - noted the authors of the scientific project.

In 1969, a meteorite was found to have fallen in southeastern Australia, now known as the Murchison meteorite, from which scientists recently found cosmic dust that is older than the Solar System, dating back about 7 billion years.
By measuring how much silicon, carbon atoms converted to helium-3 and neon-21 in the minerals of the meteorite, scientists can analyze how long they have been exposed to cosmic rays, their age, and the type of stars they come from.
The so-called stars are formed through a large amount of gas dust clouds, and after death, their own matter is sprayed into space. Over time, these matter cools and condenses into stardust and mineral particles, eventually forming a new generation of stars, planets, and asteroids with interstellar gas.
Some of these small meteorites have been thrown around the universe and have remained unchanged for billions of years, like space-time capsules, until they hit other objects.
However, only 5% of the countless meteorites that fall on Earth contain a presolar grain, the tiny solid matter that formed in interstellar space before the formation of the Sun. Between 2 and 30 microns, the analysis found that some of the pre-solar particles in the Mercheson meteorite have been formed for about 7 billion years, much older than the 4.6 billion years of the Solar System.
This result supports the hypothesis that seven billion years ago the Milky Way had just burst into a wave of stellar formation, and that the Mercheson meteorite probably came from that time.
In particular, scientists who have studied the Mercheson meteorite know that the meteorite has a difficult odor to describe, like rotten peanut butter, which is produced by some volatile organic compounds after heating or dissolving.
The study was also published in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.

My grandfather was an architect.
He was also an amateur photographer and I remember, growing up, my grandmother showed me all these slides of his travels, including to Machu Picchu, on light boxes.
My going to Machu Picchu felt like a pilgrimage — in some ways connected to my family, but also just for me.
The trek gives you a sense of how big the Inca Empire was - it's incredible to see a piece of this huge civilization.
They managed to grow crops in different altitudes and different climates and build these villages.
All the Inca trains and paths around the Andes were built to get to Machu Picchu.
For seven days we didn't see a shower, a motorbike, a plane, a cellphone.
We just saw nature.
About halfway through the trek, there were some thermal baths coming from the mountains.
Yet the rivers are really cold, like snow.
You have to get all the right clothes for many different climates — it's freezing at night, and incredibly hot in the day.
You are more with yourself.
It was hard to breathe at this altitude.
Your brain is not thinking properly; it operates differently.
This article is an edited version of a piece that originally appeared in the New York Times Magazine (NEW YORK TIME / Jaime Lowe) My grandfather was an architect and an amateur photographer.
When I was a kid, my grandmother used a light box to show me hallucinations of his travels, including Machu Picchu.
I went to Machu Picchu, like to pilgrimage, which in some way has to do with my family, but also for me personally.
This journey will make you feel how big the Inca Empire was — it is incredible to see such a large scale of civilization.
They managed to grow crops at different altitudes and climates to build these villages.
The roads around the Andes, large and small, were built to lead to Machu Picchu.
In seven days, we did not see a shower, a motorcycle, an airplane, or a mobile phone.
All we see is nature.
In the middle of the day, there are some hot springs in the mountains.
But the river is really cold, like snow.
You have to wear clothes that suit all kinds of climates - very cold at night and incredibly hot during the day.
You're closer to your heart.
At such a high altitude, it takes a lot of effort to breathe.
Your brain can't think properly; it's moving in another way.
This article is an edited version, originally published in The New York Times.

A large meteorite that fell in the United States two years ago and provoked the hunt for its debris, turned out to be rich in ancient organic matter.
Scientists are grateful to the local residents who managed to quickly find fragments of the space body.
The meteorite, which caused a stir in 2018 in the United States, presented a surprise to the scientist due to a large-scale search, which allowed to quickly detect its debris.
A large car flew over the mid-west of the United States on January 16, 2018, Gazeta.Ru told how a bright fireball was observed in the American states of Ohio, Michigan, Ontario and several parts of Canada.
According to the American Geological Service, on Tuesday, 20 / 09 local time, hundreds of Michigan residents felt a loud cotton and a concussion when the car fell.
Earth fluctuations were also recorded, the nearest seismometer, located eight kilometers from New Haven (Michigan), detected shocks with a magnitude of 2, scientists reported.
According to the estimates of the Detroit Weather Service, the car that flew into the atmosphere was a stone more than two meters in size.
Using the available information, some Michigan residents decided to try their luck in search of a meteorite (meteorites are called the debris that fell on the ground).
That's when the meteor fever started among the residents who went to look for the debris.
Scientists also had their own interest — the sooner a meteorite is found, the less time it has to undergo the destructive action of water and atmosphere.
“As soon as it touches water, metal begins to rust and minerals like olivine change, ” says Philip Heck of the Chicago Museum, author of an article in the journal Meteoritics & Planetary Science, which first described the Michigan meteorite.“ Water also contaminates numerous cracks that typically permeate meteorites and form when it is knocked out of the parent aerodrome. steroid past impact ".
With NASA weather radar, meteorite hunters estimated the speed and trajectory of the meteorite, which allowed them to calculate the approximate location of the fall.
The next two days after the fall, one of the collectors, Robert Ward, discovered a 22-gram meteorite chip on the ice of Lake Stroberry in the vicinity of Hamburg, Michigan.
Together with a friend, he decided to immediately give a meteorite for study at the Field Museum of Natural History.
In total, 24 different institutions took part in the study of its composition.
“This study is an excellent example of how civil science makes a significant contribution to collaboration with research institutions, ” Hekk says.“ The meteorite Hamburg is one of several meteorites that were quickly removed from the frozen surface and brought to the institute. ”
A few more fragments of the meteorite were discovered on the same day, and 13 - in the next two weeks, the article says.
Having received the meteorite at his disposal, Hekk used all available technical means to study it - weather radar, microscopes, spectroscopes, various types of mass spectrographs, magnetometer and computer tomography.
It turned out that the fallen meteorite belongs to the chondrites of the H4 group, rather rare meteorites.
H4 meteorites are distinguished by the fact that at the moment of separation from the parent body they are heated, which leaves untouched some of their components, including the so-called chondras, hardened drops of molten silicate substance.
The rapid search, which did not allow the meteorite to contaminate the earth's substances, has borne fruit, and scientists have found many interesting components in its composition.
First of all, these are organic substances, which in its composition were found more than 2600.
Among them are hydrocarbons, as well as compounds containing sulfur and nitrogen.
This allows scientists to speculate on how such substances were found on the early Earth billions of years ago.
"These components were formed on the parent asteroid immediately after its formation, when it was still hot after accretion and due to the decay of radioactive elements present in the early Solar System," Hekk explained. "The fact that this chondrite was rich in organic, supports the hypothesis that meteorites played an important role in bringing organic matter to the early Earth."
Analysis of the composition of the meteorite allowed scientists to claim that the meteorite separated from the parent body about 12 million years ago — that is how long it took its way to Earth, during which the substance was exposed to cosmic rays.
The asteroid itself was formed 4.5 billion years ago — only 20 million later than the formation of the Solar System itself.

The oldest material on Earth was older than the Solar System In 1969, scientists observed a very unusual phenomenon over the Australian village of Murchison.
It was then that a 100-kilogram meteorite exploded in the sky, the particles of which were scattered within a radius of several kilometers.
Scientists managed to obtain about 52 kilograms of a space object and in the 1990s microscopic particles of a mineral called silicon carbide were found inside it.
Researchers have speculated that they deal with so-called pre-solar grains, interstellar substances that were formed long before the appearance of the Sun and the Earth.
At that time, they were not able to calculate the exact age of the materials found, but recently it was finally possible to do so.
As scientists suggested, they were dealing with the most ancient materials that have ever appeared on our planet.
The discovery of a group of scientists from the United States, Switzerland and Australia was described in a scientific publication entitled Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.
By shredding the found samples of the Murchison meteorite, researchers managed to extract 40 particles of pre-solar grains.
Determining their age proved to be a very difficult task, but scientists did so using data on the time of exposure to cosmic ray grains.
This term is commonly understood as flows of high-energy particles that fly through our entire galaxy and penetrate into solids.
It is known that when interacting with these beams, new elements are formed in the presolar grains, and the longer the interaction takes place, the more these substances are observed in the end.
After studying the data on the interaction of grains with cosmic rays, scientists found that they formed between 5 and 7 billion years ago.
Based on the fact that our solar system was formed 4.6 billion years ago, it turns out that the particles found by scientists were formed long before the appearance of the Sun.
According to scientists, the materials found at the moment are the only available evidence to mankind about the existence of the period prior to the emergence of the Sun and the Earth.
In general, such materials can be found inside many meteorites that have fallen on our planet.
But it is extremely difficult to detect and recognize them, because they are embedded deep into space objects and have microscopic dimensions.
The fact that scientists managed to extract them from the Murchison meteorite is a great success.
Through the study of these particles, scientists can make various assumptions about the evolution of the universe and tell a lot of interesting things about how our planet was formed.
This is one of the most exciting research I've been working on.
These are the oldest solid materials ever found, and they can tell us how stars have formed in our galaxy, ” said one of the authors of a study called Philip Heck.

Researchers discovered a high-altitude settlement in the Peruvian Andes, which is older than the famous Machu Picchu,.
Researchers have discovered a high-altitude settlement in the Peruvian Andes, which is older than the famous Machu Picchu, Newsweek reports.
National Geographic researcher Albert Lin and local archaeologists used Drones and the revolutionary LiDAR laser technology to see what this Inca settlement was.
It is located in the archaeological area of Wat ’ a at an altitude of 3.9 kilometers, that is, 1.5 kilometers above Machu Picchu.
"There are almost no trees and vegetation, there are majestic mountains around, and there is a beautiful view of the surrounding valleys," said Lin.
The existence of the settlement has been known before.
This area was explored by traditional archaeological methods.
There were found graves, places for ceremonies, dwellings and a high wall around.

Famous Ukrainian traveler Vadim Ivlev several years ago visited Peru, where he saw one of the wonders of the world - the ancient city of the Incas Machu Picchu.
The first attempt to get to this location was unsuccessful, but over time, the Ukrainian finally managed to see one of the most famous monuments of the planet.
During the quarantine in 2020, access to Machu Picchu for tourists is limited, but Peru is developing new rules for visiting the ancient city in compliance with security rules during the pandemic.
What is unique is this mysterious place - in the column of Vadim Ivlev for RBK-Ukraine.
The first attempt to visit the monument was unsuccessful Since I was a young man, I dreamed of seeing the legendary mountain citadel with my own eyes.
But it was only after 30 years that the dream was realized.
The main purpose of the trip to Peru in 2013 was to visit Machu Picchu, one of the most outstanding world monuments.
But the first attempt to reach the mountain was unsuccessful.
We settled in the Sacred Valley, in the town of Ollantaytambo.
From there was an expedition train that was supposed to take us to the mountain of the same name.
It was February, and it was at this time of the year due to the rains that the turbulent mountain river Urubamba blurs the railway tracks laid at the bottom of the deep gorge.
We were very struck by the spectacle of the rapid flow of dirty water, along which we had to travel tens of kilometers.
In it everything was raging, boiling, splashing in all directions and stremchlev ran along the gorge.
Photo: A girl in the town of Ollantaitambo (Vadim Ivlev) We have always remembered the joy when we were invited to take a train, and our expression of face when we were ordered to leave the car.
The railroad security department canceled the trip, and we got the money back.
No one knew how long the ban would last.
And we have already bought the return tickets.
That is, having crossed the distance in half the globe, we had to sit in a rural hotel without seeing Machu Picchu.
"The car was swinging all the time, and the river was running insanely behind the windows." In order not to think about the bad and pass the time, we went to inspect the local monuments.
You never know where you'll find or where you'll lose.
That evening we were waiting for a real adventure - a trip at night to unfamiliar places and meetings with local experts who spoke exclusively in the language of the Inca.
Due to the interest of collectors in the art of the pre-Columbian era, mass production of copies of artifacts began in Peru - a large number of artisans began to make copies of various artistic value, quality and cost.
For those who want thrill, there is an opportunity under the cover of the night to visit the vault of such forgery.
Here is my advice: do not rush to put money after sunset, because in the morning next to the "underground" museum you will find the same artifacts in the windows of small shops, but twice cheaper.
However, the meeting with people of a pirate kind is worth trying.
Photo: Car number 13 to Machu Picchu (Vadim Ivlev) So, we shortened the time and waited.
Only on the third day came a happy moment.
That was our last chance.
Ironically, we still have seats in No. 13.
Our alarm is on the rise.
But at seven o'clock in the morning, our train left the station, and we finally got lucky.
The car was swinging all the time, and the river ran madly behind the windows, the waves splashed and the water stormed.
There was a feeling that the car was just floating on the river, because at each turn the stormy flow rose almost to the wheels.
In an hour and twenty-five minutes, the train arrived at the Aguas Calientes station - here we moved to the bus, which another broken hour climbed up, to the top, which drowned in dense clouds.
We had to drive 20 kilometers on a soil serpentine laid in the mountain jungle.
The bus could have fallen into the abyss several times, but we were lucky - we finally achieved our goal.
An engineering miracle that keeps many more secrets The name "Machu Picchu" from the Aymara language is translated as "the old peak".
It is a "city among the clouds", located at an altitude of 2,450 meters above sea level.
It is known from the chronicle that the Incas considered themselves children of the gods of the Sun and the Moon, who were built numerous temples.
On Mount Machu Picchu there is a majestic temple of the Sun with a cherished energetic stone altar.
It is here, at the top of the pyramid, at its peak, that the stone of the sun of Intihuatan is placed.
They say it emits energy.
That's why we took this opportunity to sincerely try to feel it.
Photo: Air at an altitude of 2450 meters above sea level - discharged (Vadim Ivlev) Now tourists are no longer allowed to touch it.
In this city ends the legendary Inca trail, it is very popular among pilgrims of spiritual and historical tourism.
On the next mountain, among the clouds, there is a temple of the Moon.
It takes three hours to climb up the death ladder.
Machu Picchu is an engineering miracle built without the use of cement, metal tools and wheels.
A lot of secrets are uncovered, but the excavations are not completed.
Archaeologists have found that most of the walls and canals of the city are hidden underground.
Are there any underground labyrinths here?
How can I get there?
It's all still a mystery.
Some paths begin near the fortress and are lost in dense jungle - no one knows where they lead.
Inside the citadel there is a terrible altar stone for sacrifice.
Also found about 100 human skeletons, half of which are female.
Photo: Incredibly beautiful panorama of the "city among the clouds" (Vadim Ivlev) In an ancient abandoned fortress, among gray stones and grass terraces, friendly animals - chinchillas play.
This is their home, their natural environment.
The air on the top is very low, so almost everyone suffered from the mountain disease, even our guide, who had blue and shaking hands.
But for all the trials, difficulties and excitement, we were awarded with an unmatched sense of delight from the incredible beauty that was seen there - on a mountain in the midst of clouds.

Science News: An international group of scientists discovered the lost capital of the ancient Mayan kingdom on a Mexican farm.
Researchers suggest that the site of the excavation, called Lacanja Tzeltal, was a large city, the capital of the Maya Kingdom of Sak Tz 'i', located in the modern state of Chiapas in southeastern Mexico.
This area was first inhabited in 750 BC, and people have lived here for a thousand years.
Archaeologists found monuments, on one of which were written texts describing rituals, battles, mythical aquatic snakes and the dance of the god of rain.
The ruins of the pyramids, the royal palace and the ball court were also discovered.
Scientists will continue to draw up a map of the city, using lidar - an optical system that uses phenomena of light absorption and scattering in optically transparent media to scan objects and create a three-dimensional picture of the surrounding space.

Following an international airport construction program near Machu Picchu, Peruvian people ’ s assurances have intensified in opposition to this action.
According to ISNA and quoted by Art News-Piper, archaeologists, historians, tour guides and other segments of the community ask the Peruvian government to stop planning to build an international airport near the historic site Machu Picchu.
The multibillion-dollar construction project of the airport, which has been proposed to shorten the travel time for tourists to the area, began earlier this year, while several bulldozers paved a large portion of the land near the entrance to the Holy Valley.
The airport is scheduled to be completed by 2023.
Tourists can visit this historic site, which is listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, using a train originating in the city of Kosko, although it takes 3 hours to reach Machu Picchu.
This historic site attracts more than a million visitors each year.
This figure is twice as much as UNESCO promised to protect this historic place.
Art historian Natalie Majlov of Peru, the University of Cambridge, who started the wave of protests, believes that the airport program in the region is very short-sighted and will jeopardize this historic site over the next long term.
He stated, "The construction of the airport in this area endangers the protection of one of the world's most valuable historical and ancient places."
The Peruvian government believes that the international airport construction project in this location will create more than 2,000 jobs and bring various economic opportunities.
Machu Picchu, known as the Lost City of Inca, was built around 1450 at the height of the Inca Empire.
It is located 50 miles from the capital of the Inca, which is why it has never been found and destroyed like other Inca cities.
Over the centuries, the forests around the complex have grown and covered it, and for this reason it has remained hidden from sight and only a few knew of its existence.
On July 24, 1911, Machu Picchu was introduced to the Western world by American historian Hiram Bingham in a speech at Yale University.
He was guided by local guidance where he occasionally visited the site.
Machu Picchu was registered as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983, and as a pure masterpiece of architecture and a unique symbol of the Inca civilization is now known as one of the seven new wonders.
The end of the message

However, these proposals are still in the hands of the High Court.
An important indicator of the importance of women's rights is the proportion of women in charge.
During the reign of Ma Ying-jeou, there were more than 10 female heads of government.
At present, only five heads of government are women. Is there any important policy to promote gender equality?
Lu Hsiu-lien first rejected Tsai Ing-wen with six words: "I doubt she knows me." Then she said seven words: "I doubt she knows me."
From an academic point of view, who is against women.
In the history of the feminist movement, the author Faroudi reviews the 1970s, where the feminist movement in the United States had made significant progress, and then fell in the 1980s, critical to political conservatism and the commercialization of the economy.
At the age of 74, Lui Sau-lien has brought a wave of women ’ s rights movements, but has not been able to maintain her original intention. Tsai Ing-wen, 62, has made only limited contributions to the promotion of women ’ s rights, but has become a counterproductive force to women ’ s rights.
Both of them valued political power over women's rights and failed to promote gender equality with political power.
I don ’ t care if the two women are in trouble with each other, just fear that the president will be judged as the key to the fight against women ’ s rights. (Author: Associate Professor, Department of Social Work, Tunghai University) (China Times)

Despite being optimistic about science and technology and relying heavily on it, Tang Feng-hsi still has an antiquated side in private.

On the other hand, after I entered the attic, I became busy, and I did not have time to play "Wish" or "Black Mirror: Pandasneki" on live TV. I could only finish reading the story written by my network friends. "
In the sunset, Tang Feng walked out of her office and into the whole vegetarian restaurant near the Social Innovation Center, where she always had dinner and went home for a walk.

Today, Tang Feng is less temperamental, and is always patient in the face of all kinds of questions.

As she faces the ideal country in her heart, she also sees light. “I believe that the present world is the best possible and the best. ” Standing in front of the gaping halo of light, she closes her eyes and is gentle and determined.

MOSCOW, June 2 – RIA Novosti.
Scientists from the National Institute of Anthropology and History Scientists from the National Institute of Anthropology and History of Mexico (INAH) published a research paper in which they explained the reason for the construction of pyramids by the ancient inhabitants of Mesoamerica, which was located in the territory of modern Central America.
A summary report on the presentation of the study is published on the website of the organization.
Scientists, after studying the famous pre-Spanish objects found in Mexico, suggested that the pyramids were sacred copies of the mountains.
In their opinion, so ancient residents imitated the nature in which they met pyramidal forms - for example, silhouettes of volcanoes.
According to the authors of the study, pre-Spanish societies of Mesoamerica sought not only to visually recreate such natural objects, but also to create some sacred meaning in them.
They noted that, for this reason, the pyramids were the centers of ancient cities.
According to archaeologists, the pyramidal form also facilitated the task of erecting a high monumental object, which was especially important for ancient people.

Tang Feng's life has always been marked "prodigy" and "genius".
But what she pursues is never an individual hero, but a collective contribution made after listening to each other; the "Mask Map", which is popular all over the world, is one example.
Tang Feng walked into the office alone, feeling a bit hot in the room, first went to the lounge to change a ventilated jacket, then walked back to the table to pick up the tablet, slowly put the radio device on the skirt, the whole process is relaxed, not like a full day trip, the time is severely squeezed.
When she was ready, she was like a chatbot who didn ’ t need any information from her staff, so she could quickly respond to every question she had. The time around seemed to suddenly start spinning at 1.5 times. Her full forehead, too, was filled with sweat.
In the cabinet, which is dominated by senior civil servants, academics and professional politicians, Tang Feng's presence is not only special, but also difficult to classify.
As a matter of fact, in 2016, Lin, who was President of the Executive Yuan, invited Tang Feng to serve as a digital political commissar, hoping to introduce her "heterogeneity gene" so that the wisdom of the public can participate more actively in public policy formulation.
Tang Fong herself hopes to become a "channel" linking the private and public sectors, and shares with the government her experience of participating in the global open community for many years.
In order to respect the work style of Tang Feng, Lin promised that she would not have to go to the Executive Yuan every day to work and conduct a full and transparent meeting, while authorizing her to solicit talent from various departments and the public to set up a public digital innovation space (PDIS) for the Executive Yuan.
Even though the cabinet had been reconstituted, neither Lai Ching-tak nor Su Chung-chang, the successors, had overruled Lin Chuan's promise.
During the peak of the COVID-19 outbreak earlier this year, the "Mask Supply and Demand Information Platform" (commonly known as Mask Map), designed by private information engineers and government departments, has been widely used in Taiwan. The model of collaboration has indeed brought a whole new look to the governance of the Government.
In addition to the mask map, the Taiwan government uses digital tools to ensure that home isolators do not move around to increase the risk of infection.
Tang Feng has been interviewed by many international media to share her experience in Taiwan.
Although it has attracted worldwide attention, Tang stressed that mask maps are not an achievement of a particular individual, but a result of overall social innovation.
When it comes to social innovation, Tang Feng defines it as: "In short, it is a matter of 'people, people help'.
Like a mask map, that is, everyone thinks it's a good idea, and everyone comes and does it. "
In her opinion, when social innovation occurs, the role of the government should be to give full support, but not to interfere in the direction.
The Government is not the main body and does not control the direction, "she said.
As a teenager, Tang was convinced that it would be more efficient to assemble wisdom than to build a car behind closed doors.
This belief makes Tang Feng-liang happy to see that any public policy, as opposed to the people affected by the policy, has very few members of the relevant government departments who have power to make decisions.

Image copyright ESO / M KORNMESSER Image caption The Sun will eventually become a white dwarf.
Is the Earth truly a lonely planet?
The latest answer is that there are at least 36 intelligent civilizations in the Milky Way, similar to the Earth, that are active and technologically advanced.
Or alien civilization, another planet.
However, because it is too far from Earth, we have very little possibility of contact with alien civilizations, and whether or not these alien civilizations have existed or have existed, may become a mystery that will never be solved.
What is the concept of being too far? The closest thing to Earth is 17,000 light-years away.
Whether an alien civilization similar to the Earth exists, or whether the Earth is an isolated planet in the Milky Way, this ancient question for the first time has specific speculations, a step closer to the answer.
Christopher Conselice and Tom Westby, professor of astrophysics at the University of Nottingham, published their research papers on the Astrobiology website (April 2020) and Astrophysics magazine (June 2020).
Your device doesn 't support playing multimedia material HD to shock the surface of the Sun is like this image copyright Getty Images Image caption How does the number of planets formula in the Solar System work out?
Exploring how many other planets in the Milky Way are likely to have Earth-like civilizations, the most important tool to date is the Drake formula, proposed by astronomer Frank Drake in 1961.
This formula contains seven factors, based on which the number of intelligent civilized planets that may exist in the Milky Way with the ability to communicate is computed.
In order to perform the calculation, all factors must be determined first.
These factors range from the average number of stars born in the Milky Way each year, the proportion of these stars that can form terrestrial planets, to how long it takes for a species of Earth civilization to have the ability to communicate and exchange information that can be detected in space.
In 2013, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) astrophysicist Sara Seager proposed her formula to calculate how many planets may have been discovered in the next few years with measurable signs of life.
The factors of this formula include the number of stars that can be observed, how many of them are surrounded by planets, how many of them can be observed, and so on.
Siegel's formula does not concern whether alien life is intelligent, whether it can use radio emission signals, and so on; she is concerned about whether the number of life on the planet is large enough to change the chemical composition of the planet's atmosphere, just like the Earth's atmosphere.
This combination of chemical components is similar to human fingerprints, and analysis of the atmosphere can be used to determine whether or not life exists on that planet and in what form it exists.
Image copyright ESA / Hubble / NASA / Janaína Ávila Image caption Some of the sun-front particles in the Mercheson meteorite (small right figure) may have come from an evolutionary star similar to the ovoid nebula.
But why haven't these two tools come up with meaningful results so far?
The key is that most of the factors that make up the formula are unknown.
Stuart Clark, author of The Big Questions: The Universe, points out in the book that the most important use of Drake's formula is to provide a way of thinking, a framework.
Because only one of the seven factors necessary for the formula operation is known, that is, how many new stars form each year, a calculation generally accepted by astronomers is that an average of seven stars are formed each year.
Other factors in Drake's formula are currently unknown and difficult to determine.
As with the Drake formula, the value of many factors is uncertain, and there is not much quantitative value to calculate, but both help to sort things out.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption Will there be creatures in the alien ocean?
Assuming that NUDT astrophysicists Conselis and Westby proposed new ideas on the basis of Drake's formula, or that the formula was adjusted with new factors and hypothetical premises, the conclusion would be more substantial than the meaningless span of "between 0 and 1 billion".
The Sun is a star, a planet orbiting a star, such as the Earth, which can form life under certain conditions.
Their calculations are based on a set of hypotheses, first of all, assuming that on other Earth-like planets in the Milky Way, the formation and evolution of intelligent life is the same as that of Earth's civilization, then a planet will naturally emerge billions of years after its birth, and then evolve.
According to the Copernicus principle of celestial biology, everything can happen if necessary, from chemical reactions to planetary formation.
Assuming there are scientific rules for the formation of intelligent life, rather than random, random, irregular, or unique, then according to the calculations of the Conselis team, the result is "at least 36", that is, the Milky Way may have at least 36 alien civilizations that are as active as Earth's civilization and have the ability to communicate.
Image copyright Science Photo Library Image Caption Copernicus "Sun-Heart" 1543 The operation of the cosmic Copernicus limit before the world was introduced into the Astrobiological Copernicus Limits (Astrobiological Copernicus Limits) concept of astrobiology.
Copernicus, a Polish priest, astronomer, mathematician, physician, and doctor of law during the Renaissance period of Europe in the 15th and 16th centuries, proposed the Sun (the Sun is the center of the universe and the Earth revolves around the Sun), leaving behind a great book, The Theory of Astrology, which radically alters the cosmology of mankind.
The Copernicus limit, known as celestial biology, has strong and weak limits.
Weakness limit refers to the possibility of intelligent life occurring at least 5 billion years after the formation of a planet, such as at most 5 billion years ago, or any time thereafter.
Under the looser Copernicus theory of the weak limit, the Conselis team deduced that the Milky Way currently has at least 928 alien intelligent civilizations.
This means that more civilization exists at a closer distance, and a survey takes about 700 years at a time.
Image copyright ESA / UCL Image caption Astronomers first discovered water in the atmosphere of a planet in a galaxy outside the Solar System in 2019, meaning it may have life-supporting signs. K2-18 is 111 light-years away from Earth.
The strong limit includes a series of very strict conditions; based on the strong limit hypothesis, life occurs between 4.5 billion and 5.5 billion years after the formation of a similar planet in the Milky Way.
The number of intelligent civilizations in the Milky Way is estimated to be between 4 and 211, of which the maximum probability is 36.
Conselis points out that this is a conservative estimate.
Because there is another set in their calculations, that is, the time when Earth civilization began to send radio signals to the universe, so far about 100 years.
Because extraterrestrial civilization is at least 17,000 light-years away from Earth, the first contact and communication between humans and aliens will have to wait another 6120 years before it can occur, if Earth civilization can continue into that day.
However, they stated in their paper, "The search for alien wisdom and civilization not only reveals the existence of life forms, but also provides a clue as to how long our own civilization will last."
Even if we have nothing to gain, we are looking for our own future and destiny. "
If alien intelligent life is found to be widespread, it suggests that Earth civilization may have existed for more than a few hundred years; if no active civilization is detected in the Milky Way, it may be a bad omen for the life of Earth civilization.
Image copyright Airbus Image caption The European Space Agency will launch the Ariel Mission in 2028, which will help confirm the existence of life on exoplanets.
According to Oliver Shorttle, an expert at Cambridge University in the UK, many of the factors in the latest calculation are too vague and need to be analyzed and clearly defined.
In an interview with The Guardian, he pointed out that these factors include how life on Earth first appeared.
Also, how many of the Earth-like planets considered suitable for survival are truly capable of producing intelligent life and civilization.
Professor Andrew Coates of Mullard Space Science Laboratory, University College London, acknowledged the rationality of the Conselis team ’ s assumptions and calculations, but the search for extraterrestrial civilization is, at least for the moment, more realistic in terms of the Solar System planets closer to Earth.
These efforts include the Exomars 2022 Mars landing and exploration program, in cooperation with the European Space Agency and Russia, and future plans to explore the Moon of Jupiter and Saturn.

The days of Hungarian culture in Chelyabinsk will open an exhibition of the famous Hungarian architect Imre Makowiec "Connecting the sky and the earth".
Introduction to the creativity of the ancestor of the school of organic architecture will be held on Thursday, April 19, at 17 o 'clock in the State Historical Museum of the Southern Urals.
A tour of the exhibition will be carried out by her Hungarian curator Lerinz Czerniusz.
As previously reported by Ural-Press-Inform, the exhibition will feature drawings, graphic drawings, photographs of the buildings of Makovetz, as well as a wooden model of the Roman Catholic Church of the Holy Spirit, erected in Paksha in the 1987-1991 years.
The organizers promise that the concept of the exhibition items will enchant and surprise guests: the roof of the building is a forehead, platbands - eyebrows, window sills - elbows.
Interestingly, the names of the architectural details in the Hungarian language fully coincide with the names of the parts of the human body.
The anthropomorphism in the ideas of Makovts connected with architecture: facades of houses - like faces, ceiling in the memorial chapel - human ribs.
"Every building is a complete thought," Makovets said.
And he explored space, and he studied the position of man in it: lying, sitting, standing.
He was interested in folk art - signs and symbols.
He tried to connect the sky and the earth in the same plane.
Imre Makovets (1935-2011) is a graduate of the Technical University of Budapest and head of the MAKONA architectural bureau, awarded many international and state awards, including the honorary member of the American Union of Architects, the Union of Architects of Scotland, the British Council of Architects, an honorary citizen of Budapest.
Among his most famous works are the buildings of the Roman Catholic Church of the Holy Spirit (Paks, Hungary), the Ecumenical Church (Devec, Hungary), the House of Health (Changer, Hungary), the Hadjmatykum Bath (Mako, Hungary).
Each building created by Makowiec remains in the history of not only Hungarian, but also world architecture as having a unique style and deep symbolism of the author 's work, combining the traditions of national architecture and innovative engineering solutions.
Photo courtesy of the State Historical Museum of the Southern Urals

The editor-in-chief of Roman Badanin's Project was called the "puppet of the West".
The Federal News Agency, which is called the largest Internet resource associated with entrepreneur Yevgeny Prigozhin and his "troll factory", has published a so-called investigation into how "anti-Russian propaganda is fabricated".
The first hero was Roman Badanin, editor-in-chief of Proekt-Media.
The publication is known for its investigations into corruption in the vicinity of Russian President Vladimir Putin.
It was the "Project" in its work "Iron masks" for the first time wrote about the minority shareholder of the bank "Russia" Svetlana Krivonogikh, who received her fortune due to her close acquaintance with Vladimir Putin.
FAN claims that Roman Badanin is recruited for anti-Russian activities by the US intelligence agencies, which have created in Russia a "machine to boost the political situation".
The main proof that Roman Badanin works for the American special services is that in 2017 he took special journalistic courses at Stanford University.
That's where he was recruited, says FAN, because Stanford, according to this agency, has a long-standing relationship with the CIA.
The "Project" itself was allegedly invented by the U.S. intelligence services with the active assistance of the Soros Foundation to facilitate the organization of a "color revolution" in Russia.
Also, a lot and in detail is told about foreign curators and foreign financing of the "Project".
"Project" in this publication is described as "an instrument for interference in the internal affairs of the Russian Federation and conducting destructive activities that threaten the security of the state", and Badanin is called "traitor" and "puppet" in the hands of Western structures.
Finally, the FAN promises new revelations of foreign agents, information about their recruitment and attempts by the United States to exert pressure on Russia – as the agency supposedly has a "array of documents" at its disposal.
Journalist Roman Badanin worked at Gazete.ru, was the editor of the policy department and deputy editor-in-chief of Kommersant.
From December 2011 to August 26, 2013 he was the editor-in-chief of the website Forbes.ru, then worked as the executive director of the Internet projects service of the information agency "Interfax".
From 2014 to 2016, Badanin headed the RBC website, then he was the editor-in-chief of the Rain TV channel.
In 2018, he launched the "Project", which has published several high-profile investigations at different times.
In them, journalists described the lives of the inhabitants of Rublevka, the relationship between Rosneft and Amaffi, the earnings of Ramzan Kadyrov and Adam Delimkhanov.
The special project "Iron masks" released in two parts is dedicated to Vladimir Putin and his close environment.
Roman Badanin / Facebook In an interview with Radio Liberty, Roman Badanin said that he did not read the "investigation" of the FAN, but he fully understands why it appeared: - The FAN Agency is a simulacre, sleep fragments of Prigozhin.
It's different, even against the background of the rest of the madmen.

A more detailed response consists of three possible parts.
The first is what is described by the term "safety".
