The second part is probably something personal.
We can't know that, but the president is a hidden person at all, which means maybe something personal is added to it.
And the third, the part that we, as journalists, can talk about more or less confidently, is the surest way to hide something.
If you do not tell people that you have a nephew named Mikhail Shelomov, it becomes much more difficult for them to know that according to the documents he is a billionaire, although he lives much more modestly, to put it mildly.
Oh, and so on.
The less information of this kind, the less leads for possible investigations, questions, interest, discussion in society. - Why is it important for you to convey to readers information about the personal life of high-ranking officials? - Fuck her, with her personal life, we have no such purpose - to write about personal life.
We actually reported Mrs. Krivonogids for the simple reason that, for a second, she got a lot of assets that were once state assets, and we don't really understand what they're doing.

Our interest is only in that. – Can this article of the FAN, an information release, have any impact on the activities of the Project, on the issues of its further investigations?
In general, what is all this aimed at? – Naturally, it is aimed at intimidating us, sending such a sign, preparing public opinion for subsequent sanctions and reprisals.
Not necessarily towards us, but towards a wider range of people.
As for us, of course, we do not plan to stop anything.
We'll tighten our security protocols, we'll be even more careful.
But, of course, we do not plan to stop, and the Iron Masks will, among other things, continue.
- They write a lot about you going to Stanford, and they care so much about it, and they say that you were there in Stanford, meeting people who recruited you to the CIA.
What were you doing at Stanford?
And if you can say who is funding your project, I will also ask you to answer this question. – The first question is quite easy to answer.

Unfortunately, the Russian legislation is designed so that we are forced not to disclose their composition, as we do not disclose the composition of individual donors.
Because the legislation in this paragraph is designed with repressive goals - to punish both those who receive funding and those who implement it, - said the editor-in-chief of the publication "Project" Roman Badanin.

Source: Ural Federal University Researchers from the Institute of Geology and Mineralogy and the Geological Institute in Ulan-Ude of the Siberian Branch of the Russian Academy of Sciences together with colleagues from the Novosibirsk State University and the Ural Federal University have opened a new mineral – uakititis.
This unusual in structure vanadium nitride was found in an iron meteorite found in 2016 in Buryatia near the village of Wakit.
This article is published in Proceedings of the 81st Annual Meeting of The Meteorological Society 2018.
The authors investigated the iron meteorite Wakit.
Its mass is only 3.96 kilograms, and its main component is camacite, an alloy of iron and nickel, a typical component of metal meteorites.
However, small impurities were also found in Wakita, including troilitis, a rare form of iron sulfide, and dobreelitis (Fe2 + Cr2S4).
Inside the microscopic inclusions containing these two minerals, vanadium nitride was also found.
His detailed microscope and spectrometer studies showed that it was 71.33 per cent vanadium, 5.58 per cent chromium, 1.56 per cent iron and 21.41 per cent nitrogen.
Thus, it is vanadium nitride with chromium and iron.
Researchers called the new mineral discovered by them a huakitit, in honor of the nearby village.
In the medium of dobreelite inside the meteorite, it forms isometric cubic crystals, and in the medium of shreiberzite - rounded "grains", the size of which is usually less than 5 micrometers in diameter.

Researchers from the National Institute of History and Anthropology of Mexico (INAH) presented a study on the ancient cult rock art in the city of Huayacocotle.
These drawings have been found to be related to mythological beings and cosmic ceremonies.
The summary report is available on the INAH website.
The rock images are located in the modern town of Cueva de la Mesita.
They are related to the mythological traditions of Otomi culture.
Scientists note that this region has been densely populated for thousands of years.
It was rich in various resources, including water - the river flows here.
In fact, it was a "sacred corridor."
In recent years, 26 sites with pictograms have been found in the gorge.
Some of them, the oldest, are related to the traditions of hunter-gatherers.
But most of the rock graphics are related to the Otomian tradition of white painting, common between 950 and 1521 AD.
Researchers distinguish in Cueva de la Mesita two main clusters with pictograms.
The first is located at an altitude of 11 meters and contains 12 graphic elements.
They're drawn on the ceiling, right above the observer's head.
According to archaeologists, this is due to astronomical motives.
Dark rock ceilings ancient artists associated with the night sky.
This cluster is particularly interested in scientists.
They compared it to the works of post-classical iconography of central Mexico and concluded that this set of drawings was necessary for the New Fire ceremony.
This ceremony was typical of many peoples of Mesoamerica who had a strong fear of the end of the world.
For example, Aztecs and Mixtecs believed that the era lasted only 52 years, at the end of which the world must be destroyed by the gods.
In order not to do so, every 52 years, New Fire ceremonies were held, in fact, to revive the world.
This 52-year calendar was ritual and the indigenous peoples of Mesoamerica were closely associated with "zodiacal" signs.
This is also pointed out by the authors of the new study.
They found that the first panel on the ceiling depicted constellations that the ancient locals had named Mika and Mamalhuastli.
It was these constellations that were associated with the New Fire ceremonies every 52 years.
In addition, scientists pay attention to other elements on the first panel.
They are some anthropomorphic characters with rings in their noses trying to bite someone.
One of them is depicted with a severed leg.
In these motives, the researchers saw the characteristics of Teskatlipok, one of the main deities of different nations.
He wore a mirror shield, watching people's affairs with his help.
The same god in his various incarnations was the creator and the destroyer of the world.
It is believed that the cult of Teskatlipok in the valley of Mexico City brought the people of Nahua, and then it spread throughout the country.
This god was given many traits that he had not originally possessed.
In this case, an image of a giant snake - Mischoatl was found in the cave.
The people of Nahua believed that Teskatlipok sometimes took the image of Mischoatl to hold the New Fire ignition ceremony, that is, the revival of the world.
As for the second panel, it is located at the ground level.
It depicts six biomorphic creatures.
According to scientists, it was conceived as a contrast to the first panel.
If the upper images hint at the celestial arch, the lower ones are probably connected to water.
Researchers believe that the creatures depicted on it can be Vemas - giant mythical beings who, according to the Otomi beliefs, lived in the pre-human age.
However, one day there was a global flood, these creatures were trapped on the tops of the hills and eventually turned into megalithic stones.
According to Anna Maria Álvarez, one of the co-authors of the study, the cave with drawings and the "sacred corridor" itself are still places of worship for the Ottomans.
They consider the Moon and stone monoliths to be entities associated with their ancestors.

TODAY 11: 43 2018-03-23T11: 43: 40 + 03: 00 Change text size: A A A Large fragment of Seimchan meteorite physics of the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research was enlightened by neutron tomography.
Scientists have received information about the composition of the asteroid and the distribution of substances in it without destroying the fragment.
The study will give scientists new knowledge about the origin of the Universe and celestial bodies.
Seimchan meteorite is named after the village in Magadan region, which fell in 1964.
The asteroid belongs to a rare and beautiful variety of meteorites - pallasites.
Such space bodies consist of grains of olive mineral with iron-nickel alloy inclusions.
Scientists conducted a study in Dubna, using a pulsed high-current reactor on fast neutrons IBR-2.
Neutron radiation penetrates into object deeper than X-ray and allows to obtain more accurate picture of internal structure.
Thanks to this, physicists built a three-dimensional model of a meteorite fragment, on which all its metal threads, size and volume of olivine grains are visible.
The results of the study of physics were sent to the international journal Meteoritics & Planetary Science in order to attract the world community to the topic, "Izvestia" writes.

= Affiliate post AdMe.ru and Depositphotos = It is unlikely that you will find a travel photographer who does not dream of going on a long trip to Latin America.
Paradise islands and azure glaciers, mysterious traces of ancient civilizations and colorful modern cities, inaccessible rocky mountains and stormy waterfalls - the Latin American region includes 46 countries, each of which has something to show.
If you are looking for a source of inspiration, look at this collection of travel photos from the authors of Depositphotos, which capture the wonders of Latin America.
Each image is filled with the atmosphere of this colorful region rich in contrasts.
Let 's start our trip to Latin America with Bolivia.
Sol de Maniana ("morning sun" in Spanish) is a geothermal region located at an altitude of more than 4,800 m above sea level.
This is part of the Altiplano-Pune volcanic complex with numerous mud lakes and pools. 2. 3. 4.
The population of Latin America is the carrier of a variety of genes.
Here the descendants of the Aztecs and Incas mixed with European colonists and Africans.
Similar processes also took place in culture.
Therefore, national carnivals, costumes and dances are one of the main attractions in countries such as Colombia, Bolivia and Brazil. 5. 6.
If you go camping in the Andes, alpaca and llamas will meet on your way more often than the locals.
The Peruvians tamed these animals more than 6,000 years ago.
Today, they are actively breeding in the highlands of South America, including the regions of Peru, Ecuador, Chile and Bolivia.
Europeans called lamas camels of the New World. 7.
Torres del Paine is a huge national park in the highlands of Chile, where tourists can feel the spirit of Patagonia.
There is a temperate climate, so even in the summer the temperature rarely rises above 11 ° C. The traditional hiking route to Torres del Payne takes from 5 to 10 days and passes through numerous rocky mountains, picturesque glaciers, fjords and blue lakes. 8. 9.
These photographs were also taken at the Torres del Paine National Park in Chile.
You can take a good look at the majestic cliffs of Cuernos del Payne, which dominate the panorama of the local landscape.
The National Park (Torres del Payne means "blue towers") is named after these rocks.
The mountains reach 3,000 meters in height. 10. 11.
Until recently, the Atacama Desert (Desierto de Atacama) was considered the most arid place on the planet, as there were no strong rains here for more than 4 centuries.
Over the past decade, climate change has led to the flowering of more than 200 plant species in the desert.
The hand of the desert is a famous sculpture near the highway that passes through the territory of Atacama. 12. 13.
In 2017, tourists could be greatly surprised to see 2 abandoned passenger aircraft among palm trees in the city of Loma-Grande in central Paraguay.
Today it is part of the entertainment infrastructure of the local hotel: visitors can rent a room with a view of the plane, swim in the pool in front of it and even have lunch inside.
Iguasu Falls is a complex of 275 waterfalls on the river of the same name.
This amazing natural object is located on the border between Brazil and Argentina.
Iguasu waterfalls are divided into several islands, which can be reached by small bridges (their total length is more than 2 km).
The highest waterfall reaches 82 m, and the height of the local rocks is 260 m, so you can enjoy the view from above. 15. 16.
There are a lot of things worth your attention in Rio de Janeiro, but we recommend you start getting acquainted with the city from the cable car to Mount Sugar Head.
It is hard to believe that this funicular was opened more than 100 years ago and became the third engineering structure of this kind in the world.
From the wagon you can enjoy breathtaking views of the city, the islands and the ocean. 17. 18.
La Boca is a famous residential area of Buenos Aires with an amazing history.
It was in this place that Pedro de Mendoza founded the capital of Argentina.
The first port of Buenos Aires was built here (although it was later moved).
The last stage of La Boca's history is linked to the Italian immigrants who have settled here.
They have a tradition of painting houses in different colors.
Most of the locals were poor, so they lacked paint of the same color and they used different colors. 19 20.
Laguna Colorada is a mineral lake in Bolivia, which has a delicate pink color due to minerals.
This lake is located on the territory of the national park and attracts tourists not only with an unusual shade, but also its inhabitants.
Here live the famous pink flamingos of James, as well as the Andean and Chilean flamingos.
If you're lucky, you'll be able to see their marriage games. 21. 22.
Machu Picchu is one of the most mysterious ancient cities in Latin America.
Scientists believe that the Incas built this settlement in the early 15th century, but soon it was abandoned.
The city remained forgotten for four centuries until Professor Hiram Bingham discovered it at the beginning of the 20th century.
In addition to the city itself, the ancient Inca road has been preserved to this day.
The Terracotta House (Casa Terracota) is a modern building built in the village of Villa de Leyva in Colombia by a local architect named Octavio Mendoza.
He used a unique technology: the house, like all the elements of furniture, is made of clay.
The architect calls his creation "the largest pottery work in the world." 24.
While in Sucre, it's hard to believe you're in the Bolivian capital.
It is a cozy colonial city that looks peaceful at any time of the year.
Sucre is located in a green valley and surrounded by picturesque mountains.
The climate here is mild, and the streets are narrow and European quiet.
At sunset, the orange roof tiles look even more spectacular than during the day.
Guajira can be called the district of Colombia.
It is one of the main places in the world to learn kitesurfing.
There is also the northernmost point of the South American continent (Cape Gallinas).
Tourists come here to relax in the lagoons surrounded by palm trees. 26. 27.
Travelling in South America will especially please those who are fond of photography of wildlife.
There are many unique animals, including birds.
The Big Tukan, the Royal Penguin, the Hyacinth Ara and the Galapagos Albatross are the most popular birds on the continent.
Ecuador is one of the best places for ornithological tours in South America, as there are 27 endemic bird species. 28.
The Perito-Moreno Glacier is located in the Argentine part of Patagonia.
This is the third largest freshwater supply in the world.
If you are very lucky, you will be able to see an interesting phenomenon here.
Approximately every five years, the glacier goes down to Lake Lago-Argentino and creates a dam.
The water in the lake can rise by 30 m, and then break through the ice dam.

In England, a meteorite was found that may contain information about the origin of life 3 21.07.2021, 12: 47 2,328 Fragment of the ancient meteorite Photo: David Clarke / EAARO He is the age of the solar system.
A small piece of rock found on a field in Gloucestershire in the UK can contain vital information about the formation of the Solar System and the origin of life itself, writes Novoe Vremya.
This is because it was not formed here, on Earth, but came to us because of the orbit of Mars, noted astronomers.
Abandoned by gravitational interaction or collision of asteroids, the fragment flew through the vast space of space, penetrated our atmosphere and landed on Earth in the form of a meteorite.
Scientists are now conducting an analysis to determine its composition, hoping to learn more about where it came from and how it was formed.
"The internal structure is fragile and loosely bonded, porous and with cracks.
It seems that it has not been subjected to thermal metamorphism, which means that it has been there, behind Mars, untouched since any of the planets was created, which means that we have a rare opportunity to explore a piece of our original past, ” said microscopist Sean Fowler of the University of Lafborough in the UK.
The age of the meteorite is about 4.6 billion years, which is about the same as the Solar System.
This means that it was formed from the same cloud of dust and gas that generated the Sun and the planets.
While the planets of the Solar System have since undergone significant events and transformations, the meteorite in Gloucestershire has remained intact in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter.
Its design means that it did not test seals due to repeated collisions.
It was until he landed in England.
His arrival was a furore, not only the first meteorite discovered on the continent in 30 years, but also a rare species known as carbon chondritis.
This means that it is a stone meteorite, not iron, consisting mainly of carbon and silicon.
These materials are less likely to survive severe atmospheric effects than iron rocks; that is why carbon chondrites are few and rare.
A blackened piece of space stone will be subjected to a number of analyses, including electron microscopy, oscillatory spectroscopy and X-ray diffraction.
These methods will help to reveal the physical structure of the stone, as well as what it consists of.
"The major part of the meteorite consists of minerals such as olivine and phylosilicates, with other mineral inclusions called chondrams.
But the composition is different from anything you can find here on Earth, and potentially unlike any other meteorites we found, "Fowler said.
Less than five percent of all meteorites found on Earth are carbon chondrites, but they are very popular: they are rich in organic materials, and scientists believe that they can contain the keys to figuring out the origin of organic matter here on Earth.
“Carbon chondrites contain organic compounds, including amino acids, that are contained in all living beings, ” said astrochemist Derek Robson of the Eastern England Astrophysical Research Organization (EAARO), who discovered a meteorite.

The famous Peruvian tourist destination, Machu Picchu, was opened to visitors after months of holiday due to the coronavirus outbreak.
According to ISNA and Frances24, Machu Picchu, the ancient citadel of Incas, which had been closed to enthusiasts for eight months due to restrictions due to the spread of the coronavirus, was opened with a ceremony.
With the reopening of this popular tourist destination, only 675 people are allowed to enter the site due to the necessity of observing daily health protocols, which is 30% of the number of daily visitors in the pre-coronavirus era.
In normal conditions, 5,000 people visited this historic site, which plays an important role in the tourism of Peru.
Corona virus quarantine in Peru has caused many losses to tourism related businesses.
In the 100 days of quarantine in Peru, many hotels, restaurants and tourism related businesses in the Kosko region went bankrupt.
Machu Picchu, built in the 15th century, was introduced to the Western world on July 24, 1911 by American historian Hiram Bingham in a speech at Yale University.
He was redirected to the site from time to time by local guidance.
Machu Picchu was registered as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983 and is known as a pure masterpiece of architecture and a unique symbol of the Inca civilization.

With the advent of new technologies, the age of 5G has come. Many people are happy with the convenience and speed of the network, but others worry about it every day.
A 70-year-old British mother-in-law claimed that she was allergic to Wi-Fi and would feel weak because of the electromagnetic waves generated by Wi-Fi, so she spent money buying equipment to prevent electromagnetic waves and even wrapping herself with a special sleeping bag.
According to the British Sun, the mother-in-law, named Rosi, from Totnes, England, currently lives in a Spanish mountain house with no electromagnetic frequency. Rosie claims to be allergic to the electromagnetic radiation generated by Wi-Fi, which can make her feel weak, fast breathing, and even feel needles on her face. The British pound protects itself from Wi-Fi.
Rosie bought a sleeping bag made of silver and copper for £400 (about HK $3900) to prevent electromagnetic waves. She used the sleeping bag during her Spanish ferry trip with her husband, and Rosie would carry a hand-held radiation detector with her, saying, "If I go to the coffee room, I will try to sit outside, I will take my detector and decide which seat is the smallest." "I am very happy
Although Rosie did a great deal of protection against electromagnetic waves, she was worried that the arrival of 5G networks would kill her. She said, “Because the signal is getting stronger, I can ’ t get into big cities anymore ”,“ If they introduce 5G, then I don ’ t know what ’ s going to happen, it ’ s a very scary problem. ”
In fact, this "EMPA" may be a psychological effect, says James Rubin, a psychiatrist at King's College, University of London, that there is strong scientific evidence that EMPA is not caused by electromagnetic signals, and that the symptoms of the disorder vary from person to person and are not considered a formal medical condition, so he speculates that the cause of the symptoms may be psychological.

Tokyo, 11: 13 – REGNUM The meteorite, which fell in early June in Japan, broke away from the asteroid, is an ordinary chondrite, the most common type of stone meteorites.
This was told by an employee of the Tokyo State Museum of Science Shigekazu Yoneda, RIA Novosti reports on July 20.
It was possible to find two parts of the meteorite, its size was assumed to be 5 cm in diameter.
Its composition includes piroxene, olivine and feldspar, these minerals are also on Earth.
Metal inclusions were also detected.
The scientist admits that only a piece of larger meteorite has been found.
The fragments have not yet been studied in detail, presumably, by 4.6 billion years, as well as by other meteorites that were formed together with the Solar System.
As reported by IA REGNUM, on July 2, residents of Japan noticed a bright meteor, and then heard the explosion.
The fall of the celestial body, called the Narasino meteorite, did not cause any damage.

Machu Picchu is a mysterious Inca town built in the mid-15th century.
This is the main attraction for which travelers go to Peru.
It is located about a hundred kilometers from the capital of the Inca Empire, the city of Cusco, in the mountains at an altitude of 2,450 meters above sea level in such a secluded place in the Andes that even Spanish colonizers could not reach it.
This city became known in 1911 thanks to an American scholar from Yale, Heirem Bingham.
Although, for the sake of justice, it should be noted that the locals always knew about Machu Picchu, but did not rush to share information with strangers.
In 2007, more than 100 million people voted for Machu Picchu to be included in the list of seven wonders of the world.
Machu Picchu is included in the list of seven wonders of the world — Until now, scientists are arguing when and why this city was created. — And most importantly — where did people (all to one!) go when Spanish colonizers began to conquer the Inca empire in 1532?
They captured the capital of Cusco quickly, because the Incas, a fairly religious people, thought that the gods they worshipped had descended to them.
But when the aliens started robbing them and killing them, the locals realized they were barbarians.
According to one version, the colonizers only after 70 years got to Machu Picchu, but there was no one there.
Earlier in this city lived the highest know and craftsmen of very high qualification - up to one and a half thousand people.
When they felt the danger, they went in an unknown direction.
They just disappeared.
So I was wondering, maybe they were people with some kind of superpowers?
After all, what I saw in the Inca Empire shocked me.
Ordinary people who lived in the XV century and did not possess civilizational construction technologies, equipment and materials, simply could not build all this!
For example, the temple of the Sun on the outskirts of Cusco (it was never completed) was constructed from stones weighing from 30 to 100 tons.
And the quarry was 16 kilometers from the construction site of this temple.
The Temple of the Sun in Cusco (Peru) — How were such stones delivered to the construction site? — That ’ s the point.
It's not just plain terrain, it's mountains.
It is impossible to roll or drag stones on such a surface.
And at the same time, these people didn't have cranes or trucks!
“It remains a mystery why the priests did not pass on knowledge to the descendants ” – There is a version that the priests of the Incas possessed superpowers and could turn solid textures into softer, lighter, and then back, – continues Boris Pavlovich. – Since then, there have been many earthquakes in these places.
Three of them are incredibly destructive.
Everything around it was destroyed!
And the monuments of Inca architecture were both standing and standing.
And that's what's interesting.
Skilled Inca builders built their structures... without mortar!
The slabs are so tightly attached to each other that even the blade of the knife does not enter.
When I read about such riddles before the trip, it seemed unbelievable to me.
But when I saw him, I couldn't believe my eyes.
Blocks weighing from 30 to 100 tons are adjusted to each other like clay!
According to researchers, the stones were cut out of huge slabs and gathered like puzzles.
Another interesting detail.
The stones in the ancient buildings were laid at such an angle that during the earthquake they were displaced, and then... they were replaced again. "As in the fairy tale." By the way, I was worried about another question: why priests, if they had a connection with extraterrestrial civilizations, from which they received superpowers to create all this, did not pass their knowledge to the descendants?
Why don't Peruvians create anything like this today?
Why don't they have the powers that their ancestors had?
I tried to answer it for myself.
And I think that's what this is about.
The priests feared to transmit superpowers.
Get these secrets into the hands of the colonizers who know how they would have used them.
They could have destroyed the world in an indestructible pursuit of enrichment.
On the cable car in Bolivia – Chitala, Peru ranks second in the world in the number of practicing shamans.
Did you talk to them?
For the knowledge of the unknown, and I'm going to such places.
I'm going up to the mountains where others don't dare climb.
I'm staying overnight in villages where, to put it mildly, it's creepy.
These are places untouched, far from civilization and even the idea of it.
As for the shamans, they behave completely.
They're very hard to understand.
But what they have other than us is obvious.
In fact, there are places of special spiritual strength.
For example, you stand at the top of Machu Picchu and feel like flying.
You seem to have dropped 50 kilograms of weight, rising to a height of 2,450 meters.
By the way, the city of Cusco is even higher - at an altitude of 3,600 meters.
I went up to a height of 4,500 meters.
I wanted to take off the seven-colored mountains.
True, I had to get there with adventures.
In the middle of the road, a wheel in the car was punctured on sharp stones, and it became dangerous to drive overnight without a spare.
But I really wanted to see these mountains.
I had to leave the car with the guide in the same place, and then get hitchhiking.
It's getting dark fast.
In the evening light, just the cosmic landscapes opened, but I was waiting for the real light.
And the miracle happened.
Just for a few seconds, the lead clouds ripped a ray of light and fell on the mountains.
It's real luck!
It was necessary to act very quickly, as it lasted literally 30 – 40 seconds.
But that's why I went up there.
It was a photo artist's luck - I believe in it!
After the shooting we went down and spent the night in a clay house in the village with one single lantern.
“The President of the island really wanted me to become the godfather of her three-year-old daughter ” – Salar de Uyuni, the world ’ s largest saltwater located in the Andes in southwestern Bolivia, made a great impression on me.
It is a dry prehistoric lake with an area of about 11,000 square kilometers and is famous for the desert-like plains of bright white salt, rock formations and islands on which many cacti grow.
It is located at an altitude of 3,663 meters.
This place is amazing!
You see, and on the ground, salt honeycombs are hexahedrons.
All the salt in them. — Have you visited the largest high mountain navigable lake Titicaca? — Yes, it is located on the border of Bolivia and Peru.
There are about 40 islands on the lake.
I visited the one where the Uros live, visited their homes.
They put me and my wife in their clothes.
The president of the island, whose name is Santa Maria Eva, really wanted me to become the godfather of her three-year-old daughter.
I didn't have a way out.
I was not sure that if I refused, I would be able to leave this island calmly (smiling).
I had to do as I was asked.
And the girl is very good, she helped mom with everything.
He and my wife started to treat me.
Truth is, I'm not risking it.
Served black chicken, eggs of unknown birds...
But the local reed ate.
And that's what's interesting.
He noted that the teeth of all residents are smooth, white, strong.
Turns out the secret is they eat the core of the cane.
“In the Uros tribe, which lives on one of the islands of Lake Titicaca, my wife and I dressed in national clothes, ” Boris Andresiuk — Chitala, that Peru is considered one of the gastronomic capitals of the world.
What kind of food did you taste the most?
It's a fish marinated in lime juice.
Oh, God's dish!
If you try it once, you'll order it later every day while you're there.
This dish is also available in Kiev restaurants.
I tried it here.
But to taste and even look, these are very different dishes.
I remember pisco from the drinks.
It's a vodka made of grapes, like Italian grappa.
I even brought a bottle and treated my friends in Kiev.
By the way, both in Peru and Argentina prices are pleasantly surprising.
Compared to Kiev, they are at least twice lower.
"I am not interested in the rest of the sea.
Lying all day on the beach is a real punishment "- What else did you bring from the trip? - A kilogram pack of maté tea.
I never drink coffee.
And wherever I go, I bring good tea everywhere.
I have the best varieties in my office.
And when my colleagues and friends come to me, they always know that I will give them special tea.
He also brought a pottery cup from Peru.
It is unusual - with a tube in the form of a spoon.
It is customary for the Incas to brew tea directly in such a cup in the form of a jug.
And, of course, we bought local crafts - knitted and embroidered things.
For example, a rug created by Uros.
Now all these things remind us of our journey.
Of course, the most important thing is photos.
One and a half thousand of them!
They allow me to remember the sensations that I have experienced while in these countries.
The route was heavy, but very interesting. – Your photos from the trip are impressive.
There were no restrictions on shooting in Machu Picchu? - You know, in many places now they are forbidden to shoot drones.
And as for filming in Machu Picchu...
I'm starting to unfold the tripod.
There are two policemen coming to me and warning me that it is forbidden to shoot with a tripod.
But still, I managed to take professional pictures.
That's why I was going this far!
Since 2007, when Machu Picchu entered the seven new wonders of the world, wishing to visit it becomes more and more, so tickets for a certain date need to be bought in advance.
The number of visits per day is limited. — What struck you Argentina? — Flight to Buenos Aires from Istanbul took eighteen hours.
I really liked Buenos Aires.
Beautiful city, with wide avenues, green.
He met us, really, in the rain.
There are very friendly people there.
Lots of beautiful monuments.
Do not believe it, but in the square in the center of the city there is a monument to Taras Shevchenko.
It was given to the Argentines by the Ukrainian diaspora.
Stunning cemetery with mausoleums...
And I really wanted to see Iguasu Falls.
It's one of the most powerful waterfalls in the world.
And the most beautiful!
It consists of 280 large and small waterfalls.
Iguaçu is one of the most powerful and beautiful waterfalls in the world — according to your photo, it ’ s just an amazing spectacle. — It ’ s true.
One of the largest waterfalls is called the Swallow of the Devil.
When you stand and see six and a half tons of water fall from a height of more than 80 meters per second, the spirit captures.
Above the waterfall you can see a rainbow.
And it is also important how everything is thought out and organized for tourists.
Very convenient bridges, from which you can watch all this.
The waterfall is located on the border of Argentina and Brazil, so it is interesting to look at it both in one country and in another.
I managed to capture it from different angles.
Of course, I liked Argentinean cuisine.
The meat and wine in Argentina are just great. - Where are you going to go in 2020? - I will answer this: "Kudi Makar calves are not harmed," continues Boris Andresiuk. - That is, where rarely steps the foot of a person.
I'm not interested in the rest of the sea at all.
Lying on the beach all day is a real punishment for me.
Turned tourist routes are not interesting to me either.
I want to see and shoot something unusual, interesting, and then, of course, tell about it. - Boris Pavlovich, how long will the photo exhibition of your works take? - Until the end of January.
On it you can see new works of large size - meter and even more.
By the way, we spent at our academy the plenum of the Union of Photographers of Ukraine.
Step by step our academy becomes the center of photo art.
I feel good about it.
So I invite the connoisseurs of beauty to the exhibition.
You can visit it for free.
My works are already being bought, and I'm happy.
I teach my students how to shoot professionally, print and design pictures.
Only in this case, the work of the photographer can bring income.
There are millionaire photographers in the world, by the way.
In particular, my idol is an Australian photographer, Peter Lick.
Every year, he sells a photo worth several hundred million dollars.
His Las Vegas workshop covers an area of about 30,000 square meters.
Peter Leek's masterpieces are printed there.
They've already been sent out all over the world.
In short, it's a whole production.
So there's something to aspire to.
I, for example, keep an eye on all the novelties in the world.
I buy an exclusive photo paper that guarantees 70 years of quality.
I'm picking up the appropriate frames.
Friends and colleagues say that my works sometimes compete with painting.
For me, this is a high appreciation, and I am grateful for it. — Creative inspiration to you, Boris Pavlovich, in the New Year! — Thank you.
And I wish the readers of FACTS that their houses be decorated with beautiful landscapes of those places where they were or would like to visit.

According to the Young Journalist Club ’ s Web Group, the meteorite is said to be a remnant of comets and asteroids that do not melt when hit by the Earth ’ s atmosphere and fall to the surface of our planet.
Appraisals show that only about 500 tons of meteorites reach Earth ’ s soil annually; some of these rocks are never found due to small dimensions.
Some are very giants, others are very expensive.
Most of the meteorites that are valued at high prices are usually of the type of palazite meteorites.
Palazites are a type of most beautiful space stones made of nickel-iron alloy with olivine crystals (semi-precious stones).
Scientists estimate that only one percent of all the meteorites that have fallen to the ground are palazite.
Of the 65,000 officially approved meteorites by 2021, only 130 of them were palazites.
But there is a much rarer form of palazite meteorites, which instead contains diamond crystals.
According to meteorologists, this diamond is made of carbon rich cosmic dust near carbon stars and supernovae.
The diamonds in the meteorites are probably older than the solar system itself.
The Palazite meteorites are named in commemoration of Peter Simon Palaz, a naturalist, traveler and professor of German natural history in the 18th century.
Palaz, a member of the St. Petersburg Academy of Sciences, the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences, and the Royal Society of London, discovered a 687 kg iron meteorite during an expedition to Siberia in 1777, and named it "Iron Palaz".
Fukang meteorite: worth $2 million of this meteorite is a kind of palazite meteorite.
Evaluation shows that the famous Fukang meteorite fell on Earth about 4.5 billion years ago, which means that its fall age is almost the same as the age of the Earth.
An American tourist in 2000, having lunch on a cliff near Fukang County, China, discovered a large piece weighing 1,3 kilograms of the iron-nickel matrix showing amber crystals in the olivine.
A stone that was later recognized as one of the most beautiful and, of course, the most expensive meteorites ever discovered in the world.
In 2005, a Chinese businessman sent the stone to Tucson, Arizona, to exhibit rocks and minerals, while 20 kilograms were separated from it, and since then, the remaining 983 kilograms have been divided into about 1,000 pieces and sold worldwide.
The largest separate piece of meteorite weighs 450 kilograms, which was priced at the Bonhams auction in New York in 2008 for about $2 million.
A piece weighing 31 kilograms of this stone is currently held at the University of Arizona.
According to some studies, the Fukang Meteorite contains 50% of olivine and 50% of nickel-iron alloy.
A piece of Vilamette Meteorite: worth more than $1.3 million of the Vilamette Meteorite, was discovered in 1902 by American miner Ellis Hughes in Oregon, while falling for about a million years.
It seems that the shale of the nickel – iron alloy is a piece of the iron core of the planet or the lunar, which is separated by another celestial mass and reached the Earth.
The people of the Indian tribe Kalkamas Chinook, who lived in the Valley of Vilamet before European colonialism, highly respect this celestial stone which is now held at the Museum of Natural History in New York.
In 1998, observers of the New York Museum of Natural History took a 28-pound piece of this massive celestial rock and gave it to Daryl Pitt, owner of the Maković meteorite complex, the largest private collection of iron meteorites.
In 2007, the value of the piece was valued by the Bunhams auction house over $1.3 million.
The main mass of Bornham meteorite: $750,000 to $1.2 million of Bornham meteorite is another very famous palazite iron meteorite that has been discovered so far.
= = History = = Bernham was discovered in Queuua County, Kansas, United States, and was first admitted to the revealed meteorite list in 1882.
Although this massive iron rock mass was recorded to weigh 2.4 tons, in 2005 a piece of it was recovered by 635 kg, called Oriented Individual, now known as the Main Mass.
Many of the burnham meteorites have been found in relatively wet soil, which has greatly oxidized them.
Dr. Harvey Nieninger spent a long time searching for pieces of Bernham, and in 1929 he dug a large hole and acquired many parts of it.
In 2016, The Main Mass of the Brenham Meteorite set the price of $750,000 to $1.2 million.
The concept junction of the asteroid, which is probably part of the asteroid belt, is another rare and very beautiful example of the palazite meteorites.
In January 2011, when two meteor hunters met Randy Korotov at the University of Washington in St. Louis to show him their last purchase, they had no idea the material value of this celestial crime.
Until a few months later, in November of that year, scientists at the University of Washington announced that the price of this space stone could be more than $85,000.
The rock, which is of the Palazite meteorite family and weighs 17 kilograms, was discovered in 2006 near the 202 people 's village of Concept Junction in northwest Missouri, and was named the 20th Palazite meteorite discovered in the United States in 2009.
According to Korotov, the price per gram of this stone is in a fragmented state and paid about $200.
Springwater Meteorite is a 1931 meteorite found near Springwater County, Ontario, Canada.
At the time of its discovery, the 4.5 billion year meteorite was composed of three large masses, weighing 38.6 kilograms, 18.6 kilograms and 10.6 kilograms, respectively.
Other pieces were discovered in the following years, including a single piece found 53 kg of weight in 2009.
The Royal Museum of Ontario purchased this large piece for about 621,000, and this large stone of the sky is now exposed to enthusiasts in this museum.
30% of spring water meteorite is made up of an iron-nickel alloy rich in kamakite and taenite, and the rest is mostly made up of olivine.
There are also some trace amounts of other minerals, including compounds from the phosphates family including faringtonite, stanfieldite, and merilite.

“Multilateral agreements and international institutions should not be seen as something valuable in themselves, ” says Rice.“ Of course, there is nothing wrong with doing something for the benefit of all humanity, but it should be in a sense a side effect. ”
At the same time, it remains childishly simple: "After all, American values are universal...

Condoleezza Rice.
Promoting the National Interest Foreign Affairs, Vol.

Well, thank you for explaining.

Rice teaches the lesson of “humanitarian flexibility ”:“ Some argue that, in the interest of human rights progress, trade with China should be abandoned.

But, of course, the greatest attention is paid to Russia.

Pretty transparent.

Democratic Party presidential candidate Hillary Clinton confessed to not remembering when she was trained to work with classified information.
This is stated in the materials published on Friday by the Federal Bureau of Investigation on the interrogation of the former Secretary of State in connection with the violation of the rules of official correspondence.
For health reasons, they state that the former First Lady confessed to the investigators that she cannot remember when she was "told or taught" how to handle classified information.
At the same time, the officers of the bureau associate this with the head injury that the candidate received before leaving the position of head of the foreign policy department.
"In December 2012, Clinton received a concussion, and she was diagnosed with a clot under New Year's Eve.
On the advice of her doctor, she could work at the State Department for only a few hours a day and could not remember the briefings that were held for her (employees of the department), "the documents say.
"She also failed to recall that anyone had expressed concern about the confidential nature of the information she had received by mail," notes the distributed material.
During the interrogation, the politician stressed that her interlocutors "were experienced saboteurs, and she had no reason to doubt their sanity and ability to work with classified information".
The FBI's disclosure published on Friday an 11-page interrogation of a Democrat and a 47-page investigation into her use of a personal e-mail server for official correspondence during her tenure as Head of State.
The materials were disclosed under the Freedom of Information Act at the request of a number of American media outlets.
Law enforcement officials retouched part of the text.
The same documents were originally submitted to the United States Congress a few weeks ago.
American media speculated that the material was deliberately distributed on Friday before the start of the three-day weekend.
On Monday, the Americans celebrate the national holiday Labor Day.
Eight phones and five tablets, Hillary Clinton, could have lost several smartphones that contained classified information.
"(Assistant Clinton) Huma Abedin and Monica Hanley said that the location of Clinton's mobile devices was often unknown after she switched to a new smartphone," said the documents.
In other cases, her assistants destroyed the politicians' phones by "splitting them into two parts or hammering them".
According to the rules, they had to hand them over to a special department responsible for the destruction of sensitive devices.
Law enforcement officials also emphasize that the former First Lady as Secretary of State "used at least eight smartphones" from which she entered her e-mail box, sent and received messages.
At the same time, according to their data, she "probably" could check the mail and using five tablets at different times.
Clinton's lawyers couldn't provide any of the smartphones, but the FBI got access to three tablets.
On one of these devices, "unclassified" e-mails from the former First Lady's mailbox were found.
Clinton ’ s response to the publication of documents The electoral headquarters of the Democratic candidate welcomed the decision of the law enforcement structure to disclose the interrogation data of the former Secretary of State.
"We are pleased that the FBI has published Hillary Clinton's interrogation materials, as we requested," said Bryan Fallon, the press secretary of staff.
He stressed that the use of the former Secretary of State's personal mailbox for official purposes "was an obvious mistake, and she (Clinton) assumes responsibility for it".
The documents "make it clear why the U.S. Department of Justice has decided that there is no reason to proceed with this case," he added.
Correspondence Case In July, the U.S. Department of Justice, under the authority of the FBI, stated that Clinton would not be charged with using her personal computer server to conduct e-mails when she was Secretary of State.
The Minister of Justice and Attorney General of the United States, Loretta Lynch, then explained that she had made such a decision after consulting with the FBI Director James Komi and the justice officers involved in the investigation.
Clinton entered the U.S. election race in April 2015.
She was nominated as an official Democratic presidential candidate at the National Congress of the Party, which was held in late July in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
If the authorities had found grounds for criminal charges against Clinton, this, according to local observers, could have disrupted her election campaign.
Therefore, the Republican opposition in the United States received the conclusions of the Ministry of Justice and the FBI with outrage.
Democrats and their allies consider the scandal surrounding Clinton's e-mails to be politicized.
It is about the fact that during her work as Secretary of State of the United States, she used unprotected private servers for official correspondence, including secret ones.
This is prohibited by the rules in force in the American administration.
