She once said, “The tree is dead because of us, what kind of text do we carry on these papers? Knowledge or garbage? Our birth is rooted in a forest of death, and if we can ’ t let such death happen, it ’ s a sin. ” Such thinking, coupled with a hard-working attitude, makes her the most revered person in the business week.
When I left Shang Zhou, I was curious about her life. Wang said with a smile: "The rise of AI, the rise of digital, people really don ’ t have to do so much anymore. So I adjusted myself to four days of work, three days of life. There are projects, writing, writing articles, participating in public welfare, and three days of doing what I like.
You know how to enjoy it. Why do you feel so much about life now?
Wang Wen-ching shared the three insights he had gained this year.
She was drinking Johnnie Walker Blue Label for a whisky tasting. "I was drinking the first one with peat, the second with fruit flavor, the third with sea salt flavor, and then the fourth, I suddenly realized how it used to be wine and meat. I could never drink it. It was a good drink for me!" It's all the same wine! It makes her scream, it's different.
"I've been drinking this wine many times, but I've never seen any of these faces before, and at this moment, I realized that the footsteps of my past life were too hurried!"
On the other hand, she loved the tourist spot of Peru — Machu Picchu. She once chatted with her friends and said, "Oh, Machu Picchu, no stone, no stone, no stone!"
“I now realize that if life is busy, the seven wonders of the world are only rotten stones; but if life is easy, even the flowers on the side of the road can smell. ” Wang Wenjing (left) looks at the sunrise in Machu Picchu, a historical monument in Peru.
(Photo: Wang Wenjing) Once again, she walked into the Da-an Forest Park, where she traveled every day, and there was a work by Yang Yingfeng, the pure white Guanyin Bodhisattva. It was a smile and sound! "Then she went on a journey with her friend and asked what her friend saw.
"Very solemn Bodhisattva!" Did you see him smiling?
"No!" said Wang Wen-ching, a friend who worked very busy. "I saw me from him."
Three times in a row, Wang Wenjing understood that in the past she was too passionate about work, totally devoted to it, but neglected that life should not be just work.
Now, life has pushed her here, "and I've learned to put it down and enjoy my life."
Reading travel is the favorite wine tasting, rotten stone, Guanyin, changed Wang Wenjing's thinking.
Today, she loves her own history, culture, and nature, so she lives her life, and has a lot of interest in reading Taiwan ’ s history and traveling with her. Sometimes she actually walks after reading it, and sometimes she comes back to study history. “The process is very enjoyable, ” she says.
For the future, she is not thinking about retirement, but about life, "I like the present state, free, not kidnapped by money, but it is not arbitrary, is a choice of opinion, if a friend after 50 years of age has such conditions but does not do so, I would feel sorry for him."
When people get to the point where they can be prosperous and simple, life seems to be open and cheerful.
With regard to her second life, Wang Wen-ching affirmatively says, "You can't just work, you must be interested, and you will be able to forget everything!" In the past, Wang Wen-ching's "I" was bigger, and now she realizes that she is "obedient" and works hard to do what she likes, and believes that God has his own plans.
As Master Nebula said to her, "In the future, life will be free!"
With regard to financial management, Wang Wen-ching frankly said that no financial management, or even a fixed deposit, is the basic saving.
In the past, I have never bought diamond rings, almost all spent on travel, about two or three weeks each trip, about the value of using 1 carat diamond, "This is my life choice, because I like to explore, so I have rich memories, this is the choice, it is also the exchange."
As for Wang Wen-ching's exercise habits, it is surprising to all of us that discipline shows great perseverance.
In the past, she never incorporated sports into the main melody of life, until five or six years ago, when she woke up and asked herself: When the age was older, when the beauty of youth was no longer beautiful, was it just that people were old and yellow?
Since then, she has taken exercise as a discipline. She has to do it twice a week: walk 40 minutes to the gym at 6: 00 a.m., finish one hour of retraining, do one hour of cardio, and get into the office at 9: 00 p.m.
“I remember going to the gym for the first time, I only had to sit up for a while. After a few years of accumulation, now I can hang my feet vertically and sit up and down in the air. ” Wang Wenjing, 54, showed great physical strength and perseverance.

The photo copyright Getty Images Image capture Mrs. Thatcher said the pandas were not "ominous" to politicians, recently published documents showing that British Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher had refused to travel to Washington with the London Zoo Panda on a plane.
It is mentioned in the documents of the newly published British National Archives.
According to these documents, Lord Zuckerman, President of the Zoological Society of London, proposed that Chia Chia Chia, Panda of the London Zoo, be taken to Washington by the First Minister Concorde in 1981.
The Smithsonian Institution, the largest scientific, cultural research museum in the world, at the time requested that Chiachia mate with a panda called Ying Ching.
But Mrs. Thatcher said that pandas are not "omens" for politicians.
Sir Robert Armstrong of the Cabinet Ministers wrote at the time, "In Lord Zuckerman's opinion, this represents certain relationships and will be very happy to announce the time to lend or deliver this panda in any way that the Prime Minister probably thinks it will be more in favor of British and American relations."
"He (Lord Zuckerman) even suggested that the Prime Minister might like to take the panda on his own plane when he leaves for Washington next month."
But Clive Whitmore, Miss Thatcher's special secretary, replied, "She said she wouldn't take a panda with her.
Pandas and politicians are not omens! "
In these documents, the initial answer of Mrs. Thatcher is also seen.
In a brief note about this idea, Mrs. Thatcher wrote with a blue pen: "I'm not taking a panda with me.
To emphasize, he underlined the words "I won't win" and "I" twice.
Mrs. Thatcher then added, "Lord Zee knows more about pandas than I do.
I'm sure he can plan for these things. "
In the National Archives, more than 11 million documents are kept, many of which are transferred from government departments to this archive, and are often exposed to the public after 30 years.

The group of asteroids sharing its orbit with Mars is actually pieces of the same fourth planet once torn out of its surface by powerful collisions.
Astronomers from Germany, Israel and France studied the surface spectra of the two Trojan asteroids of Mars and concluded that they were not similar to those of the solar system as a whole.
Judging by their composition, they are identical to the rocks on the Martian surface.
The authors believe that these bodies were knocked out during the impact collisions, which formed numerous traces on the fourth planet.
The text of the relevant work is available on the Cornell University preprint server.
The authors presented new observations of the surface of the Trojan asteroids of Mars 311999 2007 NS2 and 385250 2001 DH47 in the near infrared range.
As the new data show, their surface is composed of olive - rich rocks, as is the case with the previously discovered Trojan asteroid Eureka.
According to researchers, olivine is uncharacteristic of the surface of asteroids.
But this material is often found in the robe of the planets.
Similar to the Martian Trojans, the quantity of olivine is found in Martian surface rocks and meteorites that fell on Earth and on the isotope analysis of those that showed Martian origin.
Astronomers suggest that these asteroids originated at least a billion years ago.
That's what Eureka's estimated age is.
In order to knock out such bodies — their typical diameter exceeds a kilometer — a body of large dimensions, at least hundreds of kilometers in diameter, should have fallen on Mars.
Such an event would melt a significant part of its surface for a long time.
Mars, like Earth, is in the interior of the solar system.
If something like this could fall on it, there is no exception to the version of a similar event for the Earth.

Why do I take these lines?
After all, I didn't know Larisa Schenker, I didn't work with her in the editorial office, I didn't sit in the same room table to the table, face to face, in routine discussions and disputes, crossing views and temperaments, as the staff of the magazine had to do.
It's just that for the years of the fourteen, we've had quite a lot of personal meetings and intersections at literary evenings, parties, art exhibitions.
General programs and speeches in Brooklyn libraries and in the editorial office of Word / Word magazine in Manhattan.
Frequent telephone conversations about the presentations of the magazine in various branches of the Brooklyn Public Library and my publications in Word Magazine.
Why do I write?
I wanted to write about this bright, unique person with a very complex, explosive character, a woman who has done a lot for our literature of Russian abroad and for twenty years with a firm hand conducted a Russian-immigrant literary magazine.
And she did it on her own, sacrificing vacation and rest in general, in her spare time - evenings and weekends, as she worked five days a week as an architect at the City of New York City.
I wanted to look at it, so to speak, from the height of my, if not very noticeable, bell tower.
Those who knew Larisa very closely might say that my fringe memories of this man are not enough to write a full-blown essay paying tribute to character and personality.
I'll agree with them in part.
But on the other hand, because I do not claim exhaustion and “fullness. ”
This is my view, my experience and my right.
About Larisa and her unpredictable character, I was already heard from my friends and pen mates when, on the advice of a local writer, I decided to attend a New York Writers' Club meeting in Manhattan in 1998.
Larisa brought our writing brother and allowed to hold meetings and presentations in her editorial office.
As usual, I was on the run when the bulk of writers and listeners had not yet arrived and the chairs were not arranged.
The small, tight room immediately struck me with discomfort and idleness, bordering on desolation.
Dusty, not removed, in the corner of the old chairs (some clearly pinched), boxes with magazines and books, just rubbish somehow mixed with several beautiful paintings by Ilya Shenker.
The only decoration of the room was a portrait of Sergei Dovlatov, hung on the wall by Ilya Shenker.
In short, full visibility of a house without a master.
And the owner was, or rather, the hostess.
Oh, that's what it is!
She probably didn't care about cleaning the room.
Or maybe the comfort of the environment didn't bother her.
Have time with the materials and authors to understand and in time to issue the next issue of the journal!
It became, and the regulars of the writer 's club had no business before bringing the room into an elementary working order, not to mention creating a pleasant for the eyes and literary communication entourage.
But we could arrange such a Saturday or Sunday, remove and comb a few rooms, in which they were going.
Well, come on...
Apparently, the members of the writer's club are already so used to such a poor, slammed room that nobody was particularly cared for.
Except to scare off new authors like me...
The people were gradually pulling up.
The men began to set up chairs.
The poet BW took his honorary presidential place at the table.
There was his deputy KD next to him.
Except for the poet X, who told me to come to this club, I knew no one, and I felt lonely, not the message from where the spruce came from, say, in a pine forest.
Finally, in the midst of poetic readings (for a long time I do not remember who read this evening their netlenki), Larisa and Ilya Shenkerov appeared.
Larisa, with all the power and authority of her small but voluminous body, glittering the intelligent brown eyes present (as if claiming that she sees all through), with the confident gait of the queen of the ball went ahead.
Small, thin, with a completely naked skull, Ilya walked inaudibly from behind.
Larisa sat down at the presidential table, Ilya modestly stood up somewhere behind and got a notebook and a pencil.
Larisina's role was to observe and embed free remark, thus providing informal leadership to the unfolding action, as well as to identify new talents at the same time.
When she said, the others kept quiet.
I said to myself that the people were obviously afraid of her.
Ilya silently made portrait sketches of some of the performers.
As I wrote, I don't remember who did the solo at the time.
It's been almost twenty years, though.
Among those present was a beautiful, young dark-haired woman with blue eyes, strictly dressed in a white blouse and a black skirt.
She was included in a circular reading with a poem about a subway trip.
I liked the poem and the appearance of the poetess.
During the break, I approached her, called myself, and thanked her for the poem.
We met L.A., later became friends and friends until now.
I also approached the chairman of the BV club, showed my small first poetic collection "Monologue of the last snow" (1992 edition) and asked to include me in the list of contenders for a solo performance.
BW was extraordinarily trusting, poetically omnipresent and kind, since without even opening my book, he suggested that I was not a graphomaner and included my future presentation in the club's calendar plan of meetings.
At the end of the evening, Larisa announced that anyone who wants to purchase the latest issue of Word magazine can come to her and buy a magazine on the spot... and other books issued by her publisher.
There are many wonderful authors and interesting publications.
Or are you just reading yourself?
Someone came to Larisa.
Some pretended that they had not heard her call and were in a hurry to leave.
I was curious here, newcomer to the club.
For a little while, I prepared the money and approached Larissa with the intention of buying a magazine.
My heart is hurting so fast.
I didn't know what I was afraid of.
Well, let's say I say something wrong and Larisa will react somehow wrong...
But it's not lethal!
Larisa Shanker was the source of some unexplainable energy of unpredictability: whether he ordered execution or mercy.
I was lucky.
My humble, non-conflicting personality has aroused in Larisa a kind of complacency and even some interest.
Larisa asked me who I was.
I briefly told about myself that a mole from Moscow, by education, a philologist, an interpreter, now a librarian.
Besides, I write poems. - And don 't write prose for an hour?
We need good prose stories.
There are too many poets today.
It's a hobby of rhymopplicity.
And they're writing shit more and more.
“Have you heard? ” She said frankly and quite loudly, not shy of the speakers present.
And immediately mercifully added: “However, send your poems to the magazine.
“Thank you! ” I muttered, moving inwardly from the word“ shitty ”.
And I thought, frightened that Larisa would take my poetry to this category: "No, I'd rather not send poetry yet.
Here I will make a new book, then... "
In general, summing up the results of the evening, the first meeting at the New York Poet Club was positive for me (except for the comfort of the room) and useful: listened to other poets, compared their creations with their own, met Larisa Shanker, BV, LA, some other authors.
Larisa didn't want me to be haunted, but let's just say she treated me mercifully.
And I went to Manhattan sometimes.
Word magazine I liked the variety of genres, themes and authors (from famous to few known, but talented).
Larisa was able to combine, without preferences and prejudices, in one issue, the works of writers representing all four emigrations: the first (descendants of nobles and White Guards), the second - consisting of the so-called "displaced persons" who ended up after World War II in Germany, the third - mostly Jewish, escaped from the anti-Semitic Union of the Brezhnev era and the fourth (also mostly Jewish), which came with Russian passports after the collapse of the USSR in the feeble and hungry nineties. * * * The period from 1998 to 2001 was the hardest in my life.
After a long illness, my father died first, then, with a break of one month - a husband.
Some grief proves, makes you go into deep depression, lie on the bottom and creatively silence.
In my case, everything happened exactly the opposite: sadness came first into a violent poetic splash of poems dedicated to the memory of my father and husband, and then I began to write prose.
The first prose thing about my husband, Dmitri Istratov, "The descendant of the Istratovs' boyars.
Confession of the Wife "was accepted by the editor of the newspaper" New Russian Word "Irina Leikina and published with the continuation in four issues of the newspaper in 2002.
The decision to publish this frank, bitter, “blood written ” confession was not easy for me.
Thank you to Irina Leukina, who supported me in moments of doubt - is it worth publishing such things at all?
The two of us decided it was worth it!
Since then, I have existed in two hypostases: I write poems and prose.
Somewhere by 2003, I was finally “ripe ” to send my stories to Word and other thick and thin as well as Internet periodicals.
My first big publication in the "Word..." was the story "Artist" - from the series "Notes of the librarian".
Larisa favourably treated this story (and then to poems) and willingly published every year my creations in the magazine, for which I am very grateful.
The publications were also accompanied by preliminary telephone calls.
I called Larisa (sometimes home, sometimes at work) and asked the traditional author's questions timidly: will the magazine publish my next thing, if so, when?
Every time I was nervous, I didn't know what kind of mood she was in.
And Larisa ’ s mood, which the writers of all ranks, ages and suits (as well as any editor and publisher) were mercilessly defeated, was different: from the kind to, mildly speaking, the nerve-stressed and even explosive.
(She did not want to hold her emotions or did not know how to.)
I remember one day I called and asked what I heard about my story “Haitian ”.“ Is this about the one who was your boss? ” she asked peacefully.
Nothing seems to have predicted the storm.
And.., - I did not have time to say something, when suddenly a simple, non-selective, loud Russian mat was heard in the phone tube.
I was frightened to put the tube away from my ear, not knowing how to react to such a language ’ s “freedom. ”
Interestingly, the English mat I can tolerate absolutely calmly.
He doesn't scratch my tender soul.
And when I'm driving and I can express my wish to fuck you to some irradiant driver who really "cut me off."
But my native Russian is absurd to me for some reason.
Although, as a philologist of Slavic education, I would have to respond equally calmly and impartially to all manifestations and layers of the great and mighty.
I should, but I can't!
And here I am standing, frozen with the phone in my hand - I do not know whether to stop the conversation by pressing the off button, or whether to say something in response - how Larisa without ceremonies clarifies the situation to me: - I am not you, Lena! - What? - I do not say to you, Lena! - OK!
"Well, thank God, not me!
It seems that Larisa was talking to me in parallel with someone from the editorial staff, "I thought eternally selfishly, took a deep breath and exhale, calmed down, and we continued the conversation, as nothing had happened.
I got great news: the story "Haitian" was included in the layout of the next issue of the magazine.
In 2004, I received a new position in the Brooklyn Public Library – an adult specialist – and began to organize literary and musical evenings in the branches with the Russian-speaking population (Sheepshead Bay, Kings Bay, Homecrest, Gravesend, Brighton Beach).
In parallel with the New York Writers Club, she founded the Brooklyn Club of Russian Poetry.
Why go to the editorial office every time we have our resources in Brooklyn and a public eager for cultural and literary communication!?
Several times I invited the editorial office of Word magazine to our club.
Larisa usually came from Manhattan after working with a large group consisting of members of the editorial board and some of her favorite authors, whom she was going to introduce to the public.
The program was scheduled to begin at six pm.
Literators were almost always late, and at eight o'clock the library, like a bayonet, should be closed.
And no exceptions!
Before the meeting began, Larisa looked around all those present with a strict look and urged those sitting in the rear rows to move to the empty front.
Most of the audience obeyed immediately, not wanting to go into trouble with her.
However, there were also those who refused to obey – out of stubbornness or simply because they were more comfortable there, “in Kamchatka ”.
Larisa did not leave them alone until they accepted their part to be in the forefront.
However, my good friend once categorically refused to obey, and Larisa finally gave up and graciously allowed her not to move from her place, waving her hand on the rebel, but without forgetting to release the following remark: - If you are so uninterested, you can even leave.
Then my acquaintance stood up and left the hall in silence.
As they say, I found a braid on a stone.
Larisa usually opened the meeting with an introductory speech (on any pressing, literary or historical topic, reflected in the last issue of the magazine) for thirty to forty minutes of etak.
She was a well-educated woman and a natural speaker.
(Fidel Castro was resting.)
The material knew perfectly well.
She spoke smoothly, enthusiastically, and “did not watch the watch. ”
"Watching the clock" fell on my difficult organizational share, which I did, worrying about how we will accommodate all the authors who are obediently waiting for their turn, in the pitiful part of the time left before the closure of the library.
I was hesitant to stop Larisa.
It was fraught with scandal, and I wanted a peaceful outcome of the event.
If I was able to meet her with a look, I symbolically pointed my finger at my hand watch and made big eyes.
After all, it worked.
Larisa finished her opening remarks and began to call the authors of the magazine to the microphone one by one.
Those who prepared a substantial presentation had to be reduced urgently.
Towards the end, the authors no longer performed, but simply flashed and bowed, as if in accelerated filming.
Some unlucky people never got to the line.
They did not complain aloud about their fate, but the expression of their faces testified to the concealed resentment.
Who wants to come to such a distance from the center of world culture, the majestic Manhattan to the provincial Brooklyn with the hope to introduce themselves to colleagues and Russian-speaking audience, sit submissively for an hour and a half and leave without waiting for the line to their author 's word!
Sometimes at the presentations of "Words..."
Larisa shortened her opening speech by giving her time to a writer if he was well known.
For example, a prose of IM.
Huge in height and width, the writer IM took a lot of space at the table and about an hour of time.
He remembered the Soviet era, spoke slowly, interestingly, alive, savouring his performance, felt like at home, and seemed, did not plan to swing around.
No matter how I showed him his watch, he didn't react to my hints.
What was to be done!
I had to get up in a gentle voice, politely remind him and Larisa of the other authors included in the program.
Larisa, like every queen, had her favorites.
In the editorial office and among the authors.
Talented people, often with a difficult fate and without permanent work, interrupting random earnings.
I don't want to name names, I don't know how their personal relationship was going.
All I know is that Larisa cared about her favorites touching, gave them solo presentations in the library and in the editorial office, published from room to room, published their books.
She called me several times and asked me to pat about their possible permanent employment in our library.
But who am I to pat at the top of the library?
Who will listen to me there!
I've been telling Larisa about the job openings for clerk, assistant librarian, computer technician, or even security.
(Still urban work!)
She explained where to take questionnaires, how to fill them, how to better prepare for the interview.
Unfortunately, these attempts to find employment have not ended.
The famous poet AM, who lived in Manhattan, was treated with careful admiration.
The poet was brought to the front of the editorial office, and pleased Larisa with a smile announced to the audience his performance.
AM was old and sick by then.
Larisa tried to talk him out, but he only looked sadly at those present with beautiful blue eyes and said helplessly: “Not today.
- Well, maybe today, our dear, please.
“We were waiting for you so much! ” Larisa asked, begged.“ No, no! ”
Another time, "said AM quietly, with the voice of the robot, without emotion.
And Larisa accepted, no longer asked, did not insist, realizing the impasse.
I never introduced myself again.
The AM did not perform at all anymore and gradually faded away.
He was gone in 2009.
Somewhere after 2010, Larisino's health deteriorated.
She was confusing materials, forgetting names, events.
The outbursts of anger have increased.
A nervous teak appeared: shaking his head.
Despite her health problems, she still tried to command the parade, but in fact, the magazine was run by her assistants.
One night, she suddenly called me and asked me for the phone number and address of the printing house where the magazine was printed.
She said that she was looking for my phone number in the notebook for a long time and finally found it on the letter B. In complete confusion, I asked: "Permit, Larisa, why were you looking for me on the letter B?"
My name is Litinskaya.
We've known you for so many years!
You're a librarian.
So, by the letter B.
And librarians need to know everything.
I will try to help you, "I said and laughed.
That was our last phone call.
Then I called the editorial office and asked one of the employees to tell Larisa the phone number and address of the printing house.
It was clear...
The impact of Hurricane Sandy on New York in October 2012 on Ilya and Larisa Shanker was appalling.
Rumor has it that the couple somehow found themselves on the street in Brooklyn and lost their orientation.
Then they went to a nursing home where they lived for a while.
Soon, in September 2013, Ilya died.
At his funeral, I saw Larisa the last time.
She was brought in a wheelchair.
She was wearing a dark tracksuit.
The hair is not clean, the missing look.
In addition, a sad spectacle during an already sad event.
I was really sorry for her.
Larissa sat in this chair near the coffin for the entire funeral ceremony and at the end asked someone from the relatives: “Help me to approach him.
I want to kiss him goodbye.
Larisa survived her husband for a while.
She died in February 2015.
Larisa Shanker was an outstanding personality in all manifestations of her organizational and journalistic talent.
Along with her went the whole era of crossing four emigrations, violent literary meetings, frank interviews, hot disputes, discussion articles, scandalous stories...
Her brainchild Word, one of the most popular and favorite Russian magazines abroad, continues to live with the efforts of A. Pushkin, L. Berdnikov, N. Rafalson and other members of the editorial board and editorial board.
December 2016.
The article was first published in the journal Word\ Word, No93 Elena Litinskaya

After a Japanese tourist waited seven months to visit the ruins of Machu Picchu in Peru, officials eventually opened this historic site to the tourist.
According to ISNA and the Guardian, a Japanese tourist who was trapped in Peru due to the spread of the coronavirus and waited for seven months to visit the country 's most famous historical site, Machu Picchu, was able to visit the historic site, while the officials of the country only allowed this Japanese tourist to enter Machu Picchu.
The arrival of Yesi Takayama to Machu Picchu was thanks to the special request he had sent.
During an online press conference, the Minister of Culture of Peru stated: "He had traveled to Peru with the dream of entering Machu Picchu...
Thus, this Japanese tourist, with the responsibility to maintain this historic place, entered the ruins and was able to visit this place before returning to his country. "
The country of Peru has recorded 33,305 deaths in connection with the coronavirus.
International flights of the country have only recently resumed to seven Latin American countries.
Takayama, who bought his ticket from March, arrived at a historic site more than 500 years ago on Saturday and became the first visitor to be allowed to walk in Machu Picchu in the last seven months.
The main plan of the tourist was to stay in Peru for only a few days to visit Machu Picchu.
According to the Minister of Culture, Machu Picchu will open to domestic and foreign tourists in March.
By reopening this historic site, only 30% of its permanent capacity will be used to visit.
Machu Picchu, known as the Lost City of Inca, was built around 1450 at the height of the Inca Empire.
It is located 50 miles from the capital of the Inca, which is why it has never been found and destroyed like other Inca cities.
On July 24, 1911, Machu Picchu was introduced to the Western world by American historian Hiram Bingham in a speech at Yale University.
He was guided by local guidance where he occasionally visited the site.
Machu Picchu was registered as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983 and was recognized as a pure masterpiece of architecture and a unique symbol of the Inca civilization.
The end of the message

The temple was also used as a tomb and observation post of LIMA.
In July 2021, archaeologists discovered the Inca Temple of the Moon inside a shallow cave near the ancient city of Machu Picchu in Peru, Heritage Daily reports.
The temple is decorated with niches skillfully carved in the rock and imitation of doors inserted into the stones under the hanging cave.
The entrance to it is truly huge: its height is 8 m, and the width is 6 m. The altar of the temple is carved from a monolithic stone block.
The temple is dedicated to all three main objects of worship in the Inca religion: Hanan Pacha (heaven), Kai Pacha (earth) and Ukju Pacha (underground world), symbolically depicted as a condor, a puma and a snake.
The exact purpose of the Temple of the Moon is unknown.
Despite the name given to him by archaeologists, there is no evidence of the Inca cult of the moon.
During the full moon, the inner part of the cave temple is illuminated by the moonlight, and that is why it received this name.
Researchers suggest that this place was used not only as a temple (as evidenced by the ritual bathing complex found next to it), but also as a burial place, as well as as as an observation point (the temple is on the north-west side of the Wina Picchu peak, from where a good view opens).
Spanish sources note that the Incas perceived the caves as the place where their ancestors came from, and often buried their deceased in caves so that their souls could settle there and reunite with their ancestors.
The Incas also made sacrifices to the mountain deities in the caves.
During recent excavations in the cave temple, archaeologists found a niche with two small holes carved into it, which confirms the assumption of worship of the spirits of the mountains.
Through the holes there is a mountain Serro Yanantin, also deified by the Incas.
In Machu Picchu, the Incas conducted “pachamama ” rituals (offerings to the earth) in the temple on the so-called“ Sacred Rock ”, which offers views of Serro Yanantin.

In order to stabilize the situation in the Donbas, it is very important that the people remaining in the occupied territories feel part of Ukraine.
To do this, their lives must improve.
This opinion was expressed by former US Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice in an interview with Novoe Vremya.
Read also Poroshenko appealed to the UN Security Council on the deployment of peacekeepers in Donbas (video) "I think all participants in the negotiations will realize: Minsk [Minsk agreements] does not work.
We must take a step backwards and understand what are the necessary elements for stability in eastern Ukraine.
From the experience of such situations in other parts of the world, I can add: the Ukrainian government should already think about how to improve the lives of people in the occupied territories and in the war zone in order for these people to feel part of the Ukrainian state ", - said Condoleeezza Rice.
Former US Secretary of State also commented on the issue of the deployment of UN peacekeepers in Donbas, stressing that first, it is necessary to decide on a wider political format within which the mission should be introduced: "To talk about the deployment of UN peacekeepers without a clear political framework and a broad vision of the process in which it is, in my opinion, premature.

Biden, who is expected to enter the White House, is not the Democratic Party, which has little hope of winning the Senate, or the Republican Party, which has a seat in the House of Representatives and seems to hold the Senate majority, or Trump, who is now busy with the results of the vote.
The real victory is actually the "Trumpism" that Trump himself has deliberately and unintentionally stirred up in the American political culture.
Trumpism has the element of “personality worship ”, which can be another individual, or even an organization, such as the future Republican Party. Unlike the previous" Leninism ", which was named after the President of the United States, Trumpism is not a governance proposition that relates to the relationship between government and the private domain, especially on economic policy.
Trumpism is a political style in which populist ideas constitute a pattern of confrontation between "me" and "others", and disgust, fear, or hatred are sources of political energy.
This energy drives both Democratic and Republican voters to produce the highest presidential turnout expected to be unseen in a century: Democrats are motivated by fear or hatred of Trump for another four years; Republicans are also motivated by disgust, fear, or hatred of liberal progressives, socialists, and even deep states in right-wing conspiracy theories It's just
As a result, Biden is expected to be the most popular presidential candidate in American history, while Trump is the most successful presidential candidate to win the most votes.
This end has made American politics, especially the Republican generation, invisible to Trump himself, and influenced by the political figures and his family.
Trump has stirred up "Trumpism" in the political culture of the United States. How does the United States define "Trumpism"?
Although Trumpism has an element of "personality worship", this is not the essence of this, and this "personality" does not have to be Trump himself.
It can be another individual, or even an organization, such as the future Republican Party.
Those who constitute this "personality" do not have to have any consistent, fixed political claim.
Just as Trump has a policy of separation of parents and children from illegal immigrants, he calls himself "I love immigrants"; on the one hand, he renders racist remarks to suburban housewives, and on the other, he refers to himself as the president who has done the most for black people since Lincoln.
This seemingly paradoxical political expression is not missing, but the essence of Trumpism.
The essence of this is that Trumpists do not have any proper norms and ideals for themselves, as long as they achieve a specific goal, or even make people feel that this goal has been achieved — be aware that these goals may come from certain principles, such as anti-abortion measures — any unethical, logically inconsistent, anti-social norms or latent behavior can be completely inclusive. They were even encouraged.
On October 26, Barrett (right) took an oath of office as Supreme Court Justice at the White House. Lindsey Graham, the chairman of the Republican Senate Judiciary Committee, who was reelected in the election, was a clear example: he had made clear that the nomination of the Chief Justice should be left to the incoming President in the last year of his first term, but in October he led the Senate. The passage of the controversial appointment of Justice Amy Coney Barrett, a conservative majority of the Supreme Court's 6-3, was expected to tighten the right to abortion.
His payout was for South Carolina voters who allowed him to beat a Democratic rival who raised more than $100 million with a high vote of more than 10 percentage points.
There are many similar incidents during Trump's presidency.

During the COVID-19 outbreak, politicians and supporters of the Democratic Party will wear masks, abide by the advice of health professionals, do not endanger people 's health due to economic considerations, and other behavioral patterns or propositions will be raised to a level of compliance, not only by political institutions, but also through social media to accuse and humiliate people who fail to comply with epidemic prevention proposals......

On Friday, 109 years have passed since the discovery of Machu Picchu, the ancient fortress of the Incas in Peru, but the tourist site remains empty on this day due to the coronavirus crisis.
According to ISNA and quoted from AFP, on the 100th anniversary of the discovery of Machu Picchu by the American explorer, this monument was to be opened to the people after a long time, but with the increase in the number of people and the cancellation of the reopening program, even on the anniversary of the discovery of this valuable historical site, visitors and tourists were not.
The jewelry of the tourism industry "Peru" was closed to visitors from mid-March with the start of the quarantine laws in Peru in order to counter the outbreak of the coronavirus.
"Last year many people visited the site on the occasion of the anniversary of the discovery of Machu Picchu," said Darwin Baka, a UNESCO World Heritage Site in a conversation with AFP, but unfortunately this year there was no visitor, as there was still no clear plan to reopen Machu Picchu.
However, despite the increase in the number of coronavirus patients in Cusco, we may see the opening of the site in August. "
Kosko is the former capital of the Inca Empire, the nearest major city to Machu Picchu.
The Governor of Kosko announced in early July that if the situation improved, visitors could return from July 24 to Machu Picchu, but five days later announced that the number of people with coronavirus in the region was increasing.
According to officials, even with the opening of Machu Picchu, the number of visitors per day will be limited to 675 people, while the number of visitors per day would reach more than 5,000 people.
With the detection of 370,000 cases and more than 17,000 coronavirus deaths, Peru is one of the countries with the highest number of coronavirus infections in South America.
The quarantine caused by the crisis also caused $3.3 billion in damage to the country's tourism this year.
Machu Picchu, also known as the Lost City of Inca, was built around 1450 at the height of the Inca Empire.
It is located 50 miles from the capital of the Inca, which is why it has never been found and destroyed like other Inca cities.
On July 24, 1911, Machu Picchu was introduced to the Western world by American historian Hiram Bingham in a speech at Yale University.
He was guided by local guidance where he occasionally visited the site.
Machu Picchu was registered as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983 and was recognized as a pure masterpiece of architecture and a unique symbol of the Inca civilization.

A group of French and Japanese scientists found out that the meteorite found in the Sahara was a fragment of the protoplanet.
This is reported by Vice.
Experts reminded in a paper published in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences that an object weighing 32 kilograms was found in May last year in a remote area of the Sahara.
It was named Erg-Shesh 002 in honor of the place of fall.
The meteorite consists of magmatic rocks, and such cosmic bodies are called achondrites.
They are rarely found on Earth, and in 95% of cases they are fragments of Mars or the Moon.
Experts have found that the age of Erg-Shesh 002 is 4.565 billion years, that is, he is older than the Earth.
The achondrites usually contain basalt, but it is not found in the meteorite.
At the same time, they found andesite, a magmatic volcanic rock, which means that Erg-Shesh 002 is one of the rarest achondrites.
In addition, scientists named it one of the oldest samples of magmatic rock.
With its help, researchers will be able to understand what was the crust of the first protoplanets of the Solar System.
Last year, a group of scientists from the United States and Switzerland discovered particles over 7 billion years old inside the meteorite, the oldest solid material found on Earth.
Experts analyzed the fragments of the Murchison meteorite that fell in Australia in 1969.
To determine the age of the particles, they used the neon isotope Ne-21, which allows to determine how long the matter has been in contact with cosmic rays: the longer the interaction lasts, the more new elements are formed.

How fast can the fastest sound travel?
Scientists discovered the fastest possible sound in the environment, with a speed of 36 km / s.
Sound can move at different speeds depending on what material it is passing through.
For example, sound moves faster in warmer liquids compared to colder liquids; it can also move at different speeds in solids compared to liquids and gases.
Calculations show that sound in low-mass atoms has the fastest motion, so to understand the maximum speed that sound can travel, a group of researchers calculated the speed of sound through a solid hydrogen atom.
Hydrogen is the lowest mass of an atom, but it is not solid unless it is under high pressure, which is a million times stronger than the Earth's atmosphere.
In these very specific conditions, researchers found that sound could travel close to its theoretical limit of 127 / 460 km / h.

▲ The oldest 7 billion years old solar anterior material found in meteorites in Australia, scientists look forward to learning more about the origin of the stars.
The oldest solid matter on Earth may be 7 billion years old! Scientists announced on Monday that the meteorite that crashed in Murchison, Australia, 50 years ago contained 40 dust particles, which were detected from about 50 to 7 billion years ago, and the oldest particles are even larger than the Sun and Earth. The entire Solar System is 2.5 billion years old.
A meteorite that fell to the town of Mucchison in Victoria state, Australia, in 1969. The material found in the meteorite was born before the formation of the Solar System and is therefore known as the "presolar grains", which are produced when a star dies, and through these traces, scientists can build the history of the stars.
Philipp Heck, curator of the Field Museum in Chicago, stated that the difficulty of the study was "like finding a needle in the hay" because it was necessary to break down everything in the meteorite to the finest possible level for further detection.
The researchers described the acid-dissolving meteorite as "a rotten peanut butter."
According to Heck, "Some people think that the stars have been steadily generating, but thanks to these new discoveries, we have direct evidence of a wave of star-born babies born seven billion years ago."
The study of the oldest material on Earth allows us to know the origin of stars, carbon atoms in the human body, and even oxygen in the air. With these dust particles, we can trace the material before the solar generation.
▼ Decompose everything in the meteorite to the finest degree to observe the solar anterior matter.
(Photo / Reuters)

According to a reporter in the field of technology, the Young Journalists Club, a small Australian town called Morchison, has become one of the most important study places for astronomers, with the discovery of materials in that area.
In 1969, a large meteorite hit the ground in the area.
After passing through the atmosphere, the meteorite was torn apart, and parts of it fell a little further south from the city.
Now, over the decades, researchers in the remaining pieces of meteorite were able to discover tiny particles of star dust that, until now, has been dubbed the oldest material discovered on Earth.
According to given theories, the particles discovered in these remaining fragments are five to seven billion years ago.
While the planet is only around 4.5 billion years old, our solar system has been around 4.6 billion years.
This is one of the most amazing terrestrial discoveries about the cosmos that gives us a great opportunity to better study how stars form in the Milky Way.
Researchers are currently working on 40 particles of space dust from the city of Mucchison.
To estimate the age of the seeds, they investigated the isotopes of the neon element in it.
Neon in space reacts with cosmic rays and can therefore be considered a measure of age estimation.
Exposure to cosmic rays, which are actually very energetic and moving particles in the universe, causes neon deformation and produces isotopes.
Due to the abundance of neon isotopes, we can understand the previous conditions and thus its age.
Read more: Why hasn ’ t humans gone to Mars yet?
The dust particles of the star may have been in the direction of an asteroid that landed near Morchison.
Since these particles are older than our solar system, they can be used to obtain important information about the conditions prior to the formation of the solar system.
The current estimate of scientists from these stellar dust grains is that these seeds were created during the formation of a star.
At that time, the star formation process was happening much more than today, and these seeds are the result of one of these events.
Investigation of stellar dust requires an understanding of the life cycle of interstellar dust particles, because these particles are actually the ingredients of a large part of the cosmos, and their footprint can be found almost everywhere, from meteorites to stars and planets.
So far, with the help of the isotopes of neon age, stellar dust has been estimated, although the estimated range is large, in other words, indefinite and accurate.
Further research on these dust grains is expected to learn more about age, how to form the Milky Way Galaxy, stars, etc.
End of message /

Russian scientists looked inside the rarest meteorites Russian scientists conducted a study of a large fragment of Seimchan meteorite found in the Magadan region in 1964, reports Rossiyskaya Gazeta by the method of neutron tomography of physics of the Joint Institute of Nuclear Research (LNF JNR) received information about the composition and spatial distribution of the substance in it without destroying the object itself.
According to them, the meteorite contains all types of morphology of olive grains and large metal areas.
It belongs to the class of pallasites, the rarest and most beautiful variety of meteorites.

According to Sergey Kichanov, senior researcher of the LNF of JINR, a scientific publication was sent to the international journal Meteoritics & Planetary Science, which should draw the attention of the international meteorite community to the structural studies of Seimchan meteorite.

She said that such a meteorite is called the Pallas Iron in honor of Academician Pallas, who found in the vicinity of Krasnoyarsk the first iron-stone meteorite.
Scientists know several fragments of Seichan meteorite.

Scientists from the Lawrence National Laboratory in Berkeley, United States, investigated two meteorites that had landed on Earth since 1998.

According to experts, molecules in meteorite crystals were synthesized under unclear conditions about 4.5 billion years ago.
How the discovery will help to advance the study of the Solar System, was understood by RT.
Whether there is life on Ceres, the asteroid Geba and the dwarf planet Ceres, located in the asteroid belt (the mass of Ceres is 6 thousand times less than the mass of the Earth, the diameter of the dwarf planet is 950 km. — RT), can contain all the substances necessary for the birth of life.
They were the sources of two meteorites that fell to Earth, in the depths of which for the first time it was possible to detect water and complex organic substances — hydrocarbons and amino acids.
After several billion years of "travel" in the asteroid belt, the meteorites came under the influence of earthly attraction, and in 1998 one of them fell on a children's baseball court in Texas, and the other "landed" in Morocco.
Scientists investigated both meteorites, but 20 years ago, insufficient sensitivity of spectroscopic equipment prevented the detection of microscopic molecules inside these celestial bodies.
Now the specialists of the National Laboratory in Berkeley have turned to Raman spectroscopy - a method that allows using light to determine the chemical composition of organic materials and check them for water.
To do this, they used advanced spectrometers that recognize the smallest molecules and ions, and determined the chemical composition of tiny blue crystals of salts isolated from meteorites.
Meteorites with crystal inclusions © Dr. Queenie Hoi Shan Chan "Each salt crystal with a size of about two millimeters is essentially a small" box "filled with organic compounds and elements necessary for the birth of life.
What is even more incredible is that the source of the meteorites is the nearest dwarf planet to the Sun and at the same time the largest asteroid of Ceres, as well as the Astrode of Geb.
Probably, these celestial bodies can be a suitable place for the formation of life, ” said Quini Chan, the author of the study.
The meteorite crystals reveal traces of water, as well as oxygen and nitrogen-containing organic compounds, including amino acids, necessary for the formation of proteins.
"Reminds of a fly in amber," said the author of the study David Kilcoyne.
It is noted that meteorite crystals, like fossilized resin, retained molecules synthesized under unclear conditions 4.5 billion years ago.
Because the chemical composition of the meteorite itself differs from the composition of the crystals found on it, scientists believe that their sources are different space objects.
According to researchers, in the distant past, the paths of the ancestors of meteorites intersected, and they interacted with each other.
The organic-containing crystals came from a once water-rich "ancestor", an ocean that existed in the early Solar System, probably on Ceres.
Scientists believe that meteorites are linked to Ceres and Geb's asteroid.
The results of the study show that the crystals were filled with water, which could be in either a liquid or a solid state.
Crystals found in the meteorite © Queenie Chan / The Open University, UK Scientists believe that biomolecules or even primitive life can hide in salt crystals and "travel" through space.
In turn, collisions between asteroids helped organic compounds spread, and interactions with earth-like planets could have triggered the evolution of more complex forms of life.
The researchers concluded that the origin of life was possible elsewhere.
In their view, the new discovery, along with recent findings of water ice and mineral deposits on Ceres, makes this dwarf planet potentially livable.
Moreover, the results of the study suggest that other asteroids may also hide the elements necessary for the birth of life.
Experts intend to continue to study the chemical compositions of meteorite crystals in the hope of detecting traces of water and complex organic substances in them.

Hillary Clinton, representing the American Democratic Party, is the most dishonest candidate for the highest state office in the United States in nearly half a century.
This opinion was expressed on Sunday by the candidate for Vice President of the United States from the Republican Party Mike Pence.
The American Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) released on Friday a transcript of Clinton's survey about her use of a personal computer server to conduct official e-mail correspondence as the US Secretary of State (2009-2013), as well as accompanying documents.
It was found that the information contained in these papers refuted certain Clinton statements regarding her use of a private server in the public service.
"It's just additional evidence that Hillary Clinton has been the most dishonest presidential candidate since Richard Nixon," said Pence on NBC.
Nixon was forced to resign as head of the United States administration under threat of impeachment in 1974.
"The revelations contained in the interview with the FBI, I really believe, confirm the fact that Hillary Clinton has been the most dishonest presidential candidate since Richard Nixon," said Pence, who currently holds the position of governor of Indiana.
He called Clinton's "incomprehensible" decision to build a personal computer server as head of U.S. foreign policy.
Clinton, according to Pence, acted in such a way as to remove "from accountability to society" its actions, which could constitute a conflict of interest between the functions of the US Secretary of State and the activities of a charitable foundation established at the time by her wife Bill, the former American president.
Pence also again criticized the current US administration, led by Barack Obama, for its policy in the Middle East.
"Barack Obama and Hillary Clinton ’ s foreign policy has made the situation in the Middle East out of control.
We see Syria being destroyed in the civil war, Libya being destroyed in the civil war.
Entire countries and territories of this region of the world are under threat due to terrorism, "said Donald Trump ’ s partner in the upcoming U.S. elections in November.
At the same time, Pence noted that, in the event of a victory in November, he and Trump, for security reasons, will suspend the admission of refugees from Syria to the United States.
"Donald Trump and I think we should freeze the Syrian refugee reception program," said Pence.
He explained that, from Trump ’ s point of view, the US needs to work with “Arab countries to create safe zones ” that could leave the population of“ war-torn areas ”.

Tatneftekhiminvest-holding calculated the losses of Tatarstan petrochemistry First meeting of the Board of Directors of Tatneftekhiminvest-holding: the pandemic “bites ” 8.4% of production, KFU developed a catalyst for large petrochemistry, and Tatneft tests chargers Innovative know-how in large petrochemistry similar to the venture industry: no 100% guarantee that the forecasts of their developers will be justified in the future, representatives of the Tatar fuel and energy complex, assessing energy projects at the meeting of the Board of Directors of Tatneftekhiminvest-holding, reasoned so.
Today, they presented four completely new products for industrial implementation in chemistry, energy and agriculture.
"I am not such a cool connoisseur of chemistry, but I know that there are the olefins most marginal, and there are less.
Let 's ask specialists, "- so with the help of independent experts, President Rustam Minnikhanov" diagnosed "the validity of each of the voiced ideas.
In conclusion, he advised five "refusals" from the "club of oil and petrochemicals" to transfer historical materials about the achievements of the companies to the State Archive of the Republic of Tatarstan.
The main issue of today ’ s meeting of the Board of Directors of Tatneftekhiminvest-holding was the traditional summing up of the results of the work of the oil and gas chemical complex of Tatarstan for the pandemic year 2020.
They were presented by the CEO of the company Rafinat Yarullin.
From the first minutes, he expected to report on the deterioration of the situation in the industry on all key indicators, but it was more important to know how deep the fall turned out to be, who was in the leaders and outsiders.
However, the apocalyptic spring forecasts did not come true.
According to Rafinat Yarullin, the volume of production of enterprises of the oil and gas chemical complex of the Republic of Tatarstan decreased by 8.4% by the end of 2020.
This turned out to be better than analysts predicted in the spring of 2020, promising a decline of up to 15 – 20%.
However, the lack of accurate figures in the report of the volume of shipped products and the financial result for 2020 were somewhat embarrassing.
Perhaps the final outcome has not yet been summed up and will be heard at a later date.
Instead, the speaker was forced to announce the figures of tax deductions, which indirectly "highlight" the financial results of the industry.
According to Rafinat Yarullin, the enterprises of the industry transferred 384 billion rubles to all levels of the budget, but 54 billion rubles fell into the consolidated budget of the Republic of Tatarstan.
This is 41% less than in 2019, Yarullin said.
Photo: tatarstan.ru But it is wrong to write off all the losses of the industry on the pandemic, said the CEO of Tatneftekhiminvest-holding.
The negative impact was the unsuccessful end of 2019.
“The deterioration of the results was affected not only by the pandemic, negative trends were observed in 2019, ” said Rafinat Yarullin.“ The output of products is declining for the second year in a row. ”
The slides showed the dynamics of production decline, the starting point of which was 2019.
If 102.8% growth was achieved at the end of 2018, the first decrease to 99% occurred in 2019, and 91.6% in 2020.
Repairs to the pandemic: fuel prices are rising Negative dynamics in all sectors, except for rubber products, said Rafinat Yarullin.
In 2020, Tatneft reduced production by 13% to 25.6 million tons.
As a result, its revenue fell by 15% and its profit by 61%.
Negative revenue figures were shown by the majority of petrochemical enterprises: from 13 to 50%.
Rafinat Yarullin explained this by the fall in world prices for oil, oil products, plastics, urea.
The peak of decline came in the spring of 2020.
But in the fourth quarter, prices recovered, and for all types of petrochemical products, which helped to recover.
"KOS increases the capacity of polycarbonates to 100 thousand tons, but during the modernization it was necessary to suspend their production a little.
The supply of polyethylene to republican consumers continues to decline.
NKNH has improved production performance.
The production of rubbers, plastics and alpha-olefins is higher than at the end of 2019, ” Yarullin said.
Photo: Rinat Nazmetdinov Amazing metamorphoses occurred in the fuel market: if at the beginning of the year demand for gasoline fell, after the removal of restrictions, there was a noticeable increase in prices, which continues until now.
The oil processing complex reacted differently, replacing the downtime of each other.
TANECO increased the processing of raw materials by 12%.
Thus, the output of motor gasoline increased by 6.5%, and diesel fuel decreased by 6%, to 3.4 million tons.
The decline occurred in the second half of the year, when TANECO carried out major repairs to several plants.
At TAIF-NC, 7.3 million tons of oil were processed during the year, the production of diesel fuel increased by 19% to 2.9 million tons, and motor gasoline decreased by 11%.
The main reason is the overhaul at the end of the year.
Due to the overhaul and shortage of raw materials, there was a short-term drop in the third quarter of the release of ethylene and polyethylene Khimzavod Karpov in the search for business ideas Khimzavod Karpov reduced revenue by 17%.
According to Rafinat Yarullin, the closure of the dehydrogenation catalyst production line (this happened two years ago, but the liquidation of production still makes you aware) led to the deterioration of the indicators.
"The last investment projects were implemented in 2013, it is necessary to look for new competitive products.
I ask you to give proposals for the creation of import-substituting industries, ” Rafinat Yarullin, who himself was a member of the Board of Directors of OJSC Khimzavod named after Karpov, asked for help from the Petrochemical Club.
Recall that the governing bodies of the Mendeleev Combine changed with the arrival of Timur Metshin, the eldest son of the Metshin family.
And without that, it is obvious that a new reality has come, which has shattered the landscapes of all markets — and of petrochemicals, among other things.
According to Yarullin, the threat lies in the fact that the world market is switching to cheaper raw materials than used by Tatarstan companies.
“The market will be oversaturated, profitability will decrease, ” he warned, adding that investors need to go to medium and low-tonnage chemicals.
Photo: yandex.ru In favor of this thesis, he cited a possible ban on the sale of cars with internal combustion engines.
“The governments of 14 countries have already announced an imminent ban on this technology, ” Rafinat Yarullin said.“ Even conservative scenarios suggest a decline in oil consumption.
This once again says that all oil refining products need to be directed to the production of chemical products, ” Yarullin said.
The speaker concluded the report with a call to comply with the rules of industrial safety at hazardous enterprises.
"The fire at the petrochemical plant in Ufa was destroyed.
We had an emergency in Sarmanov district, "he recalled.
Polyethylene Derivatives: Whether a CFC catalyst is needed But much more time took the traditional pitch session with the presentation of new technologies and products for the fuel and energy sector.
COVID-19, however, did not collapse plans: four new projects were included in the agenda.
KFU scientists did not stop working during self-isolation and demonstrated, we can say, a technological startup for the production of a new catalyst for selective oligomerization of ethylene.
The head of the scientific group of KFU Professor Dmitry Yakharov made a report "Catalytic dimerization and trimerization of ethylene".
He proposed the introduction of technology for the production of catalyst in large chemical enterprises.
However, his performance, which lasted more than 30 minutes, sounded as a scientific lecture, which was understood exclusively by specialists.
"I am not such a cool connoisseur of chemistry, but I know that there are olefins most marginal, and there are - so for myself, less - the President reacted with irony at the end of the report. - But we have two large specialists, ask them...
There is another third, a scientist ", - saw the president in the hall of the rector of KNITU-KHTI Yuri Kazakov.
But first, the president asked the scientist about the practical significance of the know-how: “Will the existing capacities be suitable or do new installations need? ”
Photo: csiul.ru “While they (catalysts) are at the laboratory level, but they are easily scaled, ” said Professor Yakhvarov.
Then the president asked to evaluate the merits of developing industrial practices.
“In fact, this is an interesting work, all that is connected with the selectivity of production, ” Farid Minigulov, CEO of KOS, first said.“ In comparison with our indicators that we receive, and those that are stated in the work, there are serious differences.
It's an interesting job.
I believe that it will be necessary to reach industrial tests, ” he approved.
Head of Nizhnekamskneftekhim Ayrat Safin added that “the linear olefins that we produce today are clearly tailored to our needs, and with selectivity in one direction or another – here you need to look ”.
Then, as an independent expert, the President called for the speech of the rector of KHTI Yuri Kazakov.
He spoke about his own experience in obtaining a catalyst, saying that when introduced into production, there are many side effects that can not be foreseen in advance.
“It is important to scale what we did not catch during laboratory processes.
When they operated their plant, they really suffered. "
Photo: tatarstan.ru “These are world-class technologies, we need to support them, ” Rafinat Yarullin insisted.
“But we must understand that we must produce the raw materials that are needed to strengthen our competitive positions, ” the President concluded.
The next “express testing ” was subjected to the reliability of power supply and energy efficiency of three-phase NORMEL AC voltage regulators.
They were presented by Vladimir Davydov, Director General of ENEKSGROUP Scientific and Implementation Corporation.
The cost of a domestic normalizer with a high degree of protection is 300 thousand rubles, and the Milanese company - 1.2 million euros.
“Clearly, economic benefits are even in price, ” Davydov said.
In addition, the normalizer provides a reduction in power consumption by 15%.
In response, the President asked to assess the merits of this proposal by Ilshat Fardiyev, Director General of the Network Company.
"We need to see, study the service contract.
Let ’ s look at the pilot project, ” he suggested.
The President has instructed to select several objects.
“The changes in industry need to be regulated, ” Minnikhanov noted.“ We are interested in this innovation.
We are the fifth region in the country in terms of production. "
“Their even descendants will not know ” Rail Gazetdinov, CEO of EnergoDevelopment Group, reported on the prospects of launching eight fast charging stations for electric vehicles on the Kazan — Moscow highway.
In particular, 39 fuel stations of PJSC Tatneft operate there.
The relevant projects are being developed at Zelenodolsk Electrotechnical Plant (ZETZ).
In conclusion, they gave the floor to the head of the State Archives of the Republic of Tatarstan Gulnare Hadbrakhmanova.
She complained about five companies that refused to transfer archival documents to Gosarkhiv.
Among the rejectors are SINH, Innopolis, Kazan Fat Plant.
“Their descendants will not even know! ” she exclaimed.
The president asked me to solve the issue.
“Leave the memory of yourself and your companies, advances and achievements, ” he advised.
The president remembered how he was given a book 100 years ago.
"If there was no such work, everything... everything will be forgotten, lost," he said.
And he gave a simple argument: “Look how interesting it is for us to raise documents from 100 to 200 years ago. ”

A new book of 254 Caves, the most mysterious of the Mogao Caves, is published by the Beijing Sanlian Bookstore.
The book combines a large number of high-definition mural pictures, line drawings and illustrations, and uses a new perspective and new media to make a modern interpretation of the Northern Wei Cave, which has made Zhang Daqian, Chang Shuhong, Dong Xiwen and other artists active and submerged in copying.
Chen Haitao, one of the authors of the book, shared with Shanghai readers the secrets of the 254th Cave and his love for Dunhuang.
It is one of the most representative caves in Dunhuang.
Chen Haitao and Chen Qi, two writers of Dunhuang No. 254 Cave, visited the 254 Cave for the first time, and were also a pair of young lovers who attended the Central Academy of Fine Arts and the Beijing Film Academy.
After graduation, they left the bustling city and went to the Dunhuang Research Institute in the northwest, where they married and had children, and began studying the Dunhuang frescoes.
The two men have been studying for more than a decade.

Online History Service: The memoirs and memoirs of activists of the revolution, as well as the establishment of the Islamic system, are considered important sources of reading and writing this section of contemporary history.
The Islamic Revolution Document Center is one of the institutions that has recorded and published the memories of these activists and has published numerous such titles in the market.
Remembrance of Maryam Behrouzi (Mary) is one of those who have been devised by Wise Amiri.
In part of his debates, the editor writes: "At the same time as the beginning of the Islamic movement led by Imam Khomeini, Mrs. Behrouzi, who was one of their copyists, studied religious sciences and, after the end of his studies, set up religious meetings for women.
At these meetings, he taught religious science and lectures, and this new method attracted more interested people.
Some of the participants were interested in political issues, which is why they organized in a group called the cores of resistance and spread the proclamations of Imam.
With close proximity to the victory of the Islamic Revolution, the activities of Ms. Behrouzi and the cores of resistance increased.
In 1979, Dr. Mofatteh invited Ms. Behrouzi to give a speech at the Qaba Mosque, where she was arrested and imprisoned at one of the lecture sessions by Savak. After her release from prison, she participated in the march and demonstrations of the people against the Pahlavi government.
With the victory of the Islamic Revolution, Ms. Behrouzi 's political and religious activities increased.
He was initially a member of the Islamic Republic Party and was sent to the region on behalf of the Party to prepare reports of the unrest of the border provinces.
Iraq was also sent to the war zones to promote propaganda by the beginning of the Iraq imposed war on Iran.
For this reason, part of their memories are dedicated to this part of their lives and activities, and they have information about the political and religious activities of women before the revolution, as well as their activities in the Islamic Republic Party and their support of the Islamic warriors.
During the registration of the nominees for the first term of the Islamic Consultative Assembly, Dr. Beheshti asked Ms. Behrouzi to register to enter the parliament, and she also registered in the midterm election and entered the parliament as the representative of Tehran.
In this way, he was the representative of the people of Tehran in parliament until the fourth term.
She was an advocate of women 's rights in parliament during her term of representation, and with her activities laws such as prohibition of polygamy, the right to custody of children to the mother, pension of women with twenty years of work and...
It was passed by the House.
During this period, on behalf of Parliament, he was sent to international conferences.
An important part of Ms. Behrouzi 's memoirs is related to this period and gives new and firsthand information about the activities of the parliament after the victory of the revolution.
The formation of political factions in the parliament and their competition is another issue that Ms. Behrouzi described.
An important part of Ms. Behrouzi ’ s activity is the establishment of the Zainab community (S).
It is a socio-religious community for women that was created at the suggestion of the President of Ayatollah Khamenei.
The organization, which Mrs. Behrouzi is a founder and secretary-general, plays an important role in the electoral activities of the Parliament, the President and the Islamic Council of Tehran and also strives to promote Islamic culture.
The memory of Ms. Behrouzi of the establishment and activity of the party clarifies part of the history of the parties after the victory of the Islamic Revolution and familiarizes us with active political women.
His knowledge of political issues and familiarity with groups and political parties and their thoughts led him to give a detailed analysis of the 9th and 10th presidential elections and the events after the election in 2009, which is well known in part of his memoirs. "
In another part of the introduction, the editor introduces the various chapters of this book: “The first chapter of the book, entitled Birth, Family and Education, introduces parents and educational space, education and marriage, and part introduces the children of Ms. Behrouzi.
The second chapter, The Islamic Movement, led by Imam Khomeini, is the most detailed section of the book.
In this season, Ms. Behrouzi starts her memories in 1962 and familiarizes herself with the Imam ’ s movement and ends her visit to Iran until the arrival of the Imam.
The third chapter, "Society of Zeynab" (s), describes how the Zeynab community is established and operates, and specifically introduces one of the women 's political and religious organizations.
The fourth chapter, as the representative of the Islamic Consultative Assembly, is one of the important chapters of the book that contains information about the activities of Ms. Behrouzi in the first to fourth parliamentary of the Islamic Consultative Assembly.
In this section, Ms. Behrouzi ’ s activities in parliament have come to advocate for women ’ s rights and her missions to participate in international conferences, as well as the election of political groups to enter the parliament.
In addition, some political parties and groups have been introduced and have been told about how they are formed.
The fifth chapter, titled Parties and Elections, has played the role of peer and reformist communities in the 9th and 10th presidential elections and the activities of fundamentalists in these two periods, and has been analyzed at the end of events after the 2009 election.
This book is based on the interview of Ms. Behrouzi with the Islamic Revolution Document Center in 2009.
After accepting this plan in May 2010, in a meeting with him, they gave their consent to print memories to the Islamic Revolution Document Center.
On 16 Ramazan 1390, during a visit to the Zeynab (s) community office, I compiled the text and typed the memories for reading and correcting them, and he also gave me a CD containing personal documents and photos to print in the book.
According to the planning, after the study, the ambiguities in the text were corrected and provided to the author, but their illness and death did not make this possible on February 20, 2011.
I contacted his family to continue working, but I did not receive an answer.
After each unanswered call, I passed the steps to their secretaries in the community of Zeinab (S), but it was also inconclusive.
After transferring to the documents center of the Islamic Revolution, some ambiguities were resolved based on existing sources, but others still remain in the text, so I will remove my fault and apologize to the readers, and to the lady Behrouzi, who was a well-known figure in the history of the Islamic Revolution and whose efforts in the text of the memories are well known, I ask forgiveness of the great God. "

Thai beach Maya on the island of Phi Phi Lei closed for another five years due to the consequences of aggressive tourism.
The shores of the film "Beach" with Leonardo Di Caprio suffered from serious pollution: due to the impact of people on the environment, almost completely disappeared the area of the coral reef and several species of marine inhabitants.
Due to the uncontrolled influx of guests, many of the most beautiful points of the planet lose their pristine appearance, and residents of cities declare war on instagram workers who interfere with a quiet life.
As in the most attractive locations for photos try to fight overtourism - in the material "Izvestia".
Streets, villages and cities In early April, the residents of Paris appealed to the city authorities to set a gate on Rue Cremieux to protect themselves from the endless flow of noisy guests.
A tiny street with pastel houses was once a "secret place" in the capital of France.
The street became famous thanks to the photos of travelbloggers on Instagram: followed by imitators, the number of which is already several hundred per day.
Because of this, residents feel literally in a state of siege, walking daily to their homes through a crowd of people taking the same type of pictures on their smartphones.
Even the most understanding and tolerant residents of the street believe that guests could behave with great respect to the owners of real estate.
For example, do not look in their windows and turn on loud music in the evenings.
The rest of the residents are disturbed and clipmakers, who come to the street early in the morning, until mass tourists reach the place.
Likes Rue Kremier Photo: Depositphotos In addition to restrictions, the locals decided to fight with instagram their own methods and began to take photos of those who behave improperly.
The street has created its account on Instagram Clubcremieux, which contains "garbage of the street".
In the pictures, bloggers stand in unambiguous poses, sit or lie on the sidewalk in search of a successful angle and dress up in strange outfits to surprise their subscribers.
Most of the locals are annoyed with twines, in which girls stretch in someone's doorway.
By the way, the number of subscribers to this account has already exceeded 17,000, and judging by the number of video views, all these are real people.
Local authorities responded to the call of street residents and promised to regulate the access of excursion groups to the territory.
Information about the attractiveness of the street as a tourist object has already been removed from the websites of travel agencies and the regional committee on tourism.
