The museumer told who could have been involved in the theft of a meteorite in Chelyabinsk CHELYABINSK, July 18 – RIA Novosti.
Employees of the Chelyabinsk Museum may be involved in the theft of a fragment of a meteorite that was found by the FSB in a private collection of a geologist, said RIA Novosti Deputy Director of the State Historical Museum of the South Urals Tatyana Palagina.
According to the UFSB for the region, in early July, the department addressed the museum management with a statement about the missing from the reserves of a fragment of a meteorite weighing more than 2.5 kilograms.
As a result of operational search activities, the exhibit was found in a private collection of a geologist who provided advice to the museum.
The market value of one kilogram of meteorite, which is part of the museum fund of the Russian Federation, according to the staff of the museum, is more than 1 million rubles, the FSB informed.
"Before any thing becomes a museum object, it goes through the stages of processing, studying, documenting.
This is a short period of time.
And this process involves a rather narrow circle of museum staff, as well as invited consultants and experts.
Some of the employees turned out to be dishonest at this stage, and under the pretext of scientific activity, the fragment was taken out of the museum ", - said the interlocutor of the agency.
She clarified that the weight of the stolen meteorite fragment is about 2.7 kilograms, it has a huge historical and cultural value.
The fragment is now in the hands of the investigating authorities as evidence, she added.
The regional press service of the UFSB noted that the criminal case of theft from the museum has not yet been initiated, an investigation is underway, it is possible that the materials on the incident will be transferred to the police.
The meteorite with a supersonic speed and a bright flash of light flew over the territory of the Chelyabinsk region on the morning of February 15, 2013.
The shock wave from a meteorite called Chelyabinsk damaged buildings and knocked out many glasses, more than 1.6 thousand people were injured.
Scientists have collected fragments of the meteorite.
Their chemical analysis showed that this is an ordinary LL5 chondrite, one of the types of stone meteorites.
The weight of the largest fragment of the Chelyabinsk meteorite was 503.3 kilograms.

This study, published by the Geological Society of the United States, deals with the fact that the Incas decided to specifically erect their sanctuary right in the place where there are several geological fractures.
It is one of the most visited places in Peru and in the world.
In the middle of the Sacred Valley of the Inca Machu Picchu, which was allegedly built for the first Inca ruler Pachakutek, whose statue is in the city, rises between the Andes at an altitude of 2,430 meters above sea level.
Its architectural appearance remains intact almost six centuries after construction.
Machu Picchu even survived two earthquakes in 1650 and 1950, which destroyed colonial sites in a nearby area, but not the sacred city of the Incas.
By the way, the Spanish colonizers, when they arrived at the site of archaeological excavations, had the intention to destroy and rob the sanctuaries.
But they didn't get to Machu Picchu.
"The location of Machu Picchu is no accident," said Rualdo Menegata, a geologist from the Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil, and the author of the study.
Common sense suggests, it is noted in the publication that the right place for the construction of a sacred city - a symbol of the power of the Inca empire, would be a flat and stable area, offering a solid basis.
However, they chose a completely different option, and they did so consciously.
To find evidence of his theory, Menegat used satellite imagery and field measurements to map a dense network of fractures and cracks on the area on which the city was built.
These data showed that the fractures range from cracks in some stones to lines as long as 175 kilometers, which distinguish some of the river valleys surrounding the area.
Some of these coincide with the major breakdowns that have spawned the mountains of the central Andes over the past eight million years.
Some of the fractures go from the north-east to the south-west, while others usually pass from the north-west to the south-east, forming a kind of cross, in the center of which Machu Picchu is located.
The analysis shows that several buildings, stairs and urban areas of Machu Picchu, as well as surrounding agricultural fields, were constructed according to the cross-shaped orientation of these fractures.
"The design clearly reflects the matrix of destruction that underlies the city," Menegat said, adding that the picture was also seen in other ancient Inca cities, such as Ollantaitambo, Pisak and Cusco.
The study asserts that the area chosen for the construction of their holy city offered the Incas a large number of cracks and facilitated their work, as the stones were easier to cut out of rocks.
They lie so tightly to each other that today in Machu Picchu you can see stones that are built so precisely that it is impossible to insert a thin credit card between them.
But it wasn't the only advantage that the Inca fractures brought.
According to Menegat, the network also served the Incas as a water supply system.
"Tectonic fractures in this area directed melt and rain water directly to the citadel," says the geologist.
He claims that the earlier breakdowns have saved efforts in the construction of canals.
Other Inca culture experts argue that the location of Machu Picchu is related to their religious beliefs, as well as the need to be located in a high zone that would protect them from enemies, the powerful Chanca tribe, considered unrivaled warriors.
In general, historians and archaeologists agree that the Incas had sufficient knowledge and skills to determine whether the site was suitable for the creation of a city, in this case, one that would become an entire administrative, political and religious centre to govern the great, in their opinion, empire of Vilkabamba.
However, experts note, the history of the Incas can not be explained by 100 percent, because they did not have a writing and do not have any documents since those times.
The great administrative, political and spiritual center of the Incas still holds many mysteries, but for Menegat there is, according to him, one that has already been revealed.
"Machu Picchu clearly shows us that the Inca civilization was an empire of destroyed rocks," concluded the scientist.

ISNA / Khorasan Razavi Machu Picchu, a city with the most mysterious ancient buildings in the form of stone walls in South America, is a tourist area in Peru, located more than 2000 meters above sea level.
Machu Picchu, a city built around 1450, is a tangible evidence of the Inca Empire at the height of its power and achievement; the cut stone citadel is so strong that its cracks cannot be pierced by knife blades.
The ruins of Machu Picchu, located on the slopes of the Andes, continue to reveal the secrets of the Inca Empire.
Many believe that less known secrets are hidden under the layers of its history.
A set of palaces, squares, temples and houses may serve as a ceremonial place, military fortress, etc.
to be made.
Its amazing location is certainly perfect for any of the purposes mentioned.
The ruins are located on a long ridge surrounded by the turbulent river "Urumba" on three sides.
Researchers are still trying to discover clues to the hidden secrets of this place, located on the eastern slopes of the Andes, covered with Amazon tropical forests.
Machu Picchu seems to be in the center of a network of sites and trails.
Inca is said to have left no record of why it was built or how it was used before it was abandoned in the early 16th century.
However, engineering skills in the landscape of the area are strong evidence in Machu Picchu.
It is said that architects in Machu Picchu structures did not use any mortar and built it with precise cuts.
The ruins of Machu Picchu include 3,000 steps that provide access to areas around the mountain, and some believe that these spaces were used for planting vegetables.
The precision of cutting stones is so high and the stones are connected so that, in the words of some, it is not possible to place a credit card between them!
Other engineering benefits in this effect include earthquake resistance.
Although the Machu Picchu area is built above two fault lines, when an earthquake occurs, it is said that the stones used in some Inca structures "dance"; meaning that they go up and down with the tremors and fall back into place!
Without this construction method, many of the most famous buildings in Machu Picchu had been disintegrated for a long time.
Some believe that when the explorer Hiram Bingham encountered Machu Picchu in 1911, he sought a different city, known as Vikabamba, a fortified fortress of the Incas, in which a war was fought against Spanish conquerors and over time became known as the legendary lost city of Inca.
Another archaeologist later proved that the lost city of Incas lies west of Machu Picchu.
Machu Picchu consists of nearly 200 buildings, the most famous of which are "main temple of Machu Picchu", "city pyramid", "sun temple" and "royal tomb".
In the southern part of these ruins, there is a sacred structure known as the Temple of the Sun, which focuses on a rock mass.
There is also a temple in Machu Picchu, known as the Three Windows Building, which consists of a hall of 10.5 meters long and 4.2 meters wide with three trapezoid windows, one of the well-known Inca architectures.
Today, Machu Picchu is not isolated and actually has many visitors who have traveled to Peru.
The region compels many tourists to visit Peru to watch the history of Incas and is therefore one of the most important tourist attractions that is also economically important.
Today, but protecting this valuable building despite many tourists who do not observe the protection points when visiting the ruins has become a challenge for Peru.

▲ Meteorite.
Journalist Paul McRae and John Miller discovered a huge and rare meteorite while searching for treasure in Australia, and they decided to sell it to Geoscience Australia for US $200,000 (about NT $5.7 million) for research by scientists and tourists.
According to media reports, McRae and Miller went to Queensland in north-eastern Australia to pay for the treasure, and when McRae walked along to meet Miller, the metal detector suddenly made a big noise. McRae thought it was a good signal.
They both dug for about two hours and finally dug the stones out of the ground.
The rock was covered with dirt and seemed unremarkable, but there was always something strange to think of.
Paul suggests that the rock be cut apart, which is not unlike the ordinary stone, but feels heavier.
The two were hesitant about how to deal with the rock, and placed it in the back of the car, which was sealed for four weeks, and they finally decided to send it to the Earth Science Bureau for identification.
Steven Petkovski, curator of the National Mineral Fossil Collection, says that many people who came to the site claimed to have found meteorites, but 99% of them were not, and it was surprising and exciting when they opened the trunk.
The origin of the rock is clear, and the discovery of the rare iron-nickel meteorite by McRae and Miller, which scientists believe is likely to be a metal core fragment from an asteroid 4.5 billion years ago.
▲ Through studying meteorites, we can better understand the composition of the Solar System and asteroids.
It is understood that prior to the discovery of the meteorite, there were only five officially recognized meteorites in the world, four of which were found in the Antarctic, one in the United States, and the sixth found by McRae and Miller, the only one found in Australia and the largest, weighing 24.3 kg.
In the end, McRae and Miller decided to sell the treasure they had found to the Earth Science Bureau for $200,000 (about NT $5.7 million), allowing them to do research and offer it to tourists.
Mr Miller said that if we split him into several pieces, we could certainly sell more money. Mr McRae believes he hopes to keep the meteorite in Australia so that children can see it.
The meteorite, which is not only of scientific significance but also of art, is currently on display at the Earth Science Bureau in Canberra, Australia, and an online exhibition will be held on the 17th for people around the world.

Peru's most important tourist attraction, Machu Picchu, closed today after six weeks of sightseeing, authorities said local people were dissatisfied with the train service and decided to suspend its opening due to security concerns.
"The city of Machu Picchu will be closed from December 14 until the situation stabilizes as a result of the strike announced in the Machu Picchu area and in order to maintain the safety of tourists," said a statement issued by the Cultural Bureau of Cusco, Peru.
The city of Cusco is the nearest city to Machu Picchu.
The COVID-19 outbreak has shut down the ancient city for nearly eight months, and just six weeks after the start of sightseeing, the authorities announced this surprising measure.
The suspension is expected to continue until the end of protests by residents of Machu Picchu and Ollantaytambo, who are dissatisfied with the railway company that provides local train transport services.
People in both towns have demanded that trains between Cusco and Machu Picchu be more frequent and fare less.
The train is the only means of transportation for tourists to visit the ancient city and is widely used by local residents.
The route between Cusco and Machu Picchu is operated by two companies, Inca Rail and Peru Rail.
The peace protests, which began on September 9, have heated up over the weekend to protest against the occupation of the railway line by protesters and triggered clashes between police and civilians, and even threatened to occupy the ancient city.
Machu Picchu means "ancient mountain" in Quechua, the most enduring legend of the Inca Empire.
Before the Spanish conquered the area in the 16th century, the Inca Empire ruled over a large area of western South America for over 100 years.
The ruins of the Inca settlement were abandoned in the smoke and weeds until they were discovered by the American explorer Hiram Bingham in 1911.
Machu Picchu was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983. © 2020 AFP

The Times reported that Machu Picchu, Peru's famous site, attracted hikers and selfie enthusiasts, but the way it was built confused generations of archaeologists.
A geologist claimed to have solved the mystery of Machu Picchu and stated that it was possible to build the city on a ridge 2,430 meters above sea level, thanks to a series of broken lines, which provided construction materials.
Machu Picchu was built in the 1400s, only 76 km from the historic Inca capital of Cusco, but was a long way from the conquerors, who did not find Machu Picchu.
It may be a sacred center or a royal castle, perhaps all of them.
Machu Picchu stone blocks and mortar piles are very detailed, and even a credit card cannot be inserted into the stone cracks.
Rualdo Menegat, a scholar at the Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul in Rio Grande do Sul, says that a specific natural location gives Machu Picchu the possibility to build it. The stone block ruptures with the collision of plates, creating an unstructured area that provides ideal conditions for architecture.
He said, "The location of Machu Picchu is not a coincidence.
It is impossible to build Machu Picchu on a high mountain without a rupture of the base.
The sharp breakage makes it easy for the stone to break into the same plane, saving a lot of energy for cutting the stone. "
Menegat used satellite photographs and field survey maps to map the fractured structures and fault layers in the Machu Picchu subsoil.
Some faults are north-east-southwest, while others are north-east-southeast, intersecting in type X at the bottom of the Machu Picchu subsoil. Drawing suggests that Machu Picchu ’ s buildings are built along the fault line, just as well as an adequate and suitable stone site.
Menegat stated that the fault also helped to drain Machu Picchu during the storm, perhaps explaining why Machu Picchu was preserved to this day.

Stephen tells her that the reporter in his Washington Observer who predicted which horse would win accurately predicted the top three.

Throughout her reign, Elizabeth II managed to stay above politics and, in most cases, away from controversy.
Although she is not a Hollywood star, she is a major celebrity.

In 2012, when his mother celebrated his 60th anniversary, he would be 64 years old.
The time for my second chat with the Queen might be perfect, at a reception hosted by the Queen at St James ’ s Palace in London in June 2009. There were about 600 guests and I met the Queen with a group promoting Anglo-American friendship, the American Pilgrims Association.

But before my turn, the Queen had met many people.
I told her that I had seen her in Washington before, and I am very happy to see her again in a friendly Anglo-American setting.
"Did you come here for this interview?" she asked.
"No, my daughter is going to get married in London," I replied.
"When is the wedding?" asked the Queen.
"Fourth of July," I replied.
Then I saw once again the lively, flirting eyes flashing and flashing.

Another smile flashed, and she walked on again.

Siberian scientists found layers in Lake Zapovednoe near the place of the Tungus disaster, in which traces of space matter may be found.
This was stated by Deputy Director of the Tungusky Nature Reserve, Candidate of Biological Sciences Arthur Meydus.
Researches of the lake together with scientists of the reserve are carried out by specialists of the Institute of Nuclear Physics named after G. I. Budker, the Institute of Geology and Mineralogy named after V. S. Sobolev and the Institute of Biophysics of SB RAS.
All assumptions about the nature of the Tungus disaster that occurred in Eastern Siberia in 1908 still remain only hypotheses.
According to Meydus, the exploration of Lake Zapoveznoe gives a chance to reveal the mystery of the century.
The fact is that the bottom layers of 1908-1910 have been found, which have a lighter color and are clearly distinguished against the background of the dark thickness, which refer to other years.
They showed an increased content of potassium, titanium, rubidium, yttrium and zirconium, which may indicate a relationship with the effects of the explosion of the Tungus space body.
Now scientists intend to begin searching in light layers of microparticles of extraterrestrial origin by the method of X-ray fluorescence analysis using synchrotron radiation.
Such tandem will allow to investigate the content of elements with concentrations of up to a million percent.
And finally put an end to the controversy about the nature of the phenomenon.
According to modern models, the explosion of the Tungus object occurred at an altitude of about 8-10 km.
The wave in the epicenter, going vertically down, just cut off the branches of trees, leaving intact the trunks, and spreading to the sides - laid the forest in the area of 2 thousand square kilometers.
Comment Andrei Olkhovatov, Candidate of Physical and Mathematical Sciences, author of the book "The Tungus Phenomenon of 1908" Judging by what the media said Arthur Meydus, nothing fundamentally new in the mystery of the Tungus phenomenon has been revealed.
All this was already presented last year in Japan at the international conference "82nd Annual Meeting of Meteorite Society".
The existence of geochemical anomalies in the vicinity of the explosion has been known for half a century, but it has not been possible to reliably bind them to the remnants of the supposed cosmic body, which seems to have fallen on Tunguska.
In addition, the epicenter of the Tungus explosion is located in the centre of the Paleovolcano vent) and is adjacent to the oil and gas provinces.
So, this is a real natural storage room where there's nothing...
In fact, this was pointed out 20 years ago by the informal leader of research of the Tungus phenomenon - academician Nikolai Vladimirovich Vasiliev.
He wrote that the presence of a paleo-volcano had led to a complex biogeochemical background, when it was difficult to distinguish "cosmic" from "terrestrial".
In addition, he points out that in 1907 in Kamchatka there was a powerful eruption of the volcano Ksudach, which for a long time "rubbed" the atmosphere.
Summarizing, Vasiliev points out that "no milligram of material that could be definitively identified with the substance of the Tungus meteorite has yet been found."
Two decades have elapsed since then, and there has never been a significant new result in the search for the substance of the proposed Tungus space body.
However, in any case, new studies in Tungusk are welcome.
By the way, other researchers with an interesting program are planning to work on Tungusk this year.

Scientists found on Earth a meteorite older than the planet 2 25.07.2021, 18: 34 2,266 The age of the findings experts estimate at 4.6 billion years.
Researchers from the University of Lafborough together with colleagues from EAARO have found the oldest meteorite, which is older than the Earth, writes rusjev.net.
The age of the discovery was estimated by scientists at 4.6 billion years, with the age of the Earth being 4.54 billion years.
Today, it's the oldest meteorite found on Earth.
The meteorite was named Winchcombe by the name of the village near which it was found.
In order to study the findings, the authors used electronic microscopy, oscillatory spectroscopy and X-ray diffraction: this will provide detailed information on the crystalline structure, chemical phases and molecular interactions of the substance.
According to the authors, the meteorite consists of particles and dust, which have never been subjected to strong space collisions, that is, did not participate in the formation of protoplanetary bodies.
The internal structure is brittle and loosely bound, porous, with cracks.
Looks like he hasn't been subjected to thermal metamorphism.
That means he was there, behind Mars, untouched before any of the planets were created.
This is a rare opportunity to explore a piece of our pristine past.
The ancient meteorite is a rare example of charcoal chondrite, a cosmic rock with organic material.
The authors believe that the study of the meteorite that existed prior to the birth of life on Earth will help to understand the causes and chemistry of this process.

Employees of the National Institute of Anthropology and History of Mexico (INAH) Dr. Pedro Francisco Sánchez and archaeologist Coleta Almanza presented a new research work explaining why the ancient inhabitants of Mesoamerica built pyramids.
A summary report of the presentation of the study is published on the INAH website.
The authors of the work were 13 scientists, including from the National Autonomous University of Mexico and the Slovenian Academy of Sciences and Arts.
They analyzed known pre-Spanish objects found in Mexico.
The most significant of them are pyramids.
Scientists tried to explain the reasons why ancient people built pyramids, as well as to understand their purpose.
The explanation was quite simple.
Pyramids are located throughout Mexico.
Moreover, they are not the same, but have unique architectural elements.
Only the pyramidal structure unites them.
According to Sánchez, they are sacred copies of the mountains.
Scientists believe that the ancient inhabitants imitated the nature in which pyramidal forms were found.
For example, they could be impressed by the silhouettes of volcanoes, which were considered places where life was born.
Thus, the "copy" of the volcano could have a sacred meaning.
According to scientists, the pyramidal form is often found in nature.
For example, as a result of years of accumulation of various materials, sediments.
In addition, such a geometric shape facilitates the task of erecting a high monumental object, which was especially important for ancient people who did not even have wheels.
The paper says that the pre-Spanish societies of Mesoamerica sought not only to visually recreate the mountain, but also to lay in it a mythical, sacred flush associated with legends about the origin of life.
That is why, explorers explain, the pyramids were centers of ancient cities, erected along the central axis, relative to which all other objects were built.
Scientists agreed that the cities built around the pyramids were models - they reflected how ancient builders imagined their world.

NASA astrobiologists found traces of various sugars in meteorites, including ribose, one of the main components in genetic transcription.
The compounds were found in the Moroccan meteorite NWA 801 and in fragments of the Merchison meteorite that fell in southeastern Australia in the middle of the last century.
Although researchers have not found sugar-capable DNA, the discovery of ribose in cosmic bodies is already important — a key component of ribonucleic acid (RNA), which could have been the first in evolutionary history.

In the solar system, a dead planet in France and an international group of astronomers from Switzerland in Germany discovered the diamonds found inside the Almahata Sitta meteorite, and concluded that the celestial body was part of a destroyed protoplanet the size of Mercury or Mars, which once existed in the solar system.
The article of scientists is published in the journal Nature Comminication.
A meteorite with large diamond crystals was part of the 2008 TC3 asteroid that fell into the Nuba Desert in Sudan in 2008.
It relates to Ureilites, meteorites formed by coarse-grained rocks, olivine and piroxene.
In addition, it found a high concentration of carbon in the form of graphite and diamonds.
Scientists have estimated that more than 20 gigapascals are required to form diamonds found in Al-Mahata al-Sitta.
This indicates that crystals could not arise as a result of a collision with the surface of the Earth, but grew in the bowels of a large celestial body.
Such a body, most likely, was a planetesimal — the "embryo" of the planet.
If the protoplanet was no less than Mercury, the diamonds formed in its center.
If it was comparable in size to Mars, then the crystals were to form at the edge of the core-mantle.
According to scientists, the results of the study became the first reliable proof of the existence in the Solar System of an already disappeared planet.

According to a reporter in the field of technology, the Department of Cyberspace of the Young Journalists Club, scientists have recently discovered the oldest material on Earth.
This remains of an asteroid that hit the Earth seven billion years ago.
This space antiquity is composed of particles that were present in the galaxy before the Sun was created.
It is said that the origin of this material was a star that exploded at the time of death and that the material was sitting on a meteorite that was moving towards the Earth.
Scientists believe that the meteorite was the same type of meteorite that fell in Victoria, Australia, on 28 September 1969, weighing 100 kilograms.
The history of these antiquated particles, new analyses on meteorites, shows that the material was more than three billion years old when each hit.
It should be noted that the Sun is only about six billion and a half years old, and in fact, about 500 years before the Sun was created, this meteorite hit the Earth's formation volume.
Although the galaxy contains billions of suspended particles, no matter was discovered prior to the existence of the Sun on Earth.
This was probably due to earthquakes, volcanoes, and other changes in the Earth that had caused these particles to remain hidden inside the Earth.
At least this is the belief that Philip Heck and Robert Pritzker presented at the Nature History Exhibition.
It is said that at the time of the formation of meteorites, large amounts of particles suspended in space stick to them.
But the asteroid hit Australia, Morchison, it's a little different.
Scientists believe that these particles have almost formed the asteroid core.
They also believe that the meteorite that hit the Earth seven billion years ago was the same as Morchison, so these particles have not melted down along the way that the meteorite hit the Earth.
Most of the particles that are usually acquired by scientists are about 1 micron.
But the particles of this antique meteorite were from two to 30 microns.
Philip Heck said, "We call them rubble because we can see them with a normal microscope."

The age of star dust in the meteorite was more than 5.5 billion years. In a meteorite from Australia, scientists discovered star dust, which formed more than 5 billion years ago, long before the appearance of the Earth and the Sun.
This is reported by The Guardian.
According to scientists, this discovery made the solid material found in the meteorite, the oldest known on the planet.
The tiny granules of star dust emitted by the ancient stars give clues about how the stars were formed in the Milky Way.
The Australian meteorite accumulated star dust for billions of years that it spent in space before falling to Earth near the city of Merchison, Australia, in 1969.
"These are solid samples of stars, real star dust.
We have received the hardest materials ever discovered, and they tell us how the stars formed in our galaxy, "said Philip Heck, lead author of the research and curator of the Field Museum of Natural History in Chicago.
The meteorite contains so-called presolar grains – minerals emitted by stars at the end of their lives.
It is only now that the age of these samples has been confirmed.
To do this, the fragments of the meteorite were spread in the paste and dissolved with acid until only the grains of star dust remained.
Thus, the age of the oldest grains exceeds 5.5 billion years, while the Sun was formed 4.6 billion years ago.
Scientists also intrigued the age range of grains, most of the particles were from 4.6 to 4.9 billion years.
The discovery suggests that about 7 billion years ago, a huge number of new stars were formed in the Milky Way.
"We had more young grains than we expected.
Our hypothesis is that most of these grains were formed during the period of increased star formation, when about 50% more stars were formed than usual, "Heck added.
The discovery also confirms the observation of the mission of the European Space Agency Gaia, in which it is assumed that the enhanced star formation was caused by the merger of the Milky Way with a dwarf galaxy called Gaia-Enceladus.

The 66th U.S. Secretary of State (2005-2009), Presidential Adviser on National Security (2001-2005), Dr. Condoleeezza Rice, will take part in the 9th Yalta Annual Meeting YES.
This is stated in the press release of the Yalta annual YES meeting, transmitted by UNIAN.
Thus, on 14 September, K.Rice will open a plenary discussion on the future of global order and security.
She will then take part in the discussion, which will also be joined by EU Commissioner for Enlargement and European Neighbourhood Policy Stefan Fule; UN Deputy Secretary-General (2001-2007), Member of Parliament of India Shashi Tarur and Founder and CEO of Kaspersky Lab Evgeny Kaspersky.
On the second day of the Forum, K.Rice will take part in the energy panel, which will be held with the Deputy Prime Minister of the Russian Federation Arkady Dvorkovich; Minister of Fuel and Energy of Ukraine Yuriy Boyko; Regional Managing Director of Shell in Russia, Ukraine and the Caspian region Matthias Bixel and General Director of DTEK Maxim Timchenko.
The discussion participants will discuss the energy challenges facing Europe in the era of crisis; the development of alternative sources and energy security of the region; new models for the development of energy interaction and cooperation between the EU countries, Russia and Ukraine, as well as the impact of these factors on the transformation processes of Europe.
In addition to the former US Secretary of State, other high-level representatives of the United States will also take part in the 9th Yalta Annual Meeting.
On Friday, September 14, an interactive lunch discussion will be held on the presidential elections in the United States with the participation of the former head of administration of the 44th President of the United States (2010-2011) William Daley, as well as the 58th Speaker of the House of Representatives of the United States Newt Gingrich.
In particular, V. Daly, who headed the administration of the President of the United States Barack Obama a year ago, and N. Gingrich, who this year participated in the primaries of the Republican Party of the United States, will discuss future elections in the United States and present their vision of the future development of their country after the presidential election.
They will also discuss the possible architecture of US relations with Europe, Russia and Ukraine, as well as the vision of a new role for the US in the global world.
In the framework of the interactive discussion, the format of which will be used for the first time at the YES Forum, speakers will also give answers to the questions that will be determined by the forum participants, - said in the press release.
As UNIAN reported, the 9th Yalta annual meeting "Ukraine and the world: overcoming tomorrow's challenges together" will be held from 13 to 16 September 2012 in the Livadia Palace of Yalta.
More than 200 leading representatives of political, business and public circles from more than 20 countries of the world will discuss the main global challenges and their impact on Europe, Ukraine and the world.
The plenary part of the 9th annual Yalta meeting will open on September 14 with speeches by the President of Ukraine Viktor Yanukovych and the Prime Minister of Turkey Recep Tayp Erdoğan.
The leaders of the two countries will discuss topical issues of development of Ukraine and Turkey, as well as the future of “wide Europe ” in the twenty-first century.
The moderator of the discussion will be Alexander Kwasniewski, President of the Republic of Poland (1995-2005), Chairman of the Supervisory Board of the Yalta European Strategy (YES).
Robert Zellick, Dominique Strauss-Kan, Alexey Kudrin, Alec Ross, Eric Lander, Kairat Kelimbetov, Carl Bildt, Javier Solana and others will also take part in the panel discussions.
Ukraine in the discussions of the 9th annual Yalta YES meeting will be represented by Prime Minister of Ukraine Mykola Azarov, First Deputy Prime Minister of Ukraine Valery Khoroshkovsky; Deputy Prime Minister - Minister of Social Policy Sergey Tigipko, Minister of Economic Development and Trade of Ukraine Petro Poroshenko, Minister of Fuel and Energy of Ukraine Yuriy Boyko; Minister of Foreign Affairs Konstantin Grishchenko; Secretary of the National Security and Defense Council of Ukraine Andriy Klyuev.
The 9th Annual Meeting “Ukraine and the world: overcoming tomorrow ’ s challenges together ” is organized by the Yalta European Strategy (YES) in partnership with the Victor Pinchuk Foundation.

Inside the ancient meteorite found "fossil" ice We still can not say exactly when the meteorite Acfer 094 fell from heaven into the Algerian desert.
However, researchers have established that this oldest piece of rock is about 4.6 billion years old — about as much as the entire solar system.
In fact, it makes the meteorite a kind of time capsule.
Scientists believe that this primitive object was formed from the accretion disk of our Sun, from which the main planets were also derived.
This is very important, because in this case it is full of information about the earliest days of the Solar System and the details of its occurrence.
A team from the University of Kyoto identified previously unidentified, porous inclusions in the breed — they are widespread throughout the meteorite body.
From the outside, it looks like a sponge with tiny (not more than 1 micron in diameter) pores that slightly more red blood cells in our blood.
In combination with the mineral composition, this suggests that once in these pores microscopic ice crystals were enclosed, which have long melted.
According to the theory, the source of this meteorite is planetzimal, a kind of "grain" of the planet at the stage of its formation.
It was probably originally composed of silica grains covered with water ice.
As the asteroid grew and increased in size, it began to accumulate space dust without ice inclusions.
As a result, the entire initial years remained inside, and from above it was covered by a dense "robe".
Perhaps after that, the body approached the "snow line" of the Solar System, an area where the ice begins to sublimate from the heat of the star.
As a result, the water condensed into solid inclusions, which formed ice pores right inside the future meteorite.
After that, the object could simply fall apart, and one of them eventually went to Earth.
This theory allows us to answer a long riddle of how ice gets inside this and other meteorites that researchers find all over the globe.
A study on Acfer 094 was published in the journal Science Advances.

Publications for 24.01.17 The idea of the constant chemical composition of celestial bodies in the Solar System turned out to be a myth.
This conclusion was reached by researchers from Lund University (Sweden).
They examined 43 space objects that fell to Earth 470 million years ago and were found on the territory of Russia.
The article with the findings of scientists is published in the journal Nature.
The study was based on the discovery made in 2016 by geologist Birger Schmitz.
He discovered in one of Sweden's quarries a meteorite called Österplana 065.
The scientist described the discovered celestial body as "extinct" because of its unique chemical composition, which was not previously encountered in the study of other meteorites.
In addition, it was established that the object fell to Earth during the Ordovian period (485-443 million years ago).
This led the geologist to think that the ancient meteorites were completely different in composition.
Researchers working in the laboratory of astrogeobiology in Lund analyzed seabed deposits near the north-eastern shores of Russia.
With the help of acids, scientists dissolved several tons of sedimentary rocks to find chromium oxide grains that came to Earth as part of meteorites, writes "Lenta.ru".
Geologists conducted chemical and isotope analysis to find out what type of space bodies related to grains were.
It turned out that the ancient meteorites were really very different from the modern ones.
Scientists believe that this indicates that about 500 million years ago an event occurred in the Solar System, which affected the chemical composition of space objects.
But what exactly it was, so far, can only be guessed.
Moscow, Ivan Gridin Moscow.

In 2016, our family and friends went to Machu Picchu, Peru.
We took a three-hour train from Cusco to Machu Picchu.
The first night we stayed at a hotel in a small town in the mountains.
The next morning, we got up early, lined up for a tour to Machu Picchu Park, lined up for more than an hour, and finally got on the bus.
The coach rolled over several mountains before arriving at the entrance of Machu Picchu Park. The guide took us into the park.
Tour guide, walk and say, to introduce us to Machu Picchu history and famous attractions.
The tour guide told us that Machu Picchu was built with stone, and he also told us that the Inca Empire built Machu Picchu in 1450, a place for the Inca Empire to worship gods and nobles on holiday.
Around 1550, the Spaniards occupied the Inca Empire in search of gold, but did not find Machu Picchu.
It was not until more than 300 years later, in 1911, that an American historian discovered Machu Picchu that the present people knew about the place.
Machu Picchu shows the culture and architecture of the most powerful period of the Inca Empire.
On the summit of Machu Picchu, we can see very beautiful scenery, high mountains hidden in the clouds.
We took a lot of road, looked at different attractions, including the temple of the sun, the stone of the sun and the house of three windows, and we also saw the lovely shepherd.
Machu Picchu is majestic, its architectural masterpiece, a miracle created by the Inca Empire and named one of the seven new wonders of the world.
This is a great tourist attraction to visit.
Xu Lingkai (Irvine Chinese School, California) I love traveling because I like learning new culture, visiting new places, and trying new things.
In winter, I like to go to places that are snowy and cold; in summer, I like to go to places where the forest is dense and not hot.
I went to Vancouver, China, France, Massachusetts, New York, and Hawaii.
My favorite place is Vancouver.
It is surrounded by beautiful nature, big and interesting.
It is also close to Whistler Bike Park, a popular spot for mountain biking and skiing.
My second favorite place is New York because it has a lot of different entertainment and fun, and my grandparents live in Boston, close to New York.
In winter, I usually go to Boston and New York to visit my family, sometimes with snow.
Winter is also the best time to go to China, and I have relatives in China.
During the summer vacation, I like to go to Big Bear Mountain to ride a mountain bike and walk.
The weather is not very hot, and the scenery is very beautiful.
Anyway, I like to travel.
Whether it ’ s visiting relatives or experiencing new culture, I ’ m happy to go anywhere.

Now it is difficult to imagine that palm trees could grow beyond the polar circle, and legends describe the rich lands where the world reigned.
In ancient times, part of Russia and Scandinavia were mastered by people who enjoyed their lives until the last World Flood that brought the glacial period.
A similar situation was at the South Pole, but the space disaster quickly destroyed the legacy of hyperboreytsev. Independent researchers agree with the data of the Sumers, describing the displacement of the Earth 's poles in 12500 years.
Then, during the crust movement, all countries move to another part of the world, relative to the fixed stars.
Tomsk scientist Novgorodov believes that the glaciation of a part of the planet occurs along with warming in other places, however, according to his colleague Kondratov, the gods became guilty of the events.
They constantly perfected the Earth, causing irreversible processes, as the Bible apocryphs speak eloquently. In Syrian documents, there is a mention of extraterrestrial beings who, before the coming end of the world, destroyed their structures, and then flew to the sky to observe the development of events.
Then in the orbit of our planet revolved a huge golden house, described by Jonathan Swift, as well as information about a huge number of cities together with laboratories and workshops of humanoids can be found in the traditions of different nations. Finnish epics mention the mysterious mill of gods, but it does not belong to galaxies.
It's a fabric of the universe that can generate energy from the air.
Researchers cannot find engines in artifacts because ancestors did not need terrestrial technology.
Altai legends report large buildings with glass windows, and rarely mention wooden analogues or metal products. Perhaps, descendants of nomads during the retelling of the knowledge of ancient people could not find the appropriate images, and therefore talked about the road of crispy ice, not melting under their feet.
Taimyr was the center of the civilization of Hyperboreia and preserved the foundations of settlements that occupy a large area.
Novosibirsk researchers found an ancient metallurgical plant, which was used to work with gold and chalk.
However, many buildings have preserved the traces of the terrible destruction of great power. The Flood could only be guilty in the case of natural processes, as ancient sources mention, so for the last time the North Pole moved from Hudson Bay to its new place, where it is until now.
Then the heavy showers caused the rapid rise of water in the oceans, but now, during the flood, the lowland population is forced to move to the highlands.
One version of the experts concerns the destruction by the gods of Machu Picchu, located at a 3-kilometer altitude. There could not get the element, but only the aliens decided to destroy their creation in another way.
They collected water in a cigar-shaped UFO, after which they launched several such objects.
When three million tons of liquid fell from a height on the stone walls, they quickly collapsed.
But the northern structures on the coast of the Ice Ocean were erased from the face of the earth after a proton strike.
Such weapons struck the structures from the sky, and now the bed of the Siberian rivers kept the remains of twisted animals along with people and trees, twisted in a nuclear grinder after shelling beams. Scientists had to admit that Mohenjo Daro 5,000 years ago was a victim of nuclear battles.
But the extraterrestrial beings of 400,000 years have not used such technologies when human civilization has lived on the planet.
With the help of Google Earth, you can see a giant funnel of 100 km diameter around the complex, as well as this applies to the Popigai crater and the Kazakh Atbasar. Shumers wrote about a spacecraft equipped with such weapons that destroyed the Kaluga settlement in ancient times.
One can ask a logical question: “Why did the gods so unfairly do to the people who believe in them? ” .The aliens did not need restored temples that turned into ruins, and the Siberian finds immediately take curious researchers to the city of Labytnangi.
In ancient times, there was a secret laboratory of the gods for the reproduction of animals, preserved in the name of this place.
It was destroyed by the flood, and Dudinka also has no relation to musical instruments, because it is associated with an underground complex for rituals to the gods, where there was a huge altar. The cities of Tiksi stand in the place of the ancient gene pool of people, from where the material of the first Japanese was extracted.
In addition, even the Urals in ancient times were called the Rifei Mountains, because their peaks were seen above the water spaces.
The Babylonian Priest Belouse kept the records of the earthly rulers of extraterrestrial origin who lived 300,000 years ago.
They left clues for descendants concerning the destruction of their technologies, so that the Earthlings did not try to restore artifacts, repeating the fate of the disappeared civilizations destroyed because of curiosity.

MOSCOW, 14 Jan — RIA Novosti.
The Chilean prosecutor 's office accused the daughter-in-law of the country Michel Bachelet Nataliu Companion of fraud, reports the newspaper Comercio.
According to the publication, businessman Gonzalo Vial Koncha ordered the 33-year-old Companion company Caval to write eight reports and research works on the mining industry, for which he paid about 1.5 million dollars.
It was later revealed that the contents of the prepared works were copied from the Internet.
According to the newspaper, charges of fraud are brought not only on the sister-in-law of the president, but also on her former employee Mauricio Valero.
They are facing five years' imprisonment.
As part of the Caval investigation, Companion is also suspected of bribery and tax evasion, the publication notes.

Farah Diba ’ s life is located in a very important and sensitive period of time, since 1717, the year Farah Paa was born, is the peak years of Reza Akhani ’ s rule in Iran to go to France to study and finally, return to Iran and marry the Shah in 1338 and 19 years of partnership with the Shah in power and wealth.

Farah ’ s story is not the only fascinating story of a poor and orphan girl who, like popular stories, suddenly sat on her shoulder by the falcon of luck and found herself in the glamorous palaces of the king ’ s side. The City of the Sky - I don ’ t know how many times I have read in books and travel magazines - Machu Picchu finally set out with my wife and three old friends five years ago.
With the worldwide outbreak of COVID-19, many Hong Kong people have been stranded in other parts of the world.
On the cruise ship, in the city, in remote countries and in remote places, Hong Kong people shout.
When I saw the news that a Hong Kong man in his 60s had died of COVID-19 in Cusco, Peru, I had an involuntary twitching in my heart.
It ’ s a wonderful trip to Cusco! But what if you get sick in such a remote place? Peru is a South American highland country, and Cusco is located at an altitude of 3,360 meters above sea level.
In the plateau, air is thin, oxygen in the blood naturally drops, which can easily lead to mountain sickness.
I remember that year, I was sleeping in Cusco all night, keeping my wife from twitching!
Tourism is an indispensable part of life for many Hong Kong people.
If you have a vacation, even if you have only two or three days to work together, you can go to Taiwan, Japan, Korea, and Thailand to breathe.
Some people plan to go out once a month, and some say they want to achieve the goal of traveling to 100 countries.
I saw my friend coming back from a 42-day South American trip, less than three days later with his camera on his back. I also had a friend who was old enough to collect fruit money, but he did not meet the minimum number of days when he was staying in Hong Kong.
I am fascinated by the characteristics and tendencies of the countries of South America.
Peru, it's a place I've always wanted to visit.
In particular, Machu Picchu, the mysterious city of the sky, I do not know how many times I have read in books and travel magazines.
Finally, five years ago, my wife and three old friends left together.
We made a 20-day trip from Hong Kong to Peru, followed by two nights in Cusco, a trip to Machu Picchu, a dream city, then to the Amazon Basin, to the Amazon Forest, to the islands, and then to Puno, a visit to Lake Titicaca, a giant lake at 3815 meters above sea level; Finally, he visited Lima, the capital.
We booked the hotel online and contacted the local tour guide for reception.
From the Hong Kong airport, after more than 30 hours of flight transfer, arrived in Kusco "finally arrived!" The excitement of fatigue covered.
However, before leaving the airport, he found his heart racing, dragging his luggage for a few steps, and had to stop to support his waist to exchange air.
The thin air in the plateau has an immediate effect.
As she walked out of the airport looking for a guide, her wife had already walked one arrow to the roadside hawker stall to shop.
She took a bottle of worm-killing water and said, "Ah, bottle oxygen bottle! It 's good to buy souvenirs back to Hong Kong!"
Our guide Oscar is a native Inca lad who speaks fluent English.
He is very proud of Inca culture and is very keen to explain to us the history and culture of Peru, especially the glorious history of the Inca nation "Son of the Sun".
We only stayed in Cusco for two nights, and he said that if we didn't take the time, we might not see a winner.
So, put down your luggage and have a simple lunch, Oscar took us to Plaza de Armas and the Cathedral.
Walking in the square, I feel a little hard to walk, so when I arrive at the cathedral, I have to sit down immediately at the sight of the chair.
The main entrance of the church was equipped with chairs, pipes, and oxygen.
Three of his friends, two, did not say immediately to sit down and take up oxygen.
They said that their heads were hard to bear, their hearts were dull, and their hands and feet were powerless.
I ’ m not sick, but I ’ m tired, and I ’ m just my wife, and I ’ m just following the tour and saying to us, “OK, you ’ re sitting there and scaring! I ’ m going to talk to Oscar and tell you what you ’ ve heard. ”
It is not easy to walk back to the hotel. After each person has washed up, they plan to take a break as soon as possible after dinner.
Because the South American character is nothing but slow shooting.
The five of us ordered the meal early, but the food was delayed.
The first time in my life, I couldn't wait for food to come up and fall asleep on the dining chair.
The next day at breakfast, the wife told everyone, "Oh, well, the night Liang Wan-fu Yu went to sleep in the present day, he was suffering from altitude sickness!"
I don't know if I have altitude sickness.
Anyway, during the next day's trip, I was still taking a slow half.
Not like my wife, every time you follow Oscar and watch Sacsayhuaman, she goes back and forth to urge us to take a quick walk, otherwise the sun will not be so nice to go down the mountain.
"Oh, it's amazing, it's such a big rock, how do they move up the mountain? You have to look at the cutting points, the tights are seamless, smooth and flawless.
Modern wisdom is difficult! Can you do it faster? Hurry up! "The wife seems to be not affected by the alpine reaction at all.
On the third night, we finally arrived at the village at the foot of Machu Picchu.
The tour guide told us to sleep early after dinner. At around 5: 00 a.m., we had to queue up to Machu Picchu, and once the daily limit was full, visitors would not be able to walk up the mountain.
Everyone must obey their orders, for they have come a long way to see the dream land, the city of the sky!
I remember going to bed soon enough, but I was suddenly woken up by my wife in my sleep.
She discovers that her feet cannot help but keep shaking.
She said that at first it was only a twitch on her right foot, but soon the left foot also shook.
I asked her if she had any other discomfort, headache, vomiting, shortness of breath, fever, and so on, and she said no.
I asked her again if she had taken any drugs to prevent alpine disease from Hong Kong.
She replied: "I'm not eating!
After eating for two days, my fingers are paralyzed and I feel sick.
When I got here, I had nothing to do, and I had no food! Do I want to eat? Oh, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no
I looked at her, and I twitched every few minutes, and it was getting more and more frequent. Oh, not only her feet, her right hand began to cramp and her three limbs shook at the same time.
I am really worried, if the limbs are twitching, people will be in a coma at any time.
What is her symptom? Is it epilepsy? Is it a brain tumour? Is it not alpine disease? There is no mention of alpine symptom in all the literature about alpine disease.
Is she still conscious? I can't walk away, I can't close my eyes, only record her twitching.
Until dawn, her situation is better, I dare to inform my teammates, we can not go up the mountain with the team, more must leave the team!
My wife was really worried that I would immediately change my ticket and fly to Lima the next day before returning to Hong Kong.
I remember that afternoon, we found the only medical clinic in the village.
We tried our best to compare the condition with the doctor, but after the chicken and the duck were told to pay $50 for the consultation, we finally got only one copy of the consultation paper he signed.
However, before leaving Cusco for Lima, Oscar had to catch me on the last train to climb the mountain, and I was finally able to set foot on Machu Picchu.
It's a good trip.
As soon as the plane arrived in Lima, the wife immediately decided that the two men were self-contained and that some convulsions had disappeared.
Seeing her like this, I almost want to return to the rest of the journey for more than ten days! Of course, this is just a flash of thought, I really dare not; I fear that her cramps will start at any time.
Therefore, after returning to Hong Kong, I did not dare to slow down, and immediately arranged for my wife to do magnetic resonance, computer graphics, blood tests and so on.
Report it out: Everything's fine.
I dare to take a breath at this time.
Is the foot and hand convulsions a symptom of alpine disease?
Then I think about the symptoms of my wife.
Although physical convulsions are not a common symptom of alpine disease, her illness is closely related to the Cusco Plateau, and she has committed all taboos against alpine disease.
Therefore, I believe she suffered from alpine disease in Cusco.
So, no matter what, I decided to upload her convulsions on the relevant medical website, so that everyone can have an additional reference.
When planning travel in the future, the elevation of the area has often become one of my primary considerations.

▲ Meteor Imagination.
In a meteorite that struck Australia in 1969, scientists discovered the earliest cosmic dust dating back 7 billion years, the oldest solid matter ever discovered on Earth, a history that is 2.5 billion years earlier than our Solar System.
The meteorite was reported to have fallen in Murchison, Victoria, Australia, where a team of researchers from the United States and Switzerland obtained 40 pieces of dust known as "pre-solar grains" for analysis.
Every time a star dies, particles from a star drift into the universe and merge into a nova, which is known as the "pre-solar particle".
Philipp Heck, director of the Field Museum in Chicago and an associate professor at the University of Chicago, states that this particle is a true stellar sample.
Researchers grind meteorite fragments into powder to detect tiny particles inside.
To determine the age of the dust, scientists used a special element, neon 21, to measure its annuities.
Since cosmic rays can form new elements when they encounter matter, the longer particles are exposed to the universe, the more elements are formed, thereby determining the age.
Hecker described it as like putting a bucket in a rainstorm, through the amount of water in the bucket, to speculate how long it would be exposed in the rain.
The research team was surprised to find that some of the sun-front particles were the oldest ever discovered star dust.
The Sun formed about 4.6 billion years ago and the Earth formed 4.5 billion years ago, and these particles date from at least 4.6 to 4.9 billion years ago, the oldest dating back to 7.5 billion years ago.
According to Heck, only 10% of the particles are over 5.5 billion years old, and he believes that there may be older anterior solar particles in other meteorites, although they have not yet been discovered by humans.
Scientists say the discovery provides clues as to whether new stars form at a stable rate and whether the number of new stars varies over time.
The study was published in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.

The ancient citadel of the vanished Inca Empire, Machu Picchu, is located on a high plateau.
A team of Brazilian geologists found out why the city was built at an altitude of 2,450 meters above sea level and so it remained so well preserved.
Machu Picchu architecture has been puzzling researchers for a long time.
The buildings in the city are built of stones, but without concrete or any other fastening solutions.
The stones of ancient buildings are so tightly adjusted to each other that the bonding solution is not needed.
To build a stone city on a high-mountain plateau is at first glance a strange decision, because builders had to drag stones to the mountain, and the Incas, as you know, did not use the wheel.
But it turned out that the location of the city, on the contrary, helped in its construction.
Weak stone "The location of Machu Picchu is not an accident," says the geologist Rualdo Menegat of the Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul in Brazil. "It would be impossible to build such structures high in the mountains if there were no fractures in the rock on which the city stands."
After analyzing satellite images and data collected in the ancient city, scientists drew up a map of these fractures.
Machu Picchu was built on a site where several tectonic faults intersect.
Foto: AFP / Scanpix / LETA Two large cracks more than 100 km long go from north-east to south-west and from north-west to south-east.
There's Machu Picchu at their intersection.
Earthquakes in the region weakened the rock, and ancient quarries made it easier to cut the stones of the desired shape.
Weakened stone did not require long processing, says Brazilian geologist.
The layout of the ancient city reflects the structure of tectonic faults under it, says the researcher.
Buildings and stairs are built so that the streets go parallel to the fractures.
Moreover, the geologist claims that other Inca settlements, Ollantaytambo, Pisak and Cusco, were built on the same principles.
"Each of these settlements perfectly reflects the main directions of geological fractures in the area," says Menegat.
"On the example of Machu Picchu, we see that the Inca civilization was an empire of broken stones," says the researcher.
Water and sewerage In addition to building materials, tectonic fractures helped the Incas collect water.
The meltdown and the rainwater were striving down into the valley, along the fractures, and the Incas always knew where it could be harvested.
Foto: Reuters / Scanpix / LETA At the same time, the location of the city high in the mountains saved it from avalanches and villages frequent in this mountainous area, says a Brazilian geologist.
Another dangerous climate factor is heavy rains.
The fractures saved the city from flooding.
"About two thirds of the work on the construction of the fortress was related to the laying of an underground sewerage," says Menegat. "Thanks to the fractures, it was easier to build it.
They also explain why the city is so well preserved, "says the scientist.

Autonomous drones can shed more light on space rocks by identifying hidden locations where meteorites hit the ground.
According to ISNA and quoted by Engjet, it is easy to find large meteorites or their collision site known as "impact craters", but smaller meteorites are often neglected.
Scientists recover less than 2 percent of them.
But soon this problem will be solvable with drones.
Meteorite is a solid remnant of masses such as a comet, asteroid, or meteorite originally built in outer space and has been able to land on the surface of the Earth or another planet after passing through the atmosphere and giving feedback to the transition.
When a mass enters the atmosphere, various factors such as friction, pressure and chemical interaction with atmospheric gases cause mass warming and dispersion.
This mass is then transformed into a meteorite, and for a short time it forms a lightning bolt, which is also known as a falling star (Shahab Sagheb).
Astronomers of brilliant examples of this phenomenon are called Azargoo.
Meteors that withstand the reflectivity of the Earth ’ s atmosphere have many different sizes.
For geologists, a pyroclastic is a meteorite large enough to create an impact crater.
Each day, about 50 tons of meteorites enter the Earth's atmosphere and most of them evaporate in the layer between the mesosphere.
Meteors are mostly made of stone and metal.
When they enter the Earth's atmosphere, they cross the atmosphere over their vast expanse and leave various openings, lowlands and heights behind them.
The value of meteorites is to find the time of the Earth's creation, the Solar System, and eventually to find the time of all the cosmos's creation.
They can also be used to find the chemical structure of various parts of the planet Earth and other rocky planets.
He studied the processes that made these planets, and even the processes of making the nucleus, the first shell, the process of elemental separation in the original mantle, which is an invaluable process such as the emergence of life.
With the study of metal meteorites and seismological studies, scientists partly evaluated and measured the structure of the Earth's core.
Now, researchers have recently developed a system that has autonomous drones, and it uses machine learning technology to find smaller meteorites and their impact sites that are either hidden or impossible to access even if falling observers track them.
This technology uses a combination of ring neural networks to detect meteorites based on educational images.
These educational images help AI distinguish between space rocks and ordinary rocks.
Of course, the results of this system are not perfect.
While a test drone correctly identified the meteorites placed in certain locations, there are also some false positives in its results.
It may take some time for these drones to be reliable enough to deliver accurate results on their own.
If this technology is accurate, its implications will be significant in space science.
This system helps scientists discover and potentially recover meteorites that are too small or too far away.
This in turn can help to accurately identify the sources of meteorites and identify the compounds of these celestial rocks.
Simply put, these self-propelled drones can fill the void and gap in human understanding of the cosmic remnants that lie on Earth.
The end of the message

He was also born in Hawaii to a Protestant missionary family 100 years earlier than the series.
The young man was not interested in religion, so he decided to devote his life to science and went to study at the Massachusetts American Academy.
After that, Bingham was able to obtain a doctorate from Harvard for several years, but the scientist preferred to travel around the world, working in dusty rooms with books.
In 1906, he gave lectures to students at Yale University, but at the earliest opportunity he quickly dropped the teacher's robe and attended the Chilean Congress.
Then the hero of our history decided, after crossing the Andes, to visit the territory of Peru, where he met a local colleague, going with him to the ruins of Chokekirao.
A small city in ancient times was the golden cradle of Indian civilization and is located in the mountains, but immediately struck the American with unique monuments of Inca architecture, preserved on the terraces.
After his return, he immediately released a book dedicated to his discoveries, which was of interest to the scientific world. After the first furore, Khayram managed to convince the university council to raise funds for a scientific expedition, which was to search for the lost cities of the first inhabitants of the country.
Then the professor obtained permission from the local authorities and took with him several specialists.
The expedition made an ascent to the top of Mount Korupuna, considered to be the sanctuary of the Incas.
They believed that there was a god in the vent of the volcano, giving blessing to the brave men who had risen to his high altitude.
Bingham used the skills of mountaineering, gained in his youth, so the hike was successful.
During their conquest of the territory, the Spanish sought the lost capital of the tribe for a long time, but were unable to find it in the vicinity of Cusco.
When the group settled in a parking lot, a local resident told the Americans about the impassable paths of a village, in the depth of which ancient structures are hidden.
After receiving a decent payment for his message, he took the scientist to this place, after which the person was shocked by the sight seen.
In front of his eyes is the legendary Machu Picchu, and next to the ruins of Vilkabamba, where the last members of the family of the rulers of the country hid from the enemies.
The remains of the palaces and temples of the last capital of Vitkos were also discovered, but then the scientist did not pay attention to such an archaeological highlight. He was interested only in the first object, after which detailed maps were made for excavations.
The man had no doubt that several more times he would be at the place of sensational discovery, which happened in the future.
In addition to the old road of the tribe, a scientist during the study of caves found many valuable artifacts originating from the Bronze Age.
The real Indiana Jones was also a lucky person during the treasure hunt, because he brought 40,000 rarities from Peru along with mummies and ceramics.
It was only in 2011 that the authorities were able to return their relics back, and so far the professor dreamed of new discoveries.
Only in 1917, travel funding stopped, so he had to describe his adventures in books that became bestsellers. Britannia Mike Hedges was a descendant of the stock broker 's family, but at the age of 16 he was already traveling in Norway, and then the young man decided to visit Canada and the entire territory of South America.
It was not a regular walk for him, because the traveler dreamed of finding traces of the lost Atlantis.
He was not afraid to roam the jungle or explore the depths of the ocean with a special device.
He devoted his scientific work to huge deep-sea fish, but the real glory came to him in the 1920s.
At that time, the scientist was the head of the expedition on the territory of modern Belize and searched for traces of the Maya, burning the impassable jungle wilds along the way.
When the smoke dissipated, before the eyes of the second hero there were huge pyramids with stone steps, near which were the ruins of houses and an amphitheater.
Lubaantun was an ancient city of a disappearing civilization, so the excavations continued for several years.
During her next journey, Anna's successor noticed a strange glittering artifact lying on the remains of the altar.
The quartz skull did not even have the slightest scratch, only he had no jaw, which was then found and attached to the object. Local residents said that it was presented to people by the god Virakoch, and after analyzing the rarity, made 35,000 years ago, scientists concluded that it could belong to the Atlanteans.
Then there were no crystal deposits in the vicinity, and the Maya arrived here much later than the above time.
Many ill-wishers called the skull a forgery because of the absence of documents describing the findings of the expedition.
After this discovery, the scientist was able to continue his research, and the ancient relic still pleases tourists in the hall of the New York Museum of Indians.

Mrs TU's office was opened today to the public, and she said she had been working for Hong Kong for many years and fighting for the rights of the grassroots.
In the 1950s, she founded the Mu-kuang English College, a school that was rarely dominated by elementary students.
Seven years after her death, the school converted her former home into the TU Yesin Memorial Hall, which will be opened today.
She displayed her achievements and past campaign items, and opened her living office and rest space for public visit.
Photo: Mrs Elsie Ho was a member of the OMELCO when the British Hong Kong Government was founded in 1974 after the establishment of the Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) in Hong Kong in the 1960s. She was appointed by the Chinese Government in 1995 as a consultant to Hong Kong affairs. The school offers educational opportunities.
The school was established in 1954 to provide students with the opportunity to study as soon as possible. Before the school was completed, the school had to erect a tent to set up a temporary school. The school moved to 55 Gong Lok Road in 1972.
She died in 2015 at the age of 102. In 2019, the school plans to set up a memorial hall for her, transforming her former home into a memorial hall, which was completed this year.
The museum is divided into four parts, one part of which is the school history museum of the college, and the other part is the school history museum.
Parts 2 to 4 show her achievements, her study, and a recreation platform where fireworks were once visible in Victoria Harbour.
The platform also serves as a "classroom" for her students to tutorial and chat with.
After the death of Mrs TU, her secretary kept the study in its original form, including a photo of the Provisional Legislative Council signed by the former Chief Executive, Mr Tung Chee Hwa, and her desk, bookcase and books she read, which she had left untouched in her office.
Mrs TU's achievements include the proposal to Governor MacLehose to set up the Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC), the collection of over 20,000 signatures to protest against the fare increase of the Star Ferry, and so on.
Her promotional shoulder straps, which she ran for the Urban Council and the Legislative Council in the 1990s, were also displayed in the pavilion.
In the 1950s, Mrs TU helped the grassroots fight for their rights and raised $1 million to raise funds for the museum. Ms Leung Chao-ran, the current head of the Mu Kwong English College, said that when raising funds for the construction of the museum, a large amount of anonymous donations were attracted.
He added that since 2012, the school has been transformed into a "civilian direct education", with an annual tuition fee of just $2,000, but 60% of students are unable to pay the tuition fee.
He admits that although the treatment of teachers in the college is somewhat different from that of teachers in general, the teachers do not mind doing something meaningful for Mu-kuang.
This year marks the 65th anniversary of Muk Kwong English College. Today, the school celebrated the occasion of the opening of the TU Yat Sin Memorial Hall and the School History Museum.
Starting next month, the museum will be open for public participation through a hotline for individual or group members.
