Unfortunately, the long border with Russia will not disappear anywhere.
But there are many points that Ukrainians can control.
For the first time I visited Kiev in 1979, when Ukraine was not yet independent.
Then I had no chance to see any map or route for independent Ukraine, it was still a very Soviet space.
Only after visiting Ukraine in 2005, already as an adviser to the President on national security, I was untold surprised to see an independent country, Professor of Stanford University, 66th U.S. Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice writes on the pages of New Time.
But you should not treat independence as a proper one, you should continue to work to make your country a stable democracy.
And every time I come here, I see a lot of young people willing to dedicate themselves to it.
That's why I'm optimistic.
The most important way to preserve democracy is to avoid concentrating power in the hands of one person, as is currently the case in Hungary.
Therefore, the legislature must be strong, independent, and the judiciary must remain.
Freedom of the press and the sustainability of civil society are essential.
I am glad that in Ukraine there are politicians and citizens who understand this, which means that all this is feasible.
But not only individuals are important, institutions are important.
In creating the US constitution, the founding fathers understood that they would not be able to control who would become president or join Congress, but they created strict rules that would diminish the influence of the individual on government processes.
I think Ukraine has many friends abroad.
And, of course, the international community is helping your country.
We hope that Ukraine will come to an agreement with the IMF, which means that it will receive financial support.
But it is also important for Ukrainians to take responsibility for their own destiny into their own hands.
This is an important lesson.
No State can survive through international assistance at all times, but any State survives if it relies heavily on a society that trusts it.
Unfortunately, your long border with Russia will not disappear anywhere.
But there are a lot of things you can control, and I would focus on them.
Ukraine can do much today to strengthen its democracy and economy, strengthen its armed forces and fight corruption.
The US today is helping Ukraine with certain types of weapons so that Ukraine can defend its borders.
This is not only a sign that relations between the countries are strong, but also a recognition that the Ukrainian army is well prepared.
And defense is a good example of what you control and can improve.
Russia has committed a crime by violating international law, and this is clear to everyone today.
Ukraine is now similar to Germany, which, after World War II, was divided into two parts, but won, building strong economies and democracy.
You need to focus on this.
Fortunately, our European allies and I were able to agree on collective sanctions against Russia in response to the annexation of Crimea and actions in the Donbas.
And I think it's important to keep going.
It is important to find a way to abandon Russian gas and the North Stream.
In my opinion, creating it is not a good idea.
It is necessary to outline the scope of the permitted, providing support to the Ukrainian army.
In this regard, I support the arming of the Ukrainian military, support the deployment of highly armed NATO battalions in Poland and the Baltic States.
The Russian government has already crossed all possible red lines and deserves an adequate response.
However, it is unlikely that the US Congress will support a new package of sanctions against Rossi in the coming autumn months.
Congress is too busy preparing for the interim elections, which will last until November.
Ukrainians should understand that by the end of October all members of Congress are inside their electoral campaigns, so it is unlikely that this issue will be considered.
On the other hand, even though I am a supporter of sanctions, we must be attentive to what is happening in Russia.
We see from the protests that young Russians want a very different future for their country, and we should not forget about them and deprive them of hope.
Of course, relations between the United States and Russia are complex now, and their field is narrowed.
We will not cancel the sanctions against Russia, but we will continue to talk to the Russians in the areas on which it is important to speak, for example, on the question of North Korea.
No one is interested in North Korea having nuclear weapons capable of reaching any corner of the Earth.
And the Russians must understand that if these weapons reach Alaska, they will reach Vladivostok as well.
This is an important area of cooperation.
Similarly, with the war in Syria: we must cooperate with the Russians in resolving this situation, even if in other matters we maintain a principled line, as in the case of Crimea and Donbas.
Condoleezza Rice, New Time

Image copyright Getty Images Image caption Recently, I went to Japan and had the privilege of witnessing a ceremony.
The ceremony took place 1300 years ago in Japan and is now the 66th.
During the ceremony, the prince's concubine led a procession of priests of the Taoism to relocate objects from the old shrine to the newly built shrine.
Since the 7th century AD, the Ise Grand Shrine has been rebuilt on a prototype basis every 20 years.
The temple is one of the many rituals of Taoism.
The construction materials of the shrine can be easily damaged, and the reconstruction of the shrine can not only preserve the integrity of its characteristic buildings, but also help the next generation of craftsmen learn the construction techniques.
Japan has many of the world's oldest companies.
The country has a unique love for renovation, so it can maintain the shrine repair and ritual for thousands of years.
In these countries, many of the cultural relics and buildings created by people can be preserved for a long time.
Every piece of work can give us some inspiration.
Credit: Alamy For the past 20 years, I have worked with the Long Now Foundation to build, together with computer scientist Danny Hillis and a team of engineers, a monumental, monumental, "10,000 clocks" to alert people to long-term considerations.
Our idea is to create a clock with a large scale and a long time span that, when confronted with it, will provoke people to think about the long-term future.
We can imagine that it would be very difficult to solve such problems as climate change within five years, because the time span is too small.
But if the time span is adjusted to 500 years, even the most unlikely things are beginning to become easy to deal with.
To build a machine with a lifespan of 10,000 years, we should take into account past and present experiences and fully understand how cultural relics have been preserved.
Although we can slow down the clock, we can control the number of lattices that the pointer ticks past in 10,000 years to the length of individual human life.
But what materials should be built in thousands of years, and where should they be put? For the past 20 years, I have been studying the structures of other surviving buildings and exploring the secrets of their durability.
And do what I can to see a part of it in person.
Some sites have been preserved for reasons such as being forgotten or buried; some have been preserved in people's view because of their sheer quality; and others have applied many ingenious strategies.
Few man-made objects and tissues can last for centuries, let alone thousands of years.
Only stories, myths, religions, and a few organizations can be preserved for such a long time.
The preservation of buildings and cultural relics is not meant to be, but rather to be an accident of history.
Nuclear waste farms, genetic genealogy and seed reservoirs have been established, with the intention of setting the time span for millennia.
We have learned a lot from the experience of history and the present.
Such as materials science, engineering, and even ideological things.
When it comes to these things that inspire us to build thousands of years, I will mention a few of them.
The loss and reappearance of some of the most unique and meaningful cultural relics in history is not a deliberate attempt to preserve them.
On the contrary, it was lost and recovered by chance.
For example: Dead Sea Scrolls, Rosetta Stone, Antikythera Device, etc., could not have been preserved without the initial loss.
The Antiquicilla mechanical device is of particular significance to me, because it is also a planetary clock, and it is very advanced, leading for centuries in that era.
A 2000 year old shipwreck was discovered near Antiquicira, Greece, with a large number of oxidized gearing devices, later Antiquicilla devices.
No similar devices were found at that time.
The process of the Anticyzilla apparatus, as well as the transmission of gears and the understanding of the movement of celestial bodies, are remarkable.
It was not until 1300, when Europe began to emerge from the Middle Ages, that many ideas and mechanical principles were rediscovered.
In addition, building such a facility requires several iterations of updating.
As a result, no other similar equipment has been found, which is a deeper puzzle.
We can only explain that the device is now visible, precisely because it was lost at that time.
It was only when X-rays were used to study it that the true identity was revealed, an efficient astronomical model that, before discovering its complexity and usability, the Antiquizilla apparatus was dimmed in storage for decades.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The Antiquizilla device is an ancient mechanical device built in the 2nd century BC (Credit: Getty Images). In addition, we are also inspired by the fact that mechanical devices are more suitable for future archaeologists ’ self-recording than electronic devices.
It is not hard to imagine that, after 2000, it would be difficult to speculate on the use of our current electronic equipment in the Mediterranean Sea.
The reason why we have to build mechanical clocks is in large part because of this.
Assuming that only a few parts of the clock can remain for 10,000 years, later generations can speculate on the use of the device through its mechanical structure and, if necessary, repair it.
The predominant cities in remote areas are mobile.
A city can prosper for more than a thousand years.
However, every 10 years, the connotations of cities change.
The cultural relics cast in the city a thousand years ago, and every piece that survives, there are countless things that have not survived, destroyed by war, change, and accident.
In contrast, remote areas are more conducive to the preservation of these products.
Spectacular sites like Petra and Machu Picchu can be preserved because they are far from modern cities.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The global seed bank is located in a mountain in the Svalbard Islands, and few people visit it (Credit: Getty Images) Another advantage of the remoteness is that it adds to the mystique.
A few years ago, I went to the Global Seed Bank in the Svalbard Islands, at 78 degrees north latitude, the northernmost point of human settlement.
There is a back-up storage of the world's crop seeds, and at the time of design it was hoped that the seed bank could be used for a thousand years.
During the construction process, the designer did not realize that the seed bank would arouse the curiosity of the world.
The global seed bank was not designed to receive visits, and dignitaries came from all over the world, but were only able to visit its exterior.
And I set the time for the seed storage twice a year, when the door to the seed bank will open.
On the record of the registered visitors, I read the list of previous missions, including the names of former United States President Jimmy Carter, former United Nations Secretary-General Ban Ki-moon and others.
The remoteness of the location makes it a more curious presence.
Remote areas are not self-driving attractions.
It will take a lot of time to get to these places, and it will take a lot of time to get there.
It takes a certain amount of time to travel to these places, whether back or forth.
We have set the site for 10,000 years in West Texas, a few hours' drive from the airport, and a day's walk.
The purpose of the Wannian clock plan is to change the way people think about time.
The remote location not only gives people an expectation, but also provides a certain time to think.
The utility model has the advantages of simple structure, low cost and convenient operation.
Most of the time is spent underground.
The underground environment protects cultural relics from sunlight and usually has a very stable temperature.
High and low temperature will accelerate the process of oxidation and aging of cultural relics.
In fact, craftsmen perform rapid aging tests mainly by repeatedly lifting temperatures (which also involve chemical reactions, as discussed below).
The ornate tombs of Luxor in Egypt, the cavernous frescoes of Lascaux in the Lascaux valley of Dordogne province in south-west France, and fine art such as the Dead Sea Scrolls have been preserved underground for thousands of years.
No wonder today's global seed banks, nuclear waste disposal banks, and various archives are all built below the surface.
Image copyright The Long Now Foundation Image caption Credit: The Long Now Foundation
I have visited nuclear waste depots in the United States and Europe, and the World Seed Depot, the Mormon family tree archives, and so on.
Water cannot be prevented, as it has always been for thousands of years.
How can conflict with water be reconciled successfully?
The answer is: Stops are less rusty.
For thousands of years, the ancient Asian rice farmers have carefully drawn water, and the rice fields have proved their success in water management.
The clock must be kept not only in place, but also accurate in timing.
The metal expands and shrinks due to temperature changes, so we need a clever design to keep the length of the pendulum and other accessories in line to ensure the accuracy of the clock.
The smaller the temperature changes around the clock, the more accurate the timing will be.
However, in almost every underground storage site I visited there was a problem of water resistance.
It was therefore necessary to consider how the problem could be resolved.
Our underground base was built at the top of the hill to avoid the underground drainage area as far as possible, and even so, we think the water will seep in.
In order to avoid this possibility, we will center on the clock so that the surface is tilted around and water can flow through the bottom of the foundation without blocking it anywhere.
We can't stop the flow, but we can choose where to lead it.
Selection of materials I have consulted a number of materials scientists on what materials can create objects that will last for thousands of years.
One of them made a very convincing point of view, saying, "Everything is burning at different speeds." The aging in our eyes is actually what the scientific community calls oxidation.
It's like rusting.
Most people think of precious metals such as stones and gold, which are not easy to oxidize.
However, if the chemical environment at the time of storage is appropriate, even bodies can remain intact for thousands of years.
The mummies of Egypt are an example.
A pair of well-preserved leather shoes has been found in Armenia for more than 5,500 years.
Thus, the life span of a material is often not entirely in itself, and the environment in which it is located is even more important.
In the case of leather shoes, it was found buried in a cave covered with sheep manure, which provided a perfect oxygen-free environment and ensured temperature stability.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The pair of Mokacin soft leather shoes, which have a history of 5,500 years, are said to be the oldest leather shoes in the world (Credit: Getty Images) and we are building thousands of years of clocks to interact with people for thousands of years.
As a result, the environment is not well controlled.
The oxygen needed for human respiration degrades the material, the dust is bound to be carried on people's clothing, and the grease is covered on the skin.
Therefore, the construction of this century-old clock, accessible to all, must be durable enough in itself.
Watch bearings may be the best example.
All rotating parts of the clock require some kind of bearing surface to roll smoothly with minimal friction.
However, there are several problems with traditional bearings.
The traditional bearing is generally composed of a row of hard steel balls and two tracks (raceways) made of special shapes.
For a long time, steel and even stainless steel bearings are simply bonded together.
If the electric potential of the two bearings is different, there will also be galvanic corrosion of the metal.
If you have seen how coins are corroded on metal surfaces, this is galvanic corrosion.
In addition to these problems, common bearings need lubrication, lubrication means regular maintenance, and bearing can be contaminated with dust and gravel.
More than 20 years ago, the project was established and I started looking for suitable bearings.
We then found the perfect solution: all-ceramic bearings for satellites and spacecraft.
This kind of bearing is made of industrial ceramics with a hardness close to the diamond and can achieve permanent non-lubricating work in the vacuum environment of space.
At the time, there was only one problem with this kind of bearing: when they first knew that there was this kind of thing, they were priced tens of thousands of dollars and used only for aerospace.
However, with the advancement of the Wannian Bell project, all-ceramic bearings are becoming more and more common.
It is now also used in roller skates and fingertip gyroscopes at a cost of just $10 each.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The walls of the Taj Mahal have many treasures that can be easily taken down, helping to protect the building itself from being damaged by the Predators (Credit: Getty Images) in order to extend its useful life, as well as a strategy of sacrificing certain properties of the object itself.
We have also seen examples in nature where an attacking lizard escapes with a broken tail.
Some sites have similar conditions.
For example, in the tomb of the Egyptian Valley of Kings, the most well-preserved engraving and color paintings seem to have been completed yesterday.
Cemeteries often spend time transporting gold objects out of their graves and feel that they have scavenged their belongings and dug out all the valuable ones.
However, the wall art in the tomb remains intact.
This is rare compared to other sites.
The walls of the Taj Mahal are studded with many precious stones, which also protects the building itself.
When the robber takes out the gems, he thinks he has stolen all his wealth.
This brings an interesting question to the site of the Wannian clock.
Should we also get a "value layer" that is relatively easy to take away, and in case of theft, can protect against damage for thousands of years?
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The transformation of Afghan ideology has led to the tragedy of the destruction of the Bamiyan Buddha (Credit: Getty Images).
In recent years, the world's oldest remains have been destroyed simply because of a clash of values or ideologies.
The most heartbreaking of these is the Taliban's destruction of the Bamiyan Buddha in Afghanistan.
There is no more innocuous religious symbol than the Buddha, but it has become a threat to the Taliban, allowing them to spend weeks blowing up stunning cultural relics on the cliffs.
How, then, can we protect these priceless and culturally significant things from being stolen or destroyed?
In fact, this is the most central problem in the construction of such things in the thousands of years.
It is not difficult to solve engineering problems such as materials and operations, but human civilization is the real problem.
The civilization we hope for is a civilization that can look at the present and care for the future.
We hope that the construction of thousands of years will break down technological barriers and enhance people's moral values.
May it allow us to challenge ourselves and become the better ancestors of future generations.
Ross is the executive director of the Everpresent Foundation in San Francisco and is working with his colleagues on a project he is working on.
Visit BBC Future to read the original English text.

A man in Australia wanted to pay for an electronic detector outside in 2015. He did not expect to find a red stone because of its unique appearance. At the time, the man thought that the stone was cut open, probably a very expensive gold, and then he prepared a whole table. The device wants to cut the stone open.
But no matter what tools are used, the man cannot cut the stone, so he is prepared to take it to the museum for identification, but the end shocked the researchers.
According to Science Alert, David Hole was a man who was keen to find strange things and pay for gold when he discovered a reddish stone in Maryborough, Victoria, and even stuck some yellow dirt on the outside. It was not possible to cut the stone, but it was finally decided to take it to the Melbourne Museum for identification.
The geologist Dermot Henry, who saw the stone and thought it should be "extraordinary", cut the edge with a diamond saw and found it to be a 4.6 billion year meteorite, almost as old as Earth, but the appearance of the bump was "melted as it entered the atmosphere".
According to geologist Bill Birch, the meteorite, which weighs about 17 kg, is a common chondrite (H5) that forms at a time similar to Earth, but has a different mineral composition than Earth.
Experts have speculated that the meteorite may have come from the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter.
The asteroids in the asteroid belt occasionally collide with each other, causing rock debris to erupt into the Earth ’ s atmosphere, causing the surface to become red due to friction. The meteorite may have fallen 200 years ago and has not been severely weathered. Henry also said that the discovery of meteorites in Maryborough is rare, the second largest meteorite since the discovery of 55 kg meteorite in 2003. And worth it.

Image caption Mrs. Thatcher said, "My party will not let me lead them to the next general election, and I will not blame them."
Recent documents by the Margaret Thatcher Foundation show a more sexualized side of the Iron Lady.
Many interesting details were revealed in the memoirs of her private secretary for foreign affairs, John Coles, during her time as Prime Minister.
Two or three days after the Conservative victory in 1983, Mrs. Thatcher told Coles, "My party will not let me lead them to the next general election, and I will not blame them."
On 12 October 1984, during the Conservative Party's annual meeting in Brighton, the hotel where Mrs. Thatcher was staying was bombed by the Irish Republican Army.
The newly released document shows, from another side, Mrs. Thatcher's state of affairs.
She wrote a note to her hair stylist to cancel the hair styling scheduled for the next day.
However, she did not forget to comfort each other: "I am very happy with the way you have done your hair.
"Friday is a good day for hair styling".
Mrs. Thatcher's speech to the General Assembly was meant to be a harsh attack on the Labour Party.
But after the bomb attack, Mrs. Thatcher received hundreds of letters expressing her support and comfort, including those from the Labour Party.
This prompted Mrs. Thatcher to abandon her original speech and tone it more gently in the conference speech to "reflect the mood of the people."
At times, the thorny diplomatic problems of the Rose War were unique to the first female British Prime Minister.
After agreeing to a German horticultural association to name a rose variety in her name, Mrs. Thatcher received a complaint from a Japanese businessman.
Because six years ago, Mrs. Thatcher had agreed to let the Japanese use her name to name the rose.
Mrs. Thatcher commissioned the current British Ambassador to France, Reikot, to return a worded letter to the Japanese businessman, assuring him that the two roses were different in appearance and that Mrs. Thatcher was very fond of his masterpiece.
(Compilation: White ink: Sang Ching)

After retiring as US Secretary of State, Condoleezza Rice intends to return to teaching at Stanford University in California, reports NESru.com.
"I'll go west of Mississippi to California," she said.
In addition, Condoleezza Rice noted: "I am going to write a book on foreign policy, about the period that has been since September 11" of 2001.
I'd also like to write a book about my parents.
They were unusual people "who devoted themselves to pedagogy and the development of education".
Replying to a question about the election of Black Barack Obama as President of the United States, Condoleezza Rice stressed that she was proud that a member of the racial minority had won the presidential election.
"For me, as for an African American, this is an extraordinary event," she added.
The current Secretary of State recalled that she was born at a time when there was racial segregation in the United States.
At the same time, Condoleeezza Rice stated that the current President of the United States, George W. Bush, had one after another appointed African-American heads of American diplomacy, first Colin Powell, and then her.
"It is also extraordinary," she stressed.
According to Rice, "it was a great honor for her to represent the United States as Secretary of State."

Unexpected recognition was made by former US Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice, revealing the intrinsic aims of the US invasion of Iraq.
Political analyst, Americanist Sergei Sudakov on Sputnik radio, expressed the opinion that Rice urged Washington not to repeat mistakes.
Former US Secretary of State Condoleeezza Rice stated that the goal pursued by the US at the time of the 2003 invasion of Iraq was the overthrow of President Saddam Hussein, not the establishment of democracy in the Middle East, Sputnik reports.
"We did not enter Iraq to bring them democracy, we went there to overthrow Saddam Hussein.
It was a security issue, "Rice said.
"I would never have asked President Bush to use military power to bring democracy to Iraq and Afghanistan," she said.
Rice worked in both George Bush Jr. Administrations: she was American Secretary of State from 2005 to 2009, and served as Presidential Adviser on National Security from 2001 to 2005.
Political analyst, Americanist Sergei Sudakov on Sputnik radio, expressed the opinion that Condoleezza Rice showed some willpower in order to tell the truth.
"There were many accusations against Condoleeezza Rice by various democratic structures that she had launched a" bloody massacre in the name of democracy ".
In 2003, she said that the people of Iraq were suffering greatly and that a special regime, a special kind of democracy, should be established there.
In fact, everyone understood that the main goal of the United States, of course, was the overthrow of Saddam Hussein, so inconvenient to the administration of President George W. Bush, Jr.
Today, Condoleezza Rice, realizing that there are many attacks on the Republicans, has shown some willpower to say what she said.
It's a manly thing to do.
Because she voiced what was in the air.
She said what really happened.
You can't bring democracy on the bayonets.
That is why Condoleezza Rice says that America ’ s current goals should not repeat the mistakes made by the Bush Jr. government, ” said Sergey Sudakov.
Subscribe to the radio channel Sputnik in Telegram, so that you always have something to read: topical, interesting and useful.
And also Radio Sputnik has excellent public on VKontakte and Facebook.
And for those who love a short but capacious word, Twitter.

The big mistake is that during the pandemic many people sit and wait when everything will be like before – Boris Andresiuk (photo) Steve Jobs said: “We are here to leave a mark in the universe.
Otherwise, why are we all here? "
Now, at the time of the pandemic, these words have acquired a special meaning.
Each of us has something to think about.
Boris Andresiuk, the director of the Institute of Design and Advertising of the National Academy of Leadership of Culture and Arts, reflects on how our life can become in new realities in the project "FACTS" "World after the pandemic".
“The great psychological stress of many people has so crushed that from spring they do not leave the house ” – Boris Pavlovich, Jules Verne wrote:“ There will be a time when science will outstrip fantasy. ”
And today you can work without going to work...
"What do you think about this?" Jules Verne was a great fanatic, but I don't think even he would have been able to foresee how the world would turn in front of us for almost six months.
The new reality appeared to us as a thunder in the clear sky, so most people were not ready for it.
It is especially difficult for those who rely not on their own strength, intuition, efficiency and constant charge on the matter, but are accustomed to swimming along the stream.
These are people who live according to the principle: “Here comes tomorrow — there we will decide. ”
But it didn't come tomorrow like yesterday, and most people weren't ready for him.
And so, as much as it hurts to say, a lot of people are out of business.
How many professions in one hour turned out to be unclaimed!
A lot of people just got confused about it.
Guess and imagine the case would turn out like that, no one could.
Moreover, and now no one is ready to say how the situation will continue to develop. — What is the way out? — From my point of view, we need to reorient ourselves very quickly: to stop whining, to understand that the situation will not be better, and if it does, it will not be very soon.
Today, the biggest mistake is that many people are sitting and waiting for it to end and live like before.
But as before, there will be no more - this is a hundred percent!
The situation is different - and we need to be different: quickly adapt, change, learn.
Personally, I was struck by this fact: even experienced professors, doctors of sciences were confused.
I say to them, "Go to work, join the team, it is easier to adapt, sit down, talk, observing all the security measures."
And in response I hear more than once: “We are afraid to go outside! ”
The great psychological stress of many people has so crushed that they do not leave the house since spring.
They get food and everything they need.
Several teachers even quit, passing statements through other people.
And, you know, there's a lot of people like that -- I'm afraid even for them.
After all, especially lonely people could, by observing precautionary measures, find a spirit in communication.
In the collective, it is easier to survive any grief, and the pandemic is also a disaster, and globally.
It should be understood that excessive self-isolation, artificially reinforced by fear, can lead to serious negative psychological consequences: you can get involved in all this so that then you can not get out.
"For the first time in our life, we conducted online defense of diploma projects.
And quite successfully "- In fact, there is nothing worse than phobias...
By the way, what do you think about online education?
Many people are afraid of new realities — including distance learning format. — In spring and summer in this format, we not only held classes and examination sessions, but for the first time in our life we also conducted online defense of diploma projects!
And I have to say, quite successfully.
The students restructured very quickly, and I did not notice that such study is a burden to them.
Most people spent a lot of time online before quarantine, it's their world.
Many teachers, especially those of age, faced serious problems.
After all, what is it to read your course in an online format?
It means having good lectures, recording videos, using social media...
But the most important thing is not to lose personal contact with students, because no technique, even the most advanced, will replace it.
Soul, psychological connection is very important.
I teach myself, so I understand it well.
On the other hand, do you know what we were afraid of?

According to Mehr News Agency, from the Daily Mail, a group of researchers have discovered that if solar winds that are present within a certain period of time, they can combine free hydrogen and oxygen atoms and create water.
Scientists have long believed that meteorites contain oxygen and hydrogen.
Now, new research findings suggest the possibility of water formation on other planets other than Earth.
Researchers at the University of Cortin in Australia discovered this during experiments on a piece of meteorite that landed 50 years ago in Australia.
Katrina Milkovich of the University of Cortin Science and Technology Center says the complex process of reproducing surface water molecules is a potential mechanism for resupplying water resources on planets without other air, such as the Moon.
According to Milkovich et al., it is necessary to produce water in space to electrons such as oxygen and hydrogenin and sudden changes in temperature.
In this study, they used the Murchison meteorite sample.
The 100-kilogram meteorite landed in 1969 in Morchison Victoria, Australia.
They looked at the space rock and simultaneously studied its effects on small meteorites by simulating solar winds and using lasers.
When the celestial rocks such as meteorites and asteroids collide with each other or other celestial bodies (such as the moon), parts of their elements are crushed and dispersed.
Most of these elements are minerals with hydrogen or oxygen in them.
Researchers found that the collision between celestial bodies creates a chemical reaction in which free molecules of hydrogen and oxygen disperse.
The temperature of the combined process of these elements and solar winds reaches one million degrees Celsius, in which hydrogen and oxygen are connected.
This process successfully produces water molecules (H2O) that contain two hydrogen and one oxygen.
The research of Milkovich and his colleagues was published in the journal Nature Astronomy.

As reported by Baikal-Daily, in 2018, scientists from Yekaterinburg, Novosibirsk and Ulan-Ude told at the congress of the meteorite society in Moscow about an unusual finding made in Buryatia.
They discovered a new mineral in the iron meteorite "Wakit", found in the republic in the summer of 2016. It was then noted that the mineral was called Wakitit by the name of the area where it was found.
However, it now turned out that the new mineral was named after the Ural scientist, researcher of the Chelyabinsk meteorite Viktor Grokhovsky.
This is reported by TASS with reference to the press service of the Ural Federal University (UrFU) .- Registration of the mineral grokhovskiit - the result of joint research of employees of UrFU, Siberian and Ural branches of the Russian Academy of Sciences.
The new mineral is named after Viktor Grokhovsky, a well-known Russian meteorite researcher.
He is a professor of the Institute of Physics and Technology of UrFU, the head of meteorite expeditions of the university, - said TASS.Grokhovskiit CuCrS2 is registered in the Commission on New Minerals, Nomenclature and Classification of the International Mineralogical Association. The mineral is currently actively studied, including Siberian scientists.
Its properties can be useful in microelectronics.
It can act as a conductor, a semiconductor.
98% of the mineral consists of an iron alloy known as kamasit.
The remaining 2% are minerals formed in space.

Traces of various complex sugars, including ribose, one of the main components of genetic information, have been found inside several primitive meteorite chondrites.
Thus, these cosmic bodies could be the main suppliers of organic matter for the early Earth, the author of the material published in the scientific journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences is convinced.
According to astrobiologists, the discovery of sugars and what they could survive when they fell on our planet may indicate the participation of these substances in the birth of the first molecules of ribonucleic acids (RNA), as well as other biopolymers on Earth.
The researchers point out that all these celestial bodies are among the meteorites whose forefathers were coal-headed chondrites.
These are asteroids, which consist mostly of pure primary matter of the solar system.
They also contain a large number of organics and volatile substances.
For this reason, chondrite meteorites rarely reach the Earth in its original form.
Despite the fact that chondrites occupy planetologists and astrobiologists, still scientists can not determine exactly what substances were present in the primary matter of the Solar system and what role they were assigned to in the formation of the first "bricks of life".
Currently, two interplanetary missions are attempting to obtain answers to this question by means of samples from Ryugyu and Bennu asteroids.
Previously, scientists repeatedly stated that they found sugar in various meteorites.
But each time it turned out that there was a methodological error, or the molecules themselves fell into these celestial bodies after the fall on Earth.
Now, Japanese and American astrobiologists, when developing new methods for finding traces of extraterrestrial organics, took into account the mistakes of predecessors and found traces of complex sugars in two celestial bodies at once - in the Moroccan meteorite NWA 801 and in the fragments of the famous meteorite Merchison, which fell to south-east Australia in the middle of the last century.
In both sites, pentosis sugar molecules containing five carbon atoms and hexose were found.
All these substances contained a lot of heavy carbon-13, which proves their cosmic and abiogenic origin.
According to planetologists, their discovery suggests that asteroids played the role of the main source of organic matter for the early Earth.
However, there is such a version that sugar molecules could form and accumulate in the primary matter of the solar system without the participation of water - previously there was a hypothesis that without water it is impossible to form carbohydrates.

Many people want to know when life comes and how it is formed, so that the problem cannot be separated from where the most primitive protein comes from.
With the assistance of the most advanced technology, scientists present a 4.5 billion year meteorite shell with a magnetic iron mineral liquid molecule 3D structure.
These ancient sages retain the liquid state of the present day, or are important for the early formation of protein components – amino acids.
Researchers at the Royal Ontario Museum (ROM), an international team of scientists led by McMaster University and York University, announced on January 11 (Toronto time, Canada) a breakthrough in studying the Tagish Lake meteorite, which fell to Earth in January 2000.
Researchers at the Royal Ontario Museum (ROM), an international team of scientists led by McMaster University and York University, have recently made a breakthrough in the study of the Tagish Lake meteorite, which fell to Earth in 2000. In the new study, scientists successfully used Atom probe tomography, a 3D atom mapping technique, to map the stereoscopic spectra of single mineral atoms formed by the ancient liquid in the crust of the crater.
It is estimated that 4.5 billion years old liquids form a mineral state, which is believed to provide a key clue as to how the earliest amino acid (Amino acid) on Earth appears.
See the details of the study (click to enlarge the picture): The Solar System was full of water at the beginning of its formation.
It is generally believed that the solution brought by this water is a key element in promoting the formation of primitive amino acids (essential components of proteins) on Earth, and the subsequent birth of ancient microbes.
There is no direct evidence to prove that the chemical composition and acidity of these ancient liquids affect the evolution of life.
This sub-atomic scientific study provides important evidence that meteorite magnetite forms saturated and alkaline liquids.
These liquids provide a priority for amino acid synthesis, opening the door to microbes that emerged 4.5 billion years ago.
Beth Lymer, a PhD student at the Lassonde School of Engineering at York University and co-author of Nature, points out that amino acids are an integral part of life on Earth, but humans still have a lot of knowledge that needs to be remedied.
The study also shows that scientists may be able to understand how important molecules are synthesized and evolved to eventually form known life on Earth, as long as they control variables such as temperature and pH.
Tagish Lake's carbonaceous chondrite was recovered from the ice cap of Tagish Lake in British Columbia, Canada, in 2000 and later acquired by ROM.
It is now one of the museum ’ s chemical surface collections. (Photo Canadian Geography) A sample of the Tagish Lake meteorite used in the study was recovered from the low temperature ice lake surface and is said to have never exceeded room temperature or been exposed to liquid water.
The team was therefore confident that the liquid they measured was entirely from the meteorite.
Scientists hope to develop new methods of analysis through cutting-edge technologies such as atomic probe chromatography imaging to study alien samples brought back to Earth by spaceships, such as NASA's OSIRIS-REx mission, or other rare materials brought back from Mars sampling missions that will be launched in the near future.
Magnetite framboids in the Tagish Lake meteorite.
These spherical molecular structures indicate that they are formed in water, allowing scientists to measure the chemical properties of the solution left between particles. (Graph ROM; photo by Chi Ma) The authors believe that the solution conditions that form the formation of primordial amino acids on Earth may originate from the universe (such as falling through meteorites), or may have long been rife on Earth.
Regardless, Lee White, the leader of the research team, believes that with the help of atomic probe chromatography imaging technology, scientists have the opportunity to discover amazing things on materials that are 1,000 times smaller than human hair.
Even if only a small amount of extraterrestrial material could be brought back to each space mission, new technologies could help scientists gain a deeper understanding of the Solar System and preserve important materials for the next generation.
Source: ROM, Science Daily, Engadget

In Costa Rica, an extremely rare clay meteorite fell In late April, a unique meteorite fell in Costa Rica.
It is carbon chondrite, which means that it is more than 80% clay.
This makes it a unique "transport container", which certainly brought new savages from space to Earth — as has happened before.
Information about the unusual meteorite came when one of the families complained that the "strange stone" destroyed their dining room.
It was a chip of just under 1 kg of weight, but the impact energy was enough to easily penetrate the iron roof.
Scientists have already calculated that initially the meteorite weighed several tons and was the size of a washing machine, but in dense layers of the atmosphere fell into many fragments.
In a few days of rush searches, dozens of them were found, with a total weight of 24 kg — a small part of the entire guest from space.
Even if the rest of the debris is not soaked during the tropical rainy season, the very presence in the Earth's atmosphere, contact with oxygen, can destroy their contents.
Chondrites are extremely rare against the background of the entire number of meteorites, but are very important for science — their clay structure serves as an excellent accumulator of various particles, formations, and even traces of life.
For example, the most famous chondrite, the "meteorite from Murchison", in 1969 helped to discover an extraterrestrial species of amino acids.
Now scientists hope to get from the Costa Rican meteorite new miracles and knowledge about how the universe is built.
Most fortunate were researchers from the University of Arizona, who were the first to receive the largest and most valuable fragments.
They promise to publish the results of research as soon as possible, but they do not intend to share the finding - a unique meteorite will remain in the laboratory of the university.

The Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) legislator, Lai Shih-pao, was injured by a chair and bleeding from his head during the pull-up, according to a report by the Taiwan Freedom Times.

The Kuomintang (KMT) will vote on the results of the meeting announced by its president, Yu Shyi-kun.

The Kuomintang (Kuomintang), which is opposed to former secretary-general Chen Ju as president of the presidential palace, launched a protest against Chen Ju as president of the People's Republic of China, and prevented Chen Ju from entering the rostrum. Chen Ju made a seven-minute video on his Facebook, saying, "I am honored to serve human rights and do my best for Taiwan." With my first heart, I will spend the rest of my life protecting Taiwan and safeguarding human rights. "
Chen Ju said that she was very moved and very grateful to her for receiving many blessings from all over the world over the past few days.

Chen said that it was a pity that she had not been able to enter the Legislative Yuan that day, explaining how she had led the legislature in its transformation, set up a national human rights institution, and responded directly to many of the opposition's questions about her.

▲ In the Carmel Mountains, the Israeli Mineral Company discovered the new mineral "Carmel", which has a structure similar to that of a space meteorite, which is an unpolished stone.
(Photo / via Twitter) International Center / Synopsis reports that a mining company in Israel, Shefa Yamim, recently discovered a previously unseen mineral in the Mount Carmel in the northern part of the country, and scientists determined that the structure of the mineral was different from existing minerals, closer to outer space meteorites.
The International Association of Mineralogy (IMA) officially recognized as a new mineral and named it Carmeltazite.
Caramel has been found to be embedded in sapphire. Its main components are zirconium, aluminum and titanium oxides, and its crystal structure contains trace amounts of scandium, calcium and magnesium.
In particular, the chemical structure of Carmel differs from that of ordinary minerals, and is more similar to the Allende meteorite discovered in Mexico in 1969, whose formation on Earth dates back 65 million years, when Carmel was first discovered in the Earth's own rocks.
▲ Mount Carmel in northern Israel.
Carmel may have been formed during the Cretaceous period, and the color of new minerals produced by the high temperature and high pressure of volcanic eruptions and the mixing of lava near the Carmel Mountains is usually black, with red stripes and metallic luster.
The largest Carmel stone has been found at 33.3 carats, and geologists say it is only second to the world's toughest diamond, but may be worth more than the diamond due to its sparse quantity.
The mining company said it is not clear how large the deposits of Carmine are, and that it should be more than one place. It has been approved by the government of Israel to set up seven mining sites nearby. Since the launch of the market sale of Carmine Sapphire, known as "Carmine Sapphire", it may be used to produce high-value jewelry. There are also Internet users who say, "It's like there's a universe in it."

According to Sputnik, a group of archaeologists at the bottom of a lake near the Istaksiatl volcano in Mexico discovered the stone altar of the ancient Aztec tribe.
The shape of this temple represents a model of the universe.
This ancient site was discovered by researchers at the National Institute of Anthropology and History at a height of about four thousand meters at the bottom of the lake.
The temple is made of stones of different sizes and replaces their positions from each other of the stars and galaxies.
In addition, the enclosure is built so that when looking at a "light" effect, it appears that the stones are floating on the surface of the water, not on the lake floor.
The eptic effect and structure of this site indicate that this site represents a small model of the universe and "ancient interpretation of time and space."
This discovery also corresponds to the Aztec myth about "the Genesis of the Universe".
According to this myth in ancient times, there was no land.
One archaeologist explained that according to legend "the sea monster" Sipactli "first lived in the ocean, and then the gods of his body created the sky and the earth.

A mysterious pyramid was found in the town of Aztecs. The official website of the National Institute of Anthropology and History of Mexico (INAH) published information about a strange finding in the territory already studied.
During the conservation of the ruins of the ancient city of Tlakochkalko in an earthen mound, archaeologists discovered an unknown pyramid.
The remains of the settlement of Tlacochkalco are located in the modern Mexican city of Tlalmanalco.
It was one of the largest cities before the arrival of the Spaniards.
The mound concealed a pyramidal structure with a base size of 12 by 18 meters and 9 meters in height.
It consists of three levels.
According to experts, the pyramid had other dimensions in its original form, but now only one third of the ancient structure is preserved.
Archaeologists believe that the pyramid was built in two stages.
Initially, work was carried out on the material that would provide a decent quality of the future construction.
And since 1465, the second stage of construction, aimed at expanding the structure, began.
As a result, the base of the pyramid increased to the size of thirty-five by forty-five meters.
With the coming to power of the Aztecs of the Montezuma era, the quality of the work gradually decreased during construction, and this affected the preservation of the monument.
At the time of the conquest of the Aztecs by the Spaniards, the construction was interrupted.
The functionality of this building by scientists is still being found – whether it was a temple or a tomb, since various fragments of ceramics were found on the site by archaeologists.
Source: planet-today.ru

A small piece of stone found in a field in Gloucestershire in the west of England turned out to be an ancient meteorite.
Scientists estimate his age at 4.6 billion years.
This means that this celestial body was formed together with the Solar System.
In addition, the meteorite was older than the Earth, which is 4.54 billion years old.
According to scientists, this carbon chondrite, found by a resident of Lafborough, an employee of the Organization for Astrophysical Research of East England (EAARO), Derek Robson, flew from the asteroid belt between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter.
Scientists at the University of Lafborough are now analyzing the meteorite to determine its structure and composition.
They believe that this stone can help answer questions about the early solar system and perhaps about our own origins.

It's a overnight stay where a day and a half dollars are on the mattress.

Barks, in which their hopes for a new life, dissolve in the mud under the bodies. "I know that in the United States you can earn in two days as much as I in El Salvador received in a month.
I can repair cars, wash them.

But I'm afraid I won't be able to cross the wall, "said the Salvadoran migrant Henri Eduardo Morales.
There are gaps in the wall, and in the mountains there are trails that can not be blocked by sensors or patrols.
Trump ’ s wall problem isn ’ t that it ’ s all in holes.
It's actually finite.
This is an American patrol here now, and at night, dozens of migrants will move around the mountains. They call themselves "coyotes", guides who are taken by one of them by a famous route to spend across the border.
Dachshund — $7,000 per person.
The guarantee of success is 20%.

First, the route checks the intelligence.
See if there's a patrol or not and where the new sensors have been put.
Then we go out.
Light, no heavy backpacks - only a small stock of food and water.
On the American side, other people meet, then they lead already, "said the conductor Carlos. No matter how scared Trump compatriots gangs, who, under the guise of migrants, seek to destroy the well-being of his country, almost all illegal people in the States persecute a different dream — a salary.
And from Honduras and El Salvador, but most importantly, Mexico itself.
The 15th economy of the world with skyscrapers of the capital, mega-factories and car concern of the decade is growing almost exclusively for the sake of the American market.
The states, absorbing 80% of its oil, so planted Mexicans on this export, that they did not notice how nearly destroyed their main resource.
The reserves of black gold in Mexico remained at best for 10 years.It is good that there is something that to draw to the bottom is not under the power of any market.
Ancient pyramids of Aztecs and Maya.
Miraculously, the surviving remains of civilizations are working well for the Mexican budget.
Mysterious monuments of almost lost crops bring 20 billion tourist dollars a year.
The delayed effect of the discovery made to those to whom these 365 steps to the top allowed to pass only at the end of their lives. Without our science, the world of Maya history would only know that they built great cities.
In the middle of the last century Yuri Knorozov, not leaving the Union, without leaving the office, decrypted the ancient hieroglyphs.
Then with sadness, noticing: "To work with texts, I do not need to climb the pyramids."
He went to Mexico after all, but 40 years after his discovery.
And now in every new one on the basis of that decryption - part of his knowledge, behind which sometimes go to us. Alejandro Sesenia his candidacy for history defended in Russian.
"Before that it was impossible to read the inscriptions that are on the steels, on buildings, on codes that have a lot of information.
And now all we know about the Maya, we know thanks to Yuri Knorozov, "noted the historian.With the invaders of this land lucky a little more than New England.
The Spanish, drowning in the blood of civilization, still did not erase the peoples, leaving Mexico cities from legends, even if only as a memory of what they once were. The stone city of Teotihuacan, like a jump for thousands of years ago.
Abandoned by the builders, who survived the Aztecs and the Spanish invasion, with its great pyramids, he still reminds Mexico that it was here that was the center of the universe and here lived the Gods. In Spanish - only numbers.
There were none in Maya.
Tiny artisanal radio station, where half a hundred packages of eggs went to sound insulation, from the village of Tunkas broadcasts to the entire Yucatan.
On the peninsula, where half of the inhabitants consider themselves descendants of the indigenous people, the language of the ancestors is less and less respected. "Our language is lost.
Many people no longer remember how to pronounce words correctly.
And the kids don't know at all.
I go on air every day, so that my people do not forget the language of their ancestors, "said one of the radio presenters.
They don't have cities anymore.
But in the villages it is still possible to preserve, if not the language, at least the remnants of traditional patterns and crafts, for example, the weaving of hammocks. They really do not recognize beds.
Even chairs are rare in houses here.
Hammocks - from wall to wall.
Not exactly the usual design.
Braided to cover the whole body, like a cocoon. In the ordinary settlement of Maya, where tourists do not look at the photo shoot, Indian romance from books and movies do not breathe.
Only rare dwellings of poor people still collect from thin chakte trees, covering with branches of palm trees. "The roof usually lasts three years.
Then we have to change.
The walls will last longer.
I would not give up a house of stone, but it is expensive, and at the gathering of maize and pumpkin more than five dollars a day not to earn, "said one of the local residents. Now those Maya, who are richer, live already in stone houses.
There's electricity.
Two light bulbs give dim light.
And, of course, a hammock.
I mean, without him!
It has kids and old people swinging on it.
Women - at the fire with tortillas or with a thread at the loom.
Young healthy people are picked up by either city factories or farm fields.
Mais, corn is a Mexican gift to humanity, still, as with Maya with Aztecs, the main culture of the country, which has recently received a sensitive blow, from which it is unknown, will recover ly.Without maize, perhaps there would not be great civilizations here - with the belief that God, having crossed all other cultures, only from corn could make a decent person.
A Mexican eats half a kilogram of corn tortillas a day.
This is the real gold of Mexico, which, however, has faded a little since it turned out: more than half of the products from maize here contain signs of GMO.For a country where corn is not prayed and protected like a child, it is a killer news.
Since 2013, it has been forbidden to grow genetically modified maize in Mexico even in experimental fields.
But the GMO's loophole is still there.
There is little doubt that they broke through here with the fodder corn that Mexicans are forced to buy in the United States.
Mexico does not have a line of defense against such illegal people.
She still lacks the power to confront the world ’ s leading economy, but has always had the courage to cross the super-powers. Even when Europe lost her.
Only Mexico in 1936 decided to shelter someone who was too dangerous for both France and Norway.
The house of the most famous migrant from the Union is now a museum in the cozy metropolitan area of Koyokan, where the murder site of the famous guest will be indicated by his grandson. "I entered the house and through the open door saw the grandfather who was lying on the floor, and all around was filled with blood.
Trotsky was still alive.
I wanted to come, but he told the secretary, "Get him out.
He should not see this, "said Esteban Volkov, grandson of Lev Trotskho.Russian Esteban, aka Vsevolod, forgot finally.
On the day when an ice breaker hit the house of Leo Trotsky stopped the clock and Seva's life was 14.
He doesn't like to talk about politics, but he wears a cap with a red star.
In 92 he continues to believe that he must fully bear the truth about how his grandfather lived in exile and why he was killed. "He very much wanted to publish the work about Lenin.
But with the money we had so bad that we had to accept the offer of the American publishing house for a large fee to write a biography of Stalin.
There is no doubt that this brought his death closer, "says Esteban. At the end of his life, a fiery revolutionary and, perhaps, a failed dictator discovered the talents of a gardener and a livestock farmer.
Cactuses, which are still growing at the grave, are planted with his hands, especially he loved thin and tall, a variety of viejitos, which in Spanish means "old men".
But the main element of the farm were rabbits, which Trotsky fed personally.
Not for fun - the family had to be fed. This is also part of Mexican history, where the list of tragedies and the list of assets of the nation are equally long.
From the list, she, along with oil, may well move to the list.
Thousands of years of blue agave treatment.
Cultures that are allowed to turn into a legendary drink in only five Mexican states: often in small private factories, where it is easiest to save the technology obtained from the Aztecs, only in the last century equipped with a wheel. This technology is more than a hundred years.
Under the pressure of the stone agawa mixes with water and turns into a unique juice to taste.
It's been a long time since the big factories.
At the output, the volume of the product is negligible, which means that the value of it is as high as possible. Grinding fruits to the state of porridge, shredders of Jalisco factories with each new season swallow more and more tons of fruits similar to huge cones.
The demand for a product in the twenty-first century has increased tenfold.
Profit – at 1200% of investments.
Each piece of make-free territory is now planted by agaws, which are not always allowed to mature for the prescribed seven years.
We need to hurry up with a very expensive juice to squeeze more money.
Until the land does with them, as with oil. Once across the border with the United States, the main Mexican drink went to world markets.
Now it is this neighborhood that can lead the farmers of Jalisco to ruin.
The tequila boom in three years increased the price per kilogram of blue agave by 7 times.
In 2021, we expect the same sharp fall. "We now have six times more plants than we need to maintain at a good level and quality and prices.
But we know that in two years their harvest will overfill the market.
Prices will fall so much that manufacturers like us are unlikely to avoid bankruptcy, "said the owner of the plant Salvador Rosales.If you do not remember once lost land, the benefits of restless neighborhood in Mexico seem more than trouble.
The States, having become the main importer of the cars assembled here, have brought her to the third place in the Western Hemisphere.
Those who have settled in the United States emigrants transfer 30 billion dollars to their families a year.
America has something to put pressure on Mexico.
But it doesn't always work.
