Perhaps somewhere in the universe there are other inhabited worlds.
But until we found them, the minimum program is to prove that life outside the Earth is in some way.
How close are we to this? Recently, we are increasingly hearing about discoveries that “could indicate ” the existence of extraterrestrial life.
Only in September 2020, it became known about the discovery of phosphine gas on Venus — a potential sign of microbial life — and salt lakes on Mars, where microbes could also exist. But over the last 150 years, space researchers have repeatedly issued what they wanted for real.
There is no reliable answer to the main question.
Or is there still, but scientists are cautious by habit?
Lines in the telescope In the 1870s, Italian astronomer Giovanni Schiaparelli saw in the telescope long thin lines on the surface of Mars and declared them "canals".
The book of his discovery was explicitly entitled "Life on the Planet Mars".
"It is difficult not to see on Mars pictures similar to those that make up our terrestrial landscape," he wrote. In Italian, the word canali meant both natural and artificial ducts (the scientist himself was not sure of their nature), but when translated it lost this ambiguity.
The followers of Skiaparelli have already clearly stated the harsh Martian civilization, which in the dry climate created huge irrigation facilities. Lenin, who read the book of Percival Lowell "Mars and its canals" in 1908, wrote: "The scientific work.
It proves that Mars inhabits that the canals are a miracle of technology, that people there should be 2 / 3 times more than the local ones, plus with trunks, and are covered with feathers or animal skins, with four or six legs.... yes, our author has inflated us, describing the Martian beauties incomplete, should be according to the recipe: “The darkness of the lowest truths is more precious to us than an exalting deception. ”
Lowell was a millionaire and a former diplomat.
He was fond of astronomy and built one of the most advanced observatories in America with his money.
It was thanks to Lowell that the topic of Martian life was on the front pages of the world ’ s largest newspapers. However, already in the late 19th century, many researchers viewed the opening of “channels ” with doubt.
Observations have consistently yielded different results, with maps diverging even from Schiaparelli to Loeulle.
In 1907, biologist Alfred Wallace proved that the temperature on the surface of Mars is much lower than Lowell expected, and the atmospheric pressure is too low for the existence of water in a liquid video. The point in history with the channels was set by the interplanetary station "Mariner 9", which in the 1970s carried out a photo of the planet from space: "channels" turned out to be an optical illusion.Since the second half of the 20th century And finding a highly organized life has been reduced.
Research using spacecraft has shown that the conditions on the nearest planets are not even close to the Earth: too strong temperature drops, atmosphere without signs of oxygen, strong winds, huge pressure. On the other hand, the study of the development of life on Earth fuelled interest in finding similar processes in space.
After all, we still do not know how and thanks to which life has arisen in principle. In this direction, many events have taken place in recent years.
The main interest is the search for water, organic compounds from which protein forms of life could be formed, as well as biosignatures (substances that are produced by living beings) and possible traces of bacteria in meteorites.
Liquid proof The presence of water is a prerequisite for the existence of life as we know it.
Water acts as a solvent and catalyst for certain types of proteins.
It is also an ideal environment for chemical reactions and the transport of nutrients.
In addition, water absorbs infrared radiation, so it can retain heat - it is important for cold celestial bodies, which are quite far from the light. Observations show that water in a solid, liquid or gaseous state is on the poles of Mercury, inside meteorites and comets, as well as on Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune.
Scientists have also suggested that Jupiter's satellites Europe, Ganymede and Callisto have extensive subsurface oceans of liquid water.
They found it in some form in interstellar gas and even in such incredible places as the star photosphere. But the study of water traces can be promising for astrobiologists (specialists in extraterrestrial biology) only when there are other suitable conditions.
For example, temperatures, pressure, and chemical composition on the same Saturn and Jupiter are too extreme and changeable for living organisms to adapt to them. Another thing is the planets close to us.
Even if they look unfriendly today, they may retain small oases with "remnants of former luxury". In 2002, the Mars Odyssey orbital vehicle discovered water ice deposits under the surface of Mars.
Six years later, the Phoenix probe confirmed the results of the predecessor, receiving liquid water from an ice sample from the pole. This was consistent with the theory that liquid water was present on Mars very recently (according to astronomical measurements).
According to some data, the rains were on the Red Planet "only" 3.5 billion years ago, on the other - even 1.25 million years ago. However, immediately there was an obstacle: water on the surface of Mars can not exist in a liquid state.
At low atmospheric pressure, it immediately begins to boil and evaporate - or freezes.
Therefore, most of the known water on the surface of the planet is in a state of ice.
There was still hope that the most interesting thing was happening under the surface.
This is how the submartian salt lakes hypothesis arose.
And just the other day, it was confirmed. Scientists from the Italian Space Agency found at one of the poles of Mars a system of four lakes with liquid water, which are located at a depth of more than 1.5 kilometers.
The discovery was made with the help of radiosounding data: the device directs radio waves into the depths of the planet, and scientists from their reflection determine its composition and structure. The existence of a whole system of lakes, according to the authors of the work, suggests that for Mars this is a common phenomenon. The exact concrete concentration of salts in the Martian lakes is not yet known, as well as their composition.
According to the scientific director of the Mars program Roberto Orosei, we are talking about very strong solutions with "tens of percent" of salt. On Earth there are microbes halophyls that love high salinity, explains the microbiologist Elizaveta Bonch-Osmolovskaya.
They emit substances that help maintain water and electrical balance and protect cell structures.
But even in extremely salty underground lakes (brines) with a concentration of up to 30% of such microbes is small. According to Orosei, the Martian lakes could retain traces of the forms of life that existed when there was a warmer climate and water on the surface of the planet, and conditions resembled an early Earth.But there is another obstacle: the composition of water itself.
Martian soil is rich in perchlorates - chloric acid salts.
Perchlorate solutions freeze at significantly lower temperatures than normal or even sea water.
But the trouble is that perchlorates are active oxidants.
They contribute to the decomposition of organic molecules, which means they are harmful to microbots. Perhaps we underestimate the ability of life to adapt to the most stringent conditions.
But to prove it, you have to find at least one living cell.
"Bricks" without firing Those forms of life that live on Earth cannot be imagined without complex organic molecules containing carbon.
Each carbon atom can create up to four bonds with other atoms at the same time, giving a huge wealth of compounds.
Carbon "skeleton" is present at the basis of all organic substances - including proteins, polysaccharides and nucleic acids, which are considered the most important "bricks" of life. The hypothesis of "panspermy" just claims that life in the simplest forms came to Earth from space.
Somewhere in the interstellar space there were conditions that provided the ability to assemble complex molecules. Perhaps not in the form of a cell, but in the form of a kind of protogenome — nucleotides that are able to play and encode the information necessary for the survival of the molecule in the simplest way. For the first time, the bases for such conclusions appeared 50 years ago.
Within Marchison meteorite, which fell in Australia in 1969, molecules of uracil and xanthine were found.
These are nitrogenous bases capable of forming nucleotides, from which polymers of nucleic acids — DNA and RNA — are already formed.
The task of scientists was to determine whether these findings are a consequence of pollution on Earth, after the fall, or are of extraterrestrial origin.
And in 2008, with the help of a radiocarbon method, it was possible to establish that uracil and xanthine really formed before the meteorite fell on Earth. Now in Marcheson and similar meteorites (they are called charcoal chondrites), scientists found all kinds of bases from which both DNA and RNA are built: complex sugars, including ribose and deoxyribose, various amino acids, including essential fatty acids.
Moreover, there are signs that organic matter is formed directly in space. In 2016, with the help of the apparatus of the European Space Agency "Rosetta" it was possible to detect in the tail of the comet Gerasimenko-Churyumov traces of the simplest amino acid — glycine — and phosphorus, which is also an important component for the emergence of life.But such discoveries rather suggest how life could be brought to Earth.
Whether it can survive for a long time and develop beyond earthly conditions is still unclear.
“The large molecules, the complex molecules that we would take on Earth without options to be organic, can be synthesized in space without the participation of living beings, ” says astronomer Dmitry Vibe.“ We know that interstellar organic matter has entered the Solar System and Earth.
But there was something else going on with her — the isotope composition, symmetry, changed. "
Traces in the atmosphere Another promising way of finding life is related to bio-signatures, or biomarkers.
These are substances whose presence in the atmosphere or the ground of the planet definitely indicates the existence of life.
For example, there is a lot of oxygen in the Earth's atmosphere, which is generated by photosynthesis with the participation of plants and green algae.
It also contains a lot of methane and carbon dioxide, which produce bacteria and other living organisms in the process of gas exchange during breathing. But the detection of traces of methane or oxygen in the atmosphere (as well as water) is not yet a reason to open champagne.
For example, methane can occur in the atmosphere of star-like objects - brown dwarfs. And oxygen can be formed as a result of the splitting of water vapor under the action of strong ultraviolet radiation.
Such conditions are observed on the exoplanet GJ 1132b, where the temperature reaches 230 degrees Celsius.
Life under such conditions is impossible. For gas to be considered a biosignature, its biogenic origin must be proved, that is, it must be formed precisely as a result of the activity of living beings.
This origin of gases is indicated, for example, by their non-constancy in the atmosphere.
Observations show that on Earth methane levels fluctuate depending on the season (and the activity of living beings depends on the season). If on another planet methane then disappears from the atmosphere, then it appears (and this can be fixed for, for example, a year), then someone allocates it. One of the possible sources of "living" methane was Mars again.
Its first signs in the soil were the Viking machines, which were sent to the planet back in the 1970s — just to search for organics.
The found methane molecules in the compound with chlorine were initially perceived as evidence.
But in 2010, a number of researchers revised this point of vision. They found that the perchlorates already known to us in the Martian soil, when heated, destroy most of the organic matter.
And the samples from the Vikings were heated. In the atmosphere of Mars, traces of methane were first discovered in 2003.
The discovery immediately revived the conversation about the habitability of Mars.
The fact is that any significant amounts of this gas in the atmosphere would not have lasted long, but would have been destroyed by ultraviolet radiation.
And if methane does not destroy, scientists concluded that there is a permanent source of this gas on the Red Planet.
Still, scientists had no firm confidence: the data obtained did not exclude that the detected methane is the same "pollution". But observations obtained using the Curiosity Mars Rover in 2019 recorded an abnormal increase in methane levels.
And it turned out that now its concentration is three times higher than the level of gas recorded in 2013.
And then there was an even more mysterious thing - the concentration of methane fell again to the background values. The methane riddle still has no unambiguous solution.
According to some versions, the rover can be at the bottom of the crater, which has an underground source of methane, and its release is associated with tectonic activity of the planet. However, biosignatures may be quite unobvious.
For example, in September 2020, a team at Cardiff University found traces of phosphine gas on Venus, a special phosphorous compound that is involved in the metabolic process of anaerobic bacteria. In 2019, computer modeling showed that on planets with a solid phosphine nucleus can not be formed except as a result of the activities of living organisms.
And the amount of phosphine found on Venus spoke in favor of the fact that it is not a mistake and not a random impurity. But a number of scientists relate to the discovery with a skepticism.
Astrobiologist and reconstituted phosphorus expert Matthew Pasek suggested that there was an exotic process that was not taken into account by computer modeling.
It was he who could have taken place on Venus.
Pasek added that scientists are still not sure how life on Earth produces phosphine and whether it is produced by organisms at all.
Buried in stone Another possible feature of life, once again connected with Mars, is the presence in samples from the planet of strange structures similar to the remains of living beings.
This includes the Martian meteorite ALH84001.
It arrived from Mars about 13,000 years ago and was found in Antarctica in 1984 by snowmobile geologists in the Allan Hills area of Antarctica.
This meteorite has two features.
The first is that it is a sample of the rocks of the era of that "wet Mars", that is, the time when water could be on it.
The second is the discovery of strange structures reminiscent of fossilized biological objects.
And it turned out that they contain traces of organic matter!
However, these "petrified bacteria" have nothing to do with terrestrial microorganisms. Their size is too small for any terrestrial cell forms of life.
However, it is possible that such structures point to the precursors of life.
In 1996, David McKay of the Johnson Center of NASA and his colleagues found in the meteorite so-called pseudomorphoses — unusual crystalline structures that repeat the shape (in this case) of the biological body. Shortly after the announcement of 1996, Timothy Swindle, a planetologist from the University of Arizona, conducted an informal survey of more than 100 scientists to find out how the scientific community relates to this claim. scientists were skeptical about McKay's statements.
In particular, a number of researchers argued that these inclusions could arise from volcanic processes.
Another objection was related to the very small (nanometer) size of the structures.
However, supporters objected that nanobacteria were found on Earth as well.
There is a work that shows the fundamental indifference of modern nanobacteria from objects from ALH84001. The discussion is stalled for the same reason as in the case of Venetian phosphine: we still do not know how such structures are formed.
No one will guarantee that the similarity is not a coincidence.
Especially since there are crystals on the Earth, such as kerite, which are difficult to distinguish from the "fossilized" remains of even ordinary germs (not to mention poorly studied nanobacteria). The search for extraterrestrial life resembles fleeing behind their own shadow.
It seems that the answer to us should only come closer.
But it is moving away, turning into new difficulties and reservations.
This is how science works — the method of excluding “false positives ”.
What if the spectral analysis gave a bug?
What if methane on Mars is just a local anomaly?
What if structures like bacteria are just a game of nature?
Absolutely exclude all doubts. It is quite possible that in the universe there are constantly outbreaks of life - there and here.
And our telescopes and spectrometers and I are always late for her date.
Or, on the contrary, we come too early.
But if we believe in the Copernicus principle, which states that the universe as a whole is homogeneous and the terrestrial processes necessarily occur somewhere else, sooner or later we will cross.
It's a matter of time and technology.

In recent years, Machu Picchu, known as the "City of the Sky", has become a must-see spot for many people. Machu Picchu is located on a mountain in the Andes mountain range of more than 2,000 meters above sea level, due to its unique location and historical background. Many, for people who like to explore and visit monuments, it is very attractive!
Text: Rachel Photograph: Starry Island Photo Library The Inca site is located in Peru, South America, for 300 years.
Many of the people of Hong Kong at that time were in Cusco, which was known as the ancient capital of the Inca Empire and was an important hub leading to the "City of the Sky" Machu Picchu. These people were believed to have come from Machu Picchu because by train or bus they could reach the hot water town below Machu Picchu in less than three hours.), then take the climbing bus to the mountain "Pilgrimage".
Machu Picchu is located in the Urubamba valley, more than 2,000 meters high, surrounded by mountains and steep terrain.
This was presumed to have been built in the 15th century by the Inca Empire, but was abandoned in the mid-16th century after the Spanish invasion and extermination of the Inca Empire, and more than three hundred years later disappeared into history.
It was not until 1911 that it was discovered by American explorer Hiram Bingham, who, through his book The Lost City of the Incas, brought the city back to life. Hiram Bingham named it Machu Picchu, meaning "the ancient mountain".
As to why the Inca Empire had to build a city on high mountains in that year, there is still a wide range of views.
Since the Inca civilization did not create words, history only relies on oral tradition, which is certainly not an ordinary city, because the conditions of the mountains are not suitable for ordinary people to live in, it is inferred that this place should be a religious place, mainly used to worship Inti, the Inti of the Sun, which is revered by the Inca people.
The entire Machu Picchu site covers an area of about 13 square kilometers, which can be divided into the sacred area, the noble area inhabited by priests, and the popular area used as a civilian residence, and it is estimated that there were more than 700 priests and civilians in the peak period.
The ancient city is built with huge stone blocks, with the largest area of the sacred area, and the most precious remains such as the Sun-stone, the Sun Temple and the Three Window House.
Website: www.machupicchu.gob.pe / inicio

KIEV, 23 Mar — RIA Novosti Ukraine.
Russian researchers studied a large fragment of Seimchan meteorite, which belongs to the class of pallasites - the rarest and most beautiful variety of meteorites, they investigated it with the help of neural tomography.
According to the report, scientists from the laboratory of neutron physics of the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research (Dubna, Russia) received valuable information about the composition and spatial distribution of the substance in the meteorite without destroying the object itself.
RIA Novosti | Go to the photo bank Iron-stone meteorite Seimchan at the Urania Museum in the Moscow Planetarium.
An archival photo of Seimchan's fragment was found in 1964.
It contains all types of morphology of olive grains and large metal areas.
Thanks to neural tomography, scientists managed to construct a contrasting three-dimensional picture of meteorite components.
"The distribution of nickel in the metal matrix, the length of the metal" veins "and much more were also revealed," Izvestia writes.
The obtained information will give researchers new knowledge about the origin of the Universe and the stages of the formation of celestial bodies.
Earlier, Dutch scientists from the University of Groningen denied the hypothesis of the origin of archaea and bacteria from one single-cell ancestor through the rupture of the plasma membrane, and Israeli researchers from the University of Tel Aviv predicted global climate change that could significantly affect the duration of the summer period.

In provincial Mexico, there is a kind of free-style architecture where everyone builds as he pleases, all this happens without any projects and permits.
Description: Unusual architecture of Mexico Source: lensculture.com Alt: According to Lensculture, photographer Adam Wiseman in his project Arquitectura Libre ("Free architecture") tried to highlight the problem of modern construction in Mexico.
He has collected a large number of examples of how Mexicans build their homes without projects and norms.
Description: Unusual architecture of Mexico Source: lensculture.com Alt: Despite the absurdity of architectural forms seeming at first glance, the photographer tried to consider something more.
As he put it, "he felt the symbolism of these buildings."
Description: The unusual architecture of Mexico Source: lensculture.com Alt: So, many of the buildings show the success stories and the triumph of their owners.
In fact, the facades carry the identities of their owners to the streets of Mexican towns.
Often, the owners have enough funds to equip only the first floors of their housing.
While the upper levels have remained intact for years to come.
Description: Unusual architecture of Mexico Source: lensculture.com Alt: Previously NERUHOMI collected in their selection of the TOP of the most unusual buildings that became a symbol of a particular area.
Architects, trying to create something unusual, move away from standard norms and rules and stand out against the background of others, in the end create structures that surprise, while not always pleasant.

The authorities of Peru regretted the Japanese tourist Jesse Katayama, who was forced to stay in the country for 7 months due to restrictions on movement caused by the coronavirus pandemic.
They allowed him one of the first to visit the ancient city of the Inca Machu Picchu, a UNESCO heritage site that will be quarantined until November.
The Japanese bought a ticket to visit the sights back in March, but the coronavirus pandemic prevented him from carrying out his plan.
As a result, the tourist was stuck in Peru, in the village of Aguas Calentes in the vicinity of Machu Picchu, where only 1,600 people live, for 7 months.
He could all summer admire the capital of the Inca civilization, located at an altitude of 2,400 meters above sea level, without the opportunity to walk through the picturesque streets of the ancient city.
But Katayama was rewarded for waiting: the Peruvian authorities allowed him to be the first and only tourist to look into Machu Picchu and walk around the city without crowds of other travelers.
Jesse Katayama, a 26-year-old boxing instructor from Osaka, spent the entire quarantine in a small room he rented while traveling in Latin America.
Although the coronavirus pandemic prevented him from visiting other countries other than Peru, he still managed to visit hot springs and waterfalls, as well as Mount Putucusi, which offers a colorful view of Machu Picchu.
In his spare time, he taught local children boxing and trained to open a gym in Osaka on his return.
“I ran every day and could see Machu Picchu in detail at a distance, ” said Katayama CNN.“ I didn ’ t think I would still be able to visit the city, because it was supposed to open only next year.
But I was happy with everything. "
The Japanese began to run out of money when the tourist company Andean Roots Peru (Roads in Peru and Andes) came to help him, which helped him to agree with the Ministry of Culture of Peru on a special visit to Machu Picchu.
He was accompanied by two photographers and the director of the historical reserve José Bastante.
“The Peruvians are so kind! ” Katayama wrote on his Instagram account.“ They asked the government and the city administration, and they gave me special permission. ”
After this act of goodwill, which was told by the world media, the flow of tourists to Peru is likely to increase as soon as it becomes possible.
Machu Picchu, which was called the "New Wonder of the World" in 2007, is going to be reopened for tourists in November.
At the same time, only 30% of the average number of travelers will be allowed at regular times.
On the day, the city will be able to visit only 675 tourists in groups of eight people.
At the same time, a distance of one and a half meters should be observed.
Almost 500 years Machu Picchu was forgotten and abandoned: neither the date nor the purpose of its construction are known.
He was discovered in 1911 by American Hiram Bingham, a professor at Yale University, after the pilots saw him off the plane.
When he got here with the help of a local guide boy, he found peasants who told him that they lived there freely, without undesirable visitors, army recruits and tax collectors.
At that point in Machu Picchu was already visited by lovers of attractions, who wrote their names on granite walls with coal.
In addition to Machu Picchu, the giant geoglyphs of Nazca will open in Peru.
Images of the Nazca plateau can be viewed again from the plane, starting from November 10.
Flying over geoglyphs, the size of which reaches hundreds of meters, is a favorite entertainment for tourists coming to Peru.
And the Japanese will go home to Osaka on October 16.
In memory, the local children gave him a doll made of toilet paper and painted his portrait.
“I will cry, ” Katayama admitted.“ These 7 months have become special for me, I have discovered myself from a new side. ”

Mehr News Agency Culture Group; last year, the news of the discovery and review of an inscription was published in Semirom Castle, which contained the name "Iran".
Although pre-Islamic inscriptions and literary and geographical texts and the first centuries of the Islamic period are also full of the use of the name of Iran, this is the first time that researchers and archaeologists have been able to find an inscription belonging to the second half of the third century in which a name is given to Iran.
Also, in other inscriptions, the name of Iran may be written with other names, but in the inscription discovered from the castle Semirom is clearly and legibly mentioned the name of Iran.
Therefore, this inscription is very important and can be considered as a major discovery and reading of this inscription.
In fact, this inscription, which is historically and archetically significant, can be a decisive document in rejecting the statements of those who consider the use and existence of Iran after Islam much more recent.
The identification and reading of this inscription was carried out by Imaddin Sheikh Alkhkamai, responsible for the inscriptions and documents of the Institute of Archaeology of the University of Tehran and research expert of the Institute of Archaeology of the University of Tehran.
He read the inscription and was able to find the name of Iran from the Arabic poem written in the inscription.
With him and Cyrus Nasrollahzadeh, Associate Professor of the Department of Ancient Culture and Languages of the Institute of Linguistics and PhD of Ancient Culture and Languages, we talked about this inscription and its text and the importance of other historical inscriptions in the country, which you read: * A famous inscription that was said to have carved the name of Iran, where was discovered and how did you read it?
Sheykh al-Kamai: The fortress of Semirom is known as the fortress of stone and Bahman castle.
This castle is not unknown, but most of the archeologists have gone to it and published reports about it.
This castle is one of the top castles of Islam and, of course, some researchers have dated it before Islam.
Some researchers have also spoken about its inscriptions and even written articles.
This castle is one of the few places that show the existence of Pahlavi script and book along with Islamic inscriptions with Kofi script and ancient manuscripts up to the fourth century.
Mr. Ali Husouri published an article on the side inscription of the castle in a German magazine many years ago and used several other Arabic inscriptions to date the inscription.
The late Professor Tafazli has also cited this article in the history of pre-Islamic literature.
But there was a mistake in the history of the inscription.
Because the text of the Pahlavi inscription does not have history; the typology professor dated the Pahlavi inscription according to the Kofi inscription beside it, but he had proposed the history of the Kofi inscription a hundred years earlier.
He re-published an article, referring to about 10 inscriptions, including a prominent inscription on the rock, which he considered to be pre-Islamic.
The inscription was stolen a few years ago and was found again in March 2018 in a broken and torn form.
It seems that something happened to each of the people who stole these inscriptions.
The last person left of the group, with the notion that the inscription was a talisman, left it somewhere and told me where he had buried it.
The Cultural Heritage Office of Isfahan Province follows up and maintains it.
Years ago, one of the archaeological students had brought me pictures of the inscriptions of the castle and based on the images I had made my readings.
But to complete the task, I needed to see the original inscriptions.
Finally, in November of 98, I went to Semirom Castle and Museum to check the inscriptions.
Some of the total 27 inscriptions there are official, i.e. beginning and end and history.
A number of poems are Persian or Arabic and a number of prayers.
The subject of the inscription of the poem containing the name of Iran was discussed with the friends of the cultural heritage.
The Director of the Cultural Heritage also reflected in the news, which, of course, made mistakes in the preparation of the news.
This inscription was considered to be of the first century.
The inscription contains several verses of Arabic poetry, and there is no history in this section.
However, since the oldest inscriptions belong to the years of 265 AH to 309 AH, the history of the inscription must undoubtedly be considered between these two dates, the second half of the third century.
* What is the meaning of the poem written on the inscription?
Sheikh al-Kamai: Iranians who were called "slaves" in the first centuries of Hijri, who were also fluent in Arabic according to the conditions, were proud of their Iranian cultural past and in their poems explicitly spoke of their cultural lineage and richness and were in the mood for Iran 's return to their past.
This cultural flow of the first centuries is called the "Shaobeh Movement".
The principle of the concept of these 5 bits is the same thing, the remention and grandeur of the past history of Iran and the wish to return to that glorious era.
Unfortunately, parts of the inscription have been damaged and difficult to read.
Some words fail to know exactly where the beginning of the verse is, but since it is a poem, one or two letters of the highest prose must have been destroyed.
Given the weight and the thing that helps to read, parts of it could be more specific.
We do not know the full context and word by word, but we clearly have the words "dignity" in the first verse.
In the second verse, there is also the word "Ajam", "Malek" and "Iran".
The meaning of the second verse is: Give back the glory of the past.
In the following verses, the word "dillum and scheh-jan" is mentioned.
The other name of the city is Marv.
We have similar verses in the Shaoubieh movement that called on the ancient cities of Iran to come to help.
Here we have explicitly the name "Iran."
This inscription is written in a simple Kofi line that does not have a punctuation, and this is a problem.
Here, the form of writing letters like "N" or "R" is similar.
In this inscription, poetry rhymes with "A" and "Nun".
So the word "Iran" can be found easily in it.
Nasrollahzadeh: The oldest place in post-Islamic texts can find the name of Iran is on this inscription.
In fact, it will be the oldest use of the word "Iran".
We have it in India after that.
There are four inscriptions in a cave in the city of Mumbai, one of which is from the year 390 Yazdgardi who says they came here from Iran.
Another inscription belongs to Iranian Christians because Christianity was too much in Sassanid times.
There is also the name of Iran.
The Sassanids first introduced the word "Iran" to the meaning of Iran 's land formally into their inscriptions and administrative devices.
In Avesta, the word "Iran" is often seen.
Professor Cyrus Nasrollahzadeh * What are you supposed to do after you read this inscription?
Are you going to introduce it to others?
Sheikh al-Kamai: Everyone has a different look at the inscriptions according to the specialty he has.
The researcher does his own work and the stage of protection or introduction is entrusted to other people.
One person can't do a zero to a hundred scientific research.
I mean, that's not the essence of scientific work.
I read this inscription and tell my opinion on some issues.
Another person may see this text and prove that I have not read a word correctly or see a point in the shape of the inscription or its lines that I have not seen.
So he also raises his opinion, and the researcher brings a theoretical or a subject or evidence to confirm or correct or reject what has been said before, and this story can always continue.
But what's important is that the further we go, the closer we get to truth and accuracy and multilateral thinking.
Nasrollahzadeh: This inscription is very important because it gives us new information. It is also very important for those who work on the word Iran.
One of the reasons why the inscription is important is that because some people want to remove the name of Iran and say that there was no Iranian, this document can be used to refute their speech.
Some say that the name of Iran is mentioned in Shahnameh, but others may say that Shahnameh is a non-historical document, but on this inscription they can no longer argue.
* How should these inscriptions be kept in Iran?
Sheikh al-Kamai: Protection of specialized areas is different, but should preferably be kept in place.
Especially the scrolls that are rocky.
Nasrollahzadeh: First of all, the inscriptions should not be moved. Second, they can be molded and exposed to other people, such as the work done at the Niavaran Palace and the collection of pre-Islamic inscriptions of Iran is held together in a collection.
* What is the status of scrolls you have read or discovered?
Nasrollahzadeh: If they were ancient in protected areas, they would have a better status, such as the scrolls of Persepolis, which are preserved.
But behind the same Persepolis, there are inscriptions that cover them with a layer of chalk or tombstones that are completely gone.
Or an example that happened in the last few years and, supposing that he was in the saddle with a part of a stone inscription, something might be hidden, that stone was broken by a hammer, like the narrow polo of Bishapur.
Actually, I think, every time we see an inscription, we have to think maybe it's the last time we can see it.
Unfortunately, there is no program for documenting inscriptions or even tombstones.
It should at least be thought that if a place is destroyed either by force or error or misunderstandings, it must be documented before doing so and at least the objects and works and inscriptions and the status quo are photographed and unfortunately, in many places, the same is not done.
Sheikh al-Kamai: One of the provinces that had a significant number of stone lions was the province of Fars.
Unfortunately, there are few stone lions left in this vast province now, because some ignorant people think that there might be something valuable inside the stone and they would break them with a hammer.
Of course, in general, the status of pre-Islamic inscriptions is better than the inscriptions of the Islamic period because the number of pre-Islamic inscriptions is low and if one of them is found somewhere, a lot of attention is drawn to it, in general, they have better status than those Islamic inscriptions that went with the loader to demolish the dignity.
Ostad Amadoldin Sheikh Alkhkamai is not a week for anyone to send me a photo of an inscription or a stone and not to say that it was destroyed.
All of these inscriptions and stones are unique. There may be only four words written on an inscription about one person and nothing else remains in this world. This is the only part of the text of that inscription or tombstone.
Why do we have to eliminate these things called renovations or improvements?
Even these days I have seen that in many of the cemeteries that are still used, they replace old tombstones with new tombstones.
In the book from Astara to Starbad, the late Manuchehr Sotoudeh spoke about objects, monuments or inscriptions that are now, very optimistic, 50 percent of them are missing.
Or myself, in my early works from 65 to 69, I had taken a series of the Imams and their inscriptions in Kazeroon, and now half of them don't exist.
With limited possibilities at the time, I documented a set as much as I could do, and I published parts, but they need to be protected.
We have to inform people that this is out of the hands of one person.
Institutions that are responsible for these kinds of tasks or talk about the lack of funding or bring other reasons.
Now, unfortunately, the economic situation has become so that everyone is looking for subsoil and they don't know what they're betraying themselves and their future in spite of everything that's going on.
Iran ’ s Southern Museums are now full of Iranian stones and inscriptions because they have the financial ability to take it out of Iran or buy it and fill their museums with them.
Nasrollahzadeh: It wasn ’ t like this before, people tried to put sacred names on buildings to be less destroyed, like the Ka ’ bah of Zarathustra.
In fact, during the Islamic period, names were changed and helped to maintain them.
* I think government officials could be very good at advertising on this inscription!
Nasrollahzadeh: If its findings and information are published in other languages, foreign researchers will discuss it a lot and it will have a global reflection.
Every scientist may work on a word of it.
There is room for all 27 inscriptions there to be introduced in the form of books, and it is a discussion of why there were so many inscriptions in that castle or who were the people whose names came in the inscriptions.
Sheikh al-Kamai: The inscription was discovered and read is not the end of the work, but the beginning of the research work.
All the inscriptions that are there are connected.
Some of them may have been written for mementos, some of them may have found that they may have also had obsequious tendencies.
One word of them may indicate an accident or answer an important question for someone.
A text can be looked at and answered from a different perspective.
* Is it trying to make the research you have done on the inscriptions easier to express in order to find more audience among people?
Nasrollahzadeh: Since my main job is research and teaching at university, I have had less time to do this.
But it's possible and it's necessary to write simpler books for the general public, especially students.
For example, I know that the ancient Persian cuneiform has been published in plain language.
I accept that we have worked very little in this field, especially in ancient Iran.
People take that slice of history that they usually like and know less about other parts.
In the meantime, archaeological discoveries are more tangible to people, and they understand it better.
Sheikh al-Hakmaei: It is natural that the first task of researchers in any field is to perform specialized work.
At the university, students are actually trained and trained, each of which can reflect this in their own area and in accordance with their audience.
There are also workshops and more general programs, but I think it is the art of the media that can take the content from the experts and, in a simpler way, tell the people the important things.

Condoleezza Rice found a way to "restrain Russia" Former US Secretary of State Condolezza Rice said that Russia "crossed all possible red lines".
In order to "contain" Russia, the support of the Ukrainian army from the West is necessary, the diplomat said.
She added that she supported the deployment of NATO troops to Poland and the Baltic States, and Washington should continue to impose anti-Russian sanctions.
According to the experts, Rice is always ready to let the military “go ” in order to achieve foreign policy goals.
Former US Secretary of State Condoleeezza Rice accused Russia of "crossing all possible red lines" and violating the established boundaries of the permitted.
According to Rice, the points of no return were the "aggression" of Russia in Ukraine and the poisoning of ex-colonel of the GRU Sergei Skripal and his daughter Julia in the British city of Salisbury.
“I believe that these limits have already been crossed.
Fortunately, we [the US] and our European allies were able to agree on joint sanctions against Russia, and I think we should continue, ” said Condoleeezza Rice.
Former US Secretary of State added that in order to “deter Russia ” the Western countries need to support the Ukrainian army.
In addition, Rice called the deployment of additional NATO troops in Poland and the Baltic States “the right step ”.
According to her, the Russian authorities “deserve an adequate response ”.
According to political scientist Igor Ryabov, Condoleezza Rice belongs to the part of the neoliberal establishment of the United States, which is always ready to "waste" the military in order to achieve foreign policy goals.
“Condoleezza Rice does not know any other way to defend the interests of his country, except for the military threat on any occasion, ” the expert said.
He added that when Rice worked as a national security adviser to U.S. President George W. Bush, as well as when she was Secretary of State, she made "all possible mistakes - no US force operation had any effect."
The political scientist stressed that American officials bear no punishment for "putting the world into a series of local conflicts."
In the case of Russia, the irrelevance of this approach is that it is impossible to speak in the language of force with our country.
He added that Moscow should clearly identify its interests in the border regions.
"Do not allow interference in the affairs of the same Ukraine military advisers.
Do not allow NATO exercises in the Baltic States.
More actively work with the elites of the countries of the former USSR with a demand for their neutral status, "Ryabov added in a conversation with the FAN.
Condoleeezza Rice's words about the "red line" are an indirect quote, too, by former US President Barack Obama.
In August 2012, Barack Obama stated that the use of chemical weapons is a "red line on the sand of the desert", which will change the rules of the game of US involvement in the Syrian conflict if the Syrian authorities cross it.
As a result, however, the US not only failed to find evidence of the use of chemical weapons by Bashar Assad, but also launched a rocket “retaliation strike ” after a very long period of time.
In Washington, on the eve of the interim congressional elections, Russia remains one of the topics that even those members of the Democratic Party, whose career is far from taking off, are willing to address.
Another famous American politician, former presidential candidate and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, compared the alleged "Russian interference" in the 2016 elections with the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001 a week earlier.
The parallel, according to the politician, is that "America was attacked by another state", but in the case of Russia, "Trump did nothing."
On September 11, 2001, the largest terrorist attack occurred in the United States.
At that time, radical Islamists captured four passenger planes and sent two of them to the World Trade Center (WTC) towers in New York City.
Another passenger plane crashed into the Pentagon building, and the fourth crashed in the middle of the field after the passengers were fighting the terrorists.
The terrorists were linked to Al-Qaida (an organization banned in Russia) and its leader Osama bin Laden.
In total, nearly 3,000 people were killed in the terrorist attacks of 11 September, and some 6,300 people were injured, injured and ill.
Clinton stated that "Russia's intervention" set a precedent when the US was allegedly attacked by another state and the country's authorities did not do anything.
She added that it was very important to fully understand the situation — especially in WikiLeaks publications and the impact of Cambridge Analytica on elections.

Ku Yen Cheuk-yun: The Association of Women 's Insiders' Reconciliation and Fighting Against Domestic Violence 2018-01-14 11: 15 JJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJJ
Ku Yen-zhuan-yun, former chairman of the Federation of Women and Women's Associations, said in a statement today that she was opposed from the beginning to the end to an indefinite reconciliation with the Ministry of the Interior and the party associations, and that the premise of reconciliation must be to make clear that the Federation is not an affiliated organization of any political party, and that the property of the Federation is not unchecked by the executive units. The idea was that once the proceedings were brought, all the information was spread before a fair justice and subject to judicial and historical scrutiny, so she preferred to be replaced and not to sign an unambiguous settlement.
Ku Yen-chuo-yun said that recent rumors and comments about the association and her own people implicated that the association was willing to sign an administrative contract with the Ministry of the Interior because it was unable to face the examination.
She called on those who wish to speak out to stand up and not hide behind the media. After joining the Federation of Women and Women ’ s Associations, she continued to engage in charity activities such as labor, scholarship, relief, and so on. With dedication, good management, the assets of the Federation of Women ’ s Associations will increase without diminishing, and every penny in the Association of Women ’ s Associations will do its best to manage people ’ s responsibilities and maximize their benefits. Such smear cannot be accepted.
Ku Yen-chuo-yun argues that the Federation of Women ’ s Associations can automatically appoint four accounting firms to check the accounts, clearly explain to the public the flow of money in the operation of the Federation, and the Ministry of the Interior asked the Federation of Women ’ s Affairs to provide information on the income and expenditure of the labor force tens of years ago.
However, the financial information that the Federation of Women's Unions must keep and declare in accordance with law and practice is complete and can definitely be examined by the outside world.
She said that she was not familiar with the stock market, because she was relatively familiar with the company and had confidence in its long-term development, and believed that such investment would protect the assets of the Federation.
It has also been shown that such investments have created multiple positive gains for the Federation of Women's Associations.
The Taiwan Nai shares held by the Women's Federation account for about three thousandths of the total share, which is purely a consideration of long-term investment and financial management.
She also pointed out that the proposal of the Federation of Women and Women's Associations (FMC) has already been clearly formulated.
She was asked to give it up quickly during the negotiation process, and she refused, "How can we be in trouble when the two sides negotiate?" She and the Ministry of Home Affairs negotiated the employee care as one of the necessary conditions, and on the other hand, she clearly expressed to colleagues that if the money was frozen, she would personally be responsible for letting her colleagues recognize the preferential treatment.
Ku Yen Chuo-yun, an outsider, decided to donate a dozen donations to the Ku Family Foundation, which he criticized as insinuating murder. In fact, Ku Gong-liang Cultural Foundation sent a letter last year to the Ku Gong-liang Foundation, hoping that the Federation of Women's Associations would consider supporting the donation and work with the Ministry of Culture to build a medium-sized performance hall in the Greater Taipei area.
The objective of this donation is to build a public finance performance hall, not to donate to the operation of a single fund.
All major resolutions of the Women's Federation, including this one, must also be discussed by the Standing Committee.
The fact that the case was not passed proved the fairness and transparency of the decision making of the Federation of Women and Women.
Koo Yen-chuo-yun stressed that she was saddened by the development of the matter so far.
At the time, she withdrew her complaint in order not to allow the Women's Union to enter into a dispute.
It is a great irony to see that some people in the Federation of Women and Women have made such dishonest remarks about their former chairmen, who have been working with them for many years.
In order to defend her personal reputation and to be responsible for history, she must stand up against such injustice, and believe that the good Taiwan compatriots will support her in upholding justice and conscience and making things clear in the light of the sun.

The international team of scientists used modern laser scanning technologies to study the pyramid of the Moon, located in the Mexican Teotihuacán, one of the largest ancient cities of Mesoamerica.
As a result, a large cave of natural origin was discovered under this massive structure.
The study is published in the latest issue of The Journal of Archaeological Science, and briefly tells Ancient Origins about it.
Scientists used two new technologies that allow to "look" under the surface without excavation.
As a result, a large cave was discovered under the pyramid of the Moon in Teotihuacán.
Scientists believe it has a natural origin.
This discovery sheds new light on the history of this city.
It is known that the pyramids in it are aligned in accordance with the location of astronomical objects.
The famous Road of the Dead connected the moon pyramid, the temple of Quetzalcoatl and the pyramid of the Sun.
Moreover, under the last two objects, artificially created tunnels were previously found.
In the ancient inhabitants of Mesoamerica, the caves were mystified - they were considered entrances into the underground world, where the deceased go.
The new study describes natural voids located at a depth of 15 meters under the pyramid of the Moon.
This explains why it was in this place that Teotihuacan was built, not, say, 10 kilometers from it, where there were larger sources of water.
Now, scientists have suggested that it was the natural cave that moved the ancient inhabitants to build the pyramid of the Moon, and then the entire city.
Teotihuacan is located 40 kilometers northeast of Mexico City.
The exact date of its construction has not yet been set.
It is believed that the first settlement here arose about 600 BC.
However, the first settlers did not build monumental stone objects, which appeared much later.
For example, the Moon pyramid was erected between 100 and 450 AD and was used primarily for rituals and ceremonies, including human and animal sacrifices.
At the initial stage of existence, the population here was about six thousand people, but gradually it grew to 125 thousand.
At the time of its heyday, it was not only the largest city of pre-Columbian America, but also one of the largest cities in the world.
The study showed that the underground world of Teotihuacan was also very large.
Under it there is a whole network of caves and artificially laid tunnels.
Since the cave under the pyramid of the Moon is currently the only one of natural origin, scientists have suggested that this pyramid has become the starting point for planning the entire ancient metropolis.
In addition, the authors of the work supported the earlier hypothesis that the pyramids merely repeated the surrounding mountains in their form and symbolized the connection between the aboveground and the water underground world.

▲ Former Minister of Culture Cheng Lijun. (Photo by Li Yukang) Political Center / Taipei reports that the ruling and opposition parties have gradually laid out the 2022 local elections, and former Minister of Culture Cheng Lijun has been regarded as one of the popular candidates for the Taipei City People ’ s Progressive Party.
Cheng Li-chun did not take a positive view of the establishment of a think tank, but said that after she left office, she was constantly thinking about how to do something for Taiwan society in her civic capacity.
As for the purpose of setting up a think tank, she points out that apart from looking ahead to the future of Taiwan's youth and the next generation, it also provides a platform for people interested in public governance and a common learning platform for promoting the new thinking of democratic governance.
The magazine reported on Tuesday that Cheng Li-chun has recently reorganized the "Green Platform Foundation", where she is the chairman of the board.
In response to this, Cheng Li-chun said in her Facebook post that since she left the Ministry of Culture last year, she has reflected on her 30 years of experience outside the system and within the system, so that she can think about how she can do something for Taiwan society as a private person while accompanying her children.
Cheng says that thinking about Taiwan's future needs to be sustainable, starting with imagining the "sustainable development of the democratic community"; that is, the development of a democratic country must be carried out in a way that does not undermine the public welfare and development opportunities of the next generation. Vision.
"The future of young people is the great future of Taiwan," says Cheng Li-chun, who returned to the Youth Platform Foundation in October last year, feeling that "the future of young people is the great future of Taiwan." "Core Care".
In response to the major tasks following the transformation of the Green Platform, Cheng Li-chun pointed out that there are "social vision for the future", "promoting social dialogue", "innovative governance thinking", and "nurturing young talent."
As for the distinction between the Center for Sustainable Democracy and the Institute of Democratic Governance, Cheng said that the former has seven research platforms, including "Democratic Governance", "Warm Strength and Community", "Soft Strength and Innovative Economy", "Climate Change and Energy Governance", "Digital Transformation and Digital Governance", "Urban and Regional Governance", "Democracy Defense and International Links".
She explained that to look ahead to the future of Taiwan's young people and the next generation, it is necessary to deepen democracy, strengthen the public support system for individuals and families, and enhance social warmth.
As for the Institute of Democratic Governance, it serves as a platform for students who are interested in public governance. Through seminars, courses, workshops, and policy initiatives, the Institute will promote the new thinking of democratic governance, as well as the training of systematic public governance talents.
Cheng mentioned that many new political workers have been added to the board of directors of the Green Platform Foundation, including Hong Shen-han, Lin Fei-fan, Wu Yi-nung and Miao Boya, as well as government officials Kung Ming-hsin, Lin Min-chung and Hao Pui-chi, as well as academics Lin Chia-ho, Wu Lei, Wu Chi-min, Su Yan-tu and Fung Chiu-lan.
At the same time, she also thanked Chen Bo-chi, Zhu King-yi, Wu Nai-tak, Wu Sung, Wu Wing-yi, Chen Dong-sheng, Chen Chuan-yue, Chen Ji-ru, Ho Chan Tan, Chan Wai-hsiung, Liu Kam-tien and other 20 advanced advisors for their advice in the planning process.
In addition to the research activities of various research platforms, Cheng announced that in May this year, the Center will hold a forum on "Taiwan. Next. Sustainable Democracy".

Unusual walking is found on the ground, where to this day you can see the ruins of about five hundred mysterious ancient structures.
Most of them have a conical shape.
And in a dense masonry, no windows or doors.
Did you make the devil?
In the middle of the 18th century, the Portuguese came here.
And there are many others behind them.
And everyone made his own assumptions, put forward his own version.
Some are convinced that the ancient builders of Zimbabwe were representatives of the white race and erected all these structures in about 1,200 years B.C. purely for astronomical purposes.
Other researchers suggest that the buildings were created by some prehistoric people, like the builders of Stonehenge.
The only thing that was known for sure was that the local population called these ruins "Zimbabwe".
But none of the locals knew who, when, and why they built it all.
Since people could not write, there could be no record of the history of the region.
And the descendants of those ancient builders knew clearly only one thing - all this was created by the devil!
One of those who visited the Great Ruins of Zimbabwe was the German ethnologist and archaeologist Leo Frobenius.
And this is what he wrote: “No mortar was used.
The stone was trimmed and gave the blocks a rectangular shape.
Among the ruins were found many relics, including statuettes of Astarta, the ancient Semitic goddess of love and war, in the form of a hawk, phallic symbols of various sizes, bowls, all sorts of trinkets. "
There was a theory that Zimbabwean builders came to the south of the continent from North Africa, but no written source was found to support (or refute) this assumption.
But they found many valuable artifacts, including jewels, pearls, bracelets from Arabia, handicrafts from India, millennial Chinese porcelain...
In the citadel found birds from steatite (soapstone), installed on one-and-a-half meter stone pedestals.
A number of finds cause associations with civilizations of Ancient Egypt and pre-Columbian America.
But, it is asked, what could link Zimbabwean builders to these ancient civilizations?
Let's say the falcon is one of the first living beings to be worshipped in Egypt.
There, this bird personified the god of the Mountain, who created the sky.
His cult was spread throughout the valley of the Nile, for there Gore symbolized the sun, life, and therefore was revered as the patron saint of Pharaoh's power.
Moreover, Pharaoh was considered to be the earthly incarnation of the Mount, born of Osiris and Isis.
Figurines of birds found in Zimbabwe are clearly similar to the Egyptian god Gore.
Other researchers believe that this is not a falcon, but a steppe rash and that in Zimbabwe, bird figures were also made in honor of each ruler.
But the most important thing here is the marvellous towers.
The habitat of winged people French archaeologist and journalist Robber Charro wrote: "Among the ruins we find well-preserved, as in the Peruvian Machu Picchu, tall oval towers like silo towers, and there are no holes in their walls, as if they were inhabited only by winged creatures...
In Machu Picchu, the towers are actually called the "habitats of winged people".
And what is interesting is that such conical towers can be seen both on the Mediterranean island of Sardinia, and on the Shetland and Orkney islands near Scotland, and in many other places on the planet.
In Sardinia, these buildings, built of solid basalt and granite, were about 7 LLC.
It is a kind of monument of the Bronze Age, they are more than 3 LLC years old.
Although no one here knows their true purpose (fortress, vault, dwelling?), it is now as if an integral part of the local landscape.
The height is approximately from a three-storey building, they are not hollow: inside there are a lot of different rooms, transitions, cages, stairs, dead ends, niches, secret doors.
A to the main tower usually adjoins several (up to 18) auxiliary.
In the north of Scotland and on some adjacent islands, there are many rounded structures from 5 to 13 meters high, which arose between 100 BC and 100 AD.
Apparently, they had different assignments.
Someone thinks they're defensive structures, someone thinks they're shelters for the besieged, and someone thinks they're human and livestock.
But none of the versions is sufficiently substantiated, especially since in some towers there were deep wells or springs.
Forgotten knowledge B Tibet and the Chinese province of Sichuan also have strange ribbed towers, some with a ten-storey house.
In the southwest of China, there are more than 1 LLC of such ancient structures.
The local population does not know who, when and why they were created.
Maybe it's religious shrines, a symbol of the connection between the earth and the sky.
Maybe the forts?
Or watch, signal towers?
But then why not on the tops of the hills, but at the foot of the Himalayas, in the bottom?
And yet in different countries, all these towers usually have at least some kind of window or door.
Everywhere except Zimbabwe.
Questions arise: for what purposes were all these strange but very reliable buildings built in Zimbabwe without doors and windows, even without fighters?
Why was it necessary to make such monstrous thick walls?
What kind of winged creatures sat in such shelters?
Nobody knows that.
However, those who built these elliptical walls (ten meters thick!) and taper towers knew their job well.
And the structures that they have built have been standing for centuries as an impressive monumental testimony to the building skill and now forgotten ancient knowledge.
<! [CDATA []] > The source <! [CDATA []] > The mystery of the "star" towers of Tibet The Himalayan towers are a number of stone buildings located mainly in Tibet.
Radiocarbon analysis shows that they were built about 500 to 1200 years ago.
The height of some of them is 60 meters.
According to Science and Life, nearly twenty years ago, French traveller Michel Pessel, known for his discovery of the origins of Mekong, penetrated into inaccessible areas of Tibet and the neighbouring Chinese province of Sichuan.
In the Himalayas along the border of China, he discovered mysterious stone towers star-shaped in plan.
The Chinese authorities have only recently allowed foreigners to visit these areas.
Later, Frederick Darragon joined Peisel's research, who went to the Himalayas to study the snow leopard population, but forgot about the original purpose of the trip after she saw the towers.
Some of these mysterious structures stand in the middle of villages, others in secluded mountain valleys.
The inhabitants of the Himalayas do not know anything about the origin of the towers and some of them are used by local residents as bread for yaks and horses, in others - spontaneously there was something like cumirene - peasants bring there clay figurines as a sacrifice to powerful spirits.
But mostly these buildings are empty.
The wooden stairs, ceilings and rafters inside fell or long ago went to wood and other economic needs.
Travelling around the area is extremely difficult, as there are practically no roads.
In summer, during the rainy season, they do not allow you to pass dirt and settle down, and in winter - deep snow and the danger of avalanches.
Darragon sought help from the local Buddhist monasteries, but the monks found no records of the towers in their archives.
However, these structures are mentioned in the Chinese scientific treatises of the Ming Dynasty (1368-1644), there are records of them and in the travel diaries of some English researchers who went here in the XIX century.
But no one studied them in detail.
Over the past three years, Darragon has taken samples of 32 towers and, at her request, a radiocarbon analysis was carried out in a US laboratory to determine the age of organic materials.
Most of the towers are from 600 to 700 years old, but one of them, which is in the daytime transition from Lhasa, is from 1000 to 1200 years old.
So it was built before the Mongol tribes invaded Tibet.
However, the results of the dating can not be considered final: probably, builders used already very old wood.
As the researcher suggests, the star-shaped shape of the towers gives them seismic resistance.
Some of the towers represent 8-fined stars in the plan, while others are 12-fined.
Local residents are still building houses with sharp corners to protect against subterranean shocks.
Who built these mysterious buildings and for what - to the end is not yet clear.
It is assumed that the towers were built as watchtowers.
Some historians speak of a cult purpose: towers may symbolize a rope that, according to Tibetan legend, connects the earth with the sky.
They could also be used to expel demons.
According to one of the Taiwanese historians who traveled many of these places, the towers could serve as optical telegraph communication stations.
As a rule, each is located so that from its top you can see the tops of two adjacent towers.
They may have been lit by signal lights.
According to another version, the towers, which initially had a specific practical purpose, later became, rather, a symbol of the status, wealth of the family.
According to one legend, when a son was born in the family of the local ruler, the foundation of the tower was laid, and every year on his birthday, another floor was added to the structure.

Over the past 10 years, Vinci Wang and his partner Zhou Si-chung have explored the South American sky under the epidemic. They have travelled to Peru and Bolivia for 18 days, and have visited four great scenes.
Salar de Uyuni is the largest salt lake in the world. It has the reputation of being a mirror of the sky.
Vinci, who has always dreamed of it, said, "The best time to go to the sky is one, two, and three rainy seasons every year, because the light water on the lake becomes a mirror in the rain, so it is suffocating!"
After March, there is no mirror, only white salt, so if you don ’ t see good weather, I think it ’ s more difficult to see the sky mirror than to see the Northern Lights. "
When Vinci arrived at Salt Lake, he said, "The first feeling was overjoyed, because there was no water in the salt fields, and I couldn 't see it for more than ten minutes. At sunset, the sky is different, and we feel the beauty of nature. The world and the earth are mixed together, and we feel that human beings are really small and nothing."
Vinci said, "I have to go to a lot of places on my journey, so I have to take a lot of clothes to match the different scene, the temperature is so poor, the sky mirror is so strong because of the strong ultraviolet rays, although I have sunburnt the sun oil, but I forgot to wear my ears and neck.
In order to make the trip more memorable, Vinci not only stays at the Salt Lake Hotel, but also arranges tents and chefs to prepare a luxurious buffet lunch in the middle of Salt Lake.
Machu Picchu is the site of the Inca Empire and one of the seven wonders of the world. Built more than 2,000 meters above sea level, Vinci said: "In Machu Picchu, you can see the mountains and mountains, as if you are in the wonderland. It is mysterious and sacred. It must be a place of your life!"
Lake Titicaca is the largest lake in South America, with an elevation of more than 3,800 meters, and is the highest alpine lake in the world where large boats are navigable. The most memorable thing for Vinci is the artificial floating island in the lake, all of which are native Uros. The reed was stacked on the ground, and he took a reed boat and saw the unique floating island and aboriginal way of life, opening his eyes.
The mystery of the sky becomes more and more enthralled by the Nazca Lines, a small plane that overlooks the desert from the sky and has never been able to function.
Thank you to Vinci, the photocopier, for his life, but there was a little episode in the middle that made him sweat!
When we were in Bolivia, we suddenly stopped the electricity at the rudimentary immigration office, and the computer was not able to do anything, so we were shocked that we could not enter the country. Our passports will be copied back in place, and then the Immigration Department will keep our photocopies down for record, so let us cross the border first. "

Each of the 12 thematic sites corresponds to one of the directions of the May presidential decree and one of the national projects.
The co-chairs of the Central Staff of the All-Russian People ’ s Front distributed the curatorship over the platforms for monitoring the implementation of the May decree of the President of the Russian Federation.
This is reported by the press service of the movement after the meeting of the Central Staff.
The meeting was held with the participation of the first deputy head of the Administration of the President of Russia Sergey Kirienko in video communication with the regions.
Each of the 12 thematic sites corresponds to one of the directions of the May presidential decree and one of the national projects.
Five Co-Chairs of the Central Staff were elected at the ONF congress in November.
They were the head of the Sirius Center Elena Shmeleva, the General Director of KamAZ Sergey Kogogin, the President of the Research Institute of Emergency Pediatric Surgery and Traumatology Leonid Roshal, the member of the headquarters of the movement "Immortal Regiment" Elena Tsunaeva and the head of the organization "Russia - the country of opportunities" Alexey Komissarov.
Komissarov's core themes will be "Labour productivity and support for employment" and "Digital economy".
Kogogin will supervise "Safe and quality roads", "Small and medium business and support for individual entrepreneurial initiative", "International cooperation and export".
Shmeleva will lead the work of Science and Culture in addition to Education.
Tsunaeva will be in charge of Housing and Urban Environment and Ecology, Roshal will be in charge of Health and Demography.
Shmeleva explained that the co-chairs took the patronage over the areas closest to them.
ONF will monitor the implementation of national projects on a permanent basis, the release notes.
Thus, in 2019, more than 60 monitoring sessions are planned and about 60 surveys, more than 20 raids, more than 50 reports will be prepared, and legislative activities will continue.
The data collected and the results of the work of the regional experts of the ONF will form the basis of the annual reports on the implementation of 12 national projects, including proposals to improve their effectiveness.
ONF will send them to the Government of the Russian Federation and the Administration of the President of Russia.
Earlier it became known that the Central Staff of the All-Russian People 's Front at the meeting on Monday elected the head of the executive committee.

Lu Hsiu-lien, born in 1944, is one of the pioneers in promoting feminism in Taiwan, and is a member of the party 's external democracy movement. After her release from prison, Lu Hsiu-lien was elected to the Legislative Yuan in 1992. In March 1997, she won the Taoyuan County Governor ’ s by-election, and at the end of the year again defeated the Kuomintang Governor. In 1999, Chen Shui-bian, the Democratic Progressive Party ’ s presidential candidate, elected her deputy, and in 2000, defeated Lien Chan and Soong Chu-yu for the first time, won the Central Government. She is a member of the Green Camps, but is not the "King of Heaven".

However, if Lu Hsiu-lien ’ s true political strength is strong, it may be quite different from the actual situation. In 1992 and 1995, the second and third legislative elections were still a number of constituencies. At the end of 1996, the mayor of Taoyuan County, Liu Bangyou, was shot and killed. It's just
As for Chen Shui-bian's choice of Lu Hsiu-lien as deputy, on the one hand, it is due to the reverence of the Mei-liao generation and the sectarianism, and on the other, it is because Lu Hsiu-lien is not the "king of heaven", and does not undermine the party's factional balance, nor does she have the power to exhilate the leader.
Chen Shui-bian (left) and Lu Hsiu-lien (right), the first people in politics to feel good about themselves, were sworn in as the 10th president and vice president of the Republic of China in 2000.
The reason why Chen Shui-bian chose Lu Hsiu-lien is probably that "everyone has the right to take advantage of each other", and it is definitely not because she is "useful."

In the November 2000 issue of "New News", Lu revealed to the reporter that there was an affair between President Chen Shui-bian and Hsiao Mei-qin, director of the International Affairs Department of the DPP.
As soon as the news came out, the political scene began to rise, and Lu Hsiu-lien complained of "new news" in anger, but despite the fact that the victory was confirmed four years later, the outside world was still filled with constant whispers about the relationship between the president and the vice president as "ice."
In the 2004 319 shooting, Chen Shui-bian was injured in the abdomen by a bullet, and Lu Xiulian was shot in the knee.
This case is still highly suspected by the Blue Camp, and Chen Shui-bian's self-directed performance is about to turn the election campaign around.
In response to questions about the shooting, she denied it, but said she did not agree with the police's explanation.

In response to Lan Ying's disapproval of the election results, Lu Hsiu-lien, who once pushed for the removal of Chen Shui-bian, also questioned whether the recall vote should be aimed at the vice president at the same time.
At that time, there was also a half-joking whisper in the political arena that if Chen Shuibian was removed, it would be Lu Hsiu-lien taking over the presidency, even more unimaginable.
In short, between 2000 and 2008, the relationship between President Chen and Vice President Lui was always a subject of doubt and even ridicule. After the change of party in 2008, the Bian family burst into a scandal of sending large sums of money overseas. Lu Hsiu-lien said she had no knowledge of it, but she was really angry: "Why is she so stupid and so greedy?"
Lu Hsiu-lien has also compiled a book by the National Museum of History, which contains more than 1,300 pages of "Atypical Vice President Lu Hsiu-lien," and has not been objectionable enough to open up the old accounts, criticizing Chen Shui-bian's aides for being flooded with "Lu Group" or "Lu Group".
In 2016, Lu Hsiu-lien published a new book, "Atypical Vice President Lu Hsiu-lien", which is divided into three volumes.
Photo / United Press Department Photo Sharing Facebook's habit of political circles in Taiwan still feels that "people climb high", once they have been in high positions, it is not appropriate to go back to the lower positions, but Lu Hsiu-lien apparently does not think so.
In February 2011, she was interviewed by Apple Daily and announced that she would take part in the presidential election the following year.
She is proud to say, "The most experienced person in Taiwan is me, the only vice president in Taiwan has been me for eight years, and no vice president has been elected in the past." Of course, Tsai Ing-wen was nominated by the Democratic Progressive Party.
In October 2013, Lu Hsiu-lien announced her candidacy for mayor of Taipei City under the slogan "Restoration of the Capital".

Lu did not agree that the DPP would continue to grant him permission to run for office, saying that he was "not allowed to run for office".
On February 1 this year, Lu Hsiu-lien once again announced her candidacy for Taipei Mayor, saying that she respected the decision of the DPP to support Ke Wen-chul, but four years later, most of the party did not approve of the renewal of the concession.
In addition to emphasizing the experience of serving as county governor and vice president, 74-year-old Lu Hsiu-lien also believes that she is getting older and younger, and that "the mental and spiritual state is not as young as the young, and the young should hold hands, not be cut and opposed from generation to generation," and she wants to represent the older generation who have fought for Taiwan.
Lui Hsiu-lien, who has never mind the derision of the "Lui Hou" atmosphere, has been fighting against male authority and the Kuomintang dictatorship, and has developed a feeling of "feeling good for herself" that no one can reach.
