Will Lu Hsiu-lien become the "new traitor" of the Communist Party, just like the "11 Coroners" of that year? Whether or not the answer is correct or not, it is more certain that Lu Shu-lien does not care at all whether he is regarded as a "corpse" or not.

The copyright holder of EPFL / Hillary Sanctuary Image caption This collection of fragments received the general name of Almahata Sitta The asteroid, inside which a diamond placer was found, exploded in the Earth's atmosphere in 2008.
The study of its fragments showed that the celestial body was a fragment of the protoplanet that emerged in the early stages of the solar system.
This planet probably formed billions of years ago and had the size of modern Mars or Mercury, but was destroyed as a result of a collision with another celestial body.
This article is published in the journal Nature Communications.
The authors point out that the pressure required for the formation of diamonds of this type could exist only in the depths of a massive planet.
Using three different types of microscopy, researchers obtained data on the mineral and chemical composition of fragments of a small asteroid called 2008 TC3.
On 7 October 2008, it exploded in an earthly atmosphere at an altitude of about 37 kilometres, after which it was scattered across the sands of the Nuba Desert in northern Sudan.
The asteroid was about 4 meters in diameter and was able to collect about 50 of its fragments, which have sizes from 1 to 10 cm. The rightholder of the illustration Seti / P Jenniskens / M Shaddad Image caption The meteorites fell in the Nuba desert in northern Sudan The collection of fragments received the common name Almahata Sitta, which means in Arabic "Sixth Station" - by name of the nearest railway station.
Some impurities in the diamonds contained in these fragments could only be formed at ultra-high pressures of about 20 GPa (almost 200 thousand atmospheres), the authors of the article write.
Such conditions could exist only in the depths of a large planet.
A member of the Polytechnic Institute in Lausanne Farhang Nabi and his colleagues believe that this data is the first convincing evidence of the existence of a protoplanet that was later destroyed.
These data also confirm the theory that the planets of the Solar System we know have formed from the remnants of ten large protoplanets.
The 2008 TC3 asteroid probably originated in the first 10 million years of the solar system.
Meteorites belonging to this era belong to the Ureilite category and account for less than 1% of all objects falling on Earth.
The right holder of the illustration SPL Image caption The asteroid with diamonds was part of a large planet that existed at the early stage of the formation of the Solar System.
"Celestial bodies of the size of Mars (one of them in the collision with the Earth formed the Moon) were common and later either joined, forming larger planets, either fell on the Sun, or were thrown out of the Solar System.
"Our data convincingly indicate that the Ureilite protoplanet was the giant missing planet that was destroyed as a result of collisions," the article says.

The analysis of meteorites that come to Earth allows scientists to better understand the Solar System and its evolution.
For this purpose, material is taken from the middle of the space body, which was not contaminated by terrestrial organisms and was not exposed to the terrestrial atmosphere.
Such a pattern comes to us as it was millions and billions of years ago.
Scientists at the University of Arizona took material from the Alende meteorite, which fell in Mexico in 1969.
Some of its particles, rich in calcium and aluminum, formed 4.5 billion years ago.
Using quantum mechanics and thermodynamics, scientists modeled and analyzed the path that meteorite particles had passed during their existence.
A comparison of model predictions with a detailed analysis of the chemical composition and crystalline structure of the sample, as well as a model of how the substance was transferred to a protoplanetary disk, gave clues about the travel of these meteorite particles and the environment.
Interesting 1500 gadgets in the first three days: Huawei Band 6 set a record in Ukraine The path of meteorite particles in a dynamic protoplanetary disk / Photo The Planetary Science Journal What scientists learned The team analyzed the ancient particles in the meteorite using two electron microscopes with atomic expansion, which are located at the University of Arizona and the Hitachi plant in Japan.
It has been found that inclusions consist mainly of minerals known as spinel and perovskite, which are also found in rocks on Earth.
Accurate analysis of the spatial location of the atoms allowed the team to study in very detail the structure of crystalline structures.
To the surprise of the team, some results diverged from current theories about physical processes that are believed to be active inside protoplanetary disks.
Read on the site 5 myths about the black holes that you could hear The Path of the meteorite particle Allende Based on the data obtained, it was concluded that the particle formed in the area of the protoplanetary disk near the place where the Earth is now located.
Then the particle made a journey closer to the Sun, where it became increasingly hot.
Later, the particle was again thrown into the distant and cold areas of the young Solar System.
Eventually, the particle became an asteroid, which later disintegrated into parts.
Some of these parts were captured by Earth gravity and landed on Earth in the form of meteorites.
Now a team of scientists is waiting for samples to arrive from the asteroid Bennu to compare the samples.
The arrival of the OSIRIS-REx probe with samples will take place in 2023.

The Nazca Valley, Machu Picchu, the Pyramids of Giza and Sphinx, and Stonehenge all attract millions of tourists every year who want to touch the mystery.
After all, there are still disputes about the builders of these complexes, and the extraterrestrial version has not only a lot of fans, but also quite logical arguments in its favor.
On the territory of Kazakhstan there are no less amazing places, the riddles of which have not yet been solved Ustyurt Plateau Where: between the northern part of the Caspian Sea and the Aral Sea There is a bold opinion that this stone complex, built by unknown builders of antiquity, is nothing but a cosmodrome.
Whether it is or not, science cannot prove it, but even now there are often inexplicable phenomena on the plateau, such as bright glow in the sky, appearing in the daytime and at night of mirage.
They're all heading northeast.
"Arrows" were discovered only in 1986 during aerial photography (a walking person or a rider for obvious reasons can not see them).
The Arrow System is located more than 100 km, surpassing (on a scale) the archaeological phenomenon of the Peruvian desert of Nazca.
Archaeologists have found that the arrows were erected much earlier than the first human settlements in the area, with the settlements located south of the mysterious complex.
During the archaeological excavations, skeletons of fish were discovered, which means that once there was a sea, the waters of which went northeast, where the arrows indicate.
They may have shown the direction of the outgoing water.
But for whom are these giant signs that cannot be seen from the surface of the earth?
In addition, near the arrows, scientists discovered stone figures of animals similar to giant turtles, which were also directed to the north-east, as well as many small pyramids of unheathed stone, reminiscent of Egyptian pyramids.
Further along the indicated direction found absolutely straight road from the same stone, laid on the boundless expanses of the desert.
Kyzylkum Where: between the Syrdarya and Amudarya rivers Kyzylkum is the greatest desert of Eurasia, which was divided among themselves by three states: Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan and Turkmenistan.
The total area of Kyzylkum is 300 thousand square kilometers.
The subsoil of the desert is rich in minerals, its sands inhabit rare and endangered animals and grow unique plants.
And at the same time there are a number of poorly researched abnormal areas.
For example, in Central Kyzylkum, in the mountains, ancient rock paintings were found, depicting people in suits and something like a spaceship.
Moreover, eyewitness accounts are regularly received of unidentified objects accelerating the airspace of the desert.
On 26 September 1990, two geologists, dealing with the breakdown of drilling profiles, stumbled upon strange spots.
The analysis showed the presence of a substance of non-earthly origin.
In 2000, an automatic camera filmed an unidentified flying object moving towards the hills.
The authenticity of the photo is not confirmed, but also not refuted.
Akyrtas Where: 45 km from the city of Taraz in the Zhambyl region Akyrtas - an amazing monument of antiquity, the palace complex of the VIII – IX centuries - repeatedly awakened the minds of a variety of researchers and ufologists.
The monument represents the ruins of a building built from huge dark red stone blocks.
It has been under study for nearly a century and a half.
And all this time, the most controversial hypotheses about the purpose of the object and its creators were put forward.
Who, according to different versions, only did not build it - both Persians, Greeks, Arabs, and Romans.
In the history of ancient architecture Akyrtasu really has no analogues.
But that's not what matters.
Most of all, the scale of the construction is shocking.
The whole palace complex is built of skillfully processed stones, each weighing up to 10 tons.
The height of the foundation of the main building is sensational and is 4 m. At the same time, there are no quarries in the vicinity.
The question arises, how did the ancients bring huge stones here?
Among the inhabitants of the Zhambyl region, there are still legends that flying plates appear over the settlement from time to time.
In order to check the unearthly trace in the history of the settlement of Akyrtas, even ufologists took part in the research.
However, the version of the alien intervention in the construction of the facility for some reason could not be refuted or confirmed.
But that's not all.
There are no water sources nearby Akyrtas.
This place was unfit for life.
However, underground scientists discovered the remains of a 5.5 km long clay pipeline.
And inside the walls there were places for large columns.
However, the answer to this question is more or less clear.
The most important thing is the impact of the complex on people.
Even now, visiting these ancient ruins awakens the sleeping reserves of the human body.
And the physical sensations here are purely individual.
Some have increased eyesight and hearing, others fall into a trance state, others experience a strong physical recovery.
People who come here often feel nausea and dizziness, they feel that the earth trembles beneath their feet.
From the contact with the stones of Akyrtas, many people begin to feel the heat in their hands and feet.
And some of the stones of the settlement, on the contrary, relieve all fatigue and anxiety.
Scientists suggest that the settlement is built on the site of a tectonic fracture with huge multi-tier voids in the Earth's crust.
In their opinion, this is how people are affected by complex processes occurring in the subsoil.
Sacred valley Ak-Baur Where: 38 km from the city of Ust-Kamenogorsk, Kalbinsk mountains, West Altai tract Ak-Baur is considered one of the most mysterious places in the East Kazakhstan region.
On its territory, the foundations of the ancient buildings of the Neolithic era (5 – 3 thousand years BC), burials, a site with a marked solar clock, an "astronomical laboratory" with preserved granite slabs carrying on the information of an astrogrid with the correct image of the constellation White (Big) Bear were found.
One of the mysteries of Ak-Baur is a grotto in a granite array with a hole in the sky.
The natural opening of the "roof" of the grotto in the form of a heart has traces of processing, perhaps it was corrected by a man who made a protrusion fly to fix the movement of the main constellations of the night sky.
On the ceiling and walls of the grotto are depicted drawings, which are puzzling researchers so far.
The thing is, they're no longer found.
To this day, there are about 80 drawings: several images of a person, a mountain goat, dwellings and carriages, the rest - a variety of symbols and signs.
It would seem the most obvious to assume that our ancestors painted the stars they observed through the hole in the grotto ceiling.
Except the images are not on the map of the starry sky of our hemisphere.
This was explained by a foreign researcher.
According to his version, ancient people did not represent the northern hemisphere, but the southern hemisphere.
That is, judging from the conclusions of the scientist, the drawings in the grotto indicate that once for a very long time the axis of the Earth was radically shifted.
The central part of Ak-Baur has the shape of an amphitheater with a diameter of about 25 m. Around it granite formations up to 4 m high.
On the one hand, the amphitheater is blocked by a wall clearly created by a man. Its location indicates the structure from east to west.
In the middle of this wall stands a granite pole about a meter high.
If you install a compass on it, the shooter will point to a hill located 100 meters in the north.
On the top of the hill there is another pillar of white quartz, which, in turn, shows to the next top.
Scientists argue that if this line is moved further, on the day of the spring equinox, it will be aimed directly at the Polar Star, which originally served as a reference point for the ancient peoples.
And on one of the rocks of the Ak-Baur tract there are holes of unnatural origin.
If water is poured into one of the lower holes, then on the day of spring equinox, the sun beam at sunrise will accurately reflect in the upper hole.
Some scientists suggest that Ak-Baur is a unique energy and information generator that has clearly delineated polarity on the sides of the horizon.
There are two positive and two negative zones, which go by their radiation into the space above the Earth's crust and into the space of the Earth's crust itself.
This is a permanent information generator that has been operating for five thousand years.
Information "flows" here from huge territories and goes to space.
Barsakelmes Island Where: 200 km from the city of Aralsk (in the south-west direction) At the moment, Barsakelmes Island is a tract in the Aral Sea.
In the middle of the last century, the length of the island was 23 by 7 km, but as the sea was smaller, its size grew.
By 2000, Barsakelmes had ceased to be an island, and in the summer of 2009, even a peninsula.
The literal translation of the name of the island from Kazakh - "go - will not return".
People often disappear here, you can meet unusual animals, light poles and UFOs.
There are many legends and amazing stories about Barsakelmes Island.
They tend to be very strange phenomena and occurrences.
As a rule, all of them are related to the violation of the course of physical time — temporary anomalies.
In the book of N. Roerich "Heart of Asia" it is mentioned that at the end of the XIX century several families of Kazakhs moved to Barsakelmes.
After several months on the island, they disappeared without a trace.
In the thirties, a geodetic expedition arrived on Barsakelmes.
It had a few people and a large supply of food for a month.
A week later, one man came out of the plateau.
Nothing about the fate of the others he did not tell, he was considered crazy, because he, among other things, persistently said that he was only on the plateau for two days...
From the locals, you can hear stories about how, in the past centuries, fugitives who, in their opinion, stayed on the island for just a couple of years, returned home two or three decades later.
According to unverified data, people have also disappeared on the island and these days.
Of course, the press loves such stories, and in most cases there is no truth in the publications about Barsakelmes.
But, as wise men say, “nothing is taken... ”

They found out that water was formed from the impact of meteorites and the sun.
Astronomers have explained how to extract water on the Moon and asteroids, writes Nature Astronomy.
According to scientists, H2O molecules can be obtained from celestial bodies under the influence of solar wind.
They explained that individual hydrogen and oxygen atoms "fly" out of asteroids every time they collide with other objects and are destroyed.
If there is a stream of ionized particles from the Sun, these atoms are combined into water molecules.
That is, this process can take place even in a vacuum, which means that water can be extracted anywhere in space.
It is reported that the researchers came to this conclusion after studying a sample of a 100-kilogram Murchison meteorite that fell near a village of the same name in Australia in 1969.
Astronomers directed a laser beam on the stone to simulate a small blow, as well as simulate the solar wind.
It turned out that as a result, the chemical reaction needed for the appearance of water began.
This complex process of regeneration of surface water molecules could be a possible mechanism for replenishing water reserves on other airless bodies, such as Luna Katarina Milkovic, the author of the study. In addition, according to scientists, this increases the chances of finding water on other planets, which means finding forms of life similar to terrestrial ones.
Life previously wrote that water vapor was found in the atmosphere of one of the potentially inhabited exoplanets.
In addition, astronomers have called Earth not the best place to live.

Barbara Bush, the former First Lady of the United States, died when her physical condition deteriorated at the age of 92.
He, who had visited the hospital several times due to illness, stated that he would not continue treatment.
Join Deutsche Welle in Telegram. Barbara Bush and her husband have been married for 73 years.
She was the daughter of a magazine millionaire publisher from a small town in New York State, and met her future husband when she was 16.
George Herbert Walker Bush was Vice President of the United States in 1981 and became President of the United States in 1989.
Barbara Bush and her husband, Barbara Bush, became very popular in December 1998, when she was the First Lady of the United States.
He was active in social projects and fought against homelessness and AIDS.
He also worked in adult literacy.
In 1989, Mrs. Bush wrote a children's book describing her family from the perspective of her dog, Fred.
In 1990, another book of his was published, this time describing his female dog, Milly, a day at the White House.
The book became a public fortune and became one of the best-selling books.
The couple had six children, one of whom died of leukemia at the age of three.
George W. Bush, their eldest son, was the President of the United States from 2001 to 2009.
He called his mother the reason for allowing many women to the American leadership levels.
George W. Bush said that the reason he valued women's advice, women with strong will and independent opinion, is his "mother."
A photo of Bush's 1955 family album, Barbara Bush, with his wife and son, both U.S. presidents, opposed Donald Trump Jeb Bush, second son of Barbara Bush in 2016, and competed with Donald Trump, the current President of the United States.
In 2013, Barbara Bush spoke in an interview against her son ’ s candidate, stating that the bushes have reached the White House enough and that other families should have a chance to enter it.
However, soon after, Mrs. Bush changed her mind and supported her son's nomination.
In an interview, he explicitly said that Donald Trump did not consider the candidate appropriate.
He said, "He's not in the mood for Trump, he's not talking about how he's going to solve problems, he's just insulting and he's done terrible things about women."
Barbara Bush, after Abigail Adams, was the second woman in the United States to be the wife and mother of a president.
Abigail Adams was the wife of John Adams, the second President of the United States (1797 – 1801) and the mother of John Quincy Adams, who became President of the United States in 1824.

The exploding crater of Gale, the American Mars Rover Curiosity, stumbled upon another metal meteorite.
If the discovery is confirmed, it will become the third meteorite found on Mars in 4.5 years of work and the eighth in the history of studying the planet by rovers - the latter was found in November 2015.
The found piece of rock has a gray metallic luster and a characteristic surface texture reminiscent of regmaliptes - characteristic recesses formed as a result of evaporation of soft inclusions during heating in the atmosphere.
The discovery was made on January 12.
Three bright points on the same line on the surface of the meteorite are possible traces of the impact of the ChemCam laser tool, which analyzes the spectrum of light emitted by the evaporated rock.

The Peruvian authorities announced that they would deport five tourists who deliberately damaged Machu Picchu ’ s monuments, the Inca ’ s ancient castle and one of the most well-known tourist attractions in South America.
According to ISNA and the Guardian, Peruvian officials announced on Tuesday that tourists from Chile, Brazil, France were secretly entering the ruins of Machu Picchu, causing a rock collapse from the temple of the Sun to damage the historic complex.
One of the Argentine tourists, known as one of the main perpetrators of the destruction, is to remain in Peru to respond to the crime of harming the Peruvian cultural heritage.
This mysterious stone building of several hundred years has long been a surprise for tourists, but has recently been facing a serious damage crisis by tourists.
Not long ago, the multi-billion-dollar construction plan of an airport, which has been proposed to shorten the time of travel for tourists to the region, has received many criticisms.
Critics of the project believe that the airport construction program in the area is very short-sighted and will jeopardize this historic location in the future long-term.
The specialized magazine Anshint-Orijins also reported serious risks that threaten Machu Picchu ’ s future in the wake of the garbage collection crisis.
Machu Picchu, also known as the Lost City of Inca, was built around 1450 at the height of the Inca Empire.
The fortress is 50 miles from Kosko, the capital of the Inca, and is therefore never found and destroyed, like other Inca cities.
Over the centuries, the forests around the complex have grown and covered it, and for this reason it has remained hidden from sight and only a few knew of its existence.
On July 24, 1911, Machu Picchu was introduced to the Western world by American historian Hiram Bingham in a speech at Yale University.
He was guided by local guidance where he occasionally visited the site.
Machu Picchu was registered as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983, and as a pure masterpiece of architecture and a unique symbol of the Inca civilization, it is now known as one of the Seven New Wonders.
The end of the message

At that time, the political situation in Taiwan was turbulent, and the opposition party strongly criticized the prosecution system, while Luo Ying-shui faced the ruling and opposition legislators in an impartial manner, while standing up to defend the stance of the judicial profession, finally defusing the concerns of various sectors of the society, and won the respect and support of the procurator's colleagues.

Jiang Yihua sighed that he had gone with a "really good official" and that the people would always remember him.
He also needs a good time to sort out his feelings, "Minister Ying, go all the way."

A group of researchers from the University of Granada, the University of Durham and the Autonomous University of Barcelona managed to establish the gender and age of prehistoric artists who left a schematic message on the walls of the Spanish cave Los Machos.
The study is published in Antiquity magazine, and briefly about it tells The Art Newspaper.
Scientists analyzed two fingerprints that were previously found in the rocky shelter of Los Machos in southern Spain.
This cave is famous for the "paintings" of the Neolithic era.
Rock images were created between 4500 and 2000 BC.
Previously, it was established that the ancient artists painted with a finger.
Prehistoric "schematic art" includes simple lines, circles, geometric patterns and human figures.
According to researchers, all this "probably related to everyday life and was a materialization of the symbolic elements understandable to the communities inhabiting the area around Los Machos".
To establish the authors, scientists have literally studied the fingerprints they left on the walls.
They used the same technology they used to capture criminals.
As a result, it was determined that the prints in the cave were left by two prehistoric artists: a man, who at that time was at least 36 years old, and a young woman or a teenager between the ages of 10 and 16.
This discovery sheds new light on the personalities of the artists who created the rock art of Spain, and on the society in which they lived.
"The true value of rock art is that it represents a direct expression of the thought process of the people who created it," the authors write in their article. "These people are very often absent in the discussions of the nuances of rock art."
Commenting on the amazing discovery, Leonardo García Sanjuán, Professor of Prehistoric Studies at the University of Seville, noted that the analysis of ancient fingerprints in terms of determining the sex and age of artists is a great contribution to understanding who was involved in the creation of rock art.
"For example, gender analysis would be useful if enough fingerprints were collected," says Professor Sanjuan. "It would help to determine whether rock paintings were made in the same number by children and adults, women and men.
Or were certain age and sex groups responsible for them?
So far, we have not known much about these features. "
It is believed that prehistoric rock art has long been widely studied.
Surprisingly, there is really little information about the artists who created these ancient works.
It was supposed to be the hunters who painted the animals they were hunting.
So they painted what they saw in person.
But in the caves there are often also so-called stencils - numerous prints of palms left for some reason by ancient people.
Such works were found not only in the Spanish, but also in the French caves.
Previous studies have shown that palm prints were mostly left by women - up to 75 per cent of these were created by them.
The rest were abandoned by men and children.
Perhaps the new method will be used for gender analysis in caves around the world.

Machu Picchu is a city of ancient America, located on the territory of modern Peru at the top of the mountain range.
In 2007, this place was awarded the title of the New Wonder of the World.
There are things you will not find about Machu Picchu in the guide, Wikipedia article or video from the travel agency.
Here is all you need to know about the ancient ruins of the Inca civilization: 1.
All the stones from which the city was built were precisely carved so as to fit closely with each other.
So no solution was required to hold the walls.
And it's cooler than it seems.
After all, the buildings were built in such a way as to withstand earthquakes.
Machu Picchu is located at an altitude of 2,430 meters above sea level.
So if you've just arrived, wait a few days before climbing the top.
The body needs time to acclimatize.
Many tourists who neglect this advice risk serious health problems.
Machu Picchu is a World Heritage Site.
In 2007, Machu Picchu was recognized as one of the seven New Wonders of the World.
Known as a "lost city," he was never really lost.
There were several farmlands in the vicinity of Machu Picchu, when explorer Hiram Bingham discovered the site in 1911.
Recent studies indicate that very many local residents knew about Machu Picchu.
The Inca Empire was one of the largest in pre-Columbian America, covering Argentina, Bolivia, Chile and Ecuador.
Inca means "kings" or "emperors".
During its heyday, the Inca Empire was the backbone of the Andes, which ruled over 20 million people between 1400 and 1500.
It all ended when Emperor Atahualpa was assassinated by the Spanish conquistadors in 1533.
Local lamas really like posing for photos.
If the lama could do selfies, we would all have to close our social media accounts.
In addition to this impressive modern talent, llamas have traditionally been valued for their wool and nutrient-rich manure, which the Inca fertilized fields.
Try to google how the lama differs from alpaca, it will help to distinguish them during the trip to South America - because lama and alpaca can be found on pastures in Machu Picchu.
Lama belongs to the family of camels and grows twice as large as alpaca.
They were important resources for the Incas without other pets such as horses, goats or sheep.
In addition to the famous and popular path of the guides to Machu Picchu, there are others.
For example, a trail from Ollantaytambo, it leads through local communities and archaeological sites that are almost unvisited by tourists.
In Quechua, Machu Picchu is translated as "Old Mountain" or "Old Peak".
During your visit to Peru, you will most likely visit one of the indigenous communities where Quechua is spoken.
The history of the Quechua people dates back to the Inca civilization, and you will find many ruins throughout Peru, named in the local language.
A painter named Leonardo da Vinci painted Mona Lisa (1503-1506) in the midst of the Inca Empire.
When you arrive in Machu Picchu, can you ask yourself what else happened when the Inca Empire was at the peak of its power?
While the Incas ruled throughout the Andes, there was a surge in artistic and technological development in Europe, and China was ruled by the Ming Dynasty.
The nature around the ancient city is incredible!
Whatever leads you to Machu Picchu, you can probably say that the surroundings of the national park are as memorable as the ruins.
There is a stunning view of the Sacred Valley from above.
When you look down, you will see the Urubamba River with the mountains framing it. 10.
Machu Picchu was used as an astronomical observatory.
When you learn more about Machu Picchu, you will begin to understand the strong influence that astronomy has had on the Inca civilization and the way local buildings were built.
The stars helped the Incas identify seasonal changes indicating the best time for planting and harvesting.
The distinctive stone of Intihuatan, found in Machu Picchu, acted as an astronomical clock and was used in ceremonies.
We don't know exactly why the Incas left Machu Picchu.
While the Spanish conquistadors put an end to the Inca Empire, there is evidence that Machu Picchu's place was abandoned before that time.
Scientists have put forward the idea of a smallpox epidemic, but without any good evidence, the real cause remains a mystery.
You can get Machu Picchu's seal at the entrance to the city.
When visiting, do not forget to put a stamp in your passport at the entrance to the ancient city, writes intrepidtravel.
This is a pretty impressive souvenir and a nice reminder of your Peruvian adventure.

I think it will be difficult to even reach consensus on obtaining a plan of action for NATO membership, ” Rice said in an interview with the Ukrainian newspaper Novoe Vremya.
According to the former Secretary of State, what was said to her does not mean that Ukraine should seek membership in NATO and it is not in a position to take constructive steps in this direction.

Vladimir Borovikov, a resident of Vitebsk, claims that he found evidence of the fall of a large meteorite in the region.
According to him, a rather large space object reached the surface of the Earth more than 10,000 years ago, and its fragments survived due to the glacier period.
He discovered traces of the meteorite fall in the Liozno district, where his wife's mother resides.
On the outskirts of the village there is a depression in the relief, almost ideally a round bowl with a diameter of about 1 km and a depth of about 20 meters.
In the center - a hill in the form of an elongated hill with fairly steep slopes.
Around is a swamp area.
According to Vladimir Borovikov, during the ice age, the meteorite "hit a thick layer of ice, punched it and exploded with a slight depression into sedimentary rocks".
“And when the glacier retreated, a small round pothole remained from the crater.
The flows of water during ice melting washed deep ravines in the walls of the bowl, partially filling it with nanosubes, "he believes, reports" Soviet Belarus ".
In studying the alleged meteorite crater, the researcher collected a small collection of glassy melted and sintered artifacts, as well as strange black stones, diverse in shape, but consisting of a similar amorphous mineral.
On some samples there are small inclusions, similar to droplets of silver-white metal.
Another group of finds is the fossilized remains of ancient organisms.
According to Vladimir Borovikov, they were thrown by an explosion from more ancient deposits when a meteorite collided with the Earth.
Moreover, he claims that about five kilometres from the alleged crater found a fragment related to ordinary chondrites, the most common subgroup in the meteorite classification.
Some other artifacts found in the crater are similar in structure and composition.
On individual specimens, crumpled, deformed chondras were found — small rounded formations, which are the main structural element of 90% of meteorites — chondrites and are rapidly hardened drops of molten silicate matter, he states.
However, Belarusian scientists are not in a hurry to study the finds.

In the Bush administration, Iran was likened to "the ultimate problem box", which the White House had no plans for, but after the beginning of pressure on the Islamic Republic under the pretext of the Iranian nuclear program, Bush identified the White House ’ s approach to Iran.
Young Political Service Online: In recent years in the Trump administration, one of the most important points to be considered as the difference between the current government and previous governments was Washington ’ s attempt to impose maximum pressure on Iran, a pressure known as the crushing pressure policy and as one of the most important strategies of the current US government against Iran.
Although some believe that the Trump administration ’ s policy against Iran is a new policy, a look at the past shows that the policy of crushing pressure is in line with the policy that has been used since the Bush administration ’ s era against Iran; a policy rooted in the failure of the military threat against Iran, however, Trump ’ s openness to this policy has led many to believe that there is a serious change in it.
This policy was in the past as a transformative diplomacy, a transformation that would ultimately lead to a regime change in the Islamic Republic of Iran or could inhibit the threat of the growth and development of the Iranian nuclear industry.
In the current situation, the most important effort of the Trump administration is maximum pressure using economic sanctions and trying to limit Iran within its borders, which has shown itself well in terms of the 11th Pompeo regime against Iran.
In the Bush administration, Iran was likened to "the ultimate problem box", which the White House had no plans for, but after the beginning of pressure on the Islamic Republic under the pretext of the Iranian nuclear program, Bush identified the White House ’ s approach to Iran.
In this approach, there were two important approaches to Iran that shaped the U.S. ’ s behavior towards Iran; an attempt to mobilize international pressure against Iran to abandon its nuclear program and simultaneously threatened to change the regime, which the government posed as a military option, but in practice no action was taken.
To achieve regime change in Iran, one of the most important options of the Bush administration and Republicans towards Iran is the military option.
Because Bush had called Iran the axis of evil, he and the Republicans considered negotiating with Iran as a recognition of Iran, which they themselves called the axis of evil, so they did not want to negotiate with Iran.
According to the Washington Post in April 2006, the Bush administration was investigating the military option against Iran as part of a broader strategy to force it to leave the nuclear program by pushing it into Iran.
Although this approach was unlikely to take place in the short term, or was low in probability of effectiveness, the United States held it as a threat to Iran to stop nuclear programs as a possible option.
But the Americans themselves knew that they would eventually have to negotiate with Iran.
After Iran's nuclear successes in 2006, many American thinkers and strategists decided that Iran should be negotiated to solve the Iranian nuclear problem and, in their opinion, the Iranian crisis would be possible.
In an August 2006 study by the Carnegie Foundation, George Perkovich considered diplomacy appropriate for the present and believed that the United States should engage in direct negotiations with Iran.
In a report published by Tasnim News Agency, McFaul, Milani, and Diamond also stated in a broadly agreed strategy research plan that the only solution to the threat of Iran is the formation of democracy in Iran. In this regard, the United States must negotiate with Iran and stop the regime ’ s change strategy.
The group believes that if the broader agreement strategy is implemented, the main winner is America, whether Iran accepts it or not.
If Iran accepts, it will go to democracy, and if it has a nuclear weapon, it is not dangerous, but if it does, the Iranian regime ’ s internal legitimacy will be questioned and the legitimacy of the harder measures of the United States against Iran will be provided.
During the second period of the Bush administration (2008-2005), at the time of the U.S. Foreign Ministry Condoleezza Rice, the United States was involved in a process between the two groups; some were demanding policies to change the Iranian regime, and others, considering the experience of overthrow in Iraq, which led to an establishment of a government contrary to the U.S. ’ s will, saw that changing Iran ’ s behavior was a better way to solve Iran ’ s challenge.
Rice tried to destroy the difference.
As Secretary of State of the United States, he seemed to be leading efforts within the ministry, calling for a negotiation with Iran to resolve issues related to the supposedly armed nuclear program, a solution that avoids direct military confrontation.
Under his mandate, the Foreign Ministry began to attract some discourse and plans of regime changers, while strengthening American diplomatic efforts aimed at bringing together an international coalition for pressure on Iran to retreat, he nodded to advocates of co-op engagement.
Rice chose a policy to create a reconciliation between the two groups, which was a combination of the views of both sides of the conflict.
Under his mandate, the Foreign Ministry tried openly to put some of the rhetoric and plans of the Iranian regime ’ s change advocates on the agenda, while also giving the field to another group that advocated diplomatic efforts to interact with Iran.
Condoleeza Rice diplomacy was called "transformational diplomacy."
Transformative diplomacy, or as some political science experts say, "Force diplomacy" was a set of measures that the U.S. government initiated to change the political system in some countries that were prioritized by Washington 's foreign policy.
Iran at the time, according to Rice, was "America's biggest national security challenge", so it was at the top of the list of government movements.
Although the new approach to the Bush administration did not succeed, Obama, starting with the new government, announced that this would be the beginning of his government ’ s approach to Iran, and that the change in US approach and strategy against Iran was visible as the Obama administration began in 2009.
The Obama administration ’ s new approach to engagement and diplomacy was based on manoeuvring electoral debate, and in most of Obama ’ s talks, speaking of diplomacy and engagement as the best way against Iran, he called for a direct negotiation and dialogue with Iran.
The Obama administration's policy was to continue the Bush administration's policy, which was to push Iran through sanctions, and to invite the negotiation of its most important pillars.
At the same time, the most important anti-Iranian sanctions were imposed by the United States, leading to a regime of the hardest trade restrictions.
The U.S. government even took a semi-rigid approach to the Iranian government, most notably nuclear assassinations, as well as attacks on malicious software against Iran.
Although the policy was amended by the beginning of the JCPOA negotiations, it continued with power in the following years.
What Trump is looking for in Trump ’ s new government is also the same policy; abstaining from military involvement, along with serious sanctions, has been added to counter the increase in Iran ’ s economic influence, which, of course, has also been added to the strategic goals of capturing the oil market.
It is natural that the Trump administration hopes to begin its 2012 experience and Iran ’ s desire to begin negotiations, but Iran has so far resisted this issue with all its power, and the challenges of the United States in various parts of the world will not allow the country to press on Iran.
Time is passing in favor of Iran, and this is the point that the Trump administration understands very well.

Granite monuments and rocks of the legendary Machu Picchu, the sacred city of the Incas, may disappear in the coming decades due to the multiplication of bacteria and algae eating granite.
This is said by chemists and archaeologists who published an article in the journal Science of the Total Environment.
“These microbes, as well as algae and fungi, penetrate into the monuments through microcracks and other damage.
They are attached to their granite surface and begin to suck out minerals from it, gradually destroying the rock.
These layers crumble into the trash, causing the stone to gradually disappear, "says Hector Morillas, a chemist at the Basque University in Bilbao, Spain.
The Inca Empire was the largest and most powerful state of the New World before the arrival of the Spanish conquistadors, covering the territory now occupied by Peru, large regions of Chile and Ecuador, as well as small corners of Argentina and Bolivia.
It originated around the beginning of the 13th century, and only 200 years later, its population reached the mark of 10 million and the area was record at that time.
The state of the Incas is historically interesting in that it did not have typical features of all ancient and modern Empires of the Old World.
The Incas did not have the usual writing, code of laws, currency, metallurgy and accounting system, as well as the "classical" feudal or despotic relations between the supreme rulers and their subjects.
In its territorial structure, the Inca Empire resembled a federal state — it consisted of 4 major regions and approximately 86 provinces, in which individual peoples lived with their own culture and characteristics of local self-government.
Many of them joined the alliance voluntarily and others were conquered by the Inca armies, but in both cases they gradually assimilated the "great culture" of the empire.
One of the first such "subjects of the federation" was the so-called Sacred Valley - a mountain region adjacent to Cusco, the capital of the empire, conquered by the Incas in the first millennium of our era.
The symbol of her assimilation of this was the famous citadel Machu Picchu, built by one of the last "sons of the Sun" a century before the invasion of the conquistadors.
Archaeologists, as Morillas notes, have long noticed that the condition of many artifacts and buildings in Machu Picchu territory has deteriorated markedly in recent decades.
Many scientists began to link their degradation to the fact that the surface of these monuments was covered with germs.
Other researchers, on the contrary, believe that they protect Machu Picchu from destruction, preventing rain drops and various aggressive substances in the air from interacting with granite.
Morillas and his colleagues checked which of these theories is closer to the truth, comprehensively studying the state of the so-called "Holy Stone", a kind of analogue of Kaaba for the Incas.
Enlisting the support of the city ’ s custodians, scientists enlightened the artifact with the help of a special microscope, collected samples of microbes from the surface of the prayer stone and chopped off the microscopic fragment of the rock.
By transferring them to the laboratory, they conducted a "census" among the inhabitants of the Holy Stone and studied its chemical composition.
It turned out that both the surface and the interior layers of granite had a large number of lichens, algae and cyanobacteria that decompose granite and feed on its decay products.
Their traces, as the researchers note, met even at a relatively large depth, about 20-50 micrometers, which led to the stratification of the stone and its transformation into a loose "trumpet".
The problem is also that the granite itself has turned out to be quite porous, which makes life easier for microbes.
Now, as Morillas noted, his team is conducting similar state assessments for all buildings and stone artifacts in Machu Picchu.
Scientists are trying to understand how to protect them from further destruction, and are also looking for possible traces of whether the "invasion" of microbes was caused by some past failed attempts to restore them.

Image copyright ESA / Hubble / NASA / Janaína Ávila Image caption Some of the sun-front particles in the Mercheson meteorite (small right figure) may have come from an evolutionary star similar to the ovoid nebula.
Scientists discovered the oldest known material on Earth in a meteorite.
They discovered dust particles as long as 7.5 billion years ago in a meteorite that fell to Earth in the 1960s.
This ancient particle is formed in stars before the birth of the Solar System.
The researchers published the findings in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.
When a star dies, particles from a star float into space and then merge into new stars, planets, moons, or meteorites — known as "pre-solar grains".
“It ’ s a real stellar sample, it ’ s stellar dust, ” said Philipp Heck, director of the Field Museum in Chicago and an associate professor at the University of Chicago. A team of researchers from the United States and Switzerland analyzed the Murchison meteorite, which fell in Australia in 1969, and analyzed 40 solar dust particles from the meteorite.
According to Jennifer Greer of the Field Museum, the work began with the grinding of meteorite fragments into powder.
"The pieces eventually become a paste that smells like rotten peanut butter," Grille explains.
This odorous paste was later dissolved in acid, leaving only stardust.
"Looking for these starry dust is like a needle in the sea," says Heck.
Photo copyright Janína N.
Ávila Image Caption Scanning Electronically-microscopic Photographs of the Solar Front Particles.
In order to find out the years in which the pre-solar particles existed, researchers measured the time they were exposed to cosmic rays in space; these cosmic rays were high-energy particles that could penetrate through our Milky Way and through solids.
Some of these cosmic rays interact with the material they encounter and form new elements; the longer they are exposed, the more these elements form.
Researchers used a special (isotope) neon 21 to measure the year of these particles.
Hecker said, "This is like a bucket placed in a rainstorm.
Assuming the rain is constant, the amount of water accumulated in the bucket will tell you how long it is exposed in the rain. "
Measuring how many new elements exist tells scientists the time when grain is exposed to cosmic rays, which in turn tells us the age of these particles.
As a result, the team found that some of the solar precursor particles were the oldest ever discovered.
Based on how many times cosmic rays interact with these particles, most of these particles have a history of 4.6 – 4.9 billion years.
The Sun is 4.6 billion years old and the Earth is 4.5 billion years old.
The oldest of these particles has a history of 7.5 billion years.
Heck told the BBC that so far, "only 10% of the particles are over 5.5 billion years old, 60% are 'young' (between 4.6 and 4.9 billion years old), and the rest are between the two."
"I believe there are older pre-solar particles in Mercheson and other meteorites, but they have not yet been found."
Heck said.
Previously, the oldest anterior solar particle measured using isotopes came from about 5.5 billion years ago.
Image copyright James St John Image caption The meteorite fell in Australia in 1969.
As to whether new stars are forming at a stable rate and whether the number of new stars forming at different time periods is high or low, scientists say this discovery provides a clue to the discussion.
"As a result of these particles, we now have direct evidence that seven billion years ago there was a period of star formation in our Milky Way.
This is one of our main findings. "
Researchers have also learned that the front particles of the Sun often form a "granola" shaped cluster that travels through space.
"No one in the past thought that such a large cluster was possible," Heck explained.

The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) has discovered new ancient buildings on the territory of the archaeological site of Tihuanaco, located 15 km from Lake Titicaca in Bolivia.
According to the Ministry of Culture and Tourism of the Republic, researchers used a drone and a space satellite in their work.
According to UNESCO archaeologist José Ignacio Gallegos, experts, in particular, found a large building under the ground, which was probably a temple.
They also found that the temple of Puma Punku, located on the territory of the complex, is almost twice as large as archaeologists thought.
Recent data showed that there are two more levels of the building underground.
In addition, specialists found several more large buildings and more than a hundred small ones.
According to researchers, small round and rectangular buildings in the vicinity of Tihuanaco were residential buildings.
Archaeologists also laid on the plan of the complex several ancient roads and determined the approximate boundaries of the pre-Columbian city.
UNESCO began work on a detailed map of Tiwanako and a plan for further research into ruins last May.
Archaeologists, in particular, wanted to find out with the help of images taken by a drone and satellite, what is the probability that undiscovered ancient buildings still remain underground.
Now specialists are planning new excavations.
It is believed that the ancient city of Tihuanaco, located at an altitude of about 4 thousand meters above sea level, was the capital of the ancient Andean Empire, the heyday of which occurred between 300 BC and 1000 AD.
Its inhabitants built a grandiose system of irrigation facilities in the area of Lake Titicaca.
It is estimated that in 1180 the inhabitants left the city after a devastating attack by hostile tribes.
According to researchers, at the time of the heyday of the empire, the area of Tihuanaco was about 400-600 hectares.
Now the territory of the archaeological complex occupies only 30 hectares.

You have to have Trump 's courage and the will to play psychology from the inside, and you have to be incredibly talented and attractive.
There aren't many actors who have all these things together, but Brendan Gleason does, so I'm excited about this project.
The Diary defines all of Comey's first-hand experiences of very important situations along the way of his career in the last two decades within the U.S. government and explores how good leadership and ethics should be and how it can change decisions.
His journey takes an unprecedented look into the corridors of power.
From the prosecution of the Mafia and Martha Stewart to helping change Bush ’ s government ’ s policies on torture and electronic surveillance, to review Hillary Clinton ’ s email research and relations between the Trump and Russia campaign, Kumi has played a direct role in a number of the most important cases and policies in recent US history.
Ray took a year to investigate the project and spoke to Kumi himself, his family, and many key players, including journalists, FBI agents, and other government officials.

Image copyright Hampstead Theatre Image caption The cost of telling the truth: The job is gone, the marriage is broken, the happiness is gone, but in exchange for the lives of thousands of people.
Wang Shuping said that he had not considered the fate of a human being.
Dr. Wang Shuping died of a heart attack in the Jewish state of the United States at the age of 59.
She was an ordinary doctor who studied the epidemic, and at the age of 30 did something within her mandate that required courage, and then paid the price of her life for it.
"Whistle-blowing" is a dangerous thing, because it touches some dark interests; whistle-blowing individuals often pay a price for it.
In his last interview with the media, Wang Shuping said, "I did not consider my fate.
This is the first rule of professional ethics for doctors. "
Shortly before her death, a stage play, entitled "The King of the Palace of Hell", was staged in London, England, on the themes of AIDS Village in Henan and "Blood Economy" more than 20 years ago.
Frances Ya Chu is a half-Chinese writer whose father served as an American diplomat and whose mother was a Taiwanese.
She has seen Wang Shuping and is no stranger to the "blood-selling economy" of Henan more than 20 years ago.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The conditions of the site vary from place to place.
In 1991, she began working at the Blood Station of the Department of Health.
Soon after, she discovered a terrible security loophole in the "blood-selling economy" of the time in Henan – where HIV and hepatitis virus carriers sold blood, blood products from blood stations were contaminated, and then passed through different channels to healthy blood donors and more innocent healthy people.
The blood station itself became a public health hazard.
China then implemented a policy of banning the import of blood products in order to prevent the entry of various viruses from overseas through blood products.
However, the mainland is very lagging behind in the collection and treatment of blood, and there are no measures to prevent pollution and cross-infection.
Many people sell blood for survival, but there is no concept of the risk of viral infection in the process.
There is no screening mechanism for hepatitis and HIV in government or private blood stations.
As a result, hepatitis C virus is unhindered in blood collection stations everywhere.
Wang Shu-ping wrote a report on the outbreak, and the Department of Health ordered that blood donors be tested for hepatitis C virus, while she was transferred from the blood station by her immediate supervisor.
She then set up a clinical testing center, attached to the Department of Health, financial self-management, and the main job was to screen blood samples.
Soon, she discovered a more terrifying loophole: an HIV carrier had sold blood at four blood stations.
Writing a report asking blood stations to track down HIV carriers was told it was too expensive and things went wrong.
Image copyright Getty Images Image caption The 29-year-old man was infected with HIV in 2000 after selling blood.
He started selling blood at the age of 16 and built a house for his family.
Wang found that 13% of the more than 400 blood samples collected had HIV.
In the winter of 1995, she sat down and began writing an outbreak report.
The following year, the local health department went to Beijing to "visit" and submit the report and test data to the Ministry of Health.
Does she know that this means causing trouble for herself?
"I didn't think so.
I thought that action would be taken immediately to resolve the problem.
As a doctor, my first consideration is the interests of the patient and the public, not myself.
I have the opportunity to revise the report and it is becoming more and more clear that failure to do so will have consequences.
"I have had a huge problem with the conflict between power, money and the powerless.
I decided to stand up for the innocent people infected with hepatitis C virus and HIV, "she said in an interview on the London Hampstead Theatre website a month before Wang ’ s death.
Hampstead Theatre Image caption Before her death, she worked as a researcher at the University of Utah in the United States, where she was expelled from public office, divorced from her husband, and left her children for the United States, where she never returned to China.
In 1996, all blood stations across China closed their doors.
After the blood station resumes work, an HIV test is added before blood is drawn.
Wang Shu-ping is very happy because many people will not be infected with the virus.
Those whose interests have been undermined are unhappy, including local health officials.
One day before her death, Wang Shu-ping was interviewed on BBC Radio 4's Woman's Hour, recalling the situation: "A local health official complained publicly at a public health conference that he was so bold that he actually reported the matter to the central authorities.
I stood up and said, "I wrote the report, I'm a woman."
Later in 1996, a health official told Wang Shuping that she had been suspended and could go home to serve her husband.
Her husband was working in the Ministry of Health because his wife "whistled" to expose the "blood accident" in Henan, and was also pushed out of the agency.
The two eventually divorced.
Photo copyright Hampstead Theatre Image caption A scene from the stage play "The King of the Palace of Hell" In 2001, Wang Shuping moved to the United States alone, with the English name Sunshine, meaning Sunshine.
In that year, the Chinese government acknowledged that the Central China region was facing an AIDS crisis and that at least 500,000 people were infected with HIV after selling blood at blood stations.
Henan is the province with the worst HIV epidemic.
The Government has since opened a specialist hospital for AIDS patients.
Many years later, she married Gary Christensen, an American, and settled in Salt Lake City.
She worked as a researcher at the University of Utah.
In 2019, the Ministry of Security of China found her relatives and former colleagues in Henan, with the aim of trying to get her out of the way to prevent the stage drama from being inspired by her experience.
She didn't promise.
The King of the Palace of Hell performed as scheduled in London on 5 September.
On September 21, Wang Shuping died while climbing in Salt Lake City with her husband and friends.
She is believed to have died of a heart attack.
Image caption Wang Shu-ping and dramatist Elegant Bamboo Wang Shu-ping admit that the truth pays the price: The job is gone, the marriage is broken, the happiness is gone, but at the same time the lives of millions of people are saved.
Her friend David Cowhig tweeted his condolences after learning of Wang's death: "She is a woman with a strong will, an unlikable optimism, and a very loving heart.
"(She is) my hero.
My friend. "

MOSCOW, 13 Jan — RIA Novosti.
By studying the inclusions in the meteorite, scientists discovered particles of star dust over five billion years old.
The solar system did not exist at that time.
This is the oldest solid material ever found on Earth.
The description is given in Researching the inclusions in the meteorite, scientists have discovered particles of star dust over five billion years old.
The solar system did not exist at that time.
This is the oldest solid material ever found on Earth.
The description is given in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.
The material for the chondrites, the most common class of meteorites, was the dust of the protoplanetary cloud surrounding the Sun in the early stages of the solar system.
Given that the Sun is about 4.6 billion years old and the Earth is 4.5 billion years old, the age of most chondrites is just somewhere between these values.
However, some chondrites, in addition to protoplanetary dust, include tiny solid particles of interstellar matter, star dust that was contained in interstellar gas even before the formation of the Sun, pre-solar grains.
These are the only material evidence of the period preceding the Solar System available to man.
But it's not easy to find them.
Not only are they found in about five percent of the meteorites that have fallen to Earth, these tiny particles are the size of a fraction of micrometers.
Scientists from the United States, Switzerland and Australia managed to isolate pre-solar grains from fragments of the Murchison meteorite that fell in Australia in 1969.
To do this, researchers crushed pieces of meteorite into powder, after dissolution of which insoluble particles of silicon carbide up to eight micrometers in size remained in acid — pre-solar grains.
"It was like burning a haystack to find a needle," said Philipp Heck, the first author of the paper, in a press release from the Field Museum of Natural History in Chicago.
After the pre-solar grains were isolated, the researchers found out their age.
"To do this, we used data on the time of action on the grains of cosmic rays, which are streams of high-energy particles flying through our galaxy and penetrating into a solid," Heck explains. "When interacting with these rays, new elements are formed, and the longer the grains are exposed, the more these elements are formed in them."
Most of the pre-solar grains were between 4.6 and 4.9 billion years old, but some were significantly older, up to 5.5 billion years old.
"These are the oldest solid materials ever found, and they tell us how stars have formed in our galaxy," says Heck.
Based on the fact that star dust is formed at the stage of the death of stars and their destruction, and stars live from several million to several billion years, scientists conclude that the period of active star formation, a kind of "star baby boom", took place in our galaxy five to seven billion years ago.

On 12 January, six tourists were arrested at the ancient Inca site of Peru and the United Nations World Heritage Site of Machu Picchu.
Police said the tourists, who came from France, Brazil, Argentina and Chile, would be jailed for at least four years if convicted.
Police said park rangers on Sunday arrested six tourists, including a French, two Brazilians, two Argentines and a Chilean, after they spotted them entering the forbidden area inside the temple.
"Six tourists have been detained and investigated by the Public Ministry on suspicion of vandalism of cultural sites," said Wilbert Leyva, regional police chief of Cusco, on 13 January.
Lewa added that a "crack" had been found on a stone that had fallen from a wall, causing the floor to crack; in addition, the Ministry of Culture of the Cusco region stated that they had found dung in the temple.
Machu Picchu, known as the "City of the Sky", represents the meaning of the "ancient mountains" in the native Quechua, located on the summit of the lush mountains, during the reign of the Inca monarch Pachacuti (1438-1471).
Machu Picchu was about 100 km from Cusco, the capital of the ancient Inca Empire, and was not discovered by American explorer Hiram Bingham until 1911.
The site was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983.

Scientists came to this conclusion by studying the meteorite Almahat Sitt.
The meteorite was discovered in 2008.
It flew in the sky over the Sudan and scattered on many fragments in the Nuba desert, writes Science Alert.
This object became known as Almahata Sitta, a collection of approximately 600 meteorite fragments painstakingly discovered by researchers and named "Station Six" in honor of the nearest railway station.
The uniqueness of Almahat Sitta was that it was something unprecedented in astronomy: the first collision with the asteroid was successfully predicted by scientists.
Since then, the fragments of this asteroid, named 2008 TC3, have been analyzed by researchers in search of chemical keys to the origin of this mysterious and distant stone.
"Our amazing result implies the existence of a large, water-rich parent body," said the first author and planetary geologist Vicky Hamilton of the Southwest Research Institute in Boulder, Colorado.
In the new work, Hamilton and her colleagues analyzed only the smallest pieces of this wonderful cosmic stone.
"We were given a 50-milligram sample of Almahat Sitt for study.
We installed and polished a tiny shard and used an infrared microscope to study its composition, "Hamilton explains.
Spectral analysis showed what scientists didn't expect to find.
Inside the fragment, a fragment called AhS 202, an extremely rare form of hydrated crystals, known as amphiball, was discovered.
The formation of this type of minerals requires long periods of intense heat and pressure, which is usually not possible in carbon chondritis (CC) meteorites.
The findings suggest that the 2008 TC3 probably once belonged to a much larger body — something so large that it actually belonged to the same class as Ceres — a dwarf planet, which is the largest known object in the main belt of the asteroids of the Solar System, between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter.
"Most parent bodies of CC meteorites have a diameter of less than 100 km and therefore will not be large enough to create a range of pressure and temperature conditions presented in the mineral composition AhS 202.
Thus, our interpretation is that the original parent body AhS 202 was probably an unknown object, potentially the size of Ceres, "the authors explain in their article.
Although a giant asteroid no longer exists, this does not cancel the fact that similar to it may have been in the Solar System.
However, until scientists have found traces of these large, water-rich bodies in the recovered fragments of the 2008 TC3 meteorite.
Just as the asteroids Rugh and Bennu reveal new facts about their composition, different from most known meteorites, numerous fragments of the 2008 TC3 prove that there is more in cosmic stones than can fully explain existing hypotheses.
"We do not believe that the AhS 202 is a spectral analogue of Benn or Rugh, rather, the AhS 202 is a random source of information about the early materials of the Solar System, which are not represented by whole meteorites in our collections.
The difference between its mineralogy and the mineralogy of known CC-meteorites suggests that unique specimens, such as AhS 202, can be important missing elements in our understanding of the diversity of parent asteroids, ” the researchers conclude.

Science news: Scientists from Hungary reported the discovery of biosignature in a meteorite.
A group of scientists from Hungary asserts that the Martian rock ALH-77005 found in Antarctica contains many mineralized bio-signatures, including coke-like, filamentous structures and organic material.
An article about the study was published in the journal Open Astronomy.
"Our work is important for a wide audience, as it combines the sciences of planets, Earth, biology, chemistry and the environment and will also be interesting for many researchers in these fields," says Dr. Ildiko Gyollai of the Research Center for Astronomy and Earth Sciences in Budapest. "It will be useful for planetologists, experts studying meteorites and astrobiology, as well as for researchers of the origin of life and simply to the public, as it offers an example of a new aspect of microbial mediation in stone meteorites."
The Ahondrite meteorite ALH-77005 was found to be partially ingrown into the ice in the Allan hills during the polar mission of the Japanese National Institute in 1977-1978.
It has a rounded shape, and its surface is partially burnt, and then it is roughly polished by the ice wind.
His age is estimated at about 175 million years, and the impact of cosmic rays on him lasted about three million years.
Dr. Gyollai and co-authors analyzed the thin layer ALH-77005 using optical and FTIR-ATR microscopy.
They were able to record the presence of coke-like and filamentous structures (probably formed by iron-oxidizing microbes), organic material, and biogenic minerals such as ferrihydrite, getite, and hematite.
"Other features of the ALH-77005 biogenicity include a strong negative δ13C, as well as enrichment with iron, manganese, phosphorus and zinc in a scenario that supports impact melting," scientists say. "Our study involves the presence on Mars of microbial mediation."

A rare meteorite fell on a Costa Rican dog on a box with a dog a rare meteorite fell.
This is reported by The Popular Science.
On April 23, a meteoroid of the size of the washing machine entered the atmosphere above the city of Aguas Zarkas.
When it fell, it fell into hundreds of parts of different sizes.
A piece weighing about a kilogram pierced the roof of the house and fell on the dining table.
Another piece went into the booth where the dog was sleeping by the name of Rocky.
The table was ruined, the dog wasn't hurt.
Scientists found that the meteorite was carbon chondrite and consisted mainly of clay.
“Carbon chondrites are relatively rare, but are among the most valuable for researchers because they allow us to understand the origin of the solar system, ” says the head of the Meteorite Research Center of the University of Arizona Minakshi Wadhwa.
In addition, high-clay meteorites help to develop methods for extracting water from asteroids, which can be useful in space exploration.
The problem is that they are very short-lived and can disappear after the first rain.
According to a press release from the University of Arizona, by May 20, specialists had managed to collect about 25 kilograms of meteorites from Aguas Zarkas.
One of the fragments was found in the dog booth.
In 2018, it was reported that a resident of the American city of Grand Rapids, Michigan, used a meteorite for many years as a stop to the door, which costs about one hundred thousand dollars (6.5 million rubles).

In the Sahara desert (Africa), the remains of a meteorite were found, which could be part of a protoplanet older than Earth.
According to PNAS, a total of about 32 kg of rock was found.
Scientists have determined that the samples do not belong to the most common meteorites - chondrites.
When studying the meteorite, which was called Erg-Shesh 002, it found inclusions of the crystals of piroxene.
Therefore, scientists attributed the sample to achondrites, which consist of magmatic rocks resulting from impact processes.
It is assumed that such meteorites have formed in large bodies, which within themselves have divided into a core and a bark.
Let us explain that there are very few Achondrites on Earth, and most of them (about 95%) are debris from the Moon and Mars.
In addition, such bodies usually contain basalt, and in the found Erg-Shesh 002 it is not.
But there is a magmatic volcanic rock - andesite.
Researching a meteorite on the radioactive isotopes of aluminum and magnesium, scientists have found that his age is about 4.565 billion years.
That is, the found chip, that once was part of a huge cosmic body, older than our planet - 4.51 billion years.
According to the researchers, they found the oldest known specimens of magmatic rock.
As reported by OBOZREVATEL, scientists discovered the debris of a rare meteorite that fell in southwest England.
