The chief author of the work, Philip Heck, from the Field Museum of Natural History, Chicago, USA, says: “We found that the flow of meteorites, that is, the distribution of meteorites by variety, was significantly different from the flow we are witnessing today. ”
Meteorites over the age of 466 million years are very rare, but slightly more often you can find micromethorites of this era - tiny stones less than 2 millimeters.
Heck, along with his Swedish and Russian colleagues, was able to gather a sufficient number of ancient micrometeorites for research.
The samples of micrometeorites were then dissolved in acid, with only the microscopic crystals of the chromite mineral remaining in the insoluble residue, having the property of remaining chemically unchanged for a very long time.
An analysis of the chemical composition of this mineral showed that meteorites and micrometeorites falling on Earth 466 million years ago were significantly different from those that fell later.
34 percent of the meteorites that hit the Earth before the collision belong to the primitive achondrites class – whereas today only 0.45 percent of the meteorites that hit the Earth belong to this type.
Other ancient meteorites turned out to be fragments of Vesta – the brightest asteroid seen from Earth – which also experienced a collision that occurred about one billion years ago.

▲ The famous world cultural site Machu Picchu.
Peruvian President Martin Vizcara recently announced that a million trees would be planted on the periphery of Machu Picchu. Conservation of the site is endangered by landslides and forest fires.
He said the ambitious project is a commitment by government agencies to all people who want to protect the world's wonders.
Environmental protection experts point out that planting trees not only protects sites, but also plants and plants in nearby areas, as well as entire ecosystems.
The Peruvian authorities, in an effort to protect the treasured world heritage, have restricted the number of tourists from 2017 to less than 6,000 a day.
Machu Picchu, the most iconic site of the Inca Empire, was built around 1440 on a ridge at 2,350 to 2,430 metres above sea level, divided into three areas: Temple of the Sun, Temple of the Condor and Intihuatana Stone.

14 June 2018 09: 06 Zhongshi Electronic News Kuo Kuang-chao / General Report / General Report / General Report Meteorite collectors yell for the purchase, a small meteorite can sell several thousand RMB.
(Photo / Yunnan Network) On June 1, at 21: 40, a flying stone fell in the Mannan Village, Mannan Village, Shuangbanna County, western Yunnan Province.
It is learnt that a meteorite found was sold for RMB 160,000 (about NT $740,000).
Scientists have detected meteorite samples that date back 4.5 billion years, the same age as the Solar System.
According to the report, since the discovery of the meteorite in Manlon Village, Shuangbanna County, western Yunnan Province, meteorite collectors come to the village every day to buy the meteorite, a small meteorite can sell several thousand RMB, and a large one can sell tens of thousands to hundreds of thousands RMB.
One meteorite, 1.6 hectare, sold RMB 160,000, the highest price of any meteorite in the region.
It is estimated that more than 200 meteorites have been found in the village of Manlen, and that there may still be 50 to 100 meteorites that have not yet been sold.
Iwaguang, director of the village committee, suggested that the remaining meteorite should be added to the use of value, and that a meteorite culture should be developed from a scientific and reasonable point of view.
Xu Weibiao, chief scientist of the Zijinshan Observatory of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, conducted a slice test of the Shuangbanna meteorite in western Yunnan.
Xu explains, "These spheres date back 4.5 billion years ago, when the Sun was not the Sun, it was an oversized nebula composed of gas and dust. Sometimes lightning and lightning strike occur in the nebula. When they collide, some rocks will fall into space until one day they break into the Earth's gravitational field, are pulled into the atmosphere and fall into the Earth to become meteorites."
Xu Weibiao said that the meteorite, from the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter in the Solar System, belongs to the "L5 Ordinary Chondrite" type, which is about 4.5 billion years old as compared to the Solar System.
At present, Xu Weibiao formally submitted a naming application to the International Committee for the Nomenclature of Meteorites.
As the main meteorite falls in the village of Mangui, it is proposed to name the "Mangui meteorite".
The meteorites found are only one tenth of the original, and most have been burned off at high temperatures before falling, or have not been found after falling.
In other words, meteorites that first flew to Earth may have reached a size of 100,000 kg.
Xu Weibiao believes that this visual meteorite is of great scientific research value, is very fresh, is not weathered by material on Earth, and preserves all kinds of information on the Solar System asteroid in its original form.
(中時電報)

Scientists from Switzerland, the United States and Australia studied the Murchison meteorite, which fell in 1969 in Australia. Scientists crushed a piece of meteorite into powder and dissolved it in acid.
After the chemical reaction, the precipitate remained in the form of insoluble particles of silicon carbide, the size of which was less than 8 micrometers. According to scientific research, the age of pre-solar grains is more than 5 billion years.
In turn, the age of the Sun is equal to 4.5 billion years.

The first project on the search for extraterrestrial life was launched back in 1959 and was aimed at capturing radio signals from distant civilizations.
Today, efforts are largely focused on finding chemical signs of life on fragments of meteorites and other celestial bodies.
Until now, scientists have not succeeded too much in finding alien life, and one of the reasons for this is a lack of understanding what is considered to be its signs and what is not.
In recent years, it has been possible to open thousands of exoplanets where life could potentially exist, so the question of how it can be reliably detected is quite acute.
Advertising Specialists of the University of Glasgow have come up with a new way to find signs of life.
They explained more about it in an article in Nature Communications magazine.
Professor Leroy Kronin and his colleagues developed a concept called assembly theory.
This theory describes how living systems can be distinguished from non-living ones by identifying complex molecules that appear in large quantities and cannot be formed randomly.
The assembly theory defines molecules as biosignatures, manifestations of the effects of life activity.
“Our system is the first life detection hypothesis that can be tested experimentally, ” says Kronin.“ It is based on the idea that only living systems can produce complex molecules that are unable to form randomly in any quantity, and this allows us to bypass the problem of determining what to consider life. ”
By framing the hypothesis, the team began to develop an algorithm to assess the complexity of molecules.
To this end, scientists have introduced the concept of the number of molecular assemblies based on the number of bonds necessary for the formation of a particular molecule.
Naturally, large biogenic molecules will have more numbers than small and non-biogenic ones.
To test its method, the team used a molecular assembly number assignment algorithm for a database containing records of about 2.5 million molecules.
They then selected about a hundred more small molecules and protein (peptide) fragments to test the expected correlation between the number of molecular assemblies and the number of peptides that the molecule would generate when exposed to a mass spectrometer.
In mass spectrometry, the molecules are first ionized, and then the formed ions are separated in a magnetic or electrical field in accordance with the mass number — the ratio of the mass of the ion to its charge.
In collaboration with NASA, the team also explored samples of molecules from around the world and even from space.
Thus, specialists managed to work with a fragment of Murchison meteorite - charcoal chondrite, rich in organic molecules and samples of bottom sediments of the holocene time (30 thousand years ago) and the average miocene (14 million years ago).
On the basis of the data obtained, the team was able to show that life is the only process that can create molecules with a high number of molecular assemblies.
In addition, scientists have established that there is a certain threshold, the intersection of which clearly indicates the presence of life.
“This method allows you to identify life without any prior knowledge of its biochemistry, ” says Sara Imari Walker, co-author of the study.“ It can be used to search for alien life in future NASA missions.
This is a completely new experimental and theoretical approach that will reveal the nature of life in the universe and show how it can arise from lifeless chemical elements. "
“Living and non-living systems are distinguished by the degree in which very complex molecular structures can be formed reliably and in detectable amounts, ” adds her colleague Doug Moore.“ We have decided to show that this is the case, and to offer a useful and biochemically independent biosignature. ”
After more thorough verification and validation, the algorithm can be the basis for future searches for extraterrestrial life.
In addition, it helps to learn more about how life was born on Earth, researchers are sure.
The theoretical basis of the algorithm is one of the first methods to quantify how chemical systems process information.
“We think that this will provide an opportunity for a completely new approach to understanding the origin of living systems on Earth, in other worlds, and hopefully to identify living systems in laboratory experiments, ” Cole Matis, co-author of the study, says.“ From a practical point of view, if we can understand how living systems are capable of self-organizing and producing complex molecules, we can use this knowledge to develop and produce new drugs and new materials. ”
In the coming years, several missions aimed at finding life will go to the outer part of the solar system.
If they use spectrometers equipped with a molecular assembly number analysis algorithm, this will allow you to study the atmosphere, surface and methane lakes of Saturn satellites of Europe, Titan and Encelad for the presence of molecules that can only be formed if life is available.

68 years later, scientists identified an unknown component of the debris of a strange stone found in the remote Australian town of Wedderben.
It turned out to be a mineral that is not found in nature on Earth.
The study is published in the journal American Mineralogist, and briefly tells Science Alert about it.
The debris in question was discovered back in 1951 by the road in a remote Australian town that was at that time experiencing a period of "gold fever".
Its mass is only 210 grams.
He's very unusual in appearance.
Scientists immediately determined that this stone came to Earth from space.
Even then, the analysis showed that the same composition is not found in the nature of our planet.
However, it is only now that researchers from the California Institute of Technology (USA) have managed to solve his mystery.
Modern technologies have made it possible to determine that the meteorite contains a rare form of iron carbide, which has never been found in nature before.
This material was named edscottite in honor of an expert on meteorites and cosmochemist Edward Scott from the University of Hawaii.
Scientists call discovery significant.
There has previously been no evidence that such a unique atomic structure of iron carbide can be created naturally.
The synthetic version of this mineral has been known for decades - it is formed when smelting iron.
According to the planetologist Geoffrey Bonning of the Australian National University, who did not participate in the study, the mineral could have formed in the hot core of some unknown ancient planet.
It should be noted that in previous studies, which lasted more than half a century, traces of gold and iron were revealed in the same meteorite, as well as very rare minerals, such as kamacite, shreibersit, taenite and troilite.
Now in this list added and edscottitis.

The Incas had their own measurement system The Incas had their own elbows measurement system during the construction of buildings, according to Polish architect Anna Kubicka of Wroclaw University of Science and Technology.
And for the houses in which the representatives of the highest nobility lived, a different size was used, "Rossiyskaya Gazeta" reports, citing the publication Nauka w Polsce.
The length of the "royal elbow" was 54 cm, and the length of the "ordinary elbow" was 42 cm. It should be noted that the "royal elbow" does not coincide in length with any part of the human body.
This concept is accepted as conditional and is an unknown value.
In the construction of the palaces, the "royal elbow" was used.
Scientists conducted research in Machu Picchu.
Experts put forward a hypothesis that the city was erected in the first half of the 15th century.
The architects found out that in the process of construction they used a variety of stacking styles.
Therefore, scientists accepted the theory that the Incas used to attract men from other cities for construction work.
Ancient architects were under pressure from imperial architects, who used only a system of measures with a size of 54 cm. There is no data on whether the Incas used this measure in their other cities.

Figure 1.
A is a paleogeographic map of the Earth at the end of the Cretaceous period (66 million years ago).
Grey is represented by mountains, green is the plains, blue is the shelf, blue is the ocean.
Colored icons indicate the places where iridium anomaly is detected; the shape of the icons indicates the size of the anomaly, and their color is the maximum content of iridium (in ppb - parts per billion).
The double black circle is the Chiksulub crater.
B is an example of iridium anomaly in the Gubbio area in Italy: between two thicknesses of limestone, paleogenic top and chalk bottom, there is a thin layer of clay deposits enriched with iridium.
Yellow line shows iridium content in breed (ppb).
Figure from the article under discussion in Science Advances The hypothesis of extinction of dinosaurs about 66 million years ago, as a result of the fall of an asteroid or a large meteorite, received another significant confirmation.
The iridium-rich transition layer, which is found all over the world on the boundary of chalk and paleogenic deposits, is now found in the Chiksulub crater itself.
According to the authors of the new study, this finding clearly indicates the relationship of the impact event, which resulted in the crater, with a global iridium anomaly, which records the time of mass extinction at the turn of chalk and paleogenic periods.
After studying the sequence of deposits and geochemical indicators in the transition layer of the crater, scientists with unprecedented detail restored the chronology of cataclysm.
About 66 million years ago, at the turn of the Cretaceous and paleogenic periods, there was an ecological catastrophe that caused one of the five "great" mass extinctions.
As a result, all non-bird dinosaurs and many other marine and terrestrial organisms disappeared from the face of the Earth.
Most scientists believe that the main trigger of mel-paleogenic extinction was the fall on Earth of the celestial body.
This hypothesis was first made in the late 1970s (see the Alvarez hypothesis) and was based on the fact that in many parts of the world, geologists find a thin layer enriched with iridium and other platinoids, as well as cobalt and nickel (L. W. Alvarez et al., 1980.
Extraterrestrial Cause for the Cretaceous-Tertiary Extinction).
These elements tend to be concentrated in the mantle and nucleus of the Earth, and there are practically no sediments formed on the surface.
At the same time, they are present in asteroids, meteorites and comets, the composition of which, according to scientists, reflects the initial state of the substance of the Solar System.
In 1978, an impact structure was also found, believed to have formed about 66 million years ago, as a result of the fall of an asteroid about 10 km in diameter — the Chiksulub crater in the area of the Yucatan Peninsula in southern Mexico.
The fall of such a large body, according to scientists, could well provoke a chain of serious environmental changes that caused mass extinction (P. Schulte et al., 2010.
The Chicxulub asteroid impact and mass extinction at the Cretaceous-Paleogene boundary).
But there was not enough of the last touch linking the iridium anomaly with the Chiksulub crater — the presence of a layer enriched with iridium in the crater itself.
This allowed opponents of the "asteroid" hypothesis to propose alternative causes of mel-paleogenic extinction, the main of which is mass volcanic eruptions at the edge of chalk and paleogen, which resulted in the formation of the Decan Traps - a large magmatic province located on the Decan plateau in India, one of the largest volcanic formations on the Earth (more details see news Main cause of the meme L-Paleogene extinction — the fall of the asteroid, not the formation of the Decan Trapps, "Elements", 05.03.2020).
In 2016, within the framework of 364 expeditions of the International Ocean Discovery Program (IODP) and the International Continental Scientific Drilling Program (ICDP) from the side of the L / B Myrtle offshore platform, a well M0077 with a depth of 1335 m below the seabed was drilled in a circular lifting of the Chiksulub crater.
In the core range of 616.24 – 617.68 m, scientists found a pronounced transition layer enriched with iridium and other siderophilic and chalcophilic elements, as well as nickel sulphides.
The power of the layer, which was assigned the designation 1G, is about 75 cm. Under it are turbulent precipitations characteristic of standing waves, such as the seismic and tsunami, and above - normal pelagic (deep-sea) limestone, formed in paleogenic time, after the fall of the asteroid.
At a depth of 720 m and below are granite rocks with impact effects, as well as separate horizons of impact-molten rocks.
Above this mark, up to 617.34 m, is a 100-meter thick block impactite (zyuvit) and impact breccia, ending at the top with a transition layer, above which pelagic limestone are already located (fig. 2). Fig. 2.
The sequence of rocks in the core of the well M0077: pink is represented by granitoids, green - layers of impact-molten rocks, lilac - impactites, gray - limestone.
Geochemical testing revealed nickel anomaly in the transition layer 1G, located in the range of 616.54 – 617.34 m. The distribution of elements (Fe, Si, Ca, S, Cr, Ni) at the lower and upper boundaries of the transition layer was studied using the method of volumetric X-ray fluorescence spectrometry (XRF) of high resolution.
Figure from the article under discussion in Science Advances The transition layer 1G itself, consisting of gray-brown argillite and overlapping their green mergels, was formed, according to the authors, as a result of the final deposition of sediment raised by the seishes and tsunamis, as well as atmospheric dust.
For the initial assessment of the distribution in the 1G layer of siderophilic elements, to which iridium and other platinoids belong, scientists chose nickel as an indicator.
The results of geochemical analysis showed that there are intervals of distinct enrichment of Ni > 100 ppm in the upper and lower parts of the layer.
In individual layers with a power of several millimeters, the content of this element exceeds 200 ppm.
Correlates with nickel and the content of another indicator element — chromium.
In the lower interval (from 617.32 to 617.34 m), positive anomalies of Ni are accompanied by the highest concentrations of Re and Os and moderately increased concentrations of Ir, Ru and Pd, and in the upper (from 616.53 to 616.63 m), covering the roof of argillite and the lower part of green mergels, the concentration of Ir is maximum - up to 1 ppb.
This is about an order of magnitude higher than the average in the continental crust (about 0.04 ppb), while in the rest of the 1G layer the content of all elements is about the level of cleric values.
This is a clear iridium anomaly associated with a specific interval of 616.55 – 616.60 m in the upper part of the transition layer associated with meteorite dust, as opposed to individual samples with peak concentrations of iridium (up to 15 ppb), which are noted in the lower interval of the 1G layer of the M0077 well, as in other wells drilled in the continental part of the crane ter.
Such single peak samples, in the authors' interpretation, may be associated with sulphide mineralization resulting from the activation of the hydrothermal system in the impact crater (see news Within a million years after the fall of the asteroid near the Chiksulubsky crater, a hydrothermal system, "Elements", 25.06.2020).
In order to separate the geochemical maxima of iridium, nickel and other siderophilic elements associated with an extraterrestrial substance from sulphide mineralization, the authors conducted an isotope analysis of osmium.
Its results showed that sulphide material prevails in the lower interval.
This also confirms the large number of finds of sulphide minerals at the base of the 1G layer and the upper part of the impactite stratum (fig. 3).
Detailed cut of transition layer 1G and geochemical profiles Ir, Ni and isotope ratio of osmium 187Os / 188Os.
Colored icons indicate the different laboratories where the tests were performed.
Iridium anomaly (shown in blue) is timed to the top of the layer, where gray-brown argillites are replaced by green mergels (on a square notch).
Yellow hexagons near the core column are sulfide mineralization intervals (Sulfide intervals).
Figure from the article under discussion in Science Advances The authors of the study believe that the iridium anomaly as a whole has remained in its place, in the range of 616.55 – 616.60 m, and the increased concentration of Ir in the lower interval and throughout the 1G layer is associated with its redistribution as a result of later diagenetic and hydrothermal processes in the porous and permeable thickness of rocks disturbed by impact ♪ Oh,
Based on the fact that most of the iridium remained in place, scientists estimated the total deposition flux of this element in the Chiksulub crater at 20 ng / cm2.
For comparison, the world average for the iridium layer at the boundary of chalk and paleogen is estimated at 55 ng / cm2.
Scientists explain this by the fact that everywhere, except for the Chiksulub crater, iridium concentrates in the microscopic spinel spinels of the type of tectites (for more information on tectites, see the news Found a source of Australian tectites - the largest meteorite crater in the last million years, "Elements", 20.01.2020).
Unlike impactants resulting from percussive metamorphism and remaining in the crater, tectites fly far from the site of a meteorite or asteroid crash.
They are resistant to weathering and atmospheric effects, and they better retain impurity elements, including iridium.
And in the 1G layer, according to researchers, iridium is present in the form of nanoscale dust.
It is not possible to determine its mineral form.
The authors suggest that this dust was generated by a powerful evaporative release resulting from the contact of the heated impactor material with the highly volatile rocks of the Earth's crust and then precipitated for several years (up to 20 years).
The impact event ended with the deposition of green marbles formed in pelagic conditions at a rate of about 0.5 cm / thousand years.
Thus, the formation of a layer of mergels with a capacity of about 3 cm took about 6000 years.
After that, the system was completely balanced, and from that point on, limestone deposits, already related to the paleogenic period, began to be deposited.
Source: Steven Goderis, Honami Sato, Ludovic Ferriere, Birger Schmitz, David Burney, Pim Kaskes, Johan Vellekoop, Axel Wittmann, Toni Schulz, Stepan M. Morgan, Jan Smit, Michael T. Whalen, IDP-ICDP Expedition 364 Scientists.

Sometimes meteorites fall on Earth, but have you ever heard of a tasteless meteorite?
The Field Museum of Natural History in Chicago has recently added a new collection, a meteorite weighing about 4 pounds in size that looks like a mud block, but has a unique aroma.
It is reported that the smell is closest to the cooked sprouts of kale, or as a clue to the formation of matter in the Solar System.
The museum laughs at other "smelly" meteorite collections.
The Aguas Zarcas meteorite, which is different from other stony or metal-like meteorites, was dropped on April 23 this year in Costa Rica. It has a natural odour of organic volatile compounds such as amino acids.
In fact, many carbonaceous chondrites have different odors, but are more often damaged by terrestrial amino acids when they fall to Earth, so they are less common.
About 50,000 meteorites have been found on Earth, mainly iron, polystonite, stone, and metal mixtures, accounting for only about 4% of the total.
There is another Mercheson meteorite that fell in 1969, which smells of tar.
These meteorites are likely to be the bedrock of life on Earth billions of years ago, and scientists may be able to explore the mysteries of the Solar System through research.

Mexican scientists revealed the mystery of the ancient pyramids of the Maya 1 3.06.2020, 9: 10 4,567 illustration Photo Why grandiose buildings had this form?
Archaeologists of the National Institute of Anthropology and History of Mexico (INAH) concluded that the pyramids built by the indigenous peoples of Mesoamerica, including the Maya and Aztecs, imitate natural objects such as mountains and embankments.
On the scientific work, revealing the secret of ancient structures, reported on the website of the scientific organization, writes lenta.ru.
According to archeologist Pedro Sanchez, one of the authors of the work dedicated to the mystery of the origin of the Mexican pyramids, these structures have a geometric shape, which is most commonly found in nature.
For example, the pyramids are similar to the silhouettes of mountains and volcanoes, as well as deposits of sedimentary material.
In addition, this form is suitable for the construction of high monumental structures.
Scientists believe that the pyramids were also endowed with sacral and mythical meanings.
They became the axis or center around which the cities were built, and this was a kind of reflection of the worldview of the pre-Columbian peoples.
Mesoamerican pyramids usually have a stepped structure, and on their top there was often a temple.
They were built by Aztecs, Maya and other American indigenous cultures.

Spotnik News Agency publishes the most interesting and bizarre "Condoleeza Rice" statements about Iran.

Scientists today point out that a meteorite that struck southeastern Australia in 1969 contains the oldest solid matter ever discovered on Earth, the dust that existed billions of years before the formation of the Solar System.
The meteorite fell to Murchison, Victoria state, Australia, in 1969.
Scientists at the Field Museum in Chicago held one of the pieces for more than 50 years.
By crushing meteorite fragments into powder, the researchers examined the microparticles contained in them.
Scientists have developed methods to determine the age of stardust, in which particles of dust floating in space are constantly hit by high-energy particles of cosmic rays that divide the atoms in the stardust particles into fragments, such as carbon atoms into helium atoms.
The longer these fragments are exposed to cosmic rays, the more they accumulate, and by calculating the number of fragments in the laboratory, scientists can determine the age of stardust.
Researchers say the 40 oldest tiny dust particles in meteorite fragments date back to about 7 billion years ago, about 2.5 billion years before the formation of the Sun, Earth and other Solar System stars.
All of the dust analysed in the study is actually material produced before the formation of the Solar System, known as the "presolar grain", of which 60% is 4.6 – 4.9 billion years old and the oldest 10% dates back more than 5.6 billion years.
Researchers point out that stardust is the time capsule before the solar system is created.
The age distribution of the dust provides a clue as to the rate of star formation in the Milky Way, since the years of the massive dust are concentrated in specific time zones, suggesting that the stars are explosive rather than at constant rates.
Philipp Heck, deputy director of the Field Museum of Natural History, who led the study, said, "I am extremely excited about it.
Even if I have studied Muchison meteorites and pre-solar particles for nearly 20 years, I am still fascinated by the fact that we can study the history of our Milky Way with rock.
The study was published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences in the United States.

Cosmonauts say that from space, the Earth seems completely defenseless — a tiny blue balloon wrapped in a subtle atmosphere that seems to blow the lightest breeze.
It gets a little creepy when you realize that falling on Earth is a very small asteroid the size of Moscow, for life will come not the best times.
However, at different times in its history, the Earth has already been subjected to the fall of large meteorites and, as you can see, is intact.
Let's remember the most famous of them.
This meteor flew through the Earth's atmosphere above Siberia in 1908 and exploded just a few kilometers from the Siberian surface.
The explosion had the power of an atomic bomb and destroyed trees within 800 square kilometers.
It took years for scientists to start exploring an abandoned and uninhabited area; a hundred years later, they are still looking for convincing evidence of a meteorite falling in the form of a crater or body debris.
They say the meteorite hit Nicole Tesla, but that's just another reason for conspiracy theory.
Someone thinks the crater is hiding in the nearest lake.
Others believe that at the last moment an alien ship destroyed a meteorite so that it would not destroy the Earth.
Well, we have something to learn from aliens.
As you know, dinosaurs died, most likely as a result of an asteroid strike.
Together with them, more than half of all species on the planet have died.
Scientists are not 100% sure that it was this asteroid that triggered the so-called K-T extinction, but they have some reason to believe that the villain came from space.
Most of the soil during the event (K-T layer) contains a lot of iridium, which is much on asteroids, but little on Earth.
Scientists believe that about 65 million years ago, one or more iridium comets or meteorites fell on Earth, raising dust into the atmosphere and causing large-scale climate change.
Where did this guest fall?
No one knows, but some researchers believe that the crater on the Yucatan Peninsula in Mexico is the same place.
The Hoba Vesom meteorite of 60 tons, the Hoba meteorite, which is still in its place in Namibia, is the largest known meteorite on the planet.
The flat iron plate fell to the ground about 80,000 years ago, so we do not know for sure which pyrotechnic show was accompanied by her arrival, but only found it in 1920, when the farmer dug his field and stumbled upon the metal top.
Since then, Hoba has become a national asset, attracting thousands of visitors annually.
The Villamett Weights meteorite is about 15 tons and three meters high, this massive bony piece of iron is believed to be the remnant of the planet's iron core, which crashed billions of years ago.
Thousands of years ago, Villamette fell on our planet and was discovered only in 1902 by peace-loving Americans in the form of a healing source of the Clacamas — Tomanovos Indians.
Tomanovos now rests at the American Museum of Natural History in New York, but recently the Indian tribe made a deal with the museum so that the meteorite would remain in its place for as long as the clacamas would visit it for ceremonial purposes.
When this massive iron meteorite roared from the sky in February 1947, according to eyewitnesses, it was brighter than the sun.
And when the explosion ripped it apart, its shards shattered the ground on an area of half square kilometers in the mountains of Sikhote-Alin in Siberia.
The entrance to the atmosphere and the explosion were visible within 200 kilometers.
For many years, meteorite hunters have been searching around this area in search of well-recognized metal bars twisted and twisted in an interesting way.
Small pieces of Sikhote-Alin are still on sale.
Sulakoga Meteorite In one of the autumn days of 1954, a 31-year-old housewife from Alabama named Ann Hodges was sleeping on the couch when a five-kilogram meteorite fell from the sky.
He punched the roof and hit the woman in the thigh.
Fortunately, Hodges was bruised, while the neighbors saw a fireball in the size of a grapefruit, which slit the sky.
Hodges received her minute of fame, and later gave a meteorite to the Alabama Museum of Natural History.
In Germany, there was a precedent: the boy said that a meteorite had also fallen on him while going to school.
A 14-year-old schoolboy said he saw a flash of light, and then he was hit by a meteorite the size of a pea.
Who knew pebbles could be so dangerous.
ALH 84001 ALH 84001 (call it Al for short) was discovered in Antarctica in 1984, 13,000 years after arriving from Mars.
Yes, from Mars.
Al was born from the lava of the Martian volcano about four and a half billion years ago.
15 million years ago, he lay on the surface of Mars, and then another asteroid or meteorite released him, sending him to Earth, after which he landed on the Allan Hills in Antarctica.
Inside Al there may be evidence of early Martian life in the form of fossilized algae or a small number of bacteria.
The meteorite Orheil Meteorite Orheil burned the atmosphere in May 1864, falling into 20 pieces on the way to the French town of Orheil.
The fragments were soft enough to be cut with a knife, and very soon the remains of the meteorite spread to museums around the world.
Since then, the meteorite Orheil has caused a lot of controversy, as scientists have long wondered where the organic material brought with it came from — and what if this is proof of extraterrestrial life?
But in fact, although the meteorite itself was real, the signs of life were falsified.
How did you do that?
Some spores were glued to coal dust.
But this has already happened in our world.
In 1992, the meteorite crossed the sky over Kentucky and Pittsburgh with a greenish flame and fell in Pickskill on a parked car, which was not to blame.
It was the 1980 Chevy Malibu, which received only a massive dent and continued to drive around the world as a car that survived a meteor attack.
And the meteorite was the most ordinary piece of iron the size of a bowling ball.
What was strange was the degree of attention given to the Pixilla meteorite.
Due to the fact that he crossed the east coast, his path and trajectory were recorded in video and analyzed by scientists, but it turned out that this is the most common meteorite. And sorry.
Murchison meteorite Murchison meteorite fell into hundreds of pieces, falling in Australia in September 1969.
The largest of the pieces weighed about 50 kg, the smallest - less than 200 grams.
He fell to the ground with a massive fireball, followed by a foggy tail, and then fell apart.
Fragments are still being studied.
It turned out that the meteorite contains a wide range of amino acids, building blocks of life, so there is obvious interest in it from astrobiologists.
Alende meteorite Alende, which fell to Earth in 1969 in Mexico, split into hundreds of fragments in flight.
Taken together, they will weigh a few tons.
Fragments, of course, split into private collections.
Many of the black stones are covered with vitreous material, which is formed under the influence of high temperatures during passage through the atmosphere.
The meteorite contains particles that may be older than our solar system, olives, and even microscopic diamonds.

For violation of the specified conditions, the organizers may be behind bars.
[14] There is official censorship in the United States.
It has a lot of legislation to do with it.
As early as 1798, the United States Congress passed the Sedition Act, which prohibits "writing, printing, speaking, or publishing... any false, scandalous or vicious essay about the government, president, and congress of the United States, punishing a violation of up to two years in prison."
The same act was then adopted in 1918. Since then, many more stringent laws and acts have been passed that restrict the freedoms of the media and citizens of the United States.
A law against Internet piracy, or SOPA (Stop Online Piracy ACT), is being discussed.
After its adoption on the free of Americans on the Internet, it will be possible to put a fat point.
But the more important thing in the US is the “censorship of conspiracy ” – that is, the guidelines for all editors of print media and TV channels on how exactly to comment on a particular event: everything that the US does in the world (and they do terrible things nearby) should be presented exclusively in the pink light.
So the American media are showing total self-censorship.
[15] Oxfam, a non-profit organization, has counted all the wealth of the world and has come to a special conclusion that the distribution of money on our planet has changed significantly in 2015.
Namely, all the money of the world, formerly owned by 300 people, now belongs to 62 people.
The others are borrowing from these financiers, either directly or indirectly...
The so-called top 7 influential dynasties in the world are composed.
The essence is: the main stock of money from 5 families and "little things" in their surroundings.
The US dollar is not the property of the US state, but belongs to a network of private banks.
These banks decide how much to print and print.
And the U.S. government is "borrowing" its money from them.
These 5 families and servants not only own the dollar, but also the euro.
[16] Polyarchy – “multi-power, the power of many ” – is a questionable theory about the benefits of a political system based on the open political competition of various groups in the struggle for support of voters.
In modern political science, the term was introduced in 1953 by Robert Dahl.
Dahl sees the polyarchive as a "clean" version of existing systems and concludes that its institutions are necessary (though perhaps insufficient) to achieve an ideal democracy.
The polyarchive model is also used as a standard for measuring the level of democracy.

Andrey Chabrov Virtual travel is gaining popularity in these vague times.

So in Peru, in the footsteps of the great Incas!

We ordered the tour at the travel agency, because I am deeply convinced that if you fly to a strange country for the first time, independence - that expired medicine is harmful!

We flew out at 5 am in Moscow, and landed in the capital of Peru at 5 pm of the same day.
Meeting with the guide, quick transfer to the hotel.

Lima turned out to be a modern city, and we made a mistake, believing that we had come to a country less developed than Europe.
We were dispelled by all the misgivings we had at the agency.

But Lima relaxed and let me believe everything's gonna be okay.
We spent the evening not far from the hotel in the cafe, and did not take a single picture, although the approach to the ocean for night shooting was.
It should be noted that when you are going to a photo tour or a photo trip, you need to understand that your usual mode needs to be rebuilt - to get up until dawn, to lie at midnight and in general, very much to move, because, as the truth says, laziness is the main enemy of the photographer.
The next morning, we left for Cusco.
The landing of the plane allowed us to see the whole city.
Beauty is incredible.
After the hatches opened at the plane and we went to the ladder in the head something refused and there was a feeling of slight intoxication, dizziness.
It's nice at first.

From that moment on, our journey through Peru and Bolivia has actually begun.
The city of Cusco was the place where the history of the great Inca era began in the 11th century.
We were lucky to have a guide, a Muscovite Olga, who told us in great detail.
Once upon a time, after graduating from Moscow university, she married a Peruvian classmate and risked changing Moscow to Cusco, a bold act at the time, but which of us in Soviet times did not dream of moving from the coupon system somewhere abroad.
Lump is really the capital of the Incas, and it struck us with the contrast of the buildings of the Spanish era and the local Peruvians.
This is the confrontation of stone, cement, spire and statues on the one hand and simple shacks of clay blocks made directly in the yard from the local land, often unplastered, on the other.
And when the guide told us that in just 20 years, in the 16th century, the Spaniards conquered and eliminated the Inca Empire, which had been built for more than 400 years, it was clear why.
A pragmatic, high-tech and science-based policy of invading, longing for cheap gold on the one hand and chaste, primordial, based on harmony with nature and uniform laws for all policies on the other.
I want to mention one point about the laws of the Incas: the Spaniards themselves, when they studied the organization of the Incas state and their laws were very impressed with their harmony, reality and legal purity.
You can talk about this city for a long time, but I will note two points that have shifted my consciousness.
When we arrived at Saqsaywaman Park, we were surprised, like everyone else for the first time, that in the 12th century, people without complex tools could fit stones and blocks (up to 2 tons) to each other so tightly that even hair did not pass and modern processing with diamond saws gives worse result!
This stone theme we met later everywhere (especially in Machu Picchu), but each time we were surprised by the filigree fit even at the corner 3-D crossings of stones from the football ball, to a block the size of an SUV!
And the night view of Cusco.
There is a place above the city, an observation deck with Jesus with open arms (like in Rio de Janeiro), and from there, if you climb up and stall in advance, you can make an excellent panorama of the night Cusco, like the erupting lava of a huge volcano, its streets in thousands of lights burn, demonstrating how high ordinary people can climb and build from clay blocks of hibara, if he gets the permission to take over the mountainous terrain!
After shooting several options of panoramic frames, we went to the very center of the city for dinner.
It should be noted that in travel we have developed a standard approach to any day in terms of nutrition.
For maximum sightseeing, we have switched to a two meals a day.
I remember tea with coca leaves.
When we were served, plain green tea, with floating leaves.

But of course it turned out to be a divorce, although they exploit the associations of Europeans with coca for all 100! (what are, for example, candy with coca).

Service for three with a plus and then because of the language barrier - not everyone speaks English.
The next morning, we were happy to go to Machu Picchu.
With joy, because the head continued to hurt continuously, the air was periodically lacking, and the rise to three to four steps caused a desire to stand and rest.
By the way, this is the feeling, apparently, experienced coming from all over the world football teams, who have not yet won a single match from the Peruvian national team at her home, but at the same time ruthlessly her crushing in their homeland.
4000 meters is even funny for climbers, but it is a serious height for an ordinary European.
To combat this disease, we were offered to breathe from oxygen balons in all the hotels, but we, the guys are strong, kept and pretending to throw "no", slightly swaying.
Before visiting one of the main goals of our voyage - Machu Picchu, we visited the city of Morai, where there are famous Inca agricultural terraces, descending into which a person seems to be in a giant dish.
Not far away, in the city of Moras, there are salt mines, where the locals still produce salt for sale in the most prehistoric way.
In these rented terraces, they launch salty water and then evaporate it.
There, by the way, some local "Indian" blocking us the path and jerking on our "elegant" cameras, suddenly began to say something nervously, removing from our faces a intense confusion.
Our guide, as a panther, threw himself on him, his own tongue and threw away, and we were warned that this local wants money for professional shooting with such devices.
We, thirsty for justice, put brick expressions on our faces, pulled out the tripods, and demonstratively, turning louder, began shooting the mines, occasionally spitting towards the freebie.
So Machu Picchu, it is located below Cusco in height from sea level, only 2,800 meters.
We have a strong desire to get rid of the constant lack of air at Cusco heights and to breathe in full breasts, given that Moscow and Tula, for example, at altitudes from 250 to 350 meters, and we breathe there from birth under the appropriate pressure in the units of Blaise Pascal, i.e. adapted to the more "heavy" column of air above us, and falling to large heights, for example, 4000 meters, while the external pressure decreases (the size of the air column above us decreases), our big Russian heart continues to drive blood at the same speed as in the homeland, proportionally increasing our pressure, here and dizzy, so I want a simple Russian guy much lower!
More air than that!
Therefore, when arriving in high-altitude places, we need 2 - 3 days for adaptation until the body adjusts the necessary heart contractions and, accordingly, blood pressure.
We are surprised to find that the train is the only way to get to Machu Picchu.
I have been interested in trains since I was a child, but there was one surprise waiting for us!
The stewards that served us, like those on planes, carrying food and drink with an important and slightly stretched expression of the face, changed for one moment and began to walk on the train to music, depicting models and showing to all of us, under the applause of the Germans and Americans sweaters and jackets of 50 years old old prét-a-porter!
I had nothing but a smile, and the Americans bought it for $500, and the stewards applauded them, disappointing even more!
We arrived in the town itself and realized that our hotel is right above this railway station and all the trains during these 3 days we heard, felt, met and escorted, while at night they were for some reason the most and we never slept!
In the morning we drove to the mountain itself by bus, and only so, because UNESCO banned other types of lifts because of fear of unnecessary destruction, except for another way on foot, but this would be that you would represent 600 meters on an absolutely vertical rock on the hollowed steps.
By the way, this is the path used by those Incas who lived in Machu Picchu.
It is difficult to imagine what happened if even in the middle of the way the Inca suddenly remembered that he forgot to buy matches from the list that he received from his wife.
Picchu hit Machu.
Built of stones on stones, without a single gram of cement and lasting hundreds of years, preserved the mystery of origin and puzzled many scientists that no stone of man was found.
So people just left here.
He was abandoned for several hundred years, and only a hundred years ago he was accidentally opened! (in 1911).
No more than 2,400 people can enter Machu Picchu per day.
This is limited to the same "caring" UNESCO and now this organization requires even more restrictions on access, because in their opinion the monument is simply trampled.
By the way, opposite Machu Picchu (picchu is a mountain) is Vainu Picchu, which can also be climbed, but there is a limit of 400 people.
It is visible in all the photos from these places, as the highest mountain, but we did not succeed there, so there was too much program.
The next morning was foggy, but for the photographer there is no better luck than to get an object for shooting in a small haze and lit by the sun.
That's the last thing we were hoping for when we were up.
The sun looked literally for a few minutes.
But by that time, the tripods had already been installed and the cameras had been set up.
From the beauty that opened up and almost instantly hid behind a dense fog, it became uneasy.
For four more hours we roamed all over the slopes and enjoyed the height, nature, mystery and feeling of something eternal.
In addition, it was nice to meet a group of Russians of 20 people and already as an experienced expert of the area to tell where the best views are from!
We are gathered for a new conquest of this country.
The path lay in Puno, and then in Bolivia.
Puno is a town on the shore of Lake Titicaca.
There is such a joke in the local-- Titi is in Peru, and Kaka is in Bolivia.
This lake is a steamboat made by the British.
And there are ships that were brought and assembled on the shore of this lake.
There are those that were produced by the locals.
The latter are clearly losing on the outside.
There are only 5 species of fish in the lake.
The other couple of hundred ate the salmon brought and released into the lake.
The idea was clearly without miscalculation of the consequences.
So in any restaurant you will always be offered King fish in a lot of options.
The road to the floating islands ran along the highland Peru, in the car, I had a universal wrist assistant "Casio" with a borometer and I constantly compared the elevation marks on the columns with the readings of the electronic device.
The 4400 m is the maximum I've noted.
Absolutely straight roads and 7 hours of moving Sergey and I were put to sleep.
Two days were allocated to Puno.
The main attraction was the islands of Uros, where the legendary Tour Heyerdale built braided boats and sailed to the Easter Islands, and this is a few thousand kilometers from there and without any motor, it was not once and even in groups, which, incidentally, included Yuri Senkevich, the most famous traveler in the USSR.
Going into town, I thought he was abandoned.
Old cars, hundreds of different motorcycles and some strange combinations of bicycles and benches with wheels run through the streets.
Our hotel was five kilometers from the city, so we planned a tour the day after the islands of Uros.
In addition, the guide who met us promised to show the old Inca cemetery.
During lunch, we watched a strange dance of absolutely normally dressed people on the playground in front of the hotel.
We later learned that it was the mayor of the city with his entourage.
But what kind of weird ritual body movements he was doing in a three-piece costume was a mystery.
We just ruined a local great beer.
According to the fashionable now theory of "antifragility" sometimes it is better to just contemplate, without delving into the essence of what is happening.
And finally, the most legendary floating islands of Uros, which I remember from the "Traveller Club" since childhood.
To begin with, imagine that you always live on an island based on a peat slab about a meter in thickness, covered with cane, which grows immediately and it needs to be updated every week.
The islands are constantly drifting from the winds and sometimes the inhabitants anchor.
The locals are busy with only two things: women take guests, showing ten times a day of the scene and playing performances, and men...
We hardly saw them.
According to legend, they roam on boats along coastal areas, cutting off a cane to update the island ’ s coverage and catching fish.
In total, a thousand square meters of straw that people live on.
Inside the island are made lakes in which fish are grown, chickens and sheep roam.
There's even a school, and some houses have solar panels.
Normal life, but constantly springy surface and red color after hours two stays force more often to look at the shore.
By the way, some locals do not even have passports and they do not go to the big land, because once this escape from the land, where all the documents were thrown, was a defensive step before the fear of civil war.
One of the goals for us was to make portraits of local residents.
There's no problem with that, by the way.
It's a separate business.
The locals have long understood this and pose, smile, accept any images and for this you need to give a coin.
After the islands, we went to see the ancient cemetery of rich Incas.
It is not an abandoned place, but a state-supported object of historical value near the city of Sillustani.
We even met a group of archaeologists who opened another grave for further restoration in its original form.
The silence, the noise of the wind between our alien cup-shaped tombs, the crawling lizards and the flight of some mighty birds in the sky pacify our own soul.
It was not a place of death, it was a place of rest.
The next morning we went to Bolivia.
Through Altiplano.
To be continued.

A student of the Kazan Federal University (KFU) Claudia Petrenko discovered a substance of extraterrestrial origin at the Kamsko-Ustyinsky oil field.
The discovery attracted scientists with extremely dense microclues.
They investigated the microspheres and microparticles contained in the gypsum rock.
Researchers published in SpringerLink say that the age of the sample found may exceed 270 million years.
A preliminary study of the composition showed that iron was the main component.
Photo source: press service of Kazan Federal University (KFU).
According to the associate professor of the department of regional geology and minerals of the Institute of Geology and Oil and Gas Technologies of KFU Rail Kadyrov, scientists came to the conclusion that the microadventures from the Upper Kazan subtier have extraterrestrial genesis.
Most likely, it is about meteor ablation, the process of destruction of the surface of the meteor body when moving in the atmosphere.
“Scientists suggest that the meteor body was destroyed during the movement in the atmosphere that occurred over the territory under study 272.95 million years ago, ” the KFU released.
Tons of meteorites Mineral deposits are unique sources of meteorite discoveries.
According to the "360" geologist, Candidate of Technical Sciences Stanislav Zavacki, every year they are found hundreds of tons.
“It ’ s hard to say if there ’ s any benefit from this finding.
No organic matter has been found yet.
Chemical findings are predictable.
The structure, too.
Some say there are meteorites that came to us from Mars or other solar systems.
But I think it is necessary to be more cautious in such terms, because it is very difficult to confirm or prove otherwise.
So far, information is being collected.
Everything else is bold hypotheses and guesses.
There is no clear theory, "said the expert.
According to him, thanks to such findings, you can learn how the Solar System was born.
"If it is a meteorite that has been evidently in it.
Came from the asteroid belt that is between Mars and Jupiter.
About the meteorites from the Kuiper belt or the Oort cloud on the edges of the solar system, we can't say they're definitely ours.
They can be intercepted from another solar system, "Zavacki summed up.

In 1896, the United States Supreme Court ruled in Pless v. Ferguson that "separation but equality" did not violate the Fourteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution and established the legalization of racial segregation.
The image is a water dispenser labeled "colored" for black people.
Through various civic organizations, such as the National Association of Colored Women ’ s Clubs, the National Council of Negro Women, and the Association for the Study of Negro Life and History, they seek to improve the plight of the race in a different way than men.
In the 1920s and 1950s, black women engaged in the process of fighting for racial rights, and in fact promoted their status as black women, as well as the situation of racial and gender vulnerability. Mary McLeod Bethune (1875-1955), Mary Church Terrell (1863-1954) were two important representatives.
▲ The history of the 1920s and 1950s, compared to the high-conflict events of previous scholars, in "or in the abyss - black American women in racial predicament, 1920 – 1950", is more focused on the analysis of how black women adjust between compromise and resistance, carefully grasp opportunities, realistically accumulate energy, and achieve their goals. At the beginning of the 20th century, when the first wave of women ’ s movements was in full swing to fight for women ’ s right to vote, black women, Mary Bechu, argued that the world ’ s colored people must cooperate with each other and encourage black women to participate actively in politics.
Born in 1875, she was interested in education from an early age. She often worked as a small teacher at home, lecturing her out-of-school siblings and neighbors. In 1904, she founded the Daytina and Industrial Training School for Negro Girls, a 30-year-old school in Florida.
At the time, almost only white people were allowed to attend school, and Bechu decided to run a school for black girls, initially with only five young girls and her son.
Later, the school became more mature and became the first black school in the region to be sponsored by the white community.
With cross-racial cooperation, hundreds of black people have been trained to complete high school in just over a decade.
For Bechu, where blacks are inferior to whites and black women than white women, it is possible to pursue racial and gender equality only by fighting for equal access to education for black women.
Bechu is well aware that racial inequality is rooted in the structure of the social system, and that it is not possible to overcome all discrimination against the "black skin" by virtue of the excellence of individual black people.
On the one hand, she has increased black education opportunities and cross-racial cooperation within the system, and on the other hand, through history teaching, she has actively challenged the dominant white culture to rebuild black identity and honor in her own history and community.
In order to raise funds, Bechu spoke about the idea and invited white people to visit the school.
At the same time, she has made a clear record of all the money, creating a clean image that meets the expectations of the mainstream.
In the course design, both technical and vocational training are arranged to meet the sponsor ’ s orientation towards black education, but Bechu also insists on incorporating general education to allow black people to receive the same courses as white people.
During President Franklin Roosevelt's tenure, Becher was involved in racially related affairs in the New Deal, recommending black people to work in government departments, giving them the opportunity to actually participate in political activities.
Because of her frequent exodus, she also found that there were racial problems in Europe, and therefore pushed for cross-international cooperation to bring the issue of race in the United States into line with the issue of poverty and weakness in the international race of people of color.
On both the conservative and aggressive sides, Bechu plays a pragmatic and flexible card.
"Reading her story will be very shocking. A woman like this in the late 19th and early 20th centuries has such an eye, and the height is really ahead of schedule!" says Huang Wen-ling.
▲ In addition to being an excellent educator and a social activist, Mary Bechu has been listed as one of the greatest women in the United States.
When she was chairman of the Board of Directors of the National Alliance of Black Women, she actively participated in public affairs, and was later recommended for President Roosevelt's "New Deal" to exert real influence.
Mary Terrell, an old grandmother of the Civil Rights Movement, was born with a golden spoon, and was known as "the old grandmother of the Civil Rights Movement", compared to Bershu, whose parents were slaves.
Mary Terrell was born in Memphis, Tennessee in 1863.
Tyrell's father was the first black millionaire in the South because of the severe yellow fever epidemic in the South in the 1970s, and many whites left, leaving large tracts of land to give him a chance to become rich through his real estate business.
Terrell studied in Europe for two years at the end of the 19th century.
Despite its high social class, Terrell was discriminated against.
The middle and upper classes who dress, talk, and have good knowledge like her will be ridiculed and humiliated for their skin color, not to mention the poor black people!
Throughout the first half of the 20th century, Terrell was active in the organization of black women's societies and became a prominent black people's rights activist, which was closely related to the encouragement and support of her husband, Robert Heberton Terrell.
He was the first black person at Harvard Law School and the most rare black judge in Washington.
In order to gain the support of the then black leader, Booker T. Washinton, Terrell played an active role in helping her husband, believing that "being a wife must not hinder her husband's future because of her words and deeds".
As a result of her husband ’ s career, Terrell has been suppressed for personal rights. However, in the event of racial issues, especially education problems related to the century-old project, she has come forward and argued! She participated in the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NACP) to criticize the government ’ s racial policies and practices. As a result, Watson complained privately to Mr. Terrell, asking him to take care of his wife.
However, instead of doing so, Mr. Terrell actively encouraged his wife not to retreat, because they fought for black racial rights and did not challenge her husband's masculinity and face.
It can be said that Tyrell's success in entering the traditional family sphere after his marriage was to fight for "black racial rights" rather than "black women's rights".
▲ Before the 1960s, the segregation system was widespread in the United States, Terrell launched a number of counter-measures, such as paying money to go to the music hall, allowing only black people to attend; when the hotel refused black people to stay, rented the entire hotel to provide black people with use.
Until the age of 80, Terrell continued to fight against segregation in public places.
Bechu and Terrell both fought for the rights of the black, but how it was done, the two were on a very different spectrum.
Bershu argued that black women should be brave enough to step out of the family, take part in public affairs, and improve the collective situation of black people from the state, but Terrell, aware of the norms of white society towards women, believed that black women's primary responsibility was to become good mothers and good wives and put black families on a better track.
Black women should also strive to be better women, elegant, polite, educated (like white women), and have a good racial image.
Why is there such a difference? Mr Wong considers it necessary to include a "class" factor analysis.
Bechu is from a slave family and belongs to a lowly but professional middle class. She values the rights of the black people and believes that it is possible to improve their racial image by creating more opportunities for them.
Terrell, who is a black woman of light complexion (mulatto) and upper middle class, has advocated the promotion of lower social class women to teach them the right code of conduct in order to avoid being held hostage.
The accusation of any black woman would be applied to all black women.
Women who fought for the blacks must be more like good women in the eyes of whites. At the time, the Victorian Womanhood was the ideal female requirement of the mainstream society of the 1920s, to run the family, to maintain beauty, to observe morals, and to meet couples.
According to Huang Wen-ling, black women are often confined to what white society calls "good women" as a code of conduct.
However, for black societies, racial oppression outweighs gender inequality, so racial issues tend to give higher priority to issues of comparability, and many black men are willing to cooperate.
For example, when Terrell was hesitant about public speaking invitations, she was often encouraged by her husband to urge her to work for the black community.
It is also in the interest of racial development that Robert is willing to enter the private domain to take care of his daughter, so that his wife can be active in the public domain.
In other words, if black women are to become "good women" recognized by white society, black skin is an unwieldy burden.
However, for black women to fight for entry into the public sphere, racial factors are the key to helping.
Leave it to those who are ready for the opportunity!
Huang Wen-ling believes that it is precisely in these multiple contradictions that they can see their flexible strategies.
Compromise and resistance are the two sides of the balance, and race and gender are the main weights that affect how black women use compromise, resistance, or mutual use.
Regardless of the approach taken, it reflects that they have chosen the most advantageous way to break down the resistance and achieve the goals set.
From the perspective of historical development, "homeward" is indeed the most advantageous strategy, Bechu and Terrell are good examples.
“Crouching, getting ready, you can take your chances! They did it in the 1920s, and that ’ s what I admire most. ”
▲ In 1955, Rosa Louise McCauley Parks was arrested for refusing to give up her position to white people, leading to a "boycott of the bus movement".
The incident took place on Thursday, Friday, with 50,000 leaflets printed and a shuttle bus arranged.
In the 1920s and 1950s, Huang Wen-ling believes that it is important to see how the long journey evolved, accumulate energy, and the subdued potential of many black women.
Looking back from history, the "Freud" event of 2020 has evolved into a riot in the city.
She observed that after the 2016 US presidential election, the polarization of American society has intensified sensitive issues, and the rifts have become more serious.
If a black protest moves into disarray, it may distract the issue and render it impossible to resonate.
Next, is it possible that racial conflict is becoming more and more difficult? "says Huang Wen-ling," From a historical point of view, racial issues are structural issues, and it is difficult to resolve them at once. "
It is not useful to call for fairness. It must be fully implemented from the economy, the right to work, and the right to education, especially fair education resources and opportunities.

In Peru, 30 km west of Lake Titicaca, on the shore of another lake (Laguna Umayo) there is a place called Sillustani.

We will not pay attention to the cult and ritual purposes, which historians and archaeologists so like to talk about, but focus on the practical side. There is a version that these towers of the stall - furnaces for smelting metals.

TALLIN, 31 Aug — RIA Novosti, Nikolai Adashkevich.
Estonian Prime Minister Yuri Ratas, who is on a visit to the United States, discussed with scientists of the Stanford Research Institute at the University of the same name in California, in particular, the formation of a legal space regarding artificial intelligence, the press service of the Estonian government said on Thursday.
"The State Chancellery of Estonia has convened experts to develop a legislative framework for artificial intelligence.
We are also working on technical solutions that provide security in this rapidly developing and changing area, "said Ratas.
He reported on a pilot project of a self-driving bus, where everyone could ride in the center of Tallinn.
"To date, the pilot project has been completed, but its success means changes in the legal space of Estonia," says Ratas.
According to the Prime Minister, it is necessary to call on the public sector of Estonia, scientific institutions and entrepreneurs to establish international relations so that the republic can serve as a test site for innovative technologies and business models.
“It is often said that Estonia ’ s small size is its problem, but testing new technologies is an advantage, ” the head of the government said.
The Stanford International Research Institute (SRI) at the University is a scientific institution associated with the creation of many key technologies.
In cooperation with the private sector SRI was born, for example, the Internet, a computer mouse, color television, artificial muscles and many other innovative solutions.
At Stanford University, Ratas familiarized himself with the library and archive, which holds one of the most complete collections in North America in the Baltic States, and discussed with university scientists in the format of a round table on the development of technology and modern scientific work.
A separate meeting at Stanford University took place at Ratas' house with former US Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice, and they discussed the domestic and foreign policy of the United States, as well as the relationship between Europe and the United States.
